However, despite these sources of uncertainties, on the basis of our analysis we are confident about claiming the changing-look nature of J140244.51+155956.6, J132111.20+221612.1, and J105606.61+025213.4 being less confident for the remaining ones for the reasons previously described.

gmw_00011006.name: 5BZQ J1321+2216
gmw_00011006.overview: The blazar 5BZQ J1321+2216 was originally classified as a flat-spectrum radio-loud quasar [arXiv:2103.10861]. However, a lower signal-to-noise ratio LAMOST spectrum indicates it may instead be a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2103.10861].

sources.summary: The paper says 5BZQ J1321+2216 was originally classified as BZQ, but its lower-S/N LAMOST spectrum indicates a BZB type, possibly due to observational effects.

quotes.quote: 5BZQ J1321+2216 was originally classified as a BZQ by shaw12 . The LAMOST spectrum, although with a lower signal-to-noise ratio, indicates a BZB source type. However, this difference in classification can be due to observational effects like signal-to-noise ratio.

gmw_00011007.name: 5BZQ J1106+2812
gmw_00011007.overview: 5BZQ J1106+2812 is a blazar that has been reclassified as a BL Lacertae object based on its LAMOST spectrum [arXiv:2103.10861]. Due to a spectral gap in the observations, it is currently identified as a potential changing-look blazar [arXiv:2103.10861].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies 5BZQ J1106+2812 as BZB from the LAMOST spectrum, notes a spectral gap prevents comparison, and labels it a potential changing-look blazar.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of 5BZQ J1106+2812, classified as BZQ by shaw12 , is indeed BZB according to the LAMOST spectrum. However, we can not compare it with the literature spectrum due to the gap in the LAMOST between 5200-5800 . Considering this observational effect, we report it as a potential changing-look blazar.

gmw_00011008.name: IRAS F12115-4656
gmw_00011008.overview: IRAS F12115-4656 is a spiral galaxy with a compact spherical nucleus and disrupted outer spiral arms [arXiv:2104.06790]. The galaxy has a companion located 100 kpc to the southwest and exhibits indicators of active galactic nuclei activity [arXiv:2104.06790].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F12115-4656 as a spiral with a distant companion, AGN indicators, compact nucleus, disrupted arms, and stellar velocity map affected by arm structure.

quotes.quote: IRAS F12115-4656 : This galaxy, classified as SA(rs)b Buta+95 , has a companion at a distance of 100 kpc SW Arp&Madore+87 . Nuclear emission of [ Si VI ] and [ Ca VIII ] was detected in PiquerasLopez+12 , suggesting AGN activity. Although it was not observed with IRAC, we covered the inner 10 kpc with the NACO/Ks image. This galaxy presents a compact spherical nucleus ( 0.3 kpc), whereas the outer kiloparsecs are dominated by disrupted spiral arms. The asymmetry generated by this arm structure strongly affected the centre determination at large apertures >4 kpc, see Fig. fig:morph_12115 ).

The stellar velocity map also reflects this structure. We observed small decrements in the velocity spatially correlated with the regions between the arms, likely because of the slight loss of continuum emission, which leads to less restricted fits where the velocity is slightly biased towards the systemic value.

gmw_00011009.name: NGC 5394
gmw_00011009.overview: NGC 5394 is a companion galaxy to NGC 5395 in the middle stages of a merger [arXiv:1801.04328]. It exhibits strong star formation with a lopsided active star-forming region [arXiv:2201.02270], and its red mid-infrared spectral energy distribution suggests the presence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1801.04328].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5394 as a mid-stage merging companion of NGC 5395, with a large modeled AGN fraction and red mid-infrared SED evidence for an AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 5394 : NGC 5394 is a companion of NGC 5395 in the middle stages of merging at a projected separation of 28 kpc. We measure the AGN contribution at 58 . tob13 stated NGC 5394 as a composite, although L13 did not place an AGN in NGC 5394. The red 4.5--24 \ SED suggests an AGN is present.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5394 has stronger star formation than NGC 5395, a lopsided active star-forming region with central peak SFE, and outer-region line ratios consistent with shocks and H II emission.

quotes.quote: Arp 84 Figure fig_SFR (a) shows that star formation activity in NGC 5394 (top-right) is high compared with NGC 5395 (bottom-left).
The active star-forming region is slightly lopsided toward the nearer side to its companion, although the highest SFE is still at the centre of NGC 5394.
Roche15 indicated that high [ N II ] /H and other line ratios of the outer region of NGC 5394 are explained as a composite of shock and H II region.

gmw_00011010.name: NGC 5395
gmw_00011010.overview: NGC 5395 is a spiral galaxy that acts as the larger companion to NGC 5394 [arXiv:1801.04328]. Classified as a LINER, it features a complex distribution of star-forming regions with a star formation rate peak offset to the northeast [arXiv:1801.04328, arXiv:2201.02270].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5395 as the larger spiral companion of NGC 5394, with LINER classifications but an SED that does not require an AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 5395 : NGC 5395 is the larger spiral companion of NGC 5394. CIGALE fits f_ AGN < 10 to NGC 5395, consistent with both ver10 and L13 calling it a LINER and attributing to it 3--12 of the bolometric and mid-IR luminosities. Nothing in the SED requires an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5395 as having complex star-forming regions, a northeast-offset SFR peak, and two local SFE peaks corresponding to its star-formation peaks.

quotes.quote: NGC 5395 shows the more complicated distribution of star-forming regions.
A peak of SFR locates near the centre of NGC 5395 but 10'' (2.5 kpc) to the north-east.
NGC 5395 has two local peaks in both SFE _ mol and SFE _ gas , which correspond to the peaks of SFR described above.

gmw_00011011.name: CEERS-1163
gmw_00011011.overview: CEERS-1163 is a faint high-redshift galaxy located at a systemic redshift of z=7.448 [arXiv:2301.07072]. It is detected in [O III] and H beta emission lines, but it lacks detectable Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2301.07072].

sources.summary: The paper describes CEERS-1163 as a fainter z=7.448 galaxy near CEERS-698 and CEERS-689, detected in [O III] and H beta but lacking Ly emission.

quotes.quote: This fainter galaxy ( H=26.8 ) is detected in [ O III ] and H , indicating a systemic redshift of z=7.448 that places this system 1.2 pMpc away from CEERS-698 and 3.6 pMpc from CEERS-689. No Ly is seen in the NIRSpec spectrum. Comparing the H flux and the Ly upper limit, we find a 3 upper limit on the Ly escape fraction of <0.145 .

gmw_00011012.name: CEERS-686
gmw_00011012.overview: CEERS-686 is a faint high-redshift galaxy located at a systemic redshift of z = 7.74 [arXiv:2301.07072]. It is a strong Lyman-alpha emitter situated only 0.6 physical megaparsecs away from the galaxy EGS-zs8-1 [arXiv:2301.07072].

sources.summary: The paper describes CEERS-686 as a faint z=7.74 Ly emitter near EGS-zs8-1, with strong Ly transmission and an escape fraction much like lower-redshift emitters.

quotes.quote: CEERS-686 This is one of the fainter galaxies in the CEERS z 7 spectroscopic sample ( H=26.4 ). Jung2022 present Keck/MOSFIRE detection of strong Ly emission at z_ Ly =7.7482 , revealing one of the largest Ly EWs ( 69.1^ +29.8 _ 19.9 ) known at z>7 . The CEERS prism spectrum detects the [ O III ] doublet and H , confirming a systemic redshift of z_ sys =7.74 and placing this galaxy only 0.6 pMpc away from EGS-zs8-1. We also detect Ly (and underlying UV continuum) in the CEERS spectrum, with line flux similar to that measured with MOSFIRE.

The implied Ly EW ( 41.9 1.6 ) is somewhat lower than the Keck measurement, but still consistent within the 1 uncertainties. The Ly /H ratio in the NIRSpec spectrum indicates that a large fraction of the Ly emission is transmitted through the NIRSpec micro-shutter ( 0.325 0.028 ). This confirms that the large Ly EW is not simply due to efficient ionizing photon production, but the transmission is also enhanced. Such strong Ly emitters tend to have a large fraction of their Ly profile escaping near the systemic redshift e.g., Hashimoto2013,Erb2014,Shibuya2014,Tang2021b , but owing to the low resolution of the prism spectrum, we are unable to extract a meaningful Ly velocity offset for this galaxy.

gmw_00011013.name: CEERS-1023
gmw_00011013.overview: CEERS-1023 is a faint red galaxy located at a systemic redshift of z=7.776, placing it 2.0 pMpc away from EGS-zs8-1 [arXiv:2301.07072]. The galaxy exhibits ultraviolet reddening, less extreme ionization conditions, and no detected Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2301.07072].

sources.summary: The paper describes CEERS-1023 as a faint red z=7.776 galaxy near EGS-zs8-1, with less extreme ionization, no detected Ly, and significant UV reddening.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is reasonably faint ( H_ 160 =26.2 ) and red ( =-0.9 ), suggesting significant attenuation. We detect [ O III ] , H , and [ O II ] with NIRSpec, revealing a systemic redshift ( z_ sys =7.776 ) that places this galaxy 2.0 pMpc away from EGS-zs8-1. The observed value of O32 ( 2.80 ) is among the lowest in the sample, suggesting less extreme ionization conditions. If we were to apply a reddening correction consistent with the UV slope, the O32 ratio would be even lower (i.e., O32 =2.31 for E(B-V)=0.16 ).

We do not detect Ly , with the 3 upper limit implying a Ly escape fraction of <0.135 . While deeper spectroscopy will help put more robust constraints on the presence of Ly , the non-detection is not surprising given the very red colors seen in HST . z=8.7 Association of Ly Emitters The EGS contains two very bright galaxies with Ly emission at z 8.7 Zitrin2015,Larson2022 .

gmw_00011014.name: CEERS-1029
gmw_00011014.overview: CEERS-1029 is a bright galaxy located at a systemic redshift of z = 8.610 [arXiv:2301.07072]. It is characterized by a low Lyman-alpha escape fraction [arXiv:2301.07072], a relatively high metallicity [arXiv:2301.07072], and an exceptionally large Lyman-alpha velocity offset of 1938 km/s [arXiv:2301.07072].

sources.summary: The paper describes CEERS-1029 as a bright z=8.610 Ly emitter with low Ly EW and escape fraction, comparatively higher metallicity, and an unusually large Ly velocity offset.

quotes.quote: This bright galaxy ( H_ 160 =25.63 ) was confirmed at z_ Ly =8.665 via detection of Ly emission in Larson2022 . The Ly EW ( 4.7 ) is very low, likely signalling that this galaxy transmits a very small fraction of its line emission. The NIRSpec spectrum confirms the [ O III ] doublet, H , and [ O II ] , revealing a systemic redshift of z_ sys =8.610 with a distance of 3.9 pMpc away from CEERS-1019. This O32 ratio in CEERS-1029 ( 4.81 ) is not as large as most galaxies in our sample.

The BEAGLE models suggest that this galaxy is more metal-rich ( 12+ [ O/H ] =8.01 ) than the other Ly emitters, with a slightly lower ionization parameter ( U =-2.33 ). The NIRSpec spectrum recovers the Ly emission detected in Larson2022 , with nearly identical EW ( 4.2 1.3 ). The transmission of Ly implied by the Ly /H ratio is low ( f_ esc,Ly =0.061 0.034 ), as expected given the very low Ly EW. The velocity offset between Ly and the rest-frame optical lines is extremely large ( 1938 162 km s ^ 1 ; Fig. fig:lya_offset ).

gmw_00011015.name: CEERS-23
gmw_00011015.overview: CEERS-23 is a very faint galaxy at a redshift of z=8.881 [arXiv:2301.07072]. It hosts young stellar populations, exhibits optical emission lines, and is located near the galaxies CEERS-1019 and CEERS-1029 [arXiv:2301.07072].

sources.summary: The paper describes CEERS-23 as a very faint z=8.881 galaxy near CEERS-1019 and CEERS-1029, detected in optical lines with young stellar populations but no Ly detection.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is very faint ( H=28.4 ) and is detected in [ O III ] and H . The systemic redshift measured from rest-frame optical emission lines is z=8.881 , placing this galaxy 5.5 pMpc away from CEERS-1019 and 8.6 pMpc away from CEERS-1029. The NIRCam SED indicates a moderately large [ O III ] +H EW ( 1175 ) and young stellar populations ( 12 Myr). We do not detect Ly emission in the NIRSpec spectrum. By comparing its Ly flux upper limit to H flux we put a 3 upper limit on the Ly escape fraction of <0.422 .

gmw_00011016.name: CEERS-1025
gmw_00011016.overview: CEERS-1025 is a moderately bright galaxy located at a redshift of z = 8.715 [arXiv:2301.07072]. It features very young stellar populations, metal-poor gas, and highly efficient ionizing photon production [arXiv:2301.07072].

sources.summary: The paper describes CEERS-1025 as a z=8.715 galaxy with very young stellar populations, efficient ionizing photon production, metal-poor gas, and moderately high ionization.

quotes.quote: This moderately bright galaxy ( H_ 150 =26.2 ) is covered by NIRCam observations, with an SED indicating very young stellar populations ( 1.6 Myr) and very efficient ionizing photon production ( [ _ ion / erg ^ 1 \ Hz ] =25.8 ). NIRSpec detections of rest-frame optical lines confirm this source at z_ sys =8.715 , with a distance of 1.4 pMpc from CEERS-1019 and 3.9 pMpc from CEERS-1029. Line ratios reveal moderately large O32 ( 8.12 ), with photoionization models suggesting a combination of metal poor gas ( 12+ [ O/H ] =7.75 ) and a reasonably large ionization parameter ( U =-2.13 ). The NIRSpec spectrum does not extend blue enough to provide constraints on Ly in this galaxy, so we cannot place an upper limit on the escape fraction of Ly .

gmw_00011017.name: CEERS-44
gmw_00011017.overview: CEERS-44 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=7.10 [arXiv:2301.07072]. It is characterized by a UV-faint continuum [arXiv:2301.07072], a large Lyman-alpha escape fraction [arXiv:2311.13683], and several nearby spectroscopically confirmed neighbor galaxies [arXiv:2311.13683].

sources.summary: The paper highlights CEERS-44 as the strongest Ly emitter in the NIRSpec sample, outside the known EGS Ly associations, with UV-faint continuum and very large O32.

quotes.quote: The other exception to this discussion is CEERS-44, the strongest Ly emitter in the NIRSpec sample (EW =77.6 ) at z=7.10 . This galaxy is not in one of the known Ly associations in the EGS. It is UV-faint (M _ UV =-19.4 ) with a very large O32 value ( >18.93 ) and strong [ O III ] +H emission is evident in its NIRCam SED (EW =1786 ).

sources.summary: The paper links CEERS-44 with CEERS-80239 in the same field, noting a similar redshift, strong Lyman-alpha emission, large escape fraction, and nearby confirmed galaxies.

quotes.quote: The other new emitter we identify (CEERS-80239) is at a similar redshift as CEERS-44 (separated by 4.7 pMpc), another strong emitter (EW=77 ) in the same field Tang2023 . CEERS-44 also shows a large escape fraction ( = 0.339 0.044 ), perhaps suggesting reduced IGM attenuation to photons at z 7.1-7.2 across the field. Another four galaxies with very similar spectroscopic redshifts and close separations (0.7--3.9 pMpc) from CEERS-80239 and CEERS-44 have been identified in the CEERS program (see Tang2023 and Figure fig:spatial ).

gmw_00011018.name: Grus I
gmw_00011018.overview: Grus I is an extended, elliptical dwarf galaxy located at a distance of 125 kpc [arXiv:2005.06478]. It is characterized by an old, metal-poor stellar population with a low metallicity of [Fe/H] = -1.9 [arXiv:2005.06478].

sources.summary: The paper confirms Grus I as an extended, old, metal-poor dwarf galaxy, revising its distance, luminosity, half-light radius, ellipticity, and structural parameters from deeper Magellan/Megacam photometry.

quotes.quote: For the case of Grus I, we resolved the main sequence turn-off (MSTO) and 2 mags below it. The MSTO can be seen at g_0 24 with a photometric uncertainty of 0.03 mag. We show Grus I to be consistent with an old, metal-poor ( 13.3 Gyr, [ Fe/H ] 1.9 ) dwarf galaxy. We derive updated distance and structural parameters for Grus I using this deep, uniform, wide-field data set. We find an azimuthally averaged half-light radius more than two times larger ( 151^ +21 _ 31 pc; 4 16^ +0.54 _ 0.74 ) and an absolute V band magnitude 4.1 that is 1 magnitude brighter than previous studies.

Grus I is an extended ( r_h 4 16 ), elliptical ( =0.44 ) dwarf galaxy with a distance of 125 kpc. Like other dwarfs, Grus I has an old single, stellar population ( 13.3 Gyr) with low metallicity ( [ Fe/H ] =-1.9 ). Its luminosity ( M_V 4.1 ) and azimuthal half-light radius ( 151 pc) place it at the lower edge of the dwarf locus in the size-luminosity plane (see Figure Fig:sizelum ).

gmw_00011019.name: Indus II
gmw_00011019.overview: Indus II was originally flagged as a candidate ultra-faint dwarf galaxy based on potential blue horizontal branch member stars [arXiv:2005.06478]. However, deeper photometry reveals no coherent stellar population, indicating that the candidate is likely a false-positive caused by a chance alignment of stars [arXiv:2005.06478].

sources.summary: The paper rejects Indus II as a real dwarf galaxy or globular cluster, finding no coherent stellar population and attributing the original candidate to a chance alignment of stars.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Column 8 describes Indus II as found in the discovery paper, . ... _ 2000 (deg) ... 309.76 ... _ 2000 (deg) ... 46.16

quotes.quote: Although our photometry of Indus II is 2-3 magnitudes deeper than the DES Y1 Public release, we find no coherent stellar population at its reported location. The original detection was located in an incomplete region of sky in the DES Y2Q1 data set and was flagged due to potential blue horizontal branch member stars. The best fit isochrone parameters are physically inconsistent with both dwarf galaxies and globular clusters. We conclude that Indus II is likely a false-positive, flagged due to a chance alignment of stars along the line of sight.

In all but the background panels of Figure Fig:hess , the UFD representative old, metal-poor isochrone delineates the HB but fails to match with any other CMD feature. The derived isochrone fails to match the BHB stars that its candidacy hinged on originally. This indicates Indus II is likely a false-positive, i.e., neither a dwarf galaxy nor a globular cluster.

gmw_00011020.name: SDSS J1229+0001
gmw_00011020.overview: SDSS J1229+0001 is an isolated, compact early-type galaxy characterized by a smooth appearance and red color [arXiv:1611.03563]. It exhibits centrally concentrated star-forming activity, a substantial amount of dust, and a light distribution described by two structural components [arXiv:1611.03563].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J1229+0001 as a compact, isolated early-type galaxy with red morphology, central star formation, substantial dust, two structural components, and likely evolution toward a compact quiescent system.

quotes.quote: We report a new compact galaxy, SDSS J122958.84+000138.0 (SDSS J1229+0001), which has unique morphological and stellar population properties that are rare in observations of the nearby universe. SDSS J1229+0001 has an band absolute magnitude (M _ ) and half-light radius (R _ h ) of 17.75 mag and 520 pc, respectively. Located in a fairly low density environment, morphologically it is akin to a typical early-type galaxy as it has a smooth appearance and red colour. But, interestingly, it possesses centrally concentrated star forming activity with a significant amount of dust.

SDSS J1229+0001 also exhibits an unusually high star formation rate and dust mass that compared a typical S0 galaxy. In addition to this, we find that observed light distribution is better described by two component S ersic function. Overall size, half-light radius, measured in H-band is quite small, i.e 290 pc. Given that the H-band is more sensitive to the light of old stars than the optical is, this indicates that the galaxy will likely end up in the compact regime once star formation stops.

gmw_00011021.name: SDSS J154417.02 + 095743.8
gmw_00011021.overview: SDSS J154417.02 + 095743.8 is a bright spiral galaxy with an absolute magnitude of -20.08 mag [arXiv:1601.07368]. It hosted the supernova PTF12gcx and has a high estimated metallicity of 0.75 solar metallicity [arXiv:1601.07368].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J154417.02 + 095743.8 as the spiral host of PTF12gcx, giving photometry, nuclear emission lines, absolute magnitude, and a high metallicity estimate.

quotes.quote: PTF12gcx was hosted by a spiral galaxy, SDSS J154417.02 + 095743.8, characterized by SDSS apparent magnitudes of ugriz= 18.08(0.04), 16.92(0.01), 16.51(0.01), 16.25(0.01), 16.06(0.02) mag. A color image of the galaxy from SDSS is shown in Fig. galaimage (center-left panel). The spectrum of its nucleus (see Fig. galaimage , center-right panel) reveals the same emission lines as shown by the host of PTF09gpn, with the exception of [ O iii ] 4363 and H , which are too faint to be detected in this case. The absolute magnitude of the host galaxy in the band is M_ = 20.08 mag, which is that of a bright spiral galaxy.

Again, by using the value of the absolute magnitude in the band and arcavi10 , we estimated a metallicity of 0.75 Z _ , or 12 + log(O/H) = 8.56. This places PTF12gcx at the highest end of the known metallicity distribution of SN 1987A-like events taddia13 , together with SNe 1998A, , and 2006au.

gmw_00011022.name: J0725 + 5835
gmw_00011022.overview: J0725 + 5835 is an X-shaped radio galaxy associated with a dual jetted-active galactic nucleus system [arXiv:2211.00254]. The system consists of two interacting galaxies separated by a projected distance of 100 kpc, with the main radio host displaying active star-forming mid-infrared colors [arXiv:2211.00254].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0725 + 5835 as an X-shaped radio galaxy associated with a 100 kpc dual jetted-AGN system and evidence for jet reorientation.

quotes.quote: To clarify it, we conducted the European VLBI Network (EVN) 5 GHz observation of an XRG , which resembles the archetypal binary AGNs 0402+379 in radio morphology but it is larger in angular size. In our observation, two milliarcsec (mas) scale radio components with non-thermal radio emission are detected, each of them coincides with an optical counterpart with similar photometric redshift and (optical and infrared) magnitude, corresponding to dual active nuclei. Furthermore, with the improved VLA images, we find a bridge between the two radio cores and a jet bending in the region surrounding the companion galaxy, which further supports the interplay between the main and companion galaxies. In addition, we also report the discovery of an arcsec-scale jet in the companion. Given the projected separation of 100 kpc between the main and companion galaxies, XRG \ is likely associated with a dual jetted-AGN system.

Comparing with the VLA and EVN observations: (1) We find XRG \ is associated with a 100 kpc-scale dual jetted-AGN system, where one of them is Faranoff-Riley type I (FR I), the other one is FR II; (2) We find evidence that the jet in core B is changing its direction, which naturally forms an X-shaped structure in . Two models are discussed on the origin of jet re-orientation, including the sudden flip of black hole spin and jet precession due to the interaction of parsec-scale binary SMBHs.

sources.summary: The paper describes galaxy B as the main radio host in the pair, with active-star-forming mid-infrared colors and star formation likely self-induced by an ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: The mid-infrared magnitudes for the two cores have been collected from AllWISE Data Release 2014yCat.2328....0C as m_ W1, A =15.296 0.038 , m_ W2, A =15.018 0.075 , m_ W3, A =12.200 for core A, and m_ W1, B =15.520 0.041 , m_ W2, B =15.444 0.097 , m_ W3, B =12.089 for core B, which yield the mid-infrared colors m_ W1, A m_ W2, A =0.27 0.08 and m_ W2, A m_ W3, A =2.81 0.07 for galaxy A, and m_ W1, B m_ W2, B =0.07 0.10 and m_ W2, B m_ W3, B =3.35 0.09 for galaxy B. Galaxy A is located in the transition region from early-type spirals or Sa/SBa-type in Hubble's classification scheme, with semiquiescent star formation, see descriptions in 2019ApJS..245...25J to active star-forming galaxies, while galaxy B is located in the region of active star-forming galaxies 2019ApJS..245...25J . It is shown that 100 kpc scale galaxy pairs already have tidal interaction 2011ApJ...737..101L , while it has no help on the AGN activity 2020ApJ...904..107S . Therefore, the star-forming activity in galaxy B is more likely self-induced, e.g. an ongoing merger 2006ApJS..163....1H, 2007A&A...468...61D .

gmw_00011023.name: galaxy A
gmw_00011023.overview: Galaxy A is a massive neighboring companion galaxy that exhibits a compact radio core [arXiv:2211.00254]. It is tidally interacting with its primary companion and displays mid-infrared colors characteristic of a transition region between early-type spirals and active star-forming galaxies [arXiv:2211.00254].

sources.summary: The paper identifies galaxy A as the neighboring companion with a radio core, similar photometric redshift to core B, transition-region mid-infrared colors, and tidal interaction in the pair.

quotes.quote: There is a radio core at the symmetric center of \ driving the large-scale radio jet, while a compact (at arcsec-scales) radio component to its north-east direction is likely a massive neighboring galaxy that could affect the jet shape of the main radio galaxy. In the following, we refer to the radio core of the primary jet as core B and the compact radio component in the north-east direction as core A (see Figure fig:opt ); both core A and core B have Panoramic Survey Telescope and Rapid Response System (Pan-STARRS) counterparts (see Pan-STARRS1 i band image in Figure fig:opt ) and show similar photometric redshift z 0.42 (see Section sec:dual-agn ).

The mid-infrared magnitudes for the two cores have been collected from AllWISE Data Release 2014yCat.2328....0C as m_ W1, A =15.296 0.038 , m_ W2, A =15.018 0.075 , m_ W3, A =12.200 for core A, and m_ W1, B =15.520 0.041 , m_ W2, B =15.444 0.097 , m_ W3, B =12.089 for core B, which yield the mid-infrared colors m_ W1, A m_ W2, A =0.27 0.08 and m_ W2, A m_ W3, A =2.81 0.07 for galaxy A, and m_ W1, B m_ W2, B =0.07 0.10 and m_ W2, B m_ W3, B =3.35 0.09 for galaxy B. Galaxy A is located in the transition region from early-type spirals or Sa/SBa-type in Hubble's classification scheme, with semiquiescent star formation, see descriptions in 2019ApJS..245...25J to active star-forming galaxies, while galaxy B is located in the region of active star-forming galaxies 2019ApJS..245...25J . It is shown that 100 kpc scale galaxy pairs already have tidal interaction 2011ApJ...737..101L , while it has no help on the AGN activity 2020ApJ...904..107S .

gmw_00011024.name: VV 080
gmw_00011024.overview: VV 080 is an asymmetric, intermediate-luminosity irregular galaxy with a clumpy inner structure and a smooth outer boundary [arXiv:astro-ph/0301056]. Active star formation is concentrated in its inner H II regions, which exhibit complex gas motions and a likely gas outflow [arXiv:astro-ph/0301056].

sources.summary: The paper describes VV 080 as an asymmetric irregular galaxy with inner H II regions, modest H I content, complex ionized-gas motions, and a likely star-formation outflow.

quotes.quote: VV 080 is a nearby galaxy with a rather asymmetric outer dumbell-like boundary (which makes it resemble an interacting double system on overexposed images). It has a smooth brightness distribution of outer parts, and a clumpy bright inner structure. With its blue luminosity, this galaxy is an intermediate between normal and dwarf irregulars. The earlier data on its P--V diagram along the major axis gave evidence that this is a single object (Arkhipova et al.  ). Its H ii regions (star formation sites) are concentrated in the inner part of the galaxy with a diameter of 40 \ (2.5 kpc).

Its P--V diagram indicates very complex gas motion in the inner 2 kpc region. The small velocity gradient is interrupted by a negative `wave' with an amplitude of 50  \ in the region of knot ``b'', seemingly revealing gas outflow (shell) from the region of active star formation (SF). The width of the H i 21-cm line W_ 20 of VV 080 exceeds 100  .

gmw_00011025.name: VV 131
gmw_00011025.overview: VV 131 is a small, poorly studied dwarf low-surface-brightness barred spiral galaxy of SBm type [arXiv:astro-ph/0301056]. It is characterized by an unusually high neutral hydrogen content, with an H I mass-to-light ratio that exceeds typical values for normal late-type galaxies by a factor of six [arXiv:astro-ph/0301056].

sources.summary: The paper presents VV 131 as a poorly studied SBm dwarf with uncertain total magnitude, a low-surface-brightness barred-spiral classification, and unusually high H I content.

quotes.quote: VV 131 is a rather small and poorly studied galaxy of SBm type. Its total magnitude is quite uncertain. The LEDA database gives B_ T =17 6 0 2, or corrected B_ =16 8, although there is some evidence that these values may be related only to the inner, brightest part of the galaxy. VV and UGC catalogs give uncorrected m_ b =16 0. This is close to B =16 1, derived from the APM B magnitude, applying the empirical relation between calibrated CCD B magnitudes and APM B magnitudes, obtained by Kniazev et al. ( ).

By analogy, VV 131 may also be classified as a dwarf low surface brightness barred spiral galaxy. According to Schneider et al. ( ), the H i integrated flux of VV 131 is 9.3 Jy , which corresponds to total mass M_ HI =3.45 10 ^9 M . The M_ HI / L_ B ratio for this galaxy is unusually high ( 3 solar units), exceeding characteristic values for normal late-type galaxies by about a factor of six.

gmw_00011026.name: VV 523
gmw_00011026.overview: VV 523 is a clumpy, nearly edge-on irregular galaxy that lacks a noticeable bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0301056]. It forms an interacting triplet with the nearby galaxies NGC 3994 and NGC 3995, exhibiting active star formation and a solid-body-like rotation curve that suggests a strong disturbance [arXiv:astro-ph/0301056].

sources.summary: The paper treats VV 523 as a clumpy, edge-on irregular in an interacting triplet, with galaxy-wide star formation and a solid-body-like rotation curve suggestive of disturbance.

quotes.quote: VV 523 belongs to the category of clumpy irregulars (Casini Heidmann ), which are characterized by very clumpy structure and by active star formation. Together with the nearby NGC 3994 and NGC 3995 it forms a triplet of interacting galaxies. The galaxy is seen nearly edge-on, and unlike most disky galaxies, it does not have a noticeable bulge. By this reason it looks like a chain of small galaxies in contact.

Rotation velocity changes linearly, although some distortions of the P--V diagram with the amplitudes of 30--40 \ are noticeable in the regions of knots ``a'' and ``d'', suggesting the existence of ionized gas shells. The observed velocity distribution along the whole body of VV 523 mimics a solid-body rotation, which is often observed in edge-on late-type galaxies. This linearity may be naturally explained as a result of inner absorption, which hides the H ii regions located near the line of nodes, where the Doppler component of velocity of rotation is maximal. This absorption effect, however, should be small in the outer regions, so that the velocity difference between the opposite edges of the galaxy remains close to twice the maximal velocity of rotation. Note also, that, as shown, e.g., by Keel ( ), the large extent of the rising part of a galaxy rotation curve can be indicative of strong disturbance, which changes its normal shape (usually it looks like a solid-body only in the central part, and further from the center the velocity gradient becomes small).

gmw_00011027.name: LE 850.3
gmw_00011027.overview: LE 850.3 is a high-redshift galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift revised to z=2.694 [arXiv:0903.4017], though earlier estimates suggested a redshift near z=2.12 [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390] or z=2.24 [arXiv:0802.0497]. Its optical morphology features a diffuse tail extending to the south-west, which may indicate two closely aligned objects [arXiv:0802.0497].

sources.summary: The paper highlights Lock850.017 as a discrepant redshift case whose compact optical counterpart, strong Im+Sc photometric fit, and diffuse tail may indicate two aligned objects.

quotes.quote: The only irreconcilable discrepancy is Lock850.017 with z_ phot =1.06^ +0.24 _ 0.06 and z_ spec =2.24 . The optical counterpart to this source coincides exactly with the radio and spectroscopic position, is not blended with any neighbours and is detected with a very high signal to noise at all wavelengths. The photometry is extremely well fit by the Im+Sc template with a reduced ^2 of 0.28 0.10 and a single sharp peak in the probability distribution of the fit. However, the optical morphology shows a relatively diffuse tail extending 3'' to the south-west of the nucleus of this source. A possible cause of the discrepancy could therefore be that there are two closely aligned objects at different redshifts.

sources.summary: The paper identifies LE 850.3 as the brightest H-band target, finds a continuum break and marginal emission lines, and assigns a likely redshift near z=2.12 from the Balmer jump and [O II].

quotes.quote: LE 850.3 This source is the brightest of our targets in the H band ( H=20.4 0.2 in our extraction aperture) and has relatively good signal-to-noise in the clean parts of the spectrum. Our detection algorithm produces two emission lines, both at marginal ( <2.5 ) significance. The lines do not correspond to any plausible pair of transitions, but we note the presence of a clear continuum break at 1.2 m. We confirm the reality of this break by subtracting the spectrum of blank sky at a distance of 1.5 arcsec along the slit (extracted in an identical 1-arcsec aperture). If we identify this break as the Balmer jump, then the bluer of the two emission lines is [ O ii ] at z=2.120 0.004 , consistent with the radio--submm estimate of I02).

sources.summary: The paper revises SMM J105158.02 from an ambiguous z~2.2 identification to z=2.694 based on PAH and deeper UV spectra, strengthening a z~2.7 SMG association.

quotes.quote: We also note that one of the new PAH redshifts, SMM J105158.02, strengthens the z 2.7 SMG association discussed in blain04 .

SMM J105158.02 was identified at z 2.2 first in the UV through various low S/N absorption features. An apparent H detection consistent with z=2.239 was proposed as the true redshift ( swinbank04 ). Re-analysis of more recent, deeper UV-spectra does reveal a suite of absorption features consistent with z=2.694 (Si ii 1260 , O i /Si ii 1303 , C ii 1335 ).

gmw_00011028.name: CUDSS 14.1
gmw_00011028.overview: CUDSS 14.1, also known as CUDSS 14A, is a red, compact galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390]. Deep near-infrared spectroscopy of the galaxy has failed to detect any emission lines above a three-sigma significance level, revealing only four marginal features [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390].

sources.summary: The paper notes CUDSS 14.1, also called CUDSS 14A, is a red compact galaxy; deeper near-infrared spectroscopy still finds no significant lines, only marginal features.

quotes.quote: CUDSS 14.1 This source (also known as CUDSS 14A) was the subject of intense study by Gear et al. \ (2000). High-resolution Hubble Space Telescope and ground-based (Canada--France--Hawaii Telescope) adaptive optics images reveal it to be a red, compact galaxy. Deep near-IR JHK spectroscopy by Gear et al. \ failed to detect any emission lines to a level of 1-- 2 10^ 19 W m^ 2 . Our new spectroscopy pushes this limit almost an order of magnitude fainter, but fails to find any significant ( >3 ) lines. We detect four features (three emission lines, and one absorption line) of marginal significance, however.

gmw_00011029.name: CUDSS 14.3
gmw_00011029.overview: CUDSS 14.3 is a faint infrared galaxy with a featureless spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390]. A slightly bluer, brighter object is located approximately 2 arcseconds to its west-northwest, which also exhibits a featureless spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390].

sources.summary: The paper describes CUDSS 14.3 as a faint source with a featureless spectrum; neither it nor a nearby slightly bluer object yields a redshift, though photometric estimates are discussed.

quotes.quote: CUDSS 14.3 Apart from a spurious peak due to poor subtraction of the unsuppressed OH lines in the range 1.50--1.52 m, there are no significant features in the spectrum of this faint ( H=21.6 0.3 ) object. A brighter, slightly bluer object is located approximately 2 arcsec west and slightly north of CUDSS 14.3 (fig. 4 of Webb et al. \ 2003), whose spectrum is similarly featureless (Fig. fig:comp_spectra ). We are unable to obtain a redshift for either object. We note that both the photometric and radio--submm redshift estimates of C04 are z=1.1 0.3 , at which redshift no strong emission lines would lie within our spectral coverage (H would fall between the J and H spectroscopic windows).

gmw_00011030.name: CUDSS 14.9
gmw_00011030.overview: CUDSS 14.9 is an infrared galaxy with an apparent break in its continuum around 1.2 micrometers [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390]. The galaxy has possible calcium H and K absorption lines that suggest a redshift of approximately 2.87 [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390].

sources.summary: The paper says CUDSS 14.9 resembles LE 850.3 but has a noisier, weaker continuum break; possible Ca H+K absorption suggests z~2.87, though the authors hesitate to assign it.

quotes.quote: CUDSS 14.9 The spectrum of CUDSS 14.9 ( H=20.9 0.2 ) bears a remarkable similarity to that of LE 850.3, with an apparent break in the continuum around 1.2 m. However, the spectrum is noisier and the break is weaker -- if we make an ad hoc definition od the break strength as B = _ 1.21 m ^ 1.25 m S_ d / _ 1.14 m ^ 1.18 m S_ d , we measure B = 1.08 0.37 for CUDSS 14.9, compared to B = 1.47 0.24 for LE 850.3 (or 1.85 0.39 after removal of the [ O ii ] line). Two lines are detected by our algorithm; absorption features at 1.5216 and 1.5374 m with observed equivalent widths of 40 \ and 50 , respectively. The wavelengths of these lines are consistent with the Ca H+K lines at z 2.87 . We are hesitant about assigning this redshift, however, since there is no clear continuum break associated with the absorption lines (although there is insufficient coverage to the blue of them to make an accurate measurement of the continuum level) and the redder of the two features is affected by a night sky emission line.

gmw_00011031.name: ELAIS N2 850.2
gmw_00011031.overview: ELAIS N2 850.2 is an extremely faint high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1101.4027, arXiv:astro-ph/0405390] located at a redshift of z=2.453 [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390]. It exhibits carbon line emission [arXiv:1101.4027], multiple plausible emission features [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390], and clear 1 mm continuum emission [arXiv:1101.4027].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMM J163658+4105 as Elias N2 850.2, reports published CO data, detects its carbon line, and measures possible 2 mm and clear 1 mm continuum emission.

quotes.quote: SMM J163658+4105 This source is also known as Elias N2 850.2 (S _ 850 m =10.7 2.0 mJy, Ivison et al. \ 2002) and CO data are published in Greve et al. \ (2005, J =3) In Greve et al. \ (2005) the source is referred to as SMM J16366+4105 , Tacconi et al. \ (2006, J =3 and J =7) and Ivison et al. \ 2010a ( J =1). The \ line is detected at 0.90 0.18 Jy \ over a velocity width of 600 \ (Figure 6, left). This is the only source in which the 2 mm continuum emission may be marginally detected at S _ 142.6 GHz 0.6 mJy.

If real (as suggested by the good match to the extrapolated SCUBA flux density, Table 2), the continuum emission would contribute 40 to the \ flux density. Given the low S/N of the potential continuum flux, we do not apply this correction to the \ flux in the analysis that follows but note that such a correction would not change the statistical analysis below. The 1 mm continuum emission is detected at S _ 234 GHz =2.0 0.22 mJy (Figure 6, right), in good agreement with the extrapolation from the SCUBA measurement (and consistent with the estimate of Tacconi et al. 2006, of S _ 233 GHz =1.5 0.5 mJy, their Table 1).

sources.summary: The paper describes ELAIS N2 850.2 as extremely faint, with smoothed continuum and multiple plausible emission features yielding z=2.453, consistent with prior redshift estimates and optical work.

quotes.quote: ELAIS N2 850.2 The identification for this source is extremely faint ( H=22.4 0.4 in our spectroscopic aperture) and the continuum is only seen in our 2D spectrum after substantial smoothing. The location of the extraction apertures were determined from the acquistion image. Five plausible emission features (one of which is detected at >3 ) and one absorption feature are detected by our algorithm, although the absorption feature is close to one of the emission lines and may result from imperfect subtraction of the 1.58- m sky emission. The other four emission lines, however, are consistent with a redshift z=2.453 0.006 . This redshift is consistent with tha lack of formally-detected continuum in the J band, since the 4000- \ break will lie between the J and H atmospheric windows, and also with the redshift estimates of I02 and A03.

gmw_00011032.name: ELAIS N2 850.12
gmw_00011032.overview: ELAIS N2 850.12 is a very faint high-redshift infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390]. It has a redshift of approximately z=2.425, which is identified through three emission lines including H-alpha and [O III] [arXiv:astro-ph/0405390].

sources.summary: The paper describes ELAIS N2 850.12 as very faint but with three emission lines; two are identified as H and [O III], giving a cautious redshift of z=2.425.

quotes.quote: ELAIS N2 850.12 This identification for this source is also very faint ( H=22.2 0.4 ) and the signal-to-noise ratio of our spectrum is low. However, we find three emission lines, of which we identify two as H and [ O iii ] 5007 at z = 2.425 0.005 (the absence of detectable [ O iii ] 4959 is simply due to its low flux). This redshift is also consistent with the lack of continuum at the red end of the J band, since it places the 4000- \ break at 1.37 m (we note, however, that continuum is detected at the 3 level in the `clean' part of the J band (1.20--1.25 m). At this redshift, the [ O ii ] 3727 emission line would lie at 1.276 0.001 m, where our sensitivity is low ( 3 7 10^ 20 W m^ 2 ) due to the O _2 night-sky emission; its non-detection is therefore not at odds with a ratio [ O ii ] /H 3 (e.g. \ Kennicutt 1998), especially if there is some reddening. We note that this redshift is unexpected

gmw_00011033.name: NGC 2076
gmw_00011033.overview: NGC 2076 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:0804.4439]. It contains host-galaxy dust that caused substantial reddening to the supernova SN 2003hx, which occurred very close to the galaxy's nucleus [arXiv:0804.4439].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2076 as the lenticular host of SN 2003hx, locating the supernova near its nucleus and attributing strong reddening to host-galaxy dust with unusually small particles.

quotes.quote: Both Sn 2003hx and SN 1994D were hosted by lenticular galaxies NGC 2076 and NGC 4256 respectively.

The supernova occurred very close to the nucleus of the galaxy NGC 2076, we therefore expect substantial reddening due to the host galaxy. The optical spectra demonstrates that SN 2003hx is a highly reddened supernova (refer Section spectra ). Figure ext shows the strong interstellar NaID lines at the rest wavelength of the host galaxy and relatively weak absorption line due to the extinction in the Milky Way. The average equivalent width of the Galactic component of the NaID absorption is 0.54 0.11 , whereas the equivalent width of NaI D absorption due to the host galaxy is 5.02 0.26 .

gmw_00011034.name: SDSS J132533.22+272246.7
gmw_00011034.overview: SDSS J132533.22+272246.7 is a polar-ring galaxy candidate located at a redshift of approximately 0.061 [arXiv:1012.2116]. It features a 5 Gyr old early-type host galaxy surrounded by a faint inner ring and low-surface-brightness stellar debris, indicating an ongoing unequal-mass merger [arXiv:1012.2116].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J132533.22+272246.7, or RG, as a polar-ring galaxy candidate with a 5 Gyr early-type host, faint inclined ring, low-surface-brightness debris, and signs of an ongoing unequal-mass merger.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The object is centred at =13^ h 25^ m 33.2^ s , =+27^ 22 46.7 (J2000)

quotes.quote: We discuss the properties of an object in the Subaru Deep Field (SDF) classified as a galaxy in on-line data bases and revealed on the Subaru images as a genuine polar-ring galaxy (PRG) candidate. We analyse available photometric data and conclude that this object consists of a 5 Gyr old early-type central body surrounded by a faint, narrow inner ring tilted at a 25^ angle relative to the polar axis of the host galaxy. The halo surrounding the main stellar body exhibits a diversity of spatially extended stellar features of low surface brightness, including a faint asymmetric stellar cloud and two prominent loops. These faint features, together with the unperturbed morphology of the central host, are clear signs of a recent coalescence of two highly unequal mass galaxies, most likely a pre-existing early-type galaxy and a close-by gas-rich dwarf galaxy. The presumed stellar remnants observed near the edges of the ring, including possibly the surviving captured companion itself, indicate that the merger is still taking place.

In this paper we present an interpretation of existing observational data of the object SDSS J132533.22+272246.7 (hereafter referred to as RG) identified as a ring galaxy during a supernova survey in the Subaru Deep Field (SDF; Graur et al., in preparation). The object is centred at =13^ h 25^ m 33.2^ s , =+27^ 22 46.7 (J2000) at a redshift of z=0.061 , which puts it at a distance of about 250 Mpc, assuming H_0=73 km sec ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 .

gmw_00011035.name: NGC 1574
gmw_00011035.overview: NGC 1574 is a nearly face-on lenticular galaxy and a member of the Dorado group [arXiv:2304.06117]. It hosts a small-scale nuclear bar and a warped, rotating molecular gas disc extending less than 200 parsecs around its nucleus [arXiv:2304.06117].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1574 as a face-on S0 with a warped rotating CO disc and reports its first SMBH mass measurement from CO dynamics.

quotes.quote: NGC 1574 is a nearly face-on S0 galaxy with a small-scale bar in its nuclear regions ( Phillips96,Lau06,Lau11,Gao19 ; Figure fig:NGC1574_optical , left panel). The galaxy is one of the 46 members of the Dorado group Maia89 .

In NGC 1574, the CO gas is distributed in a disc, extending <200 pc around the optical nucleus of the galaxy. The line-of-sight CO velocity shows that the gas is rotating, but with evident kinematic distortions (i.e. s-shaped iso-velocity contours) that we found arising mainly from a position angle warp. In addition, low-amplitude non-circular motions might also be present ( 20 of the underlying circular velocity). Mild, symmetric velocity increases are clearly detected around the centre of the gas velocity curve, and can be plausibly associated with the Keplerian upturn arising from material orbiting within the SMBH SOI. This, together with an accurate prediction of the gas circular velocity caused by the luminous matter, allowed us to obtain the first measure of the SMBH mass in NGC 1574, M_ BH =(1.0 0.2) 10^ 8 M _ (1 uncertainty).

gmw_00011036.name: WNB1851+5707a
gmw_00011036.overview: WNB1851+5707a is a dying radio galaxy that features steep-spectrum relic lobes [arXiv:1011.0567]. It lacks active radio jets or bright hot spots, possessing only a faint, point-like radio core at the center of its host optical galaxy [arXiv:1011.0567].

sources.summary: The paper resolves WNB1851+5707 into two dying radio galaxies; WNB1851+5707a is the brighter component, with steep-spectrum relic lobes and only a faint central radio feature.

quotes.quote: Our VLA images reveal that WNB1851+5707 is in reality composed by two distinct dying radio galaxies. For source WNB1851+5707a we measured a spectral index =2.2 while WNB1851+5707b we measure =2.3 . This association is a really intriguing fact considering the rarity of this kind of sources. According to the interpretation that these are relic sources, we found that, at the sensitivity limits of our VLA observations, they possess neither radio jets on kpc scale nor bright hot spots inside the fossil lobes. The two hosting galaxy have nearly the same redshifts (see Tab. 1), making the possibility of a spatial casual coincidence even more unlike.

The only detected feature is a faint point-like source with a flux density of 0.4 0.1 mJy at the center of the optical galaxy. There is no evidence of active radio jets. Indeed, most of the flux density of WNB1851+5707a comes from the extended structure already mapped in the VLA observations, confirming that WNB1851+5707a is a ``de-energized'' radio source. The MERLIN image is presented in combination with the VLA A-array at a resolution of 0 4 in the left panel of Fig. 6. The MERLIN+VLA contours reveal that WNB1851+5707a is an amorphous radio source with a linear size of roughly 30 kpc 15 kpc. The radio source is also considerably asymmetric being much more extended to the south of the optical galaxy.

gmw_00011037.name: WNB1851+5707b
gmw_00011037.overview: WNB1851+5707b is a steep-spectrum dying radio galaxy [arXiv:1011.0567]. Very Large Array observations indicate that it lacks both kiloparsec-scale radio jets and bright hot spots within its fossil lobes [arXiv:1011.0567].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WNB1851+5707b as the second steep-spectrum dying radio galaxy in the WNB1851+5707 blend and discusses whether its lobes are separate or linked to WNB1851+5707a.

quotes.quote: Our VLA images reveal that WNB1851+5707 is in reality composed by two distinct dying radio galaxies. For source WNB1851+5707a we measured a spectral index =2.2 while WNB1851+5707b we measure =2.3 . This association is a really intriguing fact considering the rarity of this kind of sources. According to the interpretation that these are relic sources, we found that, at the sensitivity limits of our VLA observations, they possess neither radio jets on kpc scale nor bright hot spots inside the fossil lobes. The two hosting galaxy have nearly the same redshifts (see Tab. 1), making the possibility of a spatial casual coincidence even more unlike.

Thus, it could be that the two dying lobes of WNB1851+5707b are indeed two previous outbursts of WNB1851+5707a that are emerging from the cluster center because of the buoyancy forces. Although this scenario seems less likely, in view of the almost perfect spatial coincidence of WNB1851+5707b with the second galaxy.

gmw_00011038.name: NGC 5493
gmw_00011038.overview: NGC 5493 is a compact and massive early-type galaxy [arXiv:1502.07024]. It features an intermediate-sized disk that does not dominate the galaxy's light at large radii [arXiv:1502.07024].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5493 as a compact, massive early-type galaxy whose whole-galaxy parameters are used because prior bulge-disk decompositions disagree.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC 5493 (Krajnovi et al. \ 2013), the whole galaxy is rather compact ( R_ e = 2.46 kpc) --- although this is the largest system in our sample --- and massive ( M_* = 10^ 11 M_ ) Krajnovi et al. \ (2013) reported that their bulge/disk decomposition for NGC 5493 differed markedly from Laurikainen et al. \ (2010). Given this uncertainty, we use the whole galaxy rather than a result from a decomposition. .

As noted in Section Sec_data , NGC 1277, NGC 1332, NGC 5493 and NGC 5845 are examples of local early-type galaxies where the disk does not dominate the light at large radii, as in proto-typical lenticular galaxies. These disks are an order of magnitude larger than nuclear disks, and referred to as intermediate-sized disks.

gmw_00011039.name: NGC 7137
gmw_00011039.overview: NGC 7137 is a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1012.3471]. Its rotation curve is equally well fit by cored and cuspy dark matter halos, and it displays a nearly symmetric slit-velocity pattern [arXiv:1012.3471].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 7137 with mock halo velocity fields, noting cored and cuspy fits and a symmetric slit-velocity pattern like spherical cuspy models.

quotes.quote: Kuzio08 find the rotation curve of NGC 7137 to be equally fit by cored and cuspy haloes. In Figure LSBslits , the velocities along the slit in NGC 7137 display a close to symmetric (around = 0) 6 km s ^ 1 change in velocity. This behavior is similar to the change in the velocity seen along the spherical cuspy slits in Figure slits .

gmw_00011040.name: SDSS J075139.06+402810.9
gmw_00011040.overview: SDSS J075139.06+402810.9 is an optically quiescent quasar candidate with an early-type galaxy continuum [arXiv:2103.12154, arXiv:2208.01627]. The host galaxy exhibits very little star formation [arXiv:2103.12154], while X-ray observations confirm ongoing, lightly obscured active galactic nucleus accretion [arXiv:2208.01627, arXiv:2401.01937].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J075139.06+402810.9 as a prototypical optically quiescent quasar candidate with MIR AGN evidence, an early-type continuum, and little star formation.

quotes.quote: Here, we seek to formalise this subpopulation of AGN with a prototypical candidate, SDSS J075139.06+402810.9. This shows IR colours typical for AGN, an optical spectrum dominated by an early type galaxy continuum, an 5007 \ limiting flux about two dex below Type 2 quasars at similar IR power, and a k corrected 12 \ quasar-like luminosity of 10 ^ 45 erg s ^ 1 . These characteristics are not consistent with jet and host galaxy dilution.

Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) We use agnfitter calistro_rivera_agnfitter_2016 to fit the SED of J0751 and determine the relative galaxy and AGN system contributions to the observed emission. This method uses the multiwavelength data available to find the most likely weighted contributions to the overall SED, from sources near the AGN and in the host galaxy. Lack of IR data at wavelengths redward of WISE produces some uncertainty on these results, but we can use them to constrain further some properties of the target.

The red colour, presence of an early-type galaxy spectral continuum, and lack of optical emission lines in J0751 (Figure fig:spectracomp ) imply that there is very little star formation. The prominent 4000 \ break also distinguishes J0751 from other featureless systems such as BL Lacs, where continuum emission is dominated by nuclear jet emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents first targeted X-ray observations of SDSS J075139.06+402811.2, confirming ongoing AGN accretion in a lightly obscured, infrared-luminous optically quiescent quasar.

quotes.quote: Here, we present the first targeted simultaneous X-ray observations of an OQQ, our previously identified prototype, SDSS J075139.06+402811.2 at z =0.587. The source is significantly detected over 0.5--16 keV with XMM-Newton and NuSTAR , unambiguously confirming the presence of current accretion activity. Spectral modelling yields an intrinsic luminosity L_ 2-10 keV 4.4 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , well within the AGN regime, but underluminous relative to its infrared power. It is lightly obscured, with log N_ H [ cm ^ 2 ] 22.

We present the first targeted, simultaneous, hard and soft X-ray observations of an optically quiescent quasar, demonstrating unequivocally that OQQ J0751+4028 is an AGN. It is lightly obscured ( N_ H 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) and, while bright enough to confirm the presence of an AGN (L _ 2-10 keV =4.37 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ), our most likely model suggests it is less X-ray luminous than would be expected given its IR properties, and in comparison to an bright population.

sources.summary: The paper confirms OQQ J0751+4028 as an AGN, with rest-frame X-ray luminosity below the infrared expectation, a hard photon index, and Compton-thin obscuration.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:xrayL12comp shows OQQ J0751+4028 in context with the serendipitous observations and the empirical relationship between 12 and intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity. The unobscured rest frame 2-10 keV luminosity is 4.39 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , approximately a factor of 6 lower than expected given the 12 luminosity, but sufficient to categorically demonstrate the presence of an AGN. The photon index is harder than usual for an AGN ( =1.32 ^ +0.21 _ 0.19 ), and obscuration is approximately Compton-thin but present (log ( \ / cm ^ 2 ) = 21.96 ^ +0.16 _ 0.36 ). If is fixed to a more usual AGN-like value of 1.9, the obscuration increased only slightly to log ( N_ H / cm ^ 2 ) = 22.3.

gmw_00011041.name: UDF6462
gmw_00011041.overview: UDF6462 is a clumpy disk galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 1.570 [arXiv:1703.02550]. The galaxy shows no evidence of hosting a strong active galactic nucleus, and its infrared and radio properties are driven by star formation [arXiv:1703.02550].

sources.summary: The paper studies UDF6462 as a z=1.570 clumpy disk, finding concentrated nuclear CO(5-4), star-forming IR/radio properties, and little evidence for a strong AGN.

quotes.quote: The target galaxy, UDF6462 Bournaud+08 , is a clumpy disc galaxy, enabling us to study the molecular content during this crucial phase for galaxy formation. With more than 4h integration time, our observations reach a continuum sensitivity that is roughly a factor of 2-3 times deeper than other studies of the HUDF covering a similar wavelength range but extend over a much larger area, e.g., Dunlop+17,Walter+16 . The paper is organized as follows: in Section sec:AncillaryData we describe the properties and available data sets for the clumpy disc galaxy UDF6462.

UDF6462 is located within the HUDF area and hence has been observed by several spectroscopic and photometric surveys. Moreover, its morphological and stellar population properties have also been investigated by a number of other studies in the the literature Elmegreen_Elmegree2005,Bournaud+08,Guo+12 . Spectroscopic redshifts for UDF6462 were measured from the 3D-HST grism data Momcheva+16 , from the FORS2 follow-up of the Great Observatories Origins Deep Survey South (GOODS-S) field described in Vanzella+2008 and from the SINFONI observations of Bournaud+08 . All measurements are consistent with a z_ spec = 1.570.
From the evidence above, we conclude that UDF6472 does not host a strong AGN and its radio and IR properties can largely be ascribed to pure star formation.

gmw_00011042.name: host galaxy of GRB 050525A
gmw_00011042.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 050525A is a faint, blue star-forming galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0604109, arXiv:1701.02312] with a stellar mass below 10^9.2 solar masses [arXiv:1701.02312]. Its dust-to-gas ratio resembles that of the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:0803.2879], and its dust extinction curve disfavors Milky Way templates [arXiv:0803.2879].

sources.summary: The paper treats the GRB host as a faint contaminating galaxy: it estimates internal extinction, subtracts a blue star-forming-galaxy template normalized to host brightness and color, and sets an R=25.2 upper limit.

quotes.quote: The Galactic extinction towards GRB 050525A is A_R = 0.25 mag (Schlegel et al. 1998). The extinction inside the GRB host is uncertain. Blustin et al. (2005) suggest E(B-V) 0.1 assuming an SMC extinction curve, so that the observed R band (roughly corresponding to the rest-frame B ) suffers an additional A'_R 0.35 mag.

This spectrum, shown in the rest-frame of the GRB as a grey line (Fig. fg:spec_SN ), was smoothed with a 100 wide boxcar filter (blue line) and cleaned from the host galaxy emission features. As indicated by the light curve, this spectrum contains a significant contribution from the host galaxy. We thus subtracted from our spectrum a template of a blue star-forming galaxy (black line), normalized to the host brightness and R-I color. The use of a template (average of 70 blue star-forming galaxies, from Cimatti et al. 2002), was necessary due to the faintness of the host galaxy. An unsuccessful attempt to get its spectrum with VLT was made on 2005 Oct 1.
This observation allowed us to set a robust upper limit to the magnitude of the host ( R = 25.2 0.1 ).

sources.summary: The paper derives the GRB 050525A host-galaxy extinction curve and finds Magellanic-Cloud-like dust behavior, with a dust-to-gas ratio resembling the LMC.

quotes.quote: We focus on GRB 050525A ( z = 0.606 ), for which we also present IR observations from the Spitzer Space Telescope at t = t_ IR 2.3 days post-burst. We construct the spectral energy distribution (SED) of the afterglow at t = t_ IR and use it to derive the dust extinction curve of the host galaxy in 7 optical/UV wavebands. By comparing our derived extinction curve to known templates, we see that the Galactic or Milky Way extinction laws are disfavored versus the Small and Large Magellanic Cloud (SMC and LMC) ones, but that we cannot rule out the presence of a LMC-like 2175 \ bump in our extinction curve. The dust-to-gas ratio present within the host galaxy of GRB 050525A is similar to that found in the LMC, while about 10 to 40 more dust is required if the SMC template is assumed.

In general, the dust-to-gas ratio found in the host galaxy of GRB 050525A resembles that found in the LMC, and is intermediate between the ratios for the SMC and our Galaxy. It is also similar to that found in the circum-nuclear region of AGNs (Maiolino et al. 2001).

sources.summary: The paper identifies the source nearest the XRT/UVOT position as the host galaxy of GRB 050525A, reports host photometry, uses host-frame extinction, and fixes its R-band magnitude at 25.1.

quotes.quote: We detect four faint sources close to the Swift XRT error circle in the J band image. The source closest to the enhanced XRT position http://www.swift.ac.uk/xrt positions/ and UVOT position reported in is indicated in Figure figure2 and is assumed to be the host galaxy of GRB 050525A. The host galaxy photometry is presented in Table table2 .
The model required a Milky Way type additional extinction in the host galaxy frame.
The host galaxy R band magnitude was fixed at 25.1 2006ApJ...642L.103D .

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 050525 host as faint, not a dark-GRB host, below 10^9.2 solar masses, with SFR limits and an FMR metallicity limit of 12+(O/H)<8.1.

quotes.quote: We stress that the five galaxies in the sample for which we could not determine the metallicity (GRB 050318, GRB 050525, GRB 050802, GRB 060908, and GRB 091208B) are all faint galaxies, not hosting dark GRBs, and with stellar masses log(M _ * / M _ ) <9.2 (three of these galaxies have log(M _ * / M _ ) <8.7 ; see Vergani2015,Perley2016 ). A super-solar metallicity for a large portion of these host galaxies is therefore extremely unlikely. For two of these galaxies (GRB 050525 and GRB 050802) SFR limits are available ( Japelj2016 ; Palmerio et al. in preparation). Under the conservative hypothesis that they follow the FMR relation, we can derive limits on their metallicities from their SFR and stellar masses of 12 + ( O / H )<8.1, 8.4 , respectively.

gmw_00011043.name: UGC 3935
gmw_00011043.overview: UGC 3935 is a red spiral galaxy that contains a substantial reservoir of neutral hydrogen [arXiv:2202.01098]. Its structure features star-forming knots within its arms and dust arms that form a spiral pattern cutting across the stellar structure near the core [arXiv:2202.01098].

sources.summary: The paper reports HST structure in UGC 3935, including star-forming and dust spiral arms, substantial neutral hydrogen, and red color not caused solely by gas exhaustion.

quotes.quote: Red spirals One of these, UGC 3935, has been observed. The arms include star-forming knots, while the dust arms include a spiral pattern cutting across stellar structure near the core. This object was included in the MaNGA survey's integral-field spectroscopy Bundy , and in the associated and ongoing H I survey ( HIMANGA1 , HIMANGA2 ), which shows 3.7 10^ 10 M _ of neutral hydrogen. This is close to the derived stellar mass 4.6 10^ 10 M _ from SDSS data using the Portsmouth models PortsmouthModels , so the galaxy's red color is not due purely to gas exhaustion. The HST image is compared to an SDSS color-composite in Fig. fig-UGC3935 .

gmw_00011044.name: SDSS 133145.32+513431.2
gmw_00011044.overview: SDSS 133145.32+513431.2 is a lens-like ring system centered on the brightest galaxy in a group [arXiv:2202.01098]. High-resolution imaging reveals a partial Einstein ring spanning nearly 300 degrees around this central galaxy [arXiv:2202.01098].

sources.summary: The paper highlights SDSS 133145.32+513431.2 as a lens-like ring system whose ACS image reveals a partial Einstein ring and bounds the enclosed group mass.

quotes.quote: Ring features Among these, SDSS 133145.32+513431.2 is especially noteworthy. This object shows a subtle annular color pattern in SDSS and, more clearly, DES data DESDR1 . The ACS image reveals a partial Einstein ring (Fig. fig-ring1331 ), with typical radius 4.0 , and two main segments together spanning nearly 300 ^ around the brightest galaxy in a group (SDSS data give z=0.2894 for this galaxy). An inflection at its northern end is associated with an individual luminous galaxy. While the source redshift remain unknown, pending further information on the lensed source and additional foreground group members, knowing the lens redshift we can bound the group mass enclosed within the Einstein ring (radius 4.0 or 17.5 kpc).

gmw_00011045.name: J1333--3141
gmw_00011045.overview: J1333--3141 is a head-tail radio galaxy associated with the cluster elliptical galaxy MT 4108 [arXiv:astro-ph/0505614]. The galaxy is embedded within the A3562 radio halo, and its radio tail exhibits a smoothly steepening spectral index [arXiv:astro-ph/0505614].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1333--3141 as a head-tail radio galaxy embedded in the A3562 radio halo, associated with MT 4108, and measures its tail spectral index and synchrotron spectrum.

quotes.quote: High resolution images In Figure fig:headtail we present the high resolution images of the centre of A3562, where the head--tail radio galaxy J1333--3141 is clearly visible. Despite the high resolution, residuals of the surrounding diffuse emission from the radio halo are visible at 240 MHz and 332 MHz. J1333--3141 is associated with the cluster elliptical galaxy MT 4108 ( b_J=17.25 , v=14438 km s ^ 1 ), located at a projected distance of 1^ from the cluster dominant cD galaxy (V00).

The spectral index distribution clearly shows that we are dealing with a tailed radio galaxy. The flattest region ( _ 1.38GHz ^ 332 MHz 0 ) is coincident with the core region imaged at high resolution and high frequency in V03, and the significant transverse steepening corresponds to the tail beginning (the peak in the 1.38 GHz image). The spectral index smoothly steepens along the tail up to a value of 1 . The total synchrotron spectrum of the source J1333--3141 in the frequency range 240 MHz--8.46 GHz is shown in Figure fig:spectrum (triangles). The spectrum can be described as a power law with _ 610 MHz ^ 8.46 GHz =0.96 0.04 , with a flattening at frequencies < 332 MHz.

gmw_00011046.name: N: 73
gmw_00011046.overview: N: 73 is a low-mass, star-forming galaxy located on the star-forming main sequence [arXiv:2404.10533]. It exhibits inherently disturbed kinematics, which may be the result of gas-rich interactions such as a merger or the accretion of counter-rotating gas [arXiv:2404.10533].

sources.summary: The paper flags N: 73 as the only possible low-mass slow rotator in its sample, but notes its small size, disturbed kinematics, and uncertain final classification.

quotes.quote: Second, there are basically no slow rotators in the sample, with the exception of one galaxy (N: 73) where our statement holds at a 3 level. Galaxy N: 73 is at z = 0.28, has a stellar mass of 10 ^ 8.4 M _ , a star formation rate of log _ 10 (SFR) = -0.7 M _ yr ^ 1 , and therefore is located on the star-forming main sequence. Even if the value of _ R is seeing-corrected for this galaxy, the system is dominated by the PSF due to its small angular size, and it is still possible that we measure only the upper limit to its stellar angular momentum. This galaxy has inherently disturbed kinematics, as suggestive from the kinemetric analysis (see Section sec:maps ).

This means that N: 73 falls in the group of low mass galaxies where the angular momentum is lowered due to gas-rich interactions, either through accretion of counter-rotating gas, which subsequently forms a counter-rotating stellar components, or a more general gas-rich merger, which perturbs the observed kinematics 2020A&A...635A.129K . While the final stellar kinematics of this galaxy are still uncertain, we can conclude that there are no massive slow rotators in our sample.
Formally our only slow rotator (N: 73) is such a galaxy, but its size is too small for robust determination of its kinematic state.

gmw_00011047.overview: The broad-absorption-line quasar 0043+039 is hosted by a galaxy with a dominant disk-like morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199]. Photometric measurements of the host galaxy reveal a V-K color that is consistent with an old-burst stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199].

sources.summary: The paper compares earlier host measurements for PG0043+039 and finds a disk-like host morphology with M_K=-25.29 and a V-K color consistent with an old-burst stellar population.

quotes.quote: The broad-absorption-line (BAL) quasar PG0043+039 has been subject to 2 previous studies to determine host galaxy properties. It was observed in the i band by Veron-Cetty Woltjer veron-cetty who determined M_i=-23.9 if the host is a disk like ( =1 ) galaxy, or M_i=-24.7 for a spheroidal ( =4 ) galaxy. This quasar was also observed using the wide-field camera on HST by Boyce \ boyce , who used a cross correlation technique to determine that the host was slightly better fit by a disk galaxy with M_V=-21.6 .
We also find that the dominant morphology is disk-like and calculate M_K=-25.29 . The old burst model of Bruzual Charlot bruzual predicts V-K=3.3 which is consistent with the derived V-K=3.7 .

gmw_00011048.overview: The quasar host galaxy 0316 346 is classified as a disk galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199]. It displays evidence of a merger, including tidal tails that extend 20 kiloparsecs to the west of the quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199].

sources.summary: The paper says earlier HST work found merger evidence and a disk-like host for 0316 346; its own modeling also favors a disk galaxy with M_K=-25.44.

quotes.quote: This quasar was previously observed using the wide field camera on HST and the host was found to reveal evidence of a merger, in particular tidal tails extending 20 kpc west of the quasar. Bahcall \ bahcall also provide a 2D fit to the host properties and find that the best-fit host is disk galaxy with M_ V =-22.3 . We also calculate a best-fit disk galaxy and find M_K=-25.44 , giving V-K=3.1 , again consistent with the old burst model of Bruzual Charlot bruzual which predicts V-K=3.3 .

gmw_00011049.overview: The object 1214+180 is a quasar with a surrounding host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199]. Due to a diffraction spike from a nearby star, the basic structural parameters of this host galaxy remain poorly constrained [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199].

sources.summary: The paper reports no previous host-morphology work for 1214+180, notes that a nearby-star diffraction spike limited the fit, and says the galaxy parameters converged but were poorly constrained.

quotes.quote: There have been no previous attempts to determine the morphology of the host galaxy around this quasar possibly due to the nearby star which was utilised in this work to obtain an accurate psf. Unfortunately in our images, a diffraction spike from this star passed close to the quasar reducing the area that could be used to calculate ^2 . Although the modelling converged to give basic galaxy parameters, further analysis of the parameter space revealed that this minimum was not well constrained.

gmw_00011050.overview: The object 1354+213 is a quasar hosted by a galaxy with a low nuclear-to-host luminosity ratio [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199]. Photometric modeling indicates that this host galaxy is dominated by a disk component [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199].

sources.summary: The paper compares a prior residual-host measurement for 1354+213 with its own M_K=-25.2 fit, finding a tightly constrained disk-dominated host and low nuclear-to-host ratio.

quotes.quote: Using a psf subtraction technique, McLeod Rieke mcleod94b found a residual host galaxy with M_K=-25.6 when converted to our cosmology using the K correction from Glazebrook \ glazebrook95 and the apparent colour correction H-K=0.6 (see Section sec:calc_lum ). Our best fit host luminosity was M_K=-25.2 . Analysis shows that the luminosity and the parameter are both tightly constrained by the modelling and the best-fit =0.73 suggests that the host is dominated by a disk component. The rest-frame nuclear-to-host ratio for this quasar is only 6.8 (the apparent nuclear-to-host ratio is 4.6 ), which explains why the derived parameters have small error bars.

gmw_00011051.overview: The quasar 1636+384 is hosted by a galaxy with an approximate K-band absolute magnitude of -23.5 [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199]. This host galaxy exhibits a highly disturbed morphology with non-central light and a merging component to the northwest of the core [arXiv:astro-ph/0002199].

sources.summary: The paper reports the first host luminosity and morphology estimate for 1636+384, finding disturbed noncentral light, a possible merging component, and an approximate M_K=-23.5 host.

quotes.quote: We are not aware of any previous attempts to determine the luminosity and morphology for the host galaxy of quasar 1636+384. Preliminary deconvolution of the light revealed that the excess, non-central light displayed a morphology greatly disturbed from elliptical symmetry (as shown in Fig. fig:disturbed ). The structure includes an excess of light to the NW of the core which is interpreted as a merging component as well as light around the central core which probably originates from the host. From this image it was unclear how to distinguish between the host and the interacting companion, so the luminosity of the host was estimated by summing pixel values excluding the central pixel. This provided an approximate K band absolute magnitude of M_K=-23.5 .

gmw_00011052.name: RGG 7
gmw_00011052.overview: RGG 7 is classified as a disk galaxy that lacks a detectable bulge or pseudobulge [arXiv:2103.06289]. The galaxy features a bar, spiral structure within its disk, and a bright central point source [arXiv:2103.06289].

sources.summary: The paper models RGG 7 as a disk without a detected pseudobulge, noting a bar, spiral structure, and a central point source that may hide a tiny bulge or nuclear cluster.

quotes.quote: In addition to the 26 galaxies which have a disk and a (pseudo)bulge, 3 additional galaxies (RGG 7, 29 and 123) are best modeled with a single S e rsic component and appear to be disks without detectable (pseudo)bulges. However, it should be noted that RGG 7 and RGG 29 each have a bar in addition to the disk component, and RGG 7 also has spiral structure in the disk, which we do not model. There is also a bright point source at the center of these galaxies and it is possible we could have missed a very small bulge component (or nuclear star cluster), even with HST resolution.

gmw_00011053.name: RGG 29
gmw_00011053.overview: RGG 29 is a compact dwarf galaxy best modeled as a single disk with no detectable pseudobulge [arXiv:2103.06289]. The galaxy contains a bar structure and a bright central point source that may conceal a small bulge or nuclear star cluster [arXiv:2103.06289].

sources.summary: The paper models RGG 29 as a compact disk-like dwarf with no detected pseudobulge, a bar, and a central point source that may hide a tiny bulge or nuclear cluster.

quotes.quote: In addition to the 26 galaxies which have a disk and a (pseudo)bulge, 3 additional galaxies (RGG 7, 29 and 123) are best modeled with a single S e rsic component and appear to be disks without detectable (pseudo)bulges. However, it should be noted that RGG 7 and RGG 29 each have a bar in addition to the disk component, and RGG 7 also has spiral structure in the disk, which we do not model. There is also a bright point source at the center of these galaxies and it is possible we could have missed a very small bulge component (or nuclear star cluster), even with HST resolution.
The most compact galaxy overall in our sample is RGG 29, a galaxy best fit with a single S e rsic component with n=1.4 and r_ e 0.5 kpc.

gmw_00011054.name: RGG 40
gmw_00011054.overview: RGG 40 is a dwarf galaxy with an irregular, disturbed morphology [arXiv:2103.06289]. It lacks an obvious photometric center, resembling a Magellanic-type dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:2103.06289].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RGG 40 as an irregular dwarf not successfully modeled by GALFIT, lacking an obvious photometric center and resembling a Magellanic-type dwarf irregular.

quotes.quote: Of the 41 dwarf galaxies in our sample, 6 (15 ) have irregular/disturbed morphologies and were not successfully modeled in GALFIT (RGG 5, 40, 53, 66, 135 and 136). The HST galaxy images are shown in Figure fig:irrimgs . RGG 5, 40 and 136 do not have obvious photometric centers and resemble Magellanic-type dwarf irregulars.
With the exception of RGG 5, the irregular/disturbed dwarf galaxies all fall in the composite region of the [ OIII ] /H vs. \ [ NII ] /H narrow-line diagnostic diagram.

gmw_00011055.name: RGG 136
gmw_00011055.overview: RGG 136 is classified as an irregular dwarf galaxy that lacks an obvious photometric center [arXiv:2103.06289]. The galaxy structurally resembles a Magellanic-type dwarf irregular and falls within the composite region of the narrow-line diagnostic diagram [arXiv:2103.06289].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RGG 136 as an irregular dwarf not successfully modeled by GALFIT, lacking an obvious photometric center and resembling a Magellanic-type dwarf irregular.

quotes.quote: Of the 41 dwarf galaxies in our sample, 6 (15 ) have irregular/disturbed morphologies and were not successfully modeled in GALFIT (RGG 5, 40, 53, 66, 135 and 136). The HST galaxy images are shown in Figure fig:irrimgs . RGG 5, 40 and 136 do not have obvious photometric centers and resemble Magellanic-type dwarf irregulars.
With the exception of RGG 5, the irregular/disturbed dwarf galaxies all fall in the composite region of the [ OIII ] /H vs. \ [ NII ] /H narrow-line diagnostic diagram.

gmw_00011056.name: RGG 53
