gmw_00011056.overview: RGG 53 is a dwarf galaxy classified as having an irregular or disturbed morphology [arXiv:2103.06289]. Although it appears regular at first glance, modeling residuals reveal a spiraled interior structure [arXiv:2103.06289].

sources.summary: The paper treats RGG 53 as an irregular/disturbed dwarf: GALFIT modeling failed, it appears regular at first, but residuals reveal a spiraled interior.

quotes.quote: Of the 41 dwarf galaxies in our sample, 6 (15 ) have irregular/disturbed morphologies and were not successfully modeled in GALFIT (RGG 5, 40, 53, 66, 135 and 136).
The remaining galaxy, RGG 53, looks more regular at first glance. However, a spiraled interior appears in the residuals when attempting to model the system in GALFIT.
With the exception of RGG 5, the irregular/disturbed dwarf galaxies all fall in the composite region of the [ OIII ] /H vs. \ [ NII ] /H narrow-line diagnostic diagram.

gmw_00011057.name: RGG 66
gmw_00011057.overview: RGG 66 is an irregular, disturbed dwarf galaxy that exhibits structural signatures of interactions or mergers [arXiv:2103.06289]. On a narrow-line diagnostic diagram, the galaxy falls within the composite region [arXiv:2103.06289].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RGG 66 as an irregular/disturbed dwarf not successfully modeled by GALFIT, showing interaction or merger signatures and lying in the composite diagnostic region.

quotes.quote: Of the 41 dwarf galaxies in our sample, 6 (15 ) have irregular/disturbed morphologies and were not successfully modeled in GALFIT (RGG 5, 40, 53, 66, 135 and 136).
RGG 66 and 135 show signs of interactions/mergers, with RGG 135 displaying very elongated tidal tails.
With the exception of RGG 5, the irregular/disturbed dwarf galaxies all fall in the composite region of the [ OIII ] /H vs. \ [ NII ] /H narrow-line diagnostic diagram.

gmw_00011058.name: z7 GND 16863
gmw_00011058.overview: The high-redshift galaxy z7 GND 16863 is a massive, UV-bright Lyman-alpha emitter located at a redshift of z=7.60 [arXiv:2009.10092]. It forms a close pair with the neighboring galaxy z7 GND 34204, with a physical separation of 1.15 megaparsecs [arXiv:2009.10092].

sources.summary: The paper presents z7 GND 16863 as a massive, UV-bright z=7.60 Ly emitter in Pair B, close to z7 GND 34204 and likely forming an ionized bubble.

quotes.quote: Also, the other two galaxies (z7 GND 16863 and z7 GND 34204; ``Pair B") form a close pair at z=7.60 with a 1.15 pMpc physical separation. They are separated by only 0.35 pMpc along the LOS, and by 3 2 (0.95 pMpc) in the transverse direction.

Specifically, z7 GND 42912 at z=7.51 (in Pair A) and z7 GND 16863 at z=7.60 (in Pair B) are massive ( M_ * >10^9M_ ) and bright in their UV ( M_ UV =-21.6 and 21.2 ), which could be enough to form 1 pMpc size ionized bubbles around them. As listed in Table tab:MOSFIRE_LAE_spectra , their Ly luminosities are also bright ( > 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) enough to form 1 pMpc size ionized bubbles, based on the model (see their Figure 15).

gmw_00011059.name: z7 GND 34204
gmw_00011059.overview: The high-redshift galaxy z7 GND 34204 is a Lyman-alpha emitter with a large equivalent width of 280 angstroms [arXiv:2009.10092]. It has a ultraviolet luminosity slightly fainter than the characteristic population of galaxies at redshifts of 7 to 8, with no detected indicators of active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2009.10092].

sources.summary: The paper highlights z7 GND 34204 as an extreme high-redshift Ly emitter with EW near 280 A, no detected AGN indicator, and UV luminosity slightly below characteristic z=7-8 galaxies.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, z7 GND 34204 is emitting Ly with EW = 280 \ comparable the typical theoretical limit ( 240 -- 350 ) of Ly emission from star formation Schaerer2003a . Although it has been suggested that Ly fluorescence illuminated by a nearby quasar could contribute to large EW Ly Cantalupo2012a, Rosdahl2012a, Yajima2012a , we did not detect an indicator of AGN activity from z7 GND 34204, for instance, a significant N 5 emission. Also, z7 GND 34204 has M_ UV =-20.4 , comparable but a little fainter than the characteristic population of z 7-8 galaxies of M_ UV 21 , thus it seems that its large Ly EW is less likely due to the AGN activity.

gmw_00011060.name: Galaxy d
gmw_00011060.overview: Galaxy d is a faint foreground object located 1.7 arcseconds from the quasar SDSS J1048+4637 [arXiv:astro-ph/0503202]. It is not very massive and provides only a small gravitational lensing magnification to the background quasar's flux [arXiv:astro-ph/0503202].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy d as the closest foreground object to SDSS J1048+4637, measures its aperture magnitudes, and estimates that it produces only a small lensing magnification.

quotes.quote: For SDSS J1048+4637, the closest galaxy (d) is only 1.7 from the quasar. To measure accurate photometry for this galaxy it was necessary to subtract the bright unresolved quasar from the image (far-right panels of Fig. fig:ims ). Galaxy d has 1.5 aperture magnitudes of i'=25.67 0.05 , z'=24.97 0.11 .

It is clear that, whatever the redshift of this lensing galaxy, it is not very massive and provides only a small magnification to the flux of SDSS J1048+4637. Therefore it makes a negligible difference to the derived luminosity and black hole mass.

gmw_00011061.name: SDSS1133
gmw_00011061.overview: SDSS1133 is a variable object located 800 pc from the center of a low-redshift dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2011.11656]. It is classified as either a recoiling supermassive black hole candidate or a luminous blue variable star undergoing non-terminal outbursts [arXiv:2011.11656].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS1133 as a recoiling-SMBH candidate or possible LBV: it lies offset from a dwarf galaxy, has long variability and unusual spectra, and rebrightened in ZTF.

quotes.quote: SMBH recoil may also be the origin of the variable object SDSS J113323.97+550415.8 (SDSS1133) Koss2014 . This object is 800 pc from the center of a low redshift dwarf galaxy and has displayed AGN-like stochastic variability over >63 years. However, AGN-like variability can be mimicked by long-lived stellar transients Burke2020 and giant stellar outbursts or supernovae such as SN2009ip 2009CBET.1928....1M and UGC 2773 OT2009-1 2009CBET.1931....1B . Spectra of SDSS1133 show the presence of blue-shifted Balmer absorption lines and [ Fe II ] 7155 and [ Ca II ] 7291, 7324 forbidden emission lines which are highly unusual for an AGN. It is therefore possible that SDSS1133 is a luminous blue variable (LBV) star continuing to demonstrate non-terminal outbursts Koss2014 .

Our search strategy detected the variable object and recoiling SMBH candidate SDSS1133 Koss2014 when it re-brightened by 3 magnitudes in ZTF. Follow-up spectra showed the return of a blue continuum, blue-shifted absorption lines and [ Fe II ] 7155 and [ Ca II ] 7291, 7324 forbidden lines, suggesting that the source may be either a luminous blue variable star which continues to show non-terminal outbursts or a recoiling AGN which continues to show variability.

gmw_00011062.name: J113924.74 + 164144.0
gmw_00011062.overview: J113924.74 + 164144.0 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0693 [arXiv:1006.4163]. It exhibits tidal-tail-like extended optical features and a significantly offset, extended neutral hydrogen distribution that suggests a possible interaction with a neighboring spiral galaxy [arXiv:1006.4163].

sources.summary: The paper presents GMRT H i observations of J113924.74 + 164144.0, finding disturbed, offset H i and tidal-tail-like optical features interpreted as possible interaction signatures.

quotes.quote: J113924.74 + 164144.0 is an interesting galaxy at z = 0.0693 , i.e. D_L 305 Mpc, with tidal-tail-like extended optical features on both sides. There are two neighbouring galaxies, a spiral galaxy J113922.85 + 164136.3 which has a strikingly similar `tidal' morphology, and a faint galaxy J113923.58 + 164129.9. We report H i 21 cm observations of this field to search for signatures of possible interaction. Narrow H i emission is detected from J113924.74 + 164144.0, but J113922.85 + 164136.3 shows no detectable emission. The total H i mass detected in J113924.74 + 164144.0 is 7.7 10^9 M _ . The H i emission from the galaxy is found to be extended and significantly offset from the optical position of the galaxy. We interpret this as signature of possible interaction with the neighbouring spiral galaxy.

gmw_00011063.name: J113922.85 + 164136.3
gmw_00011063.overview: J113922.85 + 164136.3 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits a tidal morphology with extended tail-like features [arXiv:1006.4163]. Although it is a neighboring galaxy involved in a possible interaction, no H I emission has been detected from it [arXiv:1006.4163].

sources.summary: The paper describes J113922.85 + 164136.3 as a neighboring spiral with tidal morphology similar to the main galaxy but no detected H i emission in the GMRT data.

quotes.quote: There are two neighbouring galaxies, a spiral galaxy J113922.85 + 164136.3 which has a strikingly similar `tidal' morphology, and a faint galaxy J113923.58 + 164129.9. We report H i 21 cm observations of this field to search for signatures of possible interaction. Narrow H i emission is detected from J113924.74 + 164144.0, but J113922.85 + 164136.3 shows no detectable emission. The total H i mass detected in J113924.74 + 164144.0 is 7.7 10^9 M _ . The H i emission from the galaxy is found to be extended and significantly offset from the optical position of the galaxy. We interpret this as signature of possible interaction with the neighbouring spiral galaxy.
The small spiral galaxy-2 looks like a scaled down image of galaxy-1 with similar tidal-tail-like extended features.

gmw_00011064.name: J113952.31 + 164531.8
gmw_00011064.overview: J113952.31 + 164531.8 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0680 [arXiv:1006.4163]. It has a close neighboring galaxy, a disturbed optical morphology, and a possible neutral hydrogen detection that suggests it belongs to a loose group [arXiv:1006.4163].

sources.summary: The paper reports a possible H i detection for J113952.31 + 164531.8, noting disturbed optical morphology, a close neighbor, and possible group interaction at similar redshift.

quotes.quote: There is also a possible detection of H i emission from another nearby galaxy J113952.31 + 164531.8 at z = 0.0680 at a projected distance of 600 kpc, and with a total H i mass of 5.3 10^9 M _ , suggesting that all these galaxies form a loose group at z 0.069 .

Figure fig:gp5 shows the H i emission spectrum for galaxy-7 with a spectral resolution of 45 km s ^ 1 and a > 4 peak flux density of 2.8 mJy. This marginally significant emission feature at the expected systemic velocity of the galaxy coinciding with the optical position makes it most likely to be H i emission from this galaxy. The emission profile shows weak indication of asymmetry and the total estimated H i mass is 5.3 1.3 10^9 M _ for a luminosity distance of 299 Mpc quoted in NED.

About 7 away from these sources there is another galaxy J113952.31 + 164531.8 at z = 0.0680 with a close neighbour J113952.02 + 164514.8. There is a possible H i detection of the galaxy J113952.31 + 164531.8 with an estimated H i mass of about 5.3 10^9 M _ .

gmw_00011065.name: 2MASX J0423+0408
gmw_00011065.overview: 2MASX J0423+0408 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy currently undergoing a merger [arXiv:2010.13806]. The system features unusually large radio lobes and a pseudo-jet morphology driven by active galactic nucleus winds impacting the interstellar medium [arXiv:2010.13806].

sources.summary: The paper highlights 2MASX J0423+0408 as a BAT AGN host with unusually large radio lobes, merger activity, and wind-driven shocks producing pseudo-jet morphology.

quotes.quote: The case of 2MASX J0423+0408 is perhaps surprising. It has by far the largest jets in the sample, with lobes extending far outside the host galaxy, but resides squarely within the main sequence. One might expect that the star formation rate would be highly suppressed in such a target. This galaxy has been recently studied in great multi-wavelength detail by Fischer2019 , where we found that high gas velocity kinematics at large radii are likely due to mechanical driving from AGN winds impacting the ISM.

The interaction shocks the ISM, producing thermal X-ray emission and cosmic rays, which in turn promote the formation of the observed S-shaped radio structure. The result is a pseudo-jet morphology along the high density ISM lanes. The object is also undergoing a merger, which may be enhancing the star formation (and may also have triggered the outflow).

gmw_00011066.name: NGC5846-UDG1
gmw_00011066.overview: NGC5846-UDG1 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located within the NGC 5846 group [arXiv:2111.14851, arXiv:2309.15148]. It is highly enriched in globular clusters, which comprise approximately 13% of the galaxy's total light and may have contributed to its formation [arXiv:2111.14851, arXiv:2309.15148].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC5846-UDG1 is an extreme globular-cluster-rich ultra-diffuse galaxy whose stars likely formed mostly in massive dense clusters rather than diffuse star formation.

quotes.quote: The UDG NGC5846-UDG1 is among the most extreme examples of globular cluster-rich galaxies found so far. Here we present new Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) observations and analysis of this galaxy and its globular cluster system. We find that NGC5846-UDG1 hosts 54 9 globular clusters, three to four times more than any previously known galaxy with a similar luminosity, and higher than reported in previous studies. With a galaxy luminosity of L_ V, gal 6 10^7 L _ ( M_ 1.2 10^8 M _ ) and a total globular cluster luminosity of L_ V, GCs 7.6 10^6 L _ , we find that the clusters currently comprise 13 of the total light.

NGC5846-UDG1 is the first known galaxy with a present-day globular cluster-to-field stars mass ratio that is 10 with >90 confidence. Fractions that are this high have important implications for formation models of the galaxy, while providing new constraints on globular cluster mass loss.

sources.summary: NGC 5846-UDG1 is a group UDG with prior MUSE spectroscopy, reported old metal-poor stellar population, no line-index measurement here, and possible cluster-disruption origin.

quotes.quote: Details about each observation can be found in the original papers presenting the dynamical and kinematic analysis of those UDGs: the Virgo cluster UDG VCC 1287 Gannon2020G , NGC 5846-UDG1 in the NGC 5846 group Forbes2021 , UDG1137+16 from the UGC 6594 group Gannon2021 , several UDGs in the Perseus cluster (i.e., PUDG-R15, PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84 and PUDG-R24; Gannon2022 ), and Yagi358 in the Coma cluster Gannon2022b . While this does not have any impact on the full spectral fitting procedure detailed below, it means that we can not perform a line index measurement for VCC 1287, NGC 5846-UDG1, UDG1137+16, PUDG-R24 and PUDG-S74. NGC 5846-UDG1 was previously studied in Mueller2020 using MUSE spectroscopy with a slightly lower S/N than our new data (see also Heesters2023 ), and DF07 in Gu2017 from MaNGA data.

These works reported a [ Fe/H ] =--1.33 0.19 dex and an age of 11.2 1.5 for NGC 5846-UDG1 and [ Fe/H ] =--1.03 0.31 dex and an age of 9 1.5 Gyr for DF07. Some works suggest that UDGs form in the same epoch as the GCs (i.e., the failed-galaxy type; Forbes2020c ) or that they may even form entirely from disrupted clusters (i.e., NGC 5846-UDG1; Danieli2022 ), so any UDG that shares similar properties as GCs could be considered of this type.

gmw_00011067.name: J1512 + 3050
gmw_00011067.overview: J1512 + 3050 is an isolated elliptical galaxy with a high stellar mass [arXiv:2306.01050]. It hosts a LINER-type active galactic nucleus and contains kinematically unsettled neutral gas, as indicated by a multiple-peak absorption line profile [arXiv:2306.01050].

sources.summary: J1512 + 3050 is presented as an isolated massive elliptical LINER host whose redshifted, multiple-peak absorption line indicates kinematically unsettled neutral gas.

quotes.quote: The source J1512 + 3050 is an isolated galaxy with the spectroscopic redshift of z=0.08963 0.00002 Ahumada2020 . The host galaxy is an elliptical galaxy with a high stellar mass of log (M_ /M_ )=11.7 Salim2016 . Classified as a LINER-type AGN Toba2014 , J1512 + 3050 has a 1.4 GHz continuum flux of 27.8 mJy Condon1998 , corresponding to a radio power at 1.4 GHz of log (P_ 1.4 GHz / W Hz ^ 1 )=23.74 .

As shown in Figure fig:abs_profile , the \ absorption line profile of J1512 + 3050 is Doppler corrected based on the optical redshift of the host galaxy. The multiple-peak profile of the \ absorption line suggests the \ gas is kinematically unsettled. A multicomponent Gaussian fit yields the peak optical depth _ peak =0.107 0.019 and the integrated optical depth _ int =8.81 0.81 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00011068.name: J1558 + 2759
gmw_00011068.overview: J1558 + 2759 is an interacting galaxy pair that hosts a low-power radio active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2306.01050]. The system exhibits a narrow, redshifted absorption profile, which may trace infalling gas driven by the ongoing merger process [arXiv:2306.01050].

sources.summary: J1558 + 2759 is included as an interacting galaxy-pair absorber with a low-power radio AGN; its narrow redshifted absorption may trace infalling merger-driven gas.

quotes.quote: In the following analysis, we also include an \ absorber in an interacting galaxy pair J1558 + 2759 previously discovered by FAST Yu2022 to investigate the gas properties and origins in Section sec:dis . This \ absorption line has a low-power radio AGN as a background continuum source similar to these three absorbers. Therefore, we discuss the \ gas properties using the new FAST observations and previous data in .

Similarly, the absorption profile of J1558 + 2759 is narrow and redshifted, which can be contributed either by gas fueling or galactic gas clouds. Observations of \ absorptions in mergers have detected significant systemic velocity redshift, which suggests the signature of gas infall Dutta2019 . Since J1558 + 2759 is an interacting source, gas driven by the merging process could infall toward the AGN, which may connect with the gas fueling onto the SMBH Srianand2015 .

gmw_00011069.name: G4Jy 1518
gmw_00011069.overview: G4Jy 1518 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.226 [arXiv:2309.10956]. Archival radio maps indicate that it is a restarted radio galaxy, and X-ray observations reveal clear extended emission around the source [arXiv:2309.10956].

sources.summary: The paper reports clear extended X-ray emission around G4Jy 1518, classifies it as a restarted radio galaxy at z = 0.226, and confirms its host-galaxy identification.

quotes.quote: We found clear signatures of extended X-ray emission around two radio sources in the XRT images : G4Jy 1518 and G4Jy 1664. G4Jy 1518 belongs to the equatorial sample of Best et al. (1999) and it is classified as a radio galaxy lying at z =0.226. It is also listed in the Molonglo Reference Catalogue of radio sources MRC 1912-269 large81 . From a radio perspective, G4Jy 1518 appears to be a ``restarted'' radio galaxy , as shown in the archival radio maps of Figure fig:ext . The host-galaxy identification is consistent with detection of the radio core, as recently shown using MeerKAT observations sejake23 .

gmw_00011070.name: G4Jy 1664
gmw_00011070.overview: G4Jy 1664 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.15662 [arXiv:2309.10956]. It exhibits a classical Fanaroff-Riley II radio morphology, and its radio core emission is similar to Giga-Peaked radio sources [arXiv:2309.10956].

sources.summary: The paper reports clear extended X-ray emission around G4Jy 1664, identifies it as a radio galaxy with classical FR II morphology, and notes its radio core properties.

quotes.quote: G4Jy 1664 is a radio galaxy at z =0.15662 a.k.a. 6dF J2056043-195635 stickel94,jones09 with a classical FR II radio morphology, as shown in Figure fig:ext2 . The redshift estimate was originally derived from stellar absorption features, clearly present in its optical spectrum. The radio core emission appears to be similar to Giga-Peaked radio Sources odea91 .

gmw_00011071.name: G4Jy 1863
gmw_00011071.overview: G4Jy 1863 is a narrow-line giant radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0959 [arXiv:2309.10956]. Its optical structure displays several signatures of a past merger, including irregular shells and two faint arcs, while its radio lobes have detected X-ray counterparts [arXiv:2309.10956].

sources.summary: The paper describes G4Jy 1863 as a narrow-line giant radio galaxy with merger signatures and claims X-ray counterpart detections for its radio lobes.

quotes.quote: An intriguing case is G4Jy 1863, a narrow-line hard X-ray selected giant radio galaxy cusumano10,oh18,bruni20 at z =0.0959 tritton72,danziger83,ramos11 lying in the direction of the galaxy cluster Abell 4067 abell58,abell89,teague90 and shown in Figure fig:G4Jy1863ext . G4Jy 1863 shows several signatures of a past merger in its optical image, for instance it has several irregular shells and two faint arcs ramos11 . In Figure fig:G4Jy1863ext the red circles mark the positions of detected X-ray sources close to the radio structure of G4Jy 1863, and in particular those labelled as S1 and S2. These two point-like sources have a clear optical and mid-infrared counterpart, as shown in the central and in the right panel of the same figure. The proximity of these two X-ray sources prevented us from claiming the presence of extended X-ray emission according to the procedure previously described.

However, considering the number of photons associated with both the northern and the southern radio lobes, in circular regions of 20 pixel radius, in comparison with those expected in the background for the same area, we claim a detection above 3 level of confidence for the X-ray counterparts of its radio lobes.

gmw_00011072.name: CRISTAL-21
gmw_00011072.overview: CRISTAL-21, also known as HZ7, is a normal main-sequence star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.25 [arXiv:2210.10023]. It is a very extended source [arXiv:2311.17671] with a carbon emission halo that is roughly twice the size of its rest-frame ultraviolet emission [arXiv:2210.10023].

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution ALMA observations of HZ7, a normal z=5.25 main-sequence galaxy, finding extended [CII] emission roughly twice the rest-frame UV size.

quotes.quote: Here we present Atacama Large Millimeter/Submillimeter Array (ALMA) 0.3" resolution observations of the [ CII ] emission line at 158 m of HZ7, a main sequence galaxy at z=5.25 . Comparing to archival rest-frame UV observations taken by the Hubble Space Telescope (HST), we find strong evidence of the existence of extended [ CII ] emission, which we estimate to be twice the size of the rest-frame UV emission, yielding one of the first high-redshift objects where a clear signature of a [ CII ] ``Halo'' has been detected to date. For a matched Sérsic profile with n = 1, we measured a [ CII ] effective radius of 0.50 0.04 " (3.07 0.25 kpc) and an average rest-frame UV effective radius of 0.2 0.04 " ( 1.48 0.16 kpc).

In this paper we study the observations of HZ7 in particular, one of the brightest objects in the sample, with a rest-frame UV spectroscopic redshift of z = 5.2532±0.0004 Capak2015 . This galaxy seems to be a ``normal'' galaxy based on its location with respect to the main sequence of star forming galaxies (near L^ * ), at those redshifts Capak2015, Speagle2014 . Furthermore, it has good multiwavelength coverage Leauthaud2007, Capak2015, ANA2022 , which makes it an excellent target.

sources.summary: The paper identifies CRISTAL-21 as a very extended source whose visibility-fitting flux density is inconsistent at short uv distance, improves with tapering, and is excluded from stacking because of its size.

quotes.quote: We find the visibility fitting recovers the flux densities well in various sources from the brightest to the faintest among the CRISTAL galaxies, except for a very extended source (CRISTAL-21). The inconsistency of flux densities in CRISTAL-21 likely originates from extrapolation of the best-fit model toward the short uv distance regime due to a low sensitivity at 50 k . When the additional 2'' taper is applied to CRISTAL-21, we find a better match with the visibility-based estimation.
We did not use the other two galaxies (CRISTAL-03 and 21) because CRISTAL-03 is not resolved and CRISTAL-21 has extremely extended sizes therefore they may affect stacked sizes strongly.

gmw_00011073.name: KUV 08086+4037
gmw_00011073.overview: KUV 08086+4037 is a broad emission line quasar located at a redshift of z=1.78 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603121]. The galaxy has been detected at 350 microns and 1.2 mm, with a radio-to-millimeter flux ratio characteristic of a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603121].

sources.summary: The paper detects KUV 08086+4037 at 350 microns, describes its radio-to-mm ratio as star-forming, and attributes a small positional offset to pointing rather than a different source.

quotes.quote: KUV 08086+4037 This z=1.78 broad emission line quasar was discovered by Darling1996 and detected at 1.2 mm by Omont2003 . The mm to 1.4 GHz radio continuum flux ratio is compatible with a star-forming galaxy Petric et al. (2006, in preparation). We detect KUV 08086+4037 at 350 with a flux density of 69 11 \ mJy . The peak emission of KUV 08086+4037 is shifted by 3 to 4 \ from the optical or radio position, corresponding to 2 of the pointing accuracy of the CSO. However, it is unlikely that the detected emission does not come from the QSO, and the offset is probably due to a pointing model problem.

gmw_00011074.name: HS 1002+4400
gmw_00011074.overview: HS 1002+4400 is a quasar at redshift z=2.08 that displays broad emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0603121]. Its radio to millimeter continuum flux ratio is consistent with starburst emission, and it has been detected at 350 microns [arXiv:astro-ph/0603121].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HS 1002+4400 as a z=2.08 quasar with broad emission lines, radio/mm properties consistent with starburst emission, and a 350 micron detection.

quotes.quote: HS 1002+4400 is a quasar at z=2.08 which was first discovered in the Hamburg survey . It displays broad emission lines with no peculiar features, and it was detected at 1.2 mm by and in the 1.4 GHz radio continuum by Petric2005 . The radio to mm continuum flux ratio is consistent with that of a starburst. HS 1002+4400 is detected at 350 with a flux density of 77 14 \ mJy .

gmw_00011075.name: J140955.5+562827
gmw_00011075.overview: J140955.5+562827 is an optically bright, radio-quiet quasar at a redshift of z=2.56 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603121]. The galaxy hosts a massive reservoir of warm, dense molecular gas and exhibits starburst-like radio emission driven by a high star formation rate [arXiv:astro-ph/0603121].

sources.summary: The paper presents J140955.5+562827 as a bright radio-quiet quasar with massive warm molecular gas, high far-infrared luminosity, starburst-like radio emission, and strong 350 micron flux.

quotes.quote: J140955.5+562827 , hereafter refered to as J1409+5628, at z=2.56 is an optically very bright, radio-quiet quasar. It is by far the strongest mm source in the Omont2003 MAMBO survey of z 2 quasars. It has a massive reservoir of warm and dense molecular gas which is detected in CO Beelen2004 and HCN Carilli2005 , with an estimated mass of 6 10^ 10 \ M_ . Its far-IR luminosity of 4 10^ 13 L_ implies a star formation rate of several 1000 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 Beelen2004 .

The radio flux density is consistent with the radio-FIR correlation for star-forming galaxies Petric et al. (2006, in preparation). High resolution VLBA observations resolve out the radio emission, implying an intrinsic brightness temperature of 10^5 \ K at 8 GHz, which is typical for nuclear starbursts Beelen2004 . The 350 flux density of 112 12 \ mJy places J1409+5628 among the strongest submm high- z sources.

gmw_00011076.name: PSS 2322+1944
gmw_00011076.overview: PSS 2322+1944 is an optically luminous, strongly gravitationally lensed quasar at a redshift of 4.12 [arXiv:astro-ph/0608171, arXiv:astro-ph/0603121]. Its carbon monoxide emission is resolved into an Einstein ring [arXiv:0809.0939, arXiv:astro-ph/0608171], which traces a starburst disk of molecular gas surrounding the active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0608171, arXiv:astro-ph/0603121].

sources.summary: The thesis describes PSS2322+1944 as a z=4.12 molecular Einstein Ring whose CO images enable lens inversion and study of molecular gas dynamics in the quasar host galaxy.

quotes.quote: A Molecular Einstein Ring at z= 4.12: Imaging the Quasar Host Galaxy of PSS2322+1944 Through a Cosmic Lens OK, one last time. These are small . But the ones out there are far away . Small... far away... ah forget it- Father Ted The fourth paper in this thesis is a study of a radio Einstein Ring carilli at redshift 4.12, seen in the wavelength of the Carbon Monoxide 2--1 transition. The data consisted of seven images at slightly different frequencies, and the image of the ring changes with frequency due to velocity structure of the emitting CO gas cloud. Thus, there is not one single unknown source, but seven, all lensed by a common mass profile. CO is of particular interest because it is able to exist in the same conditions (temperature, etc) as molecular hydrogen - the raw fuel for star formation and the powering of active galactic nuclei.

For the radio (carbon monoxide) Einstein Ring PSS2322+1944 at redshift of 4.12, the spectacular multi-wavelength source reconstruction presented in this thesis is the first inversion using the recent new observations by 2007AAS...211.4502R . The lens inversion for this system is a necessary step towards studying the complex dynamics of the molecular gas in the source galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes J2322+1944 as a strongly lensed high-redshift QSO whose CO Einstein ring traces a starburst disk surrounding the quasar.

quotes.quote: 1 we show the CO emission from the z = 4.12 QSO J2322+1944. The source is strongly gravitationally lensed, appearing as a double QSO in the optical with 1 '' separation (Djorgovski et al. \ in prep), and a complete 'Einstein ring' in the CO 2-1 emission (Carilli et al. \ 2003). This molecular ring makes J2322+1944 one of the most stunning CO images at high redshift.

Quite importantly, a similar ring is seen for the 1.4 GHz continuum emission (Carilli et al. \ 2003). These observations have been modeled (by the above mentioned authors) in the source plane as a starburst disk surrounding the QSO with a radius of about 2 kpc. J2322+1944 thus represents perhaps the best example of a coeval starburst+AGN at high redshift.

sources.summary: The paper describes PSS 2322+1944 as a lensed z=4.12 quasar with dust, radio, CO detections, a massive molecular reservoir, ring-like CO emission, and 350 micron detection.

quotes.quote: PSS 2322+1944 is an optically luminous, lensed (magnification a=3.5 ) quasar at z=4.12 . It was detected in mm dust and radio continuum, and in various CO emission lines Omont2001, Cox2002, Carilli2002 . With a far-IR luminosity of 9 10^ 12 L_ (corrected for lensing), it harbours a massive reservoir of molecular gas detected in CO, where star formation takes place with a rate of 1000 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 .

The CO line emission is resolved into an Einstein ring, which can be modeled as a disk of dense and warm molecular gas surrounding the QSO with a radius of 2 kpc , tracing a dynamical mass of a few 10^ 10 \ M_ Carilli2003 . PSS 2322+1944 is clearly detected at 350 with a flux density of 79 11 \ mJy .

gmw_00011077.name: NGC 5953/5954
gmw_00011077.overview: NGC 5953/5954 is an interacting binary system consisting of an S0/a and Scd galaxy pair that is transferring material [arXiv:0803.3003]. The system has a high infrared luminosity and hosts active nuclei, with NGC 5953 classified as a Seyfert 2 and NGC 5954 as a LINER [arXiv:0803.3003].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5953/5954 as an interacting, infrared-luminous S0/a+Scd galaxy pair with active nuclei, material transfer, and fewer detected ULXs than expected from its star formation rate.

quotes.quote: A luminous X-ray source has been discovered in the NGC 5953/5954 system. NGC 5953/5954 is an interacting pair of galaxies with high infrared luminosity (L _ IR 10 ^ 44 ergs s ^ 1 , with an estimated total star formation rate (SFR) > 4.5 yr ^ 1 ; Kennicutt 1998). Given the high SFR of this system and the correlation between the SFR of a galaxy and the number of ULXs hosted by that galaxy (Swartz et al. 2004), there should be, at least 3 to 4 ULXs in this galaxy pair. However, we have detected only one ULX, which is above the average luminosity (L _ X > 10 ^ 40 ergs s ^ 1 ). Thus, the expected ULX population of this galaxy is not present (Gilfanov et al. 2003).

NGC 5953/5954, is a binary system with an (S0/a + Scd) pair of galaxies (KPG 468 from Karachentsev 1972), which are transferring material (Domingue et al. 2003; Hern a ndez-Toledo et al. 2003). Both galaxies host an active nucleus: NGC 5953 is a Seyfert 2 and NGC 5954 is a LINER. These two galaxies are separated by a projected distance of 6.2 kpc (Hern a ndez-Toledo et al. 2003).

gmw_00011078.name: 2XMM J112328.0+052823
gmw_00011078.overview: 2XMM J112328.0+052823 is an active galactic nucleus that is potentially classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1109.2069]. Its optical spectrum displays clear stellar absorption features from the host galaxy component [arXiv:1109.2069].

sources.summary: The paper treats 2XMM J112328.0+052823 as a possible NLS1 whose host subtraction improves the optical continuum fit and whose spectrum shows stellar absorption features.

quotes.quote: We identify 10 12 NLS1s in our sample Although 2XMM J112328.0+052823 and 1E 1346+26.7 have H FWHMs of 2000 km s^ 1 , 2050 km s^ 1 respectively, they both have H FWHM of 1700 km s^ 1 , and also share other NLS1's spectral characteristics. Thus they could both potentially be classified as NLS1s, making a total of 12. .
The first is 2XMM J112328.0+052823, in which after subtracting the host galaxy component, the observed optical continuum is closer to the slope of the SED model.
In addition to 2XMM J112328.0+052823 above, only Mrk1018 and 2XMM J125553.0+272405 show clear stellar absorption features.

gmw_00011079.name: NGC 4191
gmw_00011079.overview: NGC 4191 is an early-type galaxy characterized by two superposed, counterrotating stellar discs [arXiv:1802.09495]. It features an unusually large, asymmetric neutral hydrogen disc that extends up to 68 kiloparsecs on its northern side [arXiv:1802.09495].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4191 as an unusually H I-rich early-type galaxy with two counterrotating stellar discs and an extended, asymmetric H I disc.

quotes.quote: Amongst the \ slow rotators in Table tab:SRstats , NGC 4191 is unusual for having the largest \ mass and disc diameter. Its \ mass and diameter are, in fact, in league with the properties of nearby spirals. It was classified by davor as a ``two peak'' galaxy, meaning that it consists of two superposed, dynamically cold, counterrotating stellar discs, and these galaxies are often found to be slow rotators because the two discs' rotations interfere destructively in the mean velocity field.

Its unusual \ disc, in combination with its counterrotating stellar discs, make it particularly interesting for studies of the gas kinematics in comparison to the stellar kinematics. \ distribution and kinematics The \ in NGC 4191 is clearly peaked on the optical galaxy nucleus, with a peak column density of 3.6 20 \ at this 9 kpc spatial resolution (Figure fig:4191m0 ). \ column densities are high in a bright elongated ridge which is approximately 3.3 \ (38 kpc) in diameter and is poorly resolved in the minor axis direction. This ridge is surrounded by an extended disc of column density 1.2 20 , extending to a radius of 6 \ (68 kpc) on the north side and 4 \ (45 kpc) on the south side. The outer disc is asymmetric, having higher column densities on the south side but a larger physical extent on the north side.

And because the \ rotation speed is approximately constant out to such a large radius, the average logarithmic slope of the total density profile in NGC 4191 is 2.06 0.03, close to isothermal, as is typical for the galaxies in serra_vcirc . Finally, it is also worth noting that the \ disc in NGC 4191 is unusually large for early-type galaxies.

gmw_00011080.name: AGC 249460
gmw_00011080.overview: AGC 249460 is an H I source with no identified optical counterpart [arXiv:1802.09495]. It has an extremely large gas-to-light ratio, making it a candidate for an optically faint dark galaxy [arXiv:1802.09495].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGC 249460 as an H I source with no optical counterpart, extreme gas-to-light ratio, and possible status as an optically faint dark galaxy.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: From the VLA image we estimate the centroid of AGC 249460 to be located at 14 ^h 20 ^m 07.721 ^s , 03 27 42.761 \ (J2000), with a positional uncertainty of about 1.5 \ in each direction.

quotes.quote: Another possible member of the group, AGC 249460, is identified as an \ source in the ALFALFA .100 catalog but an optical counterpart has not yet been identified. This makes it an unusual rich object, as we will discuss in further detail below. From the VLA image we estimate the centroid of AGC 249460 to be located at 14 ^h 20 ^m 07.721 ^s , 03 27 42.761 \ (J2000), with a positional uncertainty of about 1.5 \ in each direction.

Three such objects are discussed by janowiecki ; one of them has a faint optical counterpart of about 15 \ (1.8 kpc) radius, and if we adopt that same radius here for AGC 249460 we find that its integrated magnitude must be fainter than 10.7 in g . Its luminosity is thus L_g < 2.2 10^6 L _ and its \ mass gives an extremely large M( )/ L_g > 60 . The \ mass is similar to the most gas-rich dwarf galaxies of the Local Group, such as NGC 6822, but the optical luminosity is similar to that of the faint dwarf spheroidals e.g. mcconnachie . Thus, the source has no direct analog in the Local Group, and because of the fainter surface brightness limit, it has even more extreme M( )/L ratios than the other objects discussed by janowiecki . This object may be one of the ``darkest" of the dark (optically faint) galaxies.

gmw_00011081.name: MUSE 1343
gmw_00011081.overview: MUSE 1343 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy [arXiv:2007.08172]. It is characterized by a low signal-to-noise ratio, a very compact source distribution, and an optically thick outer region [arXiv:2007.08172].

sources.summary: The paper treats MUSE 1343 as a low-S/N Lyman-alpha target whose model behavior reflects effective optical depth and parameter degeneracies in spectrum and surface-brightness fitting.

quotes.quote: The peak values of spectrum and surface brightness profile of MUSE 1185, 82, 6905, and 53 are in the orders of 10^ 18 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ^ 1 and 10^ 17 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 arcsec ^ 2 , respectively, (refer to filled circles with error bars in Figures fig-bestfit-tot-1185 , fig-bestfit-tot-82 , fig-bestfit-tot-6905 , and fig-bestfit-tot-53 ) with the mean signal-to-noise ratio ( S/N ) 4 (see the last three columns of Table tab-MUSEinfo ). On the other hand, those of MUSE 1343, 171, 547, and 364 are lower by an order of magnitude with S/N 4 (refer to Figures fig-bestfit-tot-1343 , fig-bestfit-tot-171 , fig-bestfit-tot-547 , and fig-bestfit-tot-364 and Table tab-MUSEinfo ). The posterior distributions for the target galaxies with stronger signals are sharper in general, and therefore parameter constraints are tighter (e.g., Figures fig-lnliktot-1185 , fig-lnliktot-82 , fig-lnliktot-6905 , fig-lnliktot-53 versus Figures fig-lnliktot-1343 , fig-lnliktot-171 , fig-lnliktot-547 , fig-lnliktot-364 ).

The two competing effects (the Doppler frequency shift and the effective optical-depth) seem to be canceled out in the case of MUSE 82, while the latter effect (i.e., enhanced effective optical-depth by increasing ) overwhelms the former effect (i.e., smaller frequency shift due to smaller medium velocity) in the case of MUSE 1343. The impact of on spectrum could be more significant when there is non-negligible amount of photons emitted at large radii (i.e., larger ). Indeed, all of our target galaxies are modeled to have a very compact source distribution as inferred from their UV distribution (see Table tab-MUSEinfo ), which could limit the impact of on spectrum.

When the outer part of the galaxy is optically thick (with large , small , and large _0 ), the change of spectrum with becomes negligible and the flattening of surface brightness profile by increasing extends to a large radius (e.g., MUSE 1343).

gmw_00011082.name: MUSE 171
gmw_00011082.overview: MUSE 171 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy characterized by a relatively low signal-to-noise ratio [arXiv:2007.08172]. The galaxy is modeled with a constant expanding velocity, where radial spectral variations trace the scattering history of escaping photons [arXiv:2007.08172].

sources.summary: The paper models MUSE 171 as a lower-signal Lyman-alpha galaxy with constant expanding velocity, where radial spectral changes trace scattering history.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, those of MUSE 1343, 171, 547, and 364 are lower by an order of magnitude with S/N 4 (refer to Figures fig-bestfit-tot-1343 , fig-bestfit-tot-171 , fig-bestfit-tot-547 , and fig-bestfit-tot-364 and Table tab-MUSEinfo ). The posterior distributions for the target galaxies with stronger signals are sharper in general, and therefore parameter constraints are tighter (e.g., Figures fig-lnliktot-1185 , fig-lnliktot-82 , fig-lnliktot-6905 , fig-lnliktot-53 versus Figures fig-lnliktot-1343 , fig-lnliktot-171 , fig-lnliktot-547 , fig-lnliktot-364 ).

In the case of MUSE 171, which is a relatively simple case with a constant expanding velocity, the different spectra at different locations are because of difference in the number of scatterings that photons have undergone until their escape. The peak of the spectrum tends to appear at a longer wavelength when the spectrum is measured at a larger distance from the galaxy center. Therefore, a larger shift of the spectral peak at a larger distance indicates that these photons escape after more scatterings.

gmw_00011083.overview: The galaxy 11965-3703 is a post-starburst galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2304.10419]. It features an extended emission-line region and is the only post-starburst host of this type detected by IRAS [arXiv:2304.10419].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 11965-3703 as a post-starburst EELR host, previously in the Keel voorwerpjes sample, and as the only post-starburst EELR host detected in IRAS.

quotes.quote: We identify six post-starburst galaxies with EELRs. Properties of these galaxies are shown in Table tab:targets . We show these maps for the six post-starburst galaxies with EELRs in Figure fig:method and for six representative galaxies for which we do not identify EELRs in Figure fig:method2 . One of the six galaxies (11965-3703) was also identified as an EELR host in the Keel2012 sample.
This galaxy (MaNGA plateifu 11965-3703) is a well-known AGN host that is also in the Keel2012 sample of voorwerpjes, and the only post-starburst galaxy EELR host detected in IRAS.

gmw_00011084.overview: 10845-12701 is a post-starburst galaxy that displays clear signs of disturbance in its stellar features [arXiv:2304.10419]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus outflow and exhibits asymmetric biconical patterns in its ionized gas kinematics [arXiv:2304.10419].

sources.summary: The paper describes 10845-12701 as a post-starburst EELR galaxy with disturbed stellar features, asymmetric biconical ionized-gas kinematics, a known AGN outflow, and EELR-offset gas velocities.

quotes.quote: Two of the six galaxies (10845-12701, 11965-3703) show clear signs of disturbance in their stellar features. A third galaxy (9862-12703) has a nearby object which may be a satellite galaxy associated with the central galaxy, though we do not have a redshift to confirm. Because 4/6 of the galaxies do not show obvious merger features, we conclude that the presence of merger features in the stellar light is not necessary for the galaxy to have sufficient extended gas to display an EELR.

The ionized gas kinematics show a range of properties with some (8938-6102, 9495-3702) showing evidence of rotation and others (10845-12701, 11965-3703) displaying asymmetric biconical patterns. These may be evidence of outflows, as indeed 10845-12701 is known to host an AGN outflow McElroy2015 . In one case (11965-3703), the ionized gas velocity red/blue components are aligned with the orientation of the EELR; in another (10845-12701), the ionized gas velocities are oriented 45 degrees from the EELR.

gmw_00011085.name: 2MASSXJ13412738+4042166
gmw_00011085.overview: 2MASSXJ13412738+4042166 is a late-type spiral disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.088 [arXiv:0905.4733]. It features a bar structure, a disturbed southern morphology, strong H-alpha emission, and far-infrared emission dominated by cool dust cirrus [arXiv:0905.4733].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the NGP01 counterpart as a z=0.088 late-spiral disk galaxy with a bar, disturbed southern morphology, strong H emission, and cool cirrus-dominated far-infrared emission.

quotes.quote: NGP01 The bright ( R =16.59, K =13.77) counterpart is identified as 2MASSXJ13412738+4042166 with a redshift of z=0.088 . The optical data show a disk dominated galaxy with wide arms, typical to late spirals, bar structure identified with GALFIT and a somewhat disturbed morphology at the southern edge, typical to e.g. \ merger remnants. HYPERZ fits perfectly a fully evolved (nominally 15 Gyr old) GRASIL Sc template, the ERR03 models fit a cirrus-dominated SED, while the SDSS spectrum shows strong H emission. The FIR points alone -- detections at 150 and 170 m, but only an upper limit at 90 m -- suggest a strong cool cirrus component.

gmw_00011086.name: 2MASSXJ13422216+4022017
gmw_00011086.overview: 2MASSXJ13422216+4022017 is a starburst galaxy at a redshift of z=0.131 [arXiv:0905.4733]. It features a bulge-dominated disk with wide spiral arms, a possible nuclear bar, and a ring-like structure extending toward its interacting companion galaxies [arXiv:0905.4733].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGP03 as a z=0.131 interacting galaxy with nearby companions, spiral arms, a possible nuclear bar, strong star formation, and a warm starburst-like far-infrared SED.

quotes.quote: NGP03 The bright ( R =17.12, K =14.20) counterpart is identified as 2MASSXJ13422216+4022017 at z=0.131 . It has two or three fainter galaxies clearly in close interaction. GALFIT modelling shows a large bulge-dominated disk of 6.9 kpc scale length with wide spiral arms and the subtraction of bulge and disk components reveals a ring-like structure extending towards two of the companions, and possibly bar-like structure at the nucleus. Again, the SDSS spectrum shows strong star formation. The FIR SED is now warmer (detected at 90 m but not at 180 m) and our best fitting SED template is, indeed, that of a prototype interacting starburst galaxy NGC 6090, further corroborating its starburst nature.

gmw_00011087.name: NGP20
gmw_00011087.overview: NGP20 is a bright spiral galaxy with an undisturbed disk and a small, bright optical bulge [arXiv:0905.4733]. It has a plausible photometric redshift of z=0.137, which is supported by a nearby early-type galaxy located 1.1 arcminutes away [arXiv:0905.4733].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGP20 as a bright z=0.137 Sc-template galaxy with an undisturbed disk, small bright bulge, and a plausible photometric redshift supported by a nearby early-type galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGP20 The bright ( R =17.75, K =14.80) galaxy in the detection circle has no SDSS spectra, but it does have a strong HYPERZ fit ( ^2 = 0.2 at z=0.137 ) with a fully evolved Sc template, consistent with the ERR03 fit as well. It has an undisturbed disk and a small but bright bulge in the optical. There is another bright galaxy of early type just outside the field 1.1 arcmin away (2MASX J13494050+3907555) which has a known redshift of z=0.143 making the photometric redshift of the first galaxy, the adopted counterpart, very plausible.

gmw_00011088.name: SDSSJ135054.71+385847.2
gmw_00011088.overview: SDSSJ135054.71+385847.2 is an edge-on disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.086 [arXiv:0905.4733]. The galaxy features a somewhat warped disk, has no obvious nearby major partners, and exhibits a modestly elevated star formation rate [arXiv:0905.4733].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGP22 with an edge-on disk galaxy at z=0.086, noting its warped disk, Sc-template SED fit, lower mass, modestly elevated star formation, and cirrus-dominated emission.

quotes.quote: NGP22 The only bright galaxy ( R =17.70, K =14.52) in the vicinity is the edge-on disk galaxy SDSSJ135054.71+385847.2 at a redshift of z=0.086 . The disk is somewhat warped, though there are no obvious major partners nearby. The SDSS spectrum shows H , though not much else, and the overall SED fits reasonably the evolved Sc template at that redshift, though the optical/NIR SED would be better fit with an M82 template. This galaxy is 2-3 times less massive than the previous four objects, and the SFR predicted by both GRASIL and ERR03 is also smaller, but still a little higher than ordinary quiescent galaxies. ERR03 again fits a cirrus dominated galaxy.

gmw_00011089.name: UGC 08793
gmw_00011089.overview: UGC 08793 is a nearby late-type dwarf spiral galaxy at a redshift of z=0.0081 [arXiv:0905.4733]. It features a slightly warped disk, numerous bright HII regions, and several small satellite galaxies or giant HII regions [arXiv:0905.4733].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGP24 as UGC 08793, a nearby z=0.0081 dwarf-like late-type galaxy with low infrared luminosity, a pure-cirrus SED, bright HII regions, satellites or giant HII regions, and a warped disk.

quotes.quote: NGP24 The counterpart galaxy of this FIR source is the most nearby galaxy in our sample, UGC 08793 at a redshift of z=0.0081 and it extends over 1.5 \ on the sky. Its IR luminosity is more than 2 orders of magnitude less than the other bright sample galaxies above. The SDSS spectrum of the center shows a typical late type spiral spectrum, NED classifies it as Sd, and our overall SED shape is well fit with a template of local Scd dwarf galaxy NGC 6946. This is the only case where ERR03 suggests a pure cirrus spectrum. It has numerous bright HII regions, several small satellite galaxies, or giant HII regions, and the disk appears slightly warped.

gmw_00011090.name: Q1
gmw_00011090.overview: Q1 is a bright quasar that forms a pair with a fainter companion at a projected separation of 82.5 kpc [arXiv:1803.10781]. The quasar exhibits unresolved CO(4-3) and continuum emission, with a small CO velocity dispersion and three nearby continuum emitters [arXiv:2304.02041].

sources.summary: Q1 is the brighter quasar in the ELAN0101+0201 pair; the observations use its systemic redshift and assume it photoionizes the surrounding nebula.

quotes.quote: The projected separation of these two QSOs is 10" (82.5 physical projected kpc) and the redshift separation is about 1000 km s ^ 1 . The brighter QSO (Q1) has an AB magnitude of i=18.1 and the fainter QSO (Q2) has i=21.6 ( 30 times fainter than Q1).

Since Q2 is expected to be 30 fainter than Q1 and most nebular emission is around Q1, we assume that the nebula is photoionized by Q1. We consider both optically thin and thick CGM photoionized by Q1.

sources.summary: Q0101+0201 has the sample's second-brightest CO emission, unresolved CO and continuum structure, small CO velocity dispersion, and nearby continuum emitters.

quotes.quote: Q0101+0201 : This QSO shows the second brightest CO emission in our sample and is unresolved in both CO(4-3) and continuum (Fig. fig:spectrum6 ). In addition, the CO(4-3) spectrum of the QSO shows an extremely small velocity dispersion (FWHM 151 ). The continuum image taken with the ALMA 12m array reveals three unresolved point sources, one is associated with the QSO and another two emitters are at projected distance of 190 and 230 kpc from the QSO.

gmw_00011091.name: Q2
gmw_00011091.overview: Q2 is a faint quasar with an AB magnitude of i=21.6 [arXiv:1803.10781]. It forms a pair with a brighter quasar located at a projected separation of 10 arcseconds, and its spectrum exhibits strong Lyman-alpha absorbers [arXiv:1803.10781].

sources.summary: Q2 is the fainter member of the QSO pair; its spectrum shares the detected Ly absorbers, but the paper treats Q1 as the likely nebular photoionizing source.

quotes.quote: The projected separation of these two QSOs is 10" (82.5 physical projected kpc) and the redshift separation is about 1000 km s ^ 1 . The brighter QSO (Q1) has an AB magnitude of i=18.1 and the fainter QSO (Q2) has i=21.6 ( 30 times fainter than Q1).

Including LAE1, we detect four LAE candidates and two QSOs in an 40" 60" area, within a redshift range of z = 2.44 - 2.46 (Table 1). The Pair of Ly Absorbers In the spectra of Q1 and Q2, we detect strong Ly absorbers at -710 km s ^ 1 reference to QSO Ly emission ( z=2.441 ) (gray vertical lines in Figure 2).
Since Q2 is expected to be 30 fainter than Q1 and most nebular emission is around Q1, we assume that the nebula is photoionized by Q1.

gmw_00011092.name: LAE1
gmw_00011092.overview: LAE1 is a bright Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located in an extremely dense environment [arXiv:1803.10781]. It features a highly asymmetric Lyman-alpha profile with a broad emission component that suggests the galaxy may host an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1803.10781].

sources.summary: LAE1 is a bright Ly emitter in the ELAN field with a higher velocity, asymmetric Ly profile, broad and narrow components, and a broad component suggesting it could be an AGN.

quotes.quote: Overdensity of Ly Emitters In Figure 2, we show six 1-D spectra from the KCWI datacube, including Q1 ( i=18.1 ), Q2 ( i=21.6 ), and a KCWI resolved bright LAE (LAE1). The positions labeled f1, f2 and f3 are bright nodes in the nebular region resolved by KCWI. LAE1 has a higher velocity than the nebular redshift (Fig. 1). Figure 2 shows that LAE1 has a highly asymmetric Ly profile. The Ly emission can be fit using one broad (thick orange line) component with the FWHM of 840 km s ^ 1 and one narrow component (thin orange line) with the FWHM of 170 km s ^ 1 .

From Figure 2, LAE1 has a highly asymmetric Ly profile, and contains a broad component (FWHM=840 km s ^ 1 ). The broad component is much wider than typical star-forming galaxies ( 300 -400 km s ^ 1 , e.g., trainor16 ), indicating that LAE1 could be an AGN, making this field likely hosting three AGNs in a 100 kpc scale.

gmw_00011093.name: 3FGL J0710.5+4732
gmw_00011093.overview: 3FGL J0710.5+4732 is a blazar located at a redshift of z=1.29 [arXiv:1812.10614]. During monitoring over three nights, the source remained in a gamma-ray quiescent state and exhibited no intranight optical variability [arXiv:1812.10614].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this as the sample's highest-redshift source, monitored it on three nights, and reports no significant INOV while it remained gamma-ray quiescent.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0710.5+4732: This is the highest redshift source in our sample ( z=1.29 ) and we have monitored it on three nights. Other than a few flickering, we have not found any significant INOV on any night, which is further confirmed from C and F statistics. During our monitoring campaign, the source remained quiescent in rays , thus indicating the lack of the jet activity as a probable cause of the non-detection of INOV.

gmw_00011094.name: 3FGL J0739.4+0137
gmw_00011094.overview: 3FGL J0739.4+0137 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1812.10614, arXiv:2202.07353] belonging to the intermediate-synchrotron-peaked spectral class [arXiv:2202.07353]. The blazar is known to exhibit fast intranight optical variability [arXiv:1812.10614], showing small-amplitude flares and high optical polarization during periods of elevated activity [arXiv:1812.10614].

sources.summary: The paper describes this FSRQ as a known fast INOV source and reports small-amplitude flares during an elevated state with high optical polarization.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0739.4+0137: This is a FSRQ and is known to show fast INOV 2003AJ....126...37C . 2012MNRAS.424.2625B suggested that this blazar shows high amplitude variability when close to the optical maximum. We find various small amplitude flares during its observation on 2015 March 7 (Figure fig_inov2 ). Interestingly, during this period of the elevated activity, a high optical polarization of 5 was recorded at Steward observatory. In the conventional one-zone leptonic emission models, a high activity in the optical band is also reflected at high energy rays.

sources.summary: The paper classifies this source as an ISP flat-spectrum radio quasar and reports low-amplitude INOV on one of three monitored nights.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0739.4+0137: It is a FSRQ and belongs to the spectral class ISP. It was observed for INOV on three nights over a two year period. It was found to show low amplitude INOV of about 4 on one night (24 February 2019), while on the remaining two nights INOV was not detected in this source.

gmw_00011095.name: J160426.51 + 17
gmw_00011095.overview: J160426.51 + 17 is a nearby Fanaroff-Riley class 0 radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.04 [arXiv:1909.04113]. It exhibits a flat X-ray spectral index and is considered a potential candidate for gamma-ray detection [arXiv:1909.04113].

sources.summary: J160426.51 + 17 is used as a nearby FR 0 reference with Tol 1326 379-like X-ray properties; modeling places its GeV output near Fermi limits and predicts MeV detectability.

quotes.quote: We use two FR 0s as reference (Figure FR0SED ): while one is Tol 1326 379, detected by Fermi , the other source is a nearby (z=0.04) FR 0 radio galaxy, J160426.51 + 17. It shows a flat X-ray spectral index ( _X = 1.1 0.3) and X-ray luminosity similar to Tol 1326 379 (see ) that make it a potential candidate for a ray detection. As an exercise, we adjusted the model adopted for Tol 1326 379 to reproduce the SED of J160426.51 + 17 (Figure FR0SED b): the output in the GeV window could be just at the Fermi detection limit.

As an example, in Figure FR0SED , we plotted the expected sensitivity curves for the two instruments on the SEDs of Tol 1326 379 and J160426.51 + 17. In both cases, the two telescopes would detect the two FR 0s at MeV energies.

gmw_00011096.name: HE 2355 5457
gmw_00011096.overview: HE 2355 5457 is a quasar with a detected host galaxy [arXiv:0709.2568]. This host galaxy exhibits symmetric, low-surface-brightness emission centered on the active nucleus, though it remains much fainter than the central source [arXiv:0709.2568].

sources.summary: The paper reports a high-significance host detection for HE 2355 5457, with symmetric low-surface-brightness emission centered on the QSO and two-band host fluxes consistent with PSF subtraction.

quotes.quote: Visual inspection of the PSF-subtracted QSO image showed that this excess in the quasar profile was not caused by a close companion, or by a strongly asymmetric feature, but that it was due to more or less azimuthally symmetric low surface brightness emission centered on the QSO. We conclude that our conservative null hypothesis of the QSO having no detectable host galaxy has to be rejected, with high significance for the \ band, but also (with somewhat lower significance) for the H band. However, with the galaxy contributing only 5 of the total uncorrected \ band flux, the host is still much fainter than the central nucleus.

After applying a statistical correction for oversubtraction (see Sect. \ sec:psfsub ) these values increase to 10 and 8 for the \ and H band, respectively. We then computed two-dimensional model fits (with fixed scale lengths as discussed above), and obtained integrated \ and H band fluxes for the host galaxy that are in good agreement with the corrected estimates based on PSF-subtraction. This makes HE 2355 5457 the QSO with the highest significance detection in the whole sample.

gmw_00011097.name: HE 2149 0436
gmw_00011097.overview: HE 2149 0436 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:0709.2568]. An extended host galaxy has been detected at its position after point spread function subtraction, though its luminosity is constrained by an upper limit [arXiv:0709.2568].

sources.summary: The paper reports an extended source at the HE 2149 0436 QSO position after PSF subtraction, but only in one band, giving a host-luminosity upper limit.

quotes.quote: HE 2149 0436: The field contains several (mostly faint) stars showing that PSF variations for the whole field of view are rather large. For both bands we find positive as well as negative residuals after subtraction of individual stars extracted within 30'' around the QSO. In case of the \ band, the number of sufficiently bright field stars was acceptable to create an analytical model of the spatial PSF variations (similar to the procedure by Kuhlbrodt(2004) ).

Using the best-fit analytical PSF prescription at the QSO position, we detected an extended source after PSF subtraction. The amount of uncorrected flux is 6 and increases to 13 after oversubtraction correction. The image quality of the H band data was unfortunately inferior to the \ data, and we did not succeed in resolving the host galaxy. In the following we only quote a 3 upper limit of the host galaxy luminosity, propagating into corresponding lower limits of the rest-frame B-V colour.

gmw_00011098.overview: 7815-12705 is a main-sequence galaxy [arXiv:2401.05976]. Its star-forming spaxels exhibit correlations with three distinct gas tracers, specifically CO, HCN, and HCO+ [arXiv:2401.05976].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 7815-12705 as a main-sequence ALMaQUEST galaxy whose star-forming spaxels correlate with CO, HCN, and HCO+ gas tracers and serve as comparison fits.

quotes.quote: For the main sequence galaxy 7815-12705, is found to correlate with all the three gas tracers (CO, HCN, and ), although the correlation for is only moderate ( = 0.5).

To guide our eyes when comparing objects-to-objects and spaxel types-to-spaxel types, we fit the star-forming spaxels of the main sequence galaxy 7815-12705 with the orthogonal distance regression (ODR) fitting method for the three gas tracers separately.
The best fits of the object 7815-12705 are shown in black as a reference line in the panels of the other objects as well.

gmw_00011099.name: PKS 0506 612
gmw_00011099.overview: PKS 0506 612 is a radio-loud quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0304405]. The source exhibits an intrinsically red power-law spectrum, though it likely faded in flux between observations separated by three days [arXiv:astro-ph/0304405].

sources.summary: The paper finds PKS 0506 612 has a poor optical-NIR power-law fit, likely because it faded between observations, implying an intrinsically red power-law spectrum.

quotes.quote: Three sources---PKS 0506 612, PKS 0528 + 134, and PKS 1622 297---are quite blue (although PKS 0506 612 is a poor fit, as discussed below, and should probably not be included in this group).

One source had a poor fit: PKS 0506 612. There is a large offset between the optical and NIR points, giving what appears to be substantial spectral curvature. However, it is likely that the source has faded in the three days between the two observations---if the spectrum is a smooth continuum then a reduction in flux of 1 mag is implied. This also implies the intrinsic spectrum is a red power law of slope 1.3 .

gmw_00011100.name: SBS 1518+593
gmw_00011100.overview: SBS 1518+593 is a Seyfert galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1810.11996]. The broad-line region of the galaxy exhibits velocity-resolved time lags and kinematics consistent with a Keplerian disk or virialized motion [arXiv:1810.11996].

sources.summary: The paper presents the first lag and black-hole-mass measurement for SBS 1518+593, finding mild red asymmetry and velocity-resolved lags consistent with virialized or Keplerian BLR motion.

quotes.quote: While three of the objects have been previously reverberation mapped, including velocity-resolved time lags in the cases of 3C 120 and Mrk 6, we report a time lag and corresponding black hole mass measurement for SBS 1518 + 593 for the first time. Furthermore, SBS 1518 + 593, the least asymmetric of the four, does show velocity-resolved time lags characteristic of a Keplerian disk or virialized motion more generally.

The time lag of SBS 1518 + 593 is mainly determined by the dip around 105 days in the continuum light curve and the response around 125 days in the \ light curve. The peak at 150 days in the continuum and its potential response at 180 in the emission-line light curve also provide constraint to the lag measurement, but only play a minor role because the peak in the \ light curve is already near to the end of the campaign. The \ emission line in the mean spectrum of SBS 1518 + 593 is asymmetric toward the red wing, whereas the peak of the \ in the rms spectrum is mildly redshifted (see Figure fig:meanrms ). However, its velocity-resolved lag measurement is not significantly asymmetric. The longer lags in the line core and the shorter lags in the line wings imply that the geometry and kinematics of its BLR tend to be virialized motion or a Keplerian disk.

gmw_00011101.name: NVSS J171822+423948
gmw_00011101.overview: NVSS J171822+423948 is a high-redshift blazar located at a redshift of z = 2.68 [arXiv:2401.12122]. It is identified as a candidate neutrino-emitting source temporally and spatially associated with the IceCube-201221A neutrino event [arXiv:2401.12122].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NVSS J171822+423948 as a rare z=2.68 gamma-ray blazar counterpart to 4FGL J1718.5+4237, temporally and spatially associated with IceCube-201221A.

quotes.quote: We report on multiwavelength studies of the blazar NVSS J171822+423948, which is identified as the low-energy counterpart of 4FGL J1718.5+4237, the only ray source known to be cospatial with the IceCube neutrino event IC-201221A. After a 12-year long quiescent period undetected by Fermi-LAT, ray activities with a tenfold flux increase emerge soon (a few tens of days) after arrival of the neutrino. Associated optical flares in the ZTF g , , and i bands are observed together with elevated WISE infrared fluxes. Synchronized variations suggest that both the ray emission and the neutrino event are connected to the blazar.
Furthermore, the optical spectrum reveals emission lines at a redshift z = 2.68 0.01. Thus, it is the first candidate for a neutrino-emitting blazar at the redshift above 2.

gmw_00011102.name: J0842c
gmw_00011102.overview: J0842c is a high-redshift galaxy companion to a z~6 quasar [arXiv:1907.02607]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet and optical limits suggest it is a dust-obscured luminous infrared galaxy similar to Arp 220, with star formation dominated by dust emission [arXiv:1907.02607].

sources.summary: J0842c is treated as a z~6 quasar companion whose UV/optical limits fit an Arp 220-like, dust-obscured luminous infrared galaxy with star formation dominated by dust emission.

quotes.quote: The limits on rest--frame UV/optical brightness of PJ231c, J2100c and J0842c rule out all the galaxy templates considered here, with the exception of Arp 220. These companions have infrared luminosities in the range of (ultra-) luminous galaxies (e.g. 0.9--5 10 ^ 12 L _ ; Decarli17 ).

We compute the star formation rates for PJ231c, J2100c and J0842c assuming that their SEDs are equivalent to that of Arp 220, shifted in redshift and scaled as in Figure figSEDobs . We derive their star formation rates from the dust emission in the rest-frame infrared region (SFR _ IR ), assuming that the non-obscured SFR as negligible (see Section secAnalysisSFRUV ). The obtained values range between 120 and 700 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00011103.name: J2100c
gmw_00011103.overview: J2100c is a high-redshift galaxy located near a quasar at z~6 [arXiv:1907.02607]. Its spectral energy distribution is consistent with a dust-obscured, luminous infrared galaxy similar to Arp 220, where star formation is dominated by dust emission [arXiv:1907.02607].

sources.summary: J2100c is treated as a z~6 quasar companion whose UV/optical limits fit an Arp 220-like, dust-obscured luminous infrared galaxy with star formation dominated by dust emission.

quotes.quote: The limits on rest--frame UV/optical brightness of PJ231c, J2100c and J0842c rule out all the galaxy templates considered here, with the exception of Arp 220. These companions have infrared luminosities in the range of (ultra-) luminous galaxies (e.g. 0.9--5 10 ^ 12 L _ ; Decarli17 ).

We compute the star formation rates for PJ231c, J2100c and J0842c assuming that their SEDs are equivalent to that of Arp 220, shifted in redshift and scaled as in Figure figSEDobs . We derive their star formation rates from the dust emission in the rest-frame infrared region (SFR _ IR ), assuming that the non-obscured SFR as negligible (see Section secAnalysisSFRUV ). The obtained values range between 120 and 700 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00011104.name: NGC 1339
gmw_00011104.overview: NGC 1339 is an elliptical galaxy with a rotation curve that can be traced out to two effective radii [arXiv:astro-ph/9806331]. The galaxy exhibits a steep inner rotation gradient, a flattened outer rotation, and a velocity dispersion profile that declines before rising outward [arXiv:astro-ph/9806331].

sources.summary: NGC 1339 is traced to two effective radii, with steep inner rotation, flattened outer rotation, and a velocity-dispersion profile that declines then rises outward.

quotes.quote: NGC 1339 The RC is well traced out to 2 R _e , having a steep gradient of 200 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 (assuming a distance of 18 Mpc) over the inner 5 radius. The E and W side of the RC largely flatten out after 10 . The VDP decreases linearly from 175 km s ^ 1 to 100 km s ^ 1 at 10 on both sides. After 20 , both sides of the VDP increase some 50 km s ^ 1 , reaching the outer data point (30 ).

gmw_00011105.name: FCC 136
gmw_00011105.overview: FCC 136 is a faint galaxy classified as a dwarf elliptical or a barred S0 candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/9806331]. It exhibits a poorly defined velocity dispersion, marginal rotation along a wavy rotation curve, and isophotal twisting that supports the presence of a bar structure [arXiv:astro-ph/9806331].

sources.summary: FCC 136 is described as a faint dwarf/Barred S0 candidate with poorly defined low dispersion, marginal rotation, wavy rotation curve, isophotal twisting, and bar support.

quotes.quote: FCC 136 This faint galaxy is classified as dE2 by Ferguson (1989) and as SAB0 in RC3. The VDP is poorly defined around 40 km s ^ 1 , close to our measurement limit. There is marginal evidence for rotation within the wavy RC, which increases to 15 km s ^ 1 by the outer data points (15 ). The shape of the RC, together with the fact that the inner isophotes ( < 4 ) twist through 30 ^ , while the normalized Fourier coefficient a_4 is high (Caon et al. \ 1994) supports the RC3 classification as a barred galaxy.

gmw_00011106.name: SDSS J102819.24+623502.6
gmw_00011106.overview: SDSS J102819.24+623502.6 is an isolated, fairly blue polar-disk galaxy situated in an underdense region [arXiv:0903.2325]. It possesses a massive neutral hydrogen disk, but has no detected companions within 3.5 Mpc, suggesting its gas was acquired through cold accretion rather than a merger [arXiv:0903.2325].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J102819.24+623502.6 as an isolated blue polar-disk galaxy between voids, with a massive H i disk, no detected companions, and likely cold-gas accretion rather than a merger.

quotes.quote: SDSS J102819.24+623502.6 is part of a pilot for a larger study of galaxy formation in underdense regions of the SDSS redshift survey (Stanonik et al. 2009, in preparation). Our lonely galaxy is situated at a RA Dec (J2000) of 10:28:19.02 +62:35:02.0 and a redshift of z= 0.0178446. It is fairly blue, with a g-band magnitude of 17.67 and -band magnitude of 17.41 as reported in the sixth data release from the SDSS.

With our data we can probe in H i an area of 650 kpc 650 kpc on the plane of the sky surrounding SDSS J102819.24+623502.6, with a velocity range from 4232 km s ^ 1 to 6196 km s ^ 1 . Despite the significant volume probed, no companions are detected above the 2.0 10^8 M_ limit in H i . Down to the SDSS limits, the neighborhood surrounding our lonely galaxy is empty within 3.5 Mpc. This environmental isolation makes it unlikely that the substantial 3 10^9 M_ of neutral hydrogen we detect was accreted through a merger or close encounter, and we propose cold accretion as the most likely mechanism 1977ApJ...215..483B, 2005MNRAS.363....2K,2006MNRAS.368....2D .

gmw_00011107.name: TXS 1245 197
gmw_00011107.overview: TXS 1245 197 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 1.275 [arXiv:1710.00711]. It hosts associated H I 21 cm absorption with a wide velocity span, indicating a neutral gas outflow driven at a rate of approximately 18 solar masses per year [arXiv:1710.00711].

sources.summary: The paper reports TXS 1245 197 as a high-redshift GPS/AGN source with associated H i absorption, blueshifted disturbed gas, and an inferred neutral-gas outflow rate around 18 solar masses per year.

quotes.quote: Our GMRT search for associated H i 21 cm absorption in a sample of 12 GPS sources yielded two new detections of absorption, towards TXS 1200+045 at z = 1.226 and TXS 1245 197 at z = 1.275 , and five non-detections. These are only the sixth and seventh detections of associated H i 21 cm absorption in active galactic nuclei (AGNs) at z > 1 . Both H i 21 cm absorption profiles are wide, with velocity spans between nulls of 600 km s ^ 1 (TXS 1200+045) and 1100 km s ^ 1 (TXS 1245 197).

In both absorbers, the large velocity spread of the absorption and its blueshift from the AGN, suggests that it arises in outflowing neutral gas, perhaps driven by the radio jets to high velocities. We derive mass outflow rates of M 32 M _ yr ^ 1 (TXS 1200+045) and M 18 M _ yr ^ 1 (TXS 1245 197), comparable to the mass outflow rates seen earlier in low-redshift active galactic nuclei.

gmw_00011108.name: PKS 2250 351
gmw_00011108.overview: PKS 2250 351 is a giant Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy with a projected size of 1.2 Mpc [arXiv:2001.08772]. Its host galaxy is a massive elliptical at z=0.2115 that exhibits a negligible star formation rate and highly obscured active galactic nucleus emission [arXiv:2001.08772].

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 2250 351 as a 1.2 Mpc giant FR-II radio galaxy whose 2MASS host is a massive elliptical at z=0.2115 with obscured AGN emission.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of the radio galaxy PKS 2250 351, a giant of 1.2 Mpc projected size, its host galaxy, and its environment.

Radio Morphology The existence of bright hotspots in the 5.5 and 9.5 GHz seen in the GLASS data (see Figure fig:glass ) suggest PKS 2250 351 is a Fanaroff Riley (FR) class II radio galaxy Fanaroff:74 , compatible with its total radio luminosity (see sec:radcomp ) although it is unusual in that we observe some asymmetry in the hotspots. Its projected size of 1.17 Mpc classes it as a giant radio galaxy.

The host galaxy For the first time we have identified the radio source PKS 2250 351 with the host galaxy 2MASS J22533602 3455305, a massive elliptical at z=0.2115 . It has a negligible star formation rate but prominent mid-IR emission due to accretion onto the SMBH. The AGN emission is highly obscured in the UV and optical, consistent with the central engine being obscured by a torus structure viewed from approximately side-on and with the standard unification of AGN by orientation Urry:95 .

gmw_00011109.name: ESO 215-31
gmw_00011109.overview: ESO 215-31 is a barred galaxy featuring faint inner and outer pseudo-rings [arXiv:2304.00515]. Its neutral hydrogen gas is concentrated near the ends of the bar and along the spiral arms, which exhibit ordered rotation [arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 215-31 as a barred ring galaxy with faint inner and outer pseudo-rings, ordered HI rotation, and gas concentrated near bar ends and resonance radii.

quotes.quote: ESO215-31: The top left panels in Fig fig:mosaic1 show the composite DSS optical image of the ESO 215-31 with the 1 contours overlaid on top. Also shown is the moment 1 (velocity) map for the same. This galaxy is observed to posses a bar and a faint pseudo-outer and -inner ring. The spiral arms are wound by about 180 from the ends of the bar. In terms of the 1 distribution, we find that the 1 gas is accumulated towards each end of the bar and also traces the spiral arms tightly to form an almost ring-like structure. The 1 velocity map shows uniform ordered rotation with no significant kinematic warps.

gmw_00011110.name: ESO 269-57
gmw_00011110.overview: ESO 269-57 is a spiral galaxy that features a central bar and an inner ring [arXiv:2304.00515]. Two spiral arms wind from the ends of the bar to almost form an outer ring, where neutral hydrogen gas accumulates and exhibits ordered rotational motion [arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: The paper notes ESO 269-57 has a bar, inner ring, nearly outer-ring spiral arms, regular HI distribution, ordered rotation, and gas at the outer pseudo-ring.

quotes.quote: ESO 269-57: In the bottom left panels of Fig. fig:mosaic1 , we show the optical image and the 1 velocity map for the galaxy ESO 269-57. The optical image of the image shows two prominent spirals arms winding > 180 from the ends of the bar, almost forming an outer ring. We also observe a bar and an inner ring. The 1 distribution of the galaxy is fairly regular with the velocity map showing a ordered rotational motion. We observe a ring of 1 gas accumulating at the location of the outer pseudo-ring.

gmw_00011111.name: IC 5240
gmw_00011111.overview: IC 5240 is a barred ring galaxy featuring a strong, flat-profile bar and a nearly complete stellar ring [arXiv:1405.6726, arXiv:2304.00515]. The galaxy also hosts a central X-shape structure [arXiv:1405.6726] and an atomic hydrogen gas ring that exhibits ordered circular motion [arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: The paper compares IC 5240 with simulation gtr106, describing a long, elongated barlens, strong flat-profile bar, short outer thin bar, short ledge, and central X-shape despite its inclination.

quotes.quote: The upper row compares simulation gtr106, viewed at a time t =6 Gyr after the beginning of the simulation, with an S ^4 G image of the galaxy IC 5240. This comparison involves two of the longest and most elongated barlenses and quite strong bars.
