As can be seen in the second panel from the left, the bar projected surface density profiles are quite flat, arguing for a strong bar (see Elmegreen.Elmegreen.85 and Kim.plus.15 for the observations and Athanassoula.Misiriotis.02 for the simulations).

In this example the extent of the thin bar outside the barlens component is very short, amongst the shortest in our sample. In both the observed and the simulated galaxy profiles this manifests itself by a ledge of very short extent. The part of the profile that is within the ledge rises inwards, but much less steeply than that of classical bulges. This will be discussed further in Sect. subsec:NGC936 .
Note also that in both gtr106 and IC 5240, there is an X-shape in the central regions, even though the simulated galaxy is seen exactly face-on and IC 5240 quite far from edge-on.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5240 as a barred ring galaxy with a nearly complete stellar ring, matching HI ring, ordered circular motion, and SFR enhancements at resonances.

quotes.quote: IC 5240: The top left panels in Fig. fig:mosaic3 show the composite optical image and 1 velocity map of the galaxy IC 5240. From the optical image the galaxy is observed to possess a bright bar surrounding which there is a nearly complete stellar ring. Some faint flocculent spiral arms are observed to be streaming out of the inner ring. Corresponding to the stellar ring is and 1 ring of gas. The 1 velocity map shows ordered circular motion of the 1 gas with no significant perturbations of warping observed. We are able to measure all four resonances and find that the iUHR corresponds nicely to the inner ring observed in the optical image of the galaxy and the oUHR coincides with the outer ring.

gmw_00011112.name: NGC 7098
gmw_00011112.overview: NGC 7098 is a galaxy characterized by a diffuse, weak ansae bar, an inner ring, and an outer ring [arXiv:2304.00515]. A neutral hydrogen ring closely traces this outer stellar ring, and the galaxy's velocity map displays mostly ordered rotation [arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7098 to show a weak ansae bar, inner and outer rings, an HI ring tracing the outer stellar ring, and resonance-driven ring formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 7098: The top right panels of Fig. fig:mosaic3 show the composite optical image and 1 velocity map of this galaxy. A diffuse weak ansae bar is observed along with a prominent inner ring as well as an outer ring. An 1 ring traces the outer stellar ring nicely and the velocity map shows ordered rotation for the most part. In Section sec:Bar_driven_resonance_rings we discuss how the bar drives the resonance rings in this galaxy.

gmw_00011113.name: NGC 3358
gmw_00011113.overview: NGC 3358 is a spiral galaxy featuring a bright central nucleus and two tightly wound spiral arms that form an outer ring-like structure [arXiv:2304.00515]. It is part of an interacting group of at least four galaxies, which may have triggered the ring's formation through external tidal perturbations [arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3358 as an interacting-group ring galaxy whose outer optical ring matches an HI ring while its velocity field remains ordered.

quotes.quote: NGC 3358: The centre right panels in Fig. fig:mosaic4 show the DSS optical composite image and the 1 velocity map for this galaxy. The galaxy has a bright central nucleus and two tightly wound spiral arms extend to form a ring-like outer structure. Corresponding to the outer optical ring is the 1 ring of gas. The velocity map of this galaxy shows no obvious deviation from ordered rotational motion. We note that this galaxy is part of an interacting system of at least fours galaxies part of the NGC 3347 group (REF). Therefore the origin of the ring in this galaxy may be attributed to external tidal perturbations.

gmw_00011114.name: SDSS J083803.68 + 540642.0
gmw_00011114.overview: SDSS J083803.68 + 540642.0 is an early-type ringed dwarf galaxy hosting a broad-line LINER nucleus [arXiv:1702.01033]. The galaxy features a circumgalactic ring and large-scale star-forming emission likely created by tidal accretion during a merger with a gas-rich galaxy [arXiv:1702.01033].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J083803.68 + 540642.0 as a ringed dwarf LINER with an IMBH, extended star-forming emission, and tidal-accretion features from a gas-rich merger.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a 20-kpc-sized \ emission in SDSS J083803.68 + 540642.0, a ringed dwarf galaxy ( M_ V = -17.89 mag) hosting an accreting intermediate-mass black hole at z = 0.02957 . Analysis of the \ images indicates that it is an early-type galaxy with a featureless low-surface brightness disk ( _0 = 20.39 mag arcsec ^ 2 in the V band) and a prominent, relatively red bulge ( V - I = 2.03, R_ e = 0.28 kpc or 0 48) that accounts for 81 of the total light in the I band. A circumgalactic ring of a diameter 16 kpc is also detected, with a disperse shape on its south side. The optical emission lines reveal the nucleus to be a broad-line LINER. Our MMT longslit observation indicates that the kinematics of the extended \ emission is consistent with a rotational gaseous disk, with a mean blueshifted velocity of 162  \ and mean redshifted velocity of 86  .

According to our photoionization calculations, the large-scale \ emission is unlikely to be powered by the central nucleus or by hot evolved (post-AGB) stars interspersed in the old stellar populations, but by in situ star formation; this is vindicated by the line-ratio diagnostic of the extended emission. We propose that both the ring and large-scale emitting gas are created by the tidal accretion in a collision---and then merger---with a gas-rich galaxy of a comparable mass.

gmw_00011115.name: 3FGL J0325.5+2223
gmw_00011115.overview: 3FGL J0325.5+2223 is a high-redshift MeV blazar located at a redshift of z=2.06 [arXiv:1703.10657]. The galaxy hosts a central black hole with an estimated mass of 400 million solar masses and exhibits a jet power that meets or exceeds its accretion luminosity [arXiv:1703.10657].

sources.summary: The paper studies 3FGL J0325.5+2223 as one of three high-redshift MeV blazars, modeling its SED with synchrotron, accretion-disk, and inverse-Compton components and finding powerful jets.

quotes.quote: In the present work we perform a multiwavelength study of three high redshift blazars: 3FGL J0325.5+2223 ( z=2.06 ), 3FGL J0449.0+1121 ( z= 2.15 ), and 3FGL J0453.2 2808 ( z=2.56 ), analysing quasi simultaneous data from GROND, UVOT and XRT, , and LAT. Our main focus is on the hard X-ray band recently unveiled by (3 79 keV) where these objects show a hard spectrum which enables us to constrain the inverse Compton peak and the jet power. We found that all three targets resemble the most powerful blazars, with the synchrotron peak located in the sub-millimeter range and the inverse Compton peak in the MeV range, and therefore belong to the MeV blazar class.

Using a simple one zone leptonic emission model to reproduce the spectral energy distributions, we conclude that a simple combination of synchrotron and accretion disk emission reproduces the infrared-optical spectra while the X-ray to ray part is well reproduced by the inverse Compton scattering of low energy photons supplied by the broad line region. The black hole masses for each of the three sources are calculated to be 4 10^ 8 M_ . The three studied sources have jet power at the level of, or beyond, the accretion luminosity.

gmw_00011116.name: 3FGL J0449.0+1121
gmw_00011116.overview: 3FGL J0449.0+1121 is a high-redshift MeV blazar located at a redshift of z=2.15 [arXiv:1703.10657]. The galaxy hosts a central black hole with an estimated mass of 400 million solar masses and exhibits a jet power that meets or exceeds its accretion luminosity [arXiv:1703.10657].

sources.summary: The paper studies 3FGL J0449.0+1121 as one of three high-redshift MeV blazars, modeling its SED with synchrotron, accretion-disk, and inverse-Compton components and finding powerful jets.

The black hole masses for each of the three sources are calculated to be 4 10^ 8 M_ . The three studied sources have jet power at the level of, or beyond, the accretion luminosity.

gmw_00011117.name: 3FGL J0453.2 2808
gmw_00011117.overview: 3FGL J0453.2 2808 is a high-redshift MeV blazar located at a redshift of z=2.56 [arXiv:1703.10657]. The galaxy hosts a supermassive black hole with an estimated mass of 4 billion solar masses and features a jet power that meets or exceeds its accretion luminosity [arXiv:1703.10657].

sources.summary: The paper studies 3FGL J0453.2 2808 as one of three high-redshift MeV blazars, modeling its SED with synchrotron, accretion-disk, and inverse-Compton components and finding powerful jets.

quotes.quote: In the present work we perform a multiwavelength study of three high redshift blazars: 3FGL J0325.5+2223 ( z=2.06 ), 3FGL J0449.0+1121 ( z= 2.15 ), and 3FGL J0453.2 2808 ( z=2.56 ), analysing quasi simultaneous data from GROND, UVOT and XRT, , and LAT. gmw_00011118.name: Q 0000 2619, =3.390
gmw_00011118.overview: The high-redshift damped Lyman-alpha system Q 0000 2619, located at a redshift of z = 3.390, is a metal-poor absorber [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381, arXiv:2205.04488]. This system is characterized by an exceptionally high, nearly solar argon abundance [arXiv:1409.4577] and an extremely low molecular fraction [arXiv:0801.3682].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q0000 26 as a high-redshift damped system with adopted HI from prior HIRES work, revised Fe and Si measurements, and potentially underestimated earlier metallicity.

quotes.quote: Q0000 26, z = 3.390 This high redshift damped system has been previously observed with HIRES ( ) and we adopt HI = 21.41 0.08 based on that analysis. It is a member of the LBQS ( ) statistical sample, one of the few with z_ abs > 3 The velocity profiles are presented in Figure vpQ0000 and the derived ionic column densities are listed in Table Q0000-clm . As our spectral coverage did not include FeII 1608, the Fe abundance is based solely on FeII 1611 which is a very weak transition and has a relatively low SNR.

Interestingly, we find Fe^+ to be significantly higher than the lower limit derived from the FeII 1608 transition reported by Lu et al. \ (1996), which may be the result of an error in the continuum fit to our profile. We find Si^+ to be significantly higher as well, however, both from the direct measurement of the SiII 1808 transition and by fitting a Voigt profile to the saturated SiII 1526 profile. We therefore note Lu et al. \ (1996) may have significantly underestimated the true metallicity.

sources.summary: The paper uses QSO J0003 2603, a z_em=4.01, R=18.5 quasar with an intervening metal-poor DLA and archival UVES data, to validate ESPRESSO HR42 observations.

quotes.quote: The target, QSO J0003 2603 (right ascension 00 hr 03' 22.953'' , declination 26^ 03' 18.307'' , and redshift z_ em =4.01 ), was chosen as it is: ( i ) sufficiently bright for the ESPRESSO autoguiding system (AB magnitude R = 18.5 mag), ( ii ) known to host an intervening metal-poor ( [ M/H ] 2 ) DLA absorber along the line of sight (redshift z_ abs =3.39 , and ( iii ) has archival UVES observations allowing a direct comparison between instruments Molaro00,Levshakov00 . The ESPRESSO observations were carried out on October 2 ^ nd 2021 on UT2 at Paranal (Program ID 60.A-9801(W)). Four sequential 60-minute on-target exposures were taken.

Using four hours of on-target integration, we observed the quasar QSO J0003 2603 (AB magnitude R = 18.5 mag). An archival spectrum of the same quasar obtained with UVES for approximately the same exposure time allows one to compare ESPRESSO's performance at extracting properties of an intervening DLA absorber.

sources.summary: The paper treats this high-redshift DLA as having only tentative H2 features, likely Lyman-alpha contamination, and an extremely low molecular fraction.

quotes.quote: Q 0000 2619, =3.390 : Levshakov00, Levshakov01 reported a tentative detection of H _2 in this system. However, they detected only two weak absorption features in the Lyman- forest, identified as H _2 W2-0 Q1 and L4-0 R1. The probability that these features are actually due to intervening Lyman- absorbers is high and we consider the derived J=1 column density as an upper limit only. In any case, this system has a very low molecular fraction of f<-6.87 .

sources.summary: The paper describes the z=3.39 absorber toward Q 0000-2620 as exceptional among DLAs, showing nearly solar argon abundance while most systems are argon deficient.

quotes.quote: The first measurement of \ abundance in a DLA system, namely the absorber at =3.39 towards Q 0000-2620, did not show evidence for a deficiency of argon molaro01 . The first systematic study, based on a sample of 10 measurements and 5 limits of , indicated that the absorber at =3.39 towards Q 0000-2620 is exceptional, since argon is deficient in most DLAs ( vladilo03 ; hereafter Paper I).

The DLA at z_ abs =3.39 towards Q 0000-2620 molaro01 is characterized by nearly solar argon abundances, whereas the DLA at z_ abs =4.224 towards J 1443+2724 noterdaeme08 shows evidence of argon deficiency.

gmw_00011119.name: Q 0013 0029, =1.973
gmw_00011119.overview: The high-redshift system Q 0013 0029, =1.973 is a blend of a damped Lyman-alpha system and a sub-damped Lyman-alpha system [arXiv:0801.3682]. These two components are separated by 500 km/s, and molecular hydrogen is present in both systems [arXiv:0801.3682].

sources.summary: The paper describes this DLA sightline as a blended DLA and sub-DLA separated by 500 km/s, with H2 present in both and four components up to J=5.

quotes.quote: Q 0013 0029, =1.973 : The detection of H _2 in this system has been reported for the first time by Ge97 . Petitjean02 showed that this system is actually the blend of a DLA ( N( )=20.83 ) and a sub-DLA ( N( ) 19.43 ) separated by 500 km s ^ 1 . H _2 is present in both systems, with four components detected up to J=5 .

gmw_00011120.name: Q 0347 3819, =3.025
gmw_00011120.overview: Q 0347 3819 is a high-redshift damped Lyman-alpha system located along a quasar sightline at a redshift of z = 3.025 [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381, arXiv:astro-ph/0309817]. The system exhibits weak molecular hydrogen absorption in a single component [arXiv:0801.3682] and displays fine-structure carbon absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q0347 38 as an LBQS damped system above z=3, with HI=20.8, a sulfur upper limit relevant to S/Fe interpretation, CII* absorption, and very low Ni.

quotes.quote: Q0347 38, z = 3.025 The damped system toward Q0347 38 is another member of the LBQS statistical sample, one of the four with z_ abs > 3 . We adopt HI = 20.8 0.1 based on a measurement by Pettini et al. \ (1994). This is one of the few systems where we have an estimate of S^+ , although we note SII 1259 is partially blended with the forest and should be considered an upper limit to the true S abundance, S^+ < 10^ 14.73 2 . We discuss the S/Fe ratio in detail below, noting here that the value has particular impact on interpreting the abundances of the damped systems with respect to Type II SN enrichment and dust depletion.

The system is also notable for the easily identifiable excited fine structure CII ^* 1335 transition. Unfortunately, the CII 1334 profile is so heavily saturated that no meaningful comparison can be made with the fine structure transition. Finally, we observe a very low Ni abundance for this system, [ Ni/H ] < -2.37 dex, implying [ Ni/Fe ] < -0.5 dex which may be difficult to explain within the leading explanations for the damped abundance patterns.

sources.summary: The paper uses Q0347--3819 as a z=3.025 quasar sightline whose VLT/UVES absorption spectrum provides selected unblended H2 lines for line-center measurements and variation tests.

quotes.quote: Such system has been found at z = 3.025 toward the quasar Q0347--3819 . The absorption spectrum of this QSO was obtained with FWHM 7 km s ^ 1 and S/N 15-30 per resolution element at the VLT/UVES. 15 unblended H _2 lines (selected from 80 identified H _2 transitions) which provide the most accurate line center measurements are shown in Fig. 4. For a given H _2 line, the number of pixels involved in the analysis corresponds to the number of points in the line profile shown in Fig. 4. To measure the line centers we used a method that matches the observed profiles with the synthesized ones to estimate a set of model parameters (for details, see ).

sources.summary: The paper describes this DLA as a previously reported H2 detection, reanalyzed with higher-quality spectra, showing weak molecular absorption in one component up to J=4.

quotes.quote: Q 0347 3819, =3.025 : The detection of H _2 in this system was first reported by Levshakov02 using UVES commissioning data. Subsequently Ledoux03 analysed higher quality spectra of the system. Weak molecular absorption is observed in a single component up to J=4 .

gmw_00011121.name: Q 1441 + 2737, =4.224
gmw_00011121.overview: The high-redshift system Q 1441 + 2737, also identified as PSS J1443 + 2724, is a damped Lyman-alpha absorber located at a redshift of z = 4.224 [arXiv:astro-ph/0101029, arXiv:0801.3682]. It is the highest-redshift molecular hydrogen-bearing absorber known, featuring three distinct velocity components detected across multiple rotational transitions [arXiv:0801.3682].

sources.summary: The paper treats the z_abs=4.23 system toward PSS1443+22 as a high-metallicity DLA that strongly affects scatter and redshift-split metallicity tests but is retained because HI and Fe are well measured.

quotes.quote: For example, since the publication of PW00 we have received criticism that the results were largely biased by the single system towards PSS1443+22 ( z_ abs = 4.23 ; 1 ). Removing that data point from the current set of measurements reduces the mean statistics ( <Z>_ high by 0.1 dex and < > by 0.05) but statistically the effect is only significant in the scatter as this data point dominates ( )_ high . Eliminating PSS1443+22 reduces ( )_ high by 0.06 and a new F-test indicates the z_ low and z_ high distributions are inconsistent at 80 .l. Perhaps more intriguing, however, is that if one were to split the z_ high bin at z=3.5 into two sub-samples, eliminating the PSS1443+22 system would have the effect that <Z> would decrease markedly ( 0.4 dex) at z>3.5 . We stress, however, that there is no physical justification for disregarding the system towards PSS1443+22; both the HI and Fe column densities are well measured and there may be a significant number of high metallicity systems at z>4 .

sources.summary: The paper uses VLT/UVES observations of the z=4.42 quasar PSS J1443 + 2724 to analyze a z=4.22 H2 absorber and place a proton-electron mass-ratio variation limit.

quotes.quote: A high resolution spectrum of the J1443 + 2724 quasar, which was observed with the Very Large Telescope, is used to create an accurate model of 89 Lyman and Werner band transitions whose relative frequencies are sensitive to , yielding a limit on the relative deviation from the current laboratory value of / =(-9.5 5.4_ stat 5.3_ sys ) 10^ 6 .
Here we constrain variations in at substantially higher redshift by analyzing an H _2 absorber at z=4.22 along the sightline towards the background ( z=4.42 ) quasar PSS J1443 + 2724  .

A portion of the J1443 + 2724 spectrum is shown in Fig.  fig1 . To create the spectrum, we make use of an archival dataset obtained in 2004  and a new dataset obtained in 2013 (Program ID 090.A-0304) which comprise, respectively, 7.3 and 16.7 hours (5 and 12 exposures) observation time with the Very Large Telescope/the Ultraviolet and Visual Echelle Spectrograph (VLT/UVES).

sources.summary: The paper presents this as the highest-redshift known H2-bearing DLA, where high-resolution data recover many transitions in three velocity components.

quotes.quote: Q 1441 + 2737, =4.224 : Because of the high redshift of this system (actually the highest amongst known H _2 bearing DLAs), the Lyman- forest is quite dense in the wavelength region in which H _2 lines are redshifted. However, H _2 could be detected and studied in a large number of transitions thanks to the high spectral resolution of the data. Four rotational levels are detected in no less than three velocity components Ledoux06b .

gmw_00011122.name: Q 2348 0108, =2.426
gmw_00011122.overview: The damped Lyman-alpha system Q 2348 0108, =2.426 is a high-metallicity galaxy at high redshift [arXiv:astro-ph/0607376]. It features seven molecular hydrogen components spread over a velocity range of 250 to 300 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0607376, arXiv:0801.3682], which exhibit a high total molecular hydrogen column density [arXiv:astro-ph/0607376].

sources.summary: The paper describes the z_abs=2.426 DLA toward Q 2348 011 as high-metallicity, with conspicuous, numerous H2 lines, seven molecular components across 300 km/s, and a high total H2 column density.

quotes.quote: Q 2348 011, z_ abs = 2.426 There are two DLA systems at z_ abs =2.426 and z_ abs = 2.615 toward Q 2348 011, with total neutral hydrogen column densities of respectively log N (H i ) = 20.50 0.10 and log N (H i ) = 21.30 0.08 . Conspicuous H _2 absorptions are detected in the z_ abs 2.426 DLA system, the only system to be considered here as displaying a high metallicity (see Fig. q2348 ). The molecular lines are very numerous and strong but the spectral resolution of our data is high enough to allow unambiguous detection and accurate determination of the line parameters. Seven H _2 components spread over about 300 km s ^ 1 were used for the H _2 fit.

H _2 absorption from the rotational levels J = 0 to 5 are unambiguously detected. The total H _2 column density integrated over all rotational levels is log N (H _2 ) = 18.45 ^ +0.27 _ 0.26 , corresponding to a molecular fraction log f = 1.76_ 0.36 ^ +0.37 .

sources.summary: The paper describes this DLA as having seven H2 components over 250 km/s, with complex overlapping profiles but enough transitions for accurate rotational column densities.

quotes.quote: Q 2348 0108, =2.426 : No less than seven H _2 components are detected in this system Petitjean06, Noterdaeme07 . The H _2 profile is complex with three strong and four weak components spread over about 250 km s ^ 1 , making H _2 lines from different transitions overlap. However, the large number of observed transitions and the good data quality allow an accurate measurement of column density for different rotational levels.

gmw_00011123.name: WISEA J223659.87-161406.7
gmw_00011123.overview: WISEA J223659.87-161406.7 is a galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.0717, placing it at a luminosity distance of approximately 320 megaparsecs [arXiv:2006.10442]. Its spectrum exhibits both emission and absorption features, and it is identified as the likely host galaxy of the transient PS15cey [arXiv:2006.10442].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J223659.87-161406.7 as PS15cey’s likely host, measuring a spectroscopic redshift, luminosity distance, transient offset, and S0/Sa-like spectrum.

quotes.quote: Cross-matching with galaxy catalogues suggests a potential host in the galaxy WISEA J223659.87-161406.7, though the host is identifiable in several catalogues. Within LEDA, this galaxy is recognised as PGC900758, and, according to the GLADE catalogue, has a redshift of z=0.038 . This is likely a photometric redshift however, as a spectroscopic source cannot be corroborated, and hence is not reliable enough to draw conclusions from. Owing to this uncertainty on the redshift, a spectrum of the host galaxy was taken with EFOSC2 at the New Technology Telescope (NTT), using Grism 13 (wavelength coverage 3685-9315 ), on MJD 58816.066 (2019-11-29 01:35:41 UTC) as part of the advanced Public ESO Spectroscopic Survey for Transient Objects (ePESSTO+).

The spectrum has identifiable emission and absorption features, including the \ lines and \ H K lines, as well as some H emission. From these lines, we can measure a redshift to the host of z=0.0717 0.0006 . This corresponds to a luminosity distance of 320 \ for our chosen cosmology. We estimate that PS15cey is offset by 32 4 from its host, or equivalently a physical separation of approximately 50.3 .

gmw_00011124.name: NGC 4318
gmw_00011124.overview: NGC 4318 is an elliptical galaxy classified as E3 with discy isophotes and a moderate flattening [arXiv:1105.4809]. Its stellar population has an age that increases from 6 to 8 Gyr outward, while its metallicity decreases from a solar-abundance center [arXiv:1105.4809].

sources.summary: NGC 4318 is an E3 galaxy with discy isophotes, low-luminosity Sersic index, rising rotation, declining velocity dispersion, and outward age-metallicity changes.

quotes.quote: NGC 4318 = VCC 575 An elliptical (E3) galaxy with discy isophotes, a S e rsic index typical of low-luminosity galaxies (1.66) and a moderate flattening ( _ max = 0.33 at = 65 arcsec). The rotation velocity increases to V_ max = 85 , reached at 8 arcsec ( 1 ). Then, it remains constant or decreases slightly. Over the same radial range where V rises, the velocity dispersion decreases from _0 = 82 \ to _ a_ eff = 55 , and remains constant outwards. The age is mildly raising from 6 to 8 Gyr, while the metallicity decreases outwards from a Solar centre.

gmw_00011125.name: FS 76
gmw_00011125.overview: FS 76 is an almost round, compact dwarf galaxy with a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:1105.4809]. Its velocity dispersion declines sharply outside of one half-light radius, and its decreasing central metallicity profile may indicate the occurrence of tidal stripping [arXiv:1105.4809].

sources.summary: FS 76 is a compact, nearly round dwarf with a kinematically decoupled core, declining outer velocity dispersion, possible tidal stripping, and decreasing central metallicity.

quotes.quote: FS 76 = PGC 83852 This almost round dwarf has a kinematically decoupled core. Its velocity dispersion, which initially rises with the radius, starts to decline sharply outside of one half-light radius. Its S ersic index of 2 indicates higher level of compactness than the other dEs at similar luminosity (M _B = 17.1 mag). This was interpreted as evidence for a truncated dark halo and hence, for the occurrence of tidal striping ddzh01 . Its metallicity profile regularly decreases from 0.2 to -0.2 dex in the central 3 arcsec.

gmw_00011126.name: COSMOS-4064
gmw_00011126.overview: COSMOS-4064 is an extreme emission-line galaxy characterized by strong rest-UV, [O III], and Mg II emission [arXiv:1910.11877, arXiv:2007.12197]. It is a low-mass, metal-poor system with an exceptionally young stellar population and no detected X-ray emission [arXiv:1910.11877, arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: COSMOS-4064 is an extreme emission-line galaxy with strong rest-UV and [O iii] emission, no Chandra X-ray identification, low mass, young stars, high ionization, and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: Among these, only two objects (COSMOS-4064 and COSMOS-7686, both EELGs) show detectable C iv 1548,1550 , He ii 1640 , and O iii ] 1661,1665 , as observed in a handful of spectroscopically confirmed z>6.5 galaxies e.g., Stark2015 . COSMOS-4064 and COSMOS-7686 first stand out because of their extremely strong emission in [ O iii ] , Ly , and C iii ] . As for COSMOS-4064, although the LRIS spectrum does not cover Ly , its [ O iii ] 4959,5007 EW ( 1940 403 ) is the largest in our LRIS sample. We note that neither COSMOS-4064 nor COSMOS-7686 is identified as an X-ray source in the Chandra COSMOS Legacy Survey Civano2016 . In the discussion that follows, we interpret the strong emission lines in these two sources as due primarily to active star formation.

We reported two extreme emission galaxies in our LRIS sample, COSMOS-7686 and COSMOS-4064, the properties of which resemble those of the small sample of z>6.5 galaxies with detections of C iii ] and C iv emission lines e.g., Stark2015b,Stark2015,Stark2017 . These two galaxies show noticeably strong emission from C iii ] , C iv , He ii , and O iii ] 1665 . After incorporating the observed rest-UV line EWs into SED modeling, we discovered that both objects have low stellar masses (log(M/M _ ) 8.60 ), exceptionally young stellar populations ( <20 Myr), large sSFRs ( >60 Gyr ^ 1 ), high ionization parameters (log(U) 2.0 ), and low gas-phase metallicities ( 0.25Z_ ).

sources.summary: The paper finds COSMOS-04064 shows Si III] and strong Mg II emission, and combines the new IMACS C III] measurement with earlier Keck/LRIS UV-line detections.

quotes.quote: We also detected the Si III ] 1883,1892 emission lines in COSMOS-04064 (Figure fig:uv_spec ). The Si III ] EWs (Si III ] 1883 EW =2.2 0.3 , Si III ] 1892 EW =2.6 0.2 ) of this object are comparable to those measured for low mass, metal-poor galaxies at z 0-2 Berg2018,Berg2019,Mainali2020 . Two systems show Mg II 2796,2803 emission (see Figure fig:uv_spec ). Both sources show rather intense line emission, with COSMOS-04064 (Mg II 2796 EW =7.5 0.4 , Mg II 2803 EW =2.1 0.3 ) appearing stronger than COSMOS-18358 (Mg II 2796 EW =2.9 0.2 , Mg II 2803 EW =2.2 0.2 ).

For one of the C III ] emitters in our sample (COSMOS-04064), we are not able to constrain rest-wavelengths shorter than Si III ] due to the low spectral sensitivity at the blue end of IMACS spectra. This galaxy was previously observed with Keck/LRIS in Du2020 , with multiple UV lines detected (C IV 1549 , He II 1640 , O III ] 1666 , C III ] 1908 ). The C III ] EW ( 6.9 0.3 ) measured from Du2020 is consistent with the value measured from our IMACS spectrum ( 7.2 0.3 ).

gmw_00011127.name: COSMOS-7686
gmw_00011127.overview: COSMOS-7686 is an extreme emission-line galaxy characterized by exceptionally strong Lyman-alpha and carbon III emission [arXiv:1910.11877]. It hosts a young stellar population under 20 million years old, featuring low stellar mass, high ionization, and low gas-phase metallicity [arXiv:1910.11877].

sources.summary: COSMOS-7686 is an extreme emission-line galaxy with the strongest Ly and C iii] emission among LRIS targets, no Chandra X-ray identification, low mass, young stars, high ionization, and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: Among these, only two objects (COSMOS-4064 and COSMOS-7686, both EELGs) show detectable C iv 1548,1550 , He ii 1640 , and O iii ] 1661,1665 , as observed in a handful of spectroscopically confirmed z>6.5 galaxies e.g., Stark2015 . COSMOS-4064 and COSMOS-7686 first stand out because of their extremely strong emission in [ O iii ] , Ly , and C iii ] . COSMOS-7686, with an EW _ [ O iii ] 4959,5007 =1417 236 , has the strongest emission in Ly (EW _ Ly =131.6 10.9 ) and C iii ] (EW _ C iii ] 1907,1909 =13.2 0.8 ) among the LRIS targets. We note that neither COSMOS-4064 nor COSMOS-7686 is identified as an X-ray source in the Chandra COSMOS Legacy Survey Civano2016 . In the discussion that follows, we interpret the strong emission lines in these two sources as due primarily to active star formation.

We reported two extreme emission galaxies in our LRIS sample, COSMOS-7686 and COSMOS-4064, the properties of which resemble those of the small sample of z>6.5 galaxies with detections of C iii ] and C iv emission lines e.g., Stark2015b,Stark2015,Stark2017 . These two galaxies show noticeably strong emission from C iii ] , C iv , He ii , and O iii ] 1665 . After incorporating the observed rest-UV line EWs into SED modeling, we discovered that both objects have low stellar masses (log(M/M _ ) 8.60 ), exceptionally young stellar populations ( <20 Myr), large sSFRs ( >60 Gyr ^ 1 ), high ionization parameters (log(U) 2.0 ), and low gas-phase metallicities ( 0.25Z_ ).

gmw_00011128.name: BRL 1602--174
gmw_00011128.overview: BRL 1602--174 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.04 [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567]. Its field contains only two candidate Lyman-alpha emitters due to high extinction and detector limitations [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BRL 1602--174 as a z=2.04 radio galaxy, images its field, and finds only two candidate emitters because of extinction and detector limitations.

quotes.quote: The radio source BRL 1602--174 was optically identified with a galaxy with m _R = 21.4 object along the radio axis. A spectrum of this galaxy yielded a redshift of 2.043 0.002 based on four emission lines bes99 . This places the \ line of the radio galaxy in the lowest wavelength narrow-band filter available for FORS2, the \ filter.

The field was imaged for 250 minutes in the narrow-band and for 45 minutes in the B band. Due to the high extinction towards this field A_B 1 , sch98 and the low quantum efficiency at wavelengths < 4000 \ of the FORS2 detector, only two candidate \ emitters were found. No spectra were taken in this field.

gmw_00011129.name: MRC 0052--241
gmw_00011129.overview: MRC 0052--241 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.86 [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567]. It is surrounded by an overdensity of Lyman-alpha emitting galaxies, which are clustered in a manner that suggests they are part of a forming galaxy cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MRC 0052--241 at z=2.86, confirms many neighboring Ly-alpha emitters, and argues their overdensity and clumpy redshift distribution trace a forming cluster.

quotes.quote: The optical counterpart of the radio source 0052--241 from the Molonglo Reference Catalogue lar81 was found by mcc96 to be a m_R = 23.2 object. A spectrum of this object showed strong \ emission at a redshift of z=2.86 mcc96 .

Results Of the 36 objects observed 35 were confirmed to be \ emitters at a redshift z 2.86 . The 36th candidate was most likely too faint to be detected. This candidate had the smallest line flux of the objects in the mask.

The resulting density of emitters near MRC 0052--241 is a factor 3.1^ +1.4 _ 1.0 higher than the field density. The weighted average of the three estimates of the overdensity is 3.0^ +0.9 _ 0.6 . It is interesting to compare the density of \ emitters in the 0052 field with the density of emitters near the radio galaxy MRC 0316--257 at z = 3.13 (V05). The depth and sensitivity of the imaging of the two fields are very similar (see Tables imgobs and imgres ). There are 52 candidate emitters in the 0052 field with a narrow-band magnitude brighter than 26.1, against 59 in the 0316 field. Taking into account the difference in the volume probed by the narrow-band images, the ratio of the number density is n_ 0052 /n_ 0316 = 0.8^ +0.2 _ 0.2 . Because the 0316 field is overdense in \ emitters by a factor 3.3^ +0.5 _ 0.4 (V05), this means that the 0052 field has an overdensity of \ emitting galaxies of 2.7^ +0.8 _ 0.6 , which is consistent within the errors with the other estimates. Velocity and spatial distributions The velocity histogram and the spatial distribution of the candidate and confirmed emitters are shown in Fig. \ 0052velskydist . The redshifts of the confirmed emitters are not uniformly distributed throughout the narrow-band filter (solid curve in Fig. \ 0052velskydist ). Instead, the majority of the emitters (31 out of the 37) appear to be clustered in two groups with velocity dispersions of 180 and 230 . Interestingly, a similar redshift distribution was found near the radio galaxy MRC 1138--262 pen00a . The velocity of the radio galaxy lies 70 \ from the median redshift of the emitters and falls inside the larger of the two groups. The combination of the observed overdensity of \ emitters with the clumpy redshift distribution provides evidence that the \ emitters near MRC 0052--241 are part of a forming cluster of galaxies at z = 2.86 .

gmw_00011130.name: TN J2009--3040
gmw_00011130.overview: TN J2009--3040 is a high-redshift radio source and quasar located at a redshift of 3.156 [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567]. It is surrounded by Lyman-alpha emitting galaxies whose spatial and velocity clustering may indicate an associated galaxy structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567].

sources.summary: The paper identifies TN J2009--3040 as a z=3.156 quasar/radio source and finds nearby Ly-alpha emitters whose spatial and velocity clustering may indicate a galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: This radio source was selected by deb00 as an ultra steep spectrum radio source ( = -1.36 , with f_ ^ ) and was identified with a m_K = 18.05 0.05 object which has a strong unresolved component deb02 . Spectroscopy of this object revealed three emission lines, including \ with a FWHM of 2000 \ at a redshift of 3.156. Based on the width of the emission lines and the morphology of the source, this radio source is identified as a quasar (C. De Breuck, private communications).

This could indicate that (locally) the volume density of \ emitters near the radio source TN J2009--3040 is much higher than the field density. Although the volume density of \ emitters near TN J2009--3040 is consistent with the field density at that redshift, the clustering both on the sky and in velocity space of the emitters could points to a structure of galaxies. More observations are needed to determine the reality of this clustering.

gmw_00011131.name: Lensed galaxy B
gmw_00011131.overview: Lensed galaxy B is a high-redshift galaxy that is strongly gravitationally lensed into multiple images [arXiv:1609.08848]. Spectroscopic analysis of these images reveals a redshift of z=4.56, which is supported by the galaxy's complete dropout from ACS/F435W filter observations [arXiv:1609.08848].

sources.summary: The paper discusses lensed galaxy B as a high-redshift multiply imaged source, with stacked spectra, emission-line redshift evidence, dropout behavior, and matching faint lines in other images.

quotes.quote: Lensed galaxy B : Figure fig.gmos shows stacked spectra of arc B1 from four slitlets, two in Mask 1 and two in Mask 2. We identify emission lines from , SiII 1260, OI+SiII 1303, HeII 1640, and CIV 1449 at z=4.56 . The images of B drop out completely from the ACS/F435W filter, which supports this redshift interpretation. Furthermore, the photometric redshift analysis obtained for this source from the five \ bands shows a single high significance peak around z_ phot =4.4 .

The slits placed on B2 and B3 resulted in too low S/N for an independent measurement of the redshift. Nonetheless we detect a faint emission line in these spectra that is consistent with \ at the same redshift as image B1.

gmw_00011132.name: Lensed galaxy C
gmw_00011132.overview: Lensed galaxy C is a faint, gravitationally lensed source with a possible spectroscopic redshift of z=3.01 [arXiv:1609.08848]. This potential redshift is supported by both photometric and lensing geometry analyses [arXiv:1609.08848].

sources.summary: The paper treats lensed galaxy C as a faint three-image source with low-S/N spectra, a possible CIV absorption feature, and redshift support from photometry and lensing geometry.

quotes.quote: Lensed galaxy C : We targeted C2 and C3, with a total on-target exposure time of 2400 s on each image; however since these sources are faint, the resulting spectra have low S/N. A possible absorption line is detected at 6210 . Interpreting this absorption feature as the CIV 1548,1550 lines, which are often among the most prominent rest-frame UV features in star-forming galaxies, places this source at z=3.01. We note that this putative spectroscopic redshift is also consistent with the photometric redshift analysis and favored by the lensing analysis. Nevertheless, given its low certainty we do not consider this a secure spectroscopic redshift for the purpose of lensing analysis.

gmw_00011133.name: Lensed galaxy D
gmw_00011133.overview: Lensed galaxy D is a strongly gravitationally lensed source [arXiv:1609.08848]. It is interpreted as two separate background sources, consisting of a low-redshift blue arc and a high-redshift source [arXiv:1609.08848].

sources.summary: The paper describes lensed galaxy D as a contaminated candidate with low-confidence spectroscopy, bimodal photometric redshifts, and evidence for separate low- and high-redshift background sources.

quotes.quote: Lensed galaxy D : We placed slitlets on D1, D2, and D3. As can be seen in Figure fig.masks , the slits are expected to contain light from adjacent sources (in particular, blue emission from a nearby galaxy). The spectroscopic analysis results in a low confidence redshift of z=0.837 for D1 and D2, based on Ca H K lines, and no signal in D3. The photometric redshift probability distribution function is bimodal, with a high significance peak around z=4.4 and low-significance peak around z=0.74 for images D1 and D3. Image D2 shows only one peak at z=0.74 .

Since this is the image that is most contaminated by blue light from the nearby galaxy, we argue that this interpretation is consistent with two separate redshifts for two different background sources. A blue arc at low-z, consistent with the possible z=0.837 that is suggested by the spectroscopy, and a high redshift source at z 4.4 which is likely the three-imaged lensed source D.

gmw_00011134.name: GRB 150403A
gmw_00011134.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 150403A is a spatially extended system at a redshift of z=2.06 [arXiv:2310.15967]. Observations have resolved three distinct line-emitting regions within the galaxy that exhibit varying metallicities [arXiv:2310.15967].

sources.summary: The paper observes the extended GRB 150403A host with NIRSpec IFS, detecting three line-emitting regions and finding component-dependent metallicities compared with the absorption value.

quotes.quote: The host galaxies of GRB 050820A and GRB 150403A showed evidence of spatially extended emission in the available imaging data, and NIRSpec observations were therefore performed using the integral field spectrograph (IFS), which has a 3 3 field of view. For GRB 150403A, pre-imaging data were available with the GRB Optical and Near-infrared Detector GROND; gbc+08 mounted on the 2.2 m Max Planck Institute telescope in La Silla, Chile. From these data the host galaxy appeared extended over 2 , and thus a four-point dither was used instead of nodding to avoid any of the galaxy falling out of the 3 3 \ NIRSpec field of view during a nod.

GRB 150403A An image taken of the field of GRB 150403A almost six months after the GRB with the GRB Optical/Near-infrared Detector on the MPG 2.2 m telescope in La Silla, Chile gbc+08 showed extended emission at the position of the GRB. This is confirmed with the NIRSpec IFS observations, where line emission from , , \ and \ is clearly detected in three regions of the data cube at a redshift consistent with the GRB afterglow z=2.06 ; pxt+15 (see Fig. fig:grb150403A ).

The resolved components considered in the host galaxy of GRB 150403A, on the other hand, do vary in metallicity by up to 0.3 dex. The absorption line metallicity is most consistent with the the emission line metallicity of component C in the case of the NOX22 \ and \ diagnostics, but it is most consistent with the R metallicity of component B.

gmw_00011135.name: GRB 080804
gmw_00011135.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 080804 is an emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2310.15967]. It has a stellar mass estimate of 9.28 solar masses, which is based on a single mid-infrared data point and is considered an upper limit [arXiv:2310.15967].

sources.summary: The paper compares GRB 080804 host emission-line measurements with earlier X-shooter data, treats its high R diagnostic value, and regards its SED-based stellar mass as an upper limit.

quotes.quote: For the host galaxies of GRB 080804 and GRB 090323, lower signal to noise emission line flux measurements were already reported by kmf+15 from X-shooter data, most of which are consistent at 1 with our NIRSpec measurements.

The host galaxy of GRB 080804 and region C of the host galaxy of GRB 150403A also have a large value of R , but with an uncertainty that lies within the range of values considered in the LMC23 metallicity diagnostic. For the remaining three GRBs, a single, non-refractory element was used to determine the absorption metallicity; either sulphur (GRB 030323 and GRB 050505), or zinc (GRB 080804) cfr+15 . The Fe ii column density along the line of sight to GRB 080804 is only constrained to lie within the range N_ Fe / cm ^2=14.66-15.14 (C. Ledoux private communication), which corresponds to a dust depletion correction _ Zn between 0.26 and 0.39 .

The host galaxies with mass estimates or limits are GRB 030323 M_ /M_ <9.23 ; lbc11 , GRB 050820A M_ /M_ =9.29 ; cpp+09 , GRB 080804 M_ /M_ =9.28 ; pth+16 , GRB 090323 M_ /M_ =11.2 ; mkr+10 , and GRB 121024A M_ /M_ =9.9 ; fdk+15 . The exception is in the case of the host galaxy of GRB 080804, where the stellar mass is based on a single mid-infrared data point, which can over-estimate M_ by 0.4 dex compared to stellar masses from SED fitting. This stellar mass can therefore be considered an upper limit.

gmw_00011136.name: IC 328
gmw_00011136.overview: IC 328 is a bright, late-type galaxy with a somewhat disturbed morphology [arXiv:0705.1705]. It is an actively star-forming system characterized by very blue colors and infrared emission detected in all four IRAS bands [arXiv:0705.1705].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 328 as a nearby, actively star-forming late-type galaxy in the GRB 050906 error circle, with blue colors, IRAS detections, elevated star formation, and a plausible but weakened host association.

quotes.quote: IC 328 is a bright K=11.4 galaxy and its colours, and somewhat disturbed morphology, are consistent with an actively star forming, late-type galaxy. Its observed B-band magnitude (B _0 =14.9, M_B =-20.7) is approximately L* and its R-K colour of 2.3 is very blue, also indicative of ongoing star formation. IC 328 was detected by IRAS in all four bands (10,25,60,100 m ). Converting from the observed 60 m flux to a star formation rate assuming a relation of SFR = 5.5 * (L_ 60 / 5.1 10^ 30 ergs/s/Hz ) (Kennicutt 1998) results in a star formation rate for IC 328 of 17 M _ yr ^ 1 .

Hence, while the association of GRB 050906 with IC 328 remains plausible, and would have been identified as the host galaxy by various approaches (e.g. that of Gal-Yam et al. 2006), the location at the edge of the error circle does somewhat weaken the case for an association. Of course, were GRB 050906 due to a low-luminosity NS-NS merger event within IC328 (or even IC 327) then it may be expected to be at a large distance from its parent galaxy either because it took place in the halo (eg. a globular cluster), or having been ejected outside the main body of the galaxy by a natal supernova kick.

gmw_00011137.name: J1436+4943
gmw_00011137.overview: J1436+4943 is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.125 [arXiv:1911.06338]. It acts as a strong gravitational lens, producing multiple images of a background source in a cusp configuration with an Einstein radius of 2.0 arcseconds [arXiv:1911.06338].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1436+4943 as a z=0.125 elliptical strong lens; Subaru FOCAS confirms multiple imaging in a cusp configuration and gives a secure Einstein radius used for mass-to-light analysis.

quotes.quote: J1436+4943 J1701+3722 [ O ii ] [ O iii ] H We present new observations of two z = 0.12 strong-lensing elliptical galaxies, originally discovered from the SDSS-IV MaNGA survey, using the new FOCAS IFU spectrograph on the Subaru Telescope. For , our observations confirm the identification of this system as a multiple-image lens, in a cusp configuration, with Einstein radius _ Ein = 2.0 arcsec. For , the improved data confirm earlier hints of a complex source plane, with different configurations evident in different emission lines. The new observations reveal a previously unseen inner counter-image to the [ O iii ] arc found from MaNGA, leading to a smaller revised Einstein radius of _ Ein = 1.6 arcsec. The inferred projected masses within the Einstein apertures (3.7--4.7 kpc) are consistent with being dominated by stars with an initial mass function (IMF) similar to that of the Milky Way, and a dark matter contribution of 35 per cent as supported from cosmological simulations.

The foreground galaxy is an elliptical at redshift z_ l = 0.125, with velocity dispersion = 282 10 km s ^ 1 from the single-fibre SDSS spectrum 2009ApJS..182..543A . MaNGA revealed background \ emission at z_ s = 1.231, apparently forming two separate arcs east and west of the target galaxy 2018MNRAS.477..195T . Figure fig:j14 shows the net \ emission line image for the source, from the Subaru data. The lens light has been subtracted using the neighbouring continuum, and a correction has been made to remove a radial trend in the net image (caused by gradients in spectral features in the lens galaxy). The \ image confirms the presence of an extended arc (labelled A and B) to the east, and a western counter-image (C), suggesting a cusp lens configuration. Figure fig:j14spec shows the \ doublet in the spectra for the three labelled regions. To derive the Einstein radius, we have fitted simple lensing models to the net \ image using PyAutoLens 2018ascl.soft07003N,2018MNRAS.478.4738N .

gmw_00011138.name: companion to the west
gmw_00011138.overview: The companion to the west is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.3121 [arXiv:astro-ph/9803243]. It exhibits oxygen emission lines and may have undergone strong interactions with neighboring galaxies, producing tidal debris [arXiv:astro-ph/9803243].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the companion to the west as a galaxy with z=0.3121 emission lines, favoring it as the likely absorber near PKS B1127-145 and possibly interacting tidal debris.

quotes.quote: The spectra of both companions indicate that they are galaxies, and not additional, lensed images of the quasar. Thus there is no evidence for lensing in this system. The companion object to the east shows no emission lines, and the S/N in the continuum is not good enough to determine its redshift. However, the companion to the west does show [ OIII ] 4958 and [ OII ] 5007 emission at z = 0.3121 0.0003 , making it a candidate object for causing the neutral hydrogen absorption at similar redshift.

The new emission object is a close companion to the west of the quasar and lies at a projected distance of 3.9 or 11 h^ 1 _ 100 kpc from the quasar sightline. Based on comparison with other objects in our image for which apparent magnitudes are given in Bergeron and Boiss e (1991), it has an apparent magnitude of m_r = 22.3 .

A column density of neutral gas of 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 is unlikely at this galactic radius, and we consider the new emission object, although smaller and fainter, to be the more likely absorber given its proximity to the quasar sightline. It is also possible that the three galaxies at similar redshift have undergone strong interaction with each other, in which case the absorbing gas could be tidal debris.

gmw_00011139.name: SDSS J224024.1--092748
gmw_00011139.overview: SDSS J224024.1--092748 is a Green Bean galaxy containing an active galactic nucleus that accounts for most of its total luminosity [arXiv:1502.07754]. The system features a co-rotating ionized gas cloud and regions of fast shocks, which may be driven by a radio jet [arXiv:1502.07754].

sources.summary: The paper maps SDSS J224024.1--092748 as a Green Bean quasar ionization echo with AGN-dominated emission, shocks, and a co-rotating ionized cloud linked to prior quasar activity.

quotes.quote: We use integral field spectroscopy from the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph to analyse the 3D kinematics, ionization state, temperature and density of ionized gas in the GB SDSS J224024.1--092748 . We model the emission line spectrum of each spaxel as a superposition of up to three Gaussian components and analyse the physical properties of each component individually. Two narrow components, tracing the velocity fields of the disc and an ionized gas cloud, are superimposed over the majority of the galaxy. Fast shocks produce hot ( T_e 20,000 K ), dense ( n_e 100 cm^ 3 ), turbulent ( 600 km s^ 1 ), bright regions with enhanced \ and \ ratios.

The most prominent such spot is consistent with a radio jet shock-heating the interstellar medium. However, the AGN is still responsible for 82 per cent of the galaxy's total \ luminosity, strengthening the case for previous quasar activity. The ionized gas cloud has a strong kinematic link to the central AGN and is co-rotating with the main body of the galaxy, suggesting that it may be the remnant of a quasar-driven outflow. Our analysis of J224024.1--092748 indicates that GBs provide a unique fossil record of the transformation from the most luminous quasars to weak AGN.

gmw_00011140.name: Carina dwarf spheroidal galaxy
gmw_00011140.overview: The Carina dwarf spheroidal galaxy is characterized by a stellar population that formed across four distinct epochs [arXiv:1705.09020]. These star formation events occurred approximately 13, 7, 3.5, and 1.5 billion years ago, resulting in distinct subgiant and main-sequence components [arXiv:1705.09020].

sources.summary: The paper models Carina's CMD as four star-formation epochs with distinct metallicities, mass fractions, and CMD features, using its centered photometric sample to constrain population structure.

quotes.quote: We investigate the color-magnitude diagram (CMD) of the Carina dwarf spheroidal galaxy using data of stetson11 and synthetic CMDs based on isochrones of dotter08 , in terms of the parameters [ Fe/H ] , age, and , for the cases when (i) is held constant and (ii) is varied. The data are well described by four basic epochs of star formation, having [ Fe/H ] = --1.85, --1.5, --1.2, and -1.15 and ages  13, 7, 3.5 , and 1.5 Gyr, respectively (for = 0.1 (constant ) and = 0.2, 0.1, --0.2, --0.2 (variable )), with small spreads in [ Fe/H ] and age of order 0.1 dex and 1 -- 3 Gyr. Within an elliptical radius 13.1 , the mass fractions of the populations, at their times of formation, were (in decreasing age order) 0.34, 0.39, 0.23, and 0.04.

This formalism reproduces five observed CMD features (two distinct subgiant branches of old and intermediate-age populations, two younger, main-sequence components, and the small color dispersion on the red giant branch (RGB).

The Carina CMD Figure  fig:carcmd presents the data of Stetson (2014, private communication) in the ( , ) -- plane for all of the 31000 stars within an elliptical region centered on RA = 06 41 36 and Dec = --50 58 26 (2000) and elliptic radius _ ellip = 13.1 , with e = 0.33 and position angle 65 ^ irwin95 .

gmw_00011141.name: HCG 31 A
gmw_00011141.overview: HCG 31 A is currently undergoing a merger with the neighboring galaxy HCG 31 C [arXiv:2304.02857]. While ionization in the system is primarily driven by star formation, the nucleus of HCG 31 A also shows a minor contribution from shocks [arXiv:2304.02857].

sources.summary: The paper presents HCG 31 F as a knotty tidal-dwarf candidate with unusual flat kinematics, low metallicity, metal-poor gas accretion, and young high-SFR star formation.

quotes.quote: Our main findings are: (i) that there is clearly disturbed kinematics due to the merger event that the system is experiencing; (ii) that the ionization is produced exclusively via star formation except for the nucleus of the galaxy HCG 31 A, where there is a small contribution of shocks; (iii) that there is low oxygen abundance distributed homogeneously through the system; (iv) that there is a prominent population of carbon Wolf-Rayet stars in the central zone of the group; and (v) that there are clear evidences of the tidal origin of the galaxies HCG 31 E, HCG 31 H, and HCG 31 F because they show quite high oxygen abundances for their stellar mass.

The object F is composed of three different knots: F1, F2, and F3. This object is the most plausible TDG candidate in HCG 31 ( iglesias-paramo&vilchez97 , Richer+03 , lopez-sanchez+04 , mendesdol+06 , amram+07 , verdes-montenegro+05 , gallagher+10 ), along with member R mendesdol+06 (out of our FoV). Member F does not show a velocity gradient in our position-velocity diagram; our velocity gradient seems flat, with no substantial variations in velocity.

The H kinematics of member F is quite peculiar, because it shows no rotation in any axis amram+07 , and, our radial velocity map (Section radvel_results ) confirmed that result. The HI kinematics of F verdes-montenegro+05 shows that it is kinematically decoupled from the tail, with an axis of rotation that is perpendicular to the axis of the tail, suggesting that this object was already decoupled from the tail. proposed that this object was accreting material from the tail, but that scenario is difficult to prove, and simulations are needed to confirm it. In this work we detect high SFRs and high EW(H ) (young ages) in member F, which is fully consistent with this scenario.

sources.summary: The paper treats HCG 31 C as part of the central merger, noting its Wolf-Rayet features, super star cluster, oxygen abundance transition, and interaction with HCG 31 A.

quotes.quote: An interesting result obtained by these authors was the detection of a Wolf-Rayet bump in the spectrum of the galaxy HCG 31 C, which indicates the presence of Wolf-Rayet stars with very young ages (< 4 Myrs). This bump can be identified with the blend of the spectral lines He _ II \ 4686 \ , C _ III /C _ IV \ 4650 \ and N _ III \ 4634,4640 \ . Moreover, the most important emission in the bump arises from the helium lines. The presence of such stars is strong evidence of a young starburst in this system.

This demonstrates that the HCG 31 A + C system cannot be described as a single rotating disk, despite exhibiting a rotational pattern. This reinforces the hypothesis proposed by A07, where they suggest that what we are observing in the central region is the merging of the HCG 31 A and HCG 31 C galaxies.
In this figure we observed a slight transition from \ = 8.23 0.16 (N2) in HCG 31 C to = 8.40 0.16 (N2) in HCG 31 A.

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs HCG 31 A's star-formation history, identifies shocks near its nucleus, and interprets it as merging with HCG 31 C in the group's central region.

quotes.quote: We also reconstructed the star formation history of the galaxies HCG 31 A, C, B and F, measured the emission-line fluxes, and performed a stellar population synthesis. Our main findings are: (i) that there is clearly disturbed kinematics due to the merger event that the system is experiencing; (ii) that the ionization is produced exclusively via star formation except for the nucleus of the galaxy HCG 31 A, where there is a small contribution of shocks; (iii) that there is low oxygen abundance distributed homogeneously through the system; (iv) that there is a prominent population of carbon Wolf-Rayet stars in the central zone of the group; and (v) that there are clear evidences of the tidal origin of the galaxies HCG 31 E, HCG 31 H, and HCG 31 F because they show quite high oxygen abundances for their stellar mass.

This demonstrates that the HCG 31 A + C system cannot be described as a single rotating disk, despite exhibiting a rotational pattern. This reinforces the hypothesis proposed by A07, where they suggest that what we are observing in the central region is the merging of the HCG 31 A and HCG 31 C galaxies.

The SFH of galaxy A is very similar to that of the A+C complex, with an important underlying old stellar population at 1 Gyr, another peak at 10 ^ 7 yrs to 10 ^ 8 yrs and the current star formation event at 10 ^ 6 yrs to 10 ^ 7 yrs ago. It should be noted that the SFH of galaxy A represents only the SFH of the extended body that is seen in the optical images.

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 31 B as a western kinematic member with measured rotation, a metallicity pattern around knots and bridge material, and a central abundance drop.

quotes.quote: We identified three different kinematic entities: i) the central region (A+C), (ii) a western member called galaxy B, and (iii) the southern tidal tail composed of three sub-regions (E1, E2), (H1, H2, H3) and (F1, F2, F3).

For galaxy B, A07 derived the following kinematic parameters; PA = 135 3 ^ , i = 60 5 ^ , and rotational velocity is 120 km s ^ 1 . Our velocity gradient (applying an inclination correction assuming i = 60 5 ^ ) is consistent with the determinations of A07 and also with the HI map presented by .

Galaxy B showed a fairly homogeneous distribution of metallicity at the surroundings of the knot B2 with 8.3 and a drop to lower metallicities at B1 and B3 with \ 8.1 . The bridge that connects B and A+C seemed to have the same metallicity as the main body of B.

gmw_00011142.name: 5C 3.178
gmw_00011142.overview: Located southeast of M31, 5C 3.178 is a point-like radio source and suspected BL Lac object at a redshift of z=0.2517 [arXiv:1302.3388]. It has exhibited transient, hard gamma-ray emission attributed to a high-energy flare [arXiv:1302.3388].

sources.summary: The paper identifies transient hard gamma-ray emission from 5C 3.178, argues it is a BL Lac flare, and suggests the source could be observable by Cherenkov telescopes.

quotes.quote: We report the serendipitous discovery of transient, point-like high energy \ emission coincident with the position of the suspected BL Lac object 5C 3.178 . The source was detected using the \ Large Area Telescope (LAT) at a significance level of 8 during a 200 day period which began on November 17th 2011 (MJD 55882). The observed \ emission is characterised by a moderate 0.2-300 GeV flux F_ 0.2-300 GeV =(8.22 2.04) 10^ 9 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and a hard power law spectrum with spectral index =1.76 0.09 .

5C 3.178 is a point-like radio source located to the southeast of M31 at ( _ J2000 , _ J2000 ) = (00^ h 47^ m 55^ s .220, = +39^ 48'57''.60) 2011NewA...16..503M . The source was initially discovered using the Cambridge one-mile radio telescope 1969MNRAS.144..101P and has been provisionally identified as a BL Lac object based on the absence of detectable emission features in its optical spectrum 1987AJ.....93....1B,1995ApJS..101..255D . Multi-wavelength counterparts have been identified at near infra-red 2006AJ....131.1163S, 2011NewA...16..503M , optical 1995ApJS..101..255D , ultraviolet 2009MNRAS.399..728F and X-ray 1999A&A...349..389V,2008A&A...480..611S frequencies. A source redshift of z=0.2517 0.001 has been established on the basis of optical absorption features imprinted by the host galaxy 1995ApJS..101..255D .
On the basis of this combined evidence, it seems reasonable to attribute the observed \ emission to a HE flare of the BL Lac object 5C 3.178 .

gmw_00011143.name: GMP3792
gmw_00011143.overview: GMP3792 is an early-type galaxy located in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1308.6581]. It is classified as a slow rotator that shows no evidence of global rotation extending to large radii, and it features a dynamically hot dispersion map [arXiv:1308.6581].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GMP3792 as a Coma slow rotator, finds no global rotation to large radii, and notes its non-central dispersion map is dynamically hot.

quotes.quote: If we select SRs morphologically based on the absence of significant rotation we find four examples: GMP2921, GMP3329, GMP3792 and GMP2975. If we classify SRs as all galaxies with < , then there are also four examples in Fig. fig:lam-eps : GMP2921, GMP3329, GMP3792 and GMP3433.

GMP2921 and GMP3329 show no evidence of rotation and are undisputed SRs. Similarly GMP3792 and GMP2975 show no evidence of global rotation extending to large radii. All four of these morphological SRs have <0.1 .

The majority of the dispersion maps in Fig. fig:kina are blue (dynamically cold, <100 ), while one dispersion map stands out from all the others as being dynamically much hotter than the others; that of GMP2921. This galaxy is likely the most massive in the cluster and is one of the two central dominant galaxies. However, the other centrally dominant galaxy, GMP3329, has a very moderate dispersion map in comparison; indeed the dispersion map of the non-central galaxy GMP3792 is just as dynamically hot.

gmw_00011144.name: GMP2975
gmw_00011144.overview: GMP2975 is an early-type galaxy located in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1308.6581]. It is classified as a morphological slow rotator because its velocity map shows no evidence of global rotation extending to large radii [arXiv:1308.6581].

sources.summary: The paper treats GMP2975 as a morphological slow rotator: it lacks global rotation despite lying in the fast-rotator region of the diagram, possibly because of measurement scatter.

quotes.quote: If we select SRs morphologically based on the absence of significant rotation we find four examples: GMP2921, GMP3329, GMP3792 and GMP2975.

Similarly GMP3792 and GMP2975 show no evidence of global rotation extending to large radii. All four of these morphological SRs have <0.1 . Although GMP2975 is consistent with being a fast rotator in the diagram ( =0.022, =0.08), suggesting it is an oblate nearly isotropic ellipsoid seen face on, it does not show global rotation in its velocity map. Were it a FR, the necessary alignment along the line of sight would be extremely rare; in 3D (260 galaxies), no fast rotator with , <0.1 was found ATLAS3DIII . Assuming GMP2975 is a SR, either (underestimated) measurement error scattered it above , or slow rotators are occasionally found above this fast/slow division.

gmw_00011145.name: Hercules
gmw_00011145.overview: Hercules is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy that is likely undergoing tidal disruption in its extended stellar halo [arXiv:2403.00921]. It has a velocity dispersion of 1.9 km/s and exhibits a line-of-sight velocity gradient along its major axis that is consistent with zero [arXiv:2403.00921].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes Hercules member stars, finding a lower velocity dispersion, no significant observed velocity gradient, and dynamical models indicating likely tidal disruption in its extended stellar halo.

quotes.quote: The Hercules ultra-faint dwarf galaxy (UFD) has long been hypothesized to be tidally disrupting, yet no conclusive evidence has been found for tidal disruption owing partly to difficulties in identifying Hercules member stars. In this work, we present a homogeneous re-analysis of new and existing observations of Hercules, including the detection of a new potential member star located 1 ( 1.7 kpc) west of the center of the system. In addition to measuring the line-of-sight velocity gradient, we compare predictions from dynamical models of stream formation to these observations.

We report an updated velocity dispersion measurement based on 28 stars, 1.9^ +0.6 _ 0.6 , which is significantly lower than previous measurements. We find that the line-of-sight velocity gradient is 1.8^ +1.8 _ 1.8 along the major axis of Hercules, consistent with zero within 1 . Our dynamical models of stream formation, on the other hand, can reproduce the morphology of the Hercules UFD, specifically the misalignment between the elongation and the orbital motion direction. Additionally, these dynamical models indicate that any radial velocity gradient from tidal disruption would be too small, 0.00^ +0.97 _ 0.91 , to be detectable with current sample sizes. Combined with our analysis of the tidal radius evolution of the system as a function of its orbital phase, we argue that it is likely that Hercules is indeed currently undergoing tidal disruption in its extended stellar halo with a line-of-sight velocity gradient too small to be detected with current observational datasets.

gmw_00011146.name: 1WGA J2223.7-0206
gmw_00011146.overview: 1WGA J2223.7-0206 is a narrow-line quasar located at a redshift of z=0.46 [arXiv:astro-ph/0402530]. It exhibits a blue optical continuum with typical active galactic nucleus emission lines, an ultra-soft X-ray spectrum, and rapid flux variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0402530].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1WGA J2223.7-0206 as a z=0.46 narrow-line QSO with ultra-soft X-rays, rapid variability, AGN optical lines, high luminosity, and a small inferred black-hole mass.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: WGA J 2223.7-0206 was detected in both bands at the coordinates =22:23:45.10 and =-02:06:41 (J2000, 0.9'' of error radius)

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a Narrow Line QSO located at about 1.3' from the Broad Line Radio Galaxy 3C445. The source, , although already revealed by ROSAT, has never been optically identified previously. An observation of 3C445 has allowed, for the first time, an accurate X-ray spectral study of , revealing an ultra-soft spectrum and fast flux variations typical of Narrow Line AGN.

The optical observations, triggered by the X-ray study, confirm the Narrow Line AGN nature of this source. The continuum is blue with typical AGN emission lines, pointing to a redshift z=0.46 . The full width half maximum of H _ is 2000 km s ^ 1 and the flux ratio [ O iii ] /H =0.21 . The optical luminosity ( M_R=-23.2 ) and the point-like appearance in the optical images identify as a Narrow Line QSO.

gmw_00011147.name: dark cloud chain
aliases_external: MHGS J22561910-3357270 / dark cloud chain Source (b)
gmw_00011147.overview: The dark cloud chain is a massive neutral hydrogen complex that lacks a definitive counterpart in ultraviolet, optical, infrared, or radio continuum imaging [arXiv:2112.02033]. It consists of seven peak concentrations interconnected in a tenuous chain structure spanning 400 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2112.02033].

sources.summary: The paper presents the dark cloud chain as a massive GAMA G23 H I complex with seven peaks, no clear optical counterpart, extreme gas mass-to-light, weak kinematics, and possible interaction-driven origins.

quotes.quote: We discovered a relatively massive \ source (or complex of sources) that did not have a definitive counterpart in the UV, optical, or infrared imaging, nor in the radio continuum image produced from these MeerKAT observations (Yao et al. ApJ submitted). We dub this complex the ``dark cloud chain" or ``dark cloud complex" throughout this paper.

The cloud complex, with a total mass of 10^ 10.0 M_ , lies in proximity to a large galaxy group with M_ dyn 10^ 13.5 M_ . We identify seven \ peak concentrations, interconnected as a tenuous `chain' structure, extending 400 kpc from east-to-west, with the largest (central) concentration containing 10 ^ 9.7 M_ in \ gas distributed across 50 kpc. The main source is not detected in ultra-violet, optical or infrared imaging. The implied gas mass-to-light ( M_ H I / L_ ) is extreme ( > 1000) even in comparison to other `dark clouds'. The complex has very little kinematic structure (110 km s ^ 1 ), making it difficult to identify cloud rotation.

gmw_00011148.name: PKS 1649--062
gmw_00011148.overview: PKS 1649--062 is a radio galaxy and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1103.2595]. Observations show 21-cm absorption toward its southwest radio lobe, which may indicate either gravitationally bound gas or an inclined disk with a large dynamical mass [arXiv:1103.2595].

sources.summary: The paper reports 21-cm absorption toward one lobe of PKS 1649--062 and interprets it as either nearby bound gas or an inclined disk with a very large dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: In the case of the detection, the absorption only occurs towards one of the two resolved radio lobes in PKS 1649--062. If the absorption is due to another intervening galaxy, or cool \ gas in the intergalactic medium, covering only the south-west lobe, then, being at the same redshift, this is likely to be gravitationally bound to the optical object identified as PKS 1649--062. If the absorption is due to an inclined disk centred between the lobes, intervening the SW lobe while being located behind the NE lobe, by assuming that it covers the emission peak at 150 kpc from the nucleus, we estimate a dynamical mass of 3 10^ 12   \ for the disk.

gmw_00011149.name: SDSS J080101.41+184840.7
gmw_00011149.overview: SDSS J080101.41+184840.7 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2202.13839] located at a redshift of approximately 0.13954 [arXiv:2202.13839]. Spectropolarimetric modeling indicates that the source is viewed from an intermediate orientation [arXiv:2202.13839] and exhibits a high accretion rate [arXiv:1701.08781].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J080101.41+184840.7 as an intermediate-orientation NLSy1, using spectropolarimetry and STOKES modeling with steep X-ray spectra and a polar-scattering component.

quotes.quote: Using the FORS2 instrument at the European Southern Observatory's (ESO) Very Large Telescope, We performed spectropolarimetric observations of three NLSy1: Mrk 1044, SDSS J080101.41+184840.7, and IRAS 04416+1215. We then modeled the region in the reduced spectra using IRAF spectral fitting procedures and estimated the Stokes parameters and the viewing angles of the three sources. The viewing angles recovered for the three sources indicate that they occupy separate locations in the viewing angle plane, from an almost face-on (IRAS 04416+1215) to an intermediate (SDSS J080101.41+184840.7), to a highly inclined (Mrk 1044) orientation.

The source SDSS J080101.41+184840.7 ( z = 0.13954 0.00001 ; RA 08:01:01.41, DEC +18:48:40.78 from NED) with V mag = 16.88, from 2010A&A...518A..10V , is the second object in our sample. Liu_Henzen2021 modeled XMM-Newton spectrum of SDSS J080101.41+184840.7 and obtained a steep photon index (2.33 0.06) using a power-law model modified by Galactic absorption. However, in the model used by Liu_Henzen2021 , the viewing angle was not one of the model parameters.
The cases of Mrk 1044 and SDSS J080101.41+184840.7 are different. Modeling the full line profile using just a BC was not possible. Thus, we decided to add the NCs marked in black.

sources.summary: SDSS J080101.41+184840.7 is one of four high-accretion RM-selected AGN observed with XMM-Newton; the paper fits its UV-optical SED with a thin disk and finds ordinary soft X-ray behavior.

quotes.quote: Here we present, for the first time, X-ray observations of four RM-selected candidates to have slim AD (SDSS J075101.42+291419.1, SDSS J080101.41+184840.7, SDSS J081441.91+212918.5, and SDSS J100055.71+314001.2) that have been observed recently by XMM-Newton (PI: Shai Kaspi). These new AGN with the highest accretion rates >20 were observed for the purpose of testing the slim-disc idea in the most extreme known cases. We follow the work of paper  I to fit the IR-optical-UV SED of these RM-selected AGN (see Appendix  appendix for a more explanations where the spectral analysis is presented). We found that the UV-optical SED of these four RM-selected AGN can also be fitted by thin AD models presenting moderate amount of reddening ( A_ V 0.4 ). We also found that the soft X-ray emission, as well as the Comptonized power law, of these AGN with the largest dimensionless accretion rate ( = 20 -- 200, are surprisingly similar to those from the parent sample ( 3) that have lower accretion rates.

gmw_00011150.name: SDSS J100055.71+314001.2
gmw_00011150.overview: SDSS J100055.71+314001.2 is an active galactic nucleus selected for its high accretion rate [arXiv:1701.08781]. Its ultraviolet-optical spectral energy distribution can be fitted by thin accretion disk models, and it exhibits ordinary soft X-ray emission [arXiv:1701.08781].

sources.summary: SDSS J100055.71+314001.2 is one of four high-accretion RM-selected AGN observed with XMM-Newton; the paper fits its UV-optical SED with a thin disk and finds ordinary soft X-ray behavior.

quotes.quote: Here we present, for the first time, X-ray observations of four RM-selected candidates to have slim AD (SDSS J075101.42+291419.1, SDSS J080101.41+184840.7, SDSS J081441.91+212918.5, and SDSS J100055.71+314001.2) that have been observed recently by XMM-Newton (PI: Shai Kaspi). These new AGN with the highest accretion rates >20 were observed for the purpose of testing the slim-disc idea in the most extreme known cases. We follow the work of paper  I to fit the IR-optical-UV SED of these RM-selected AGN (see Appendix  appendix for a more explanations where the spectral analysis is presented). We found that the UV-optical SED of these four RM-selected AGN can also be fitted by thin AD models presenting moderate amount of reddening ( A_ V 0.4 ). We also found that the soft X-ray emission, as well as the Comptonized power law, of these AGN with the largest dimensionless accretion rate ( = 20 -- 200, are surprisingly similar to those from the parent sample ( 3) that have lower accretion rates.

gmw_00011151.name: GH106
gmw_00011151.overview: GH106 is a radio-quiet dwarf galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2211.00050]. At Very Large Array scales, the source is compact and unresolved, while Very Long Baseline Array observations show marginally resolved structures with extensions from the compact emission regions [arXiv:2211.00050].

sources.summary: The paper treats GH106 as a radio-quiet AGN-hosting dwarf galaxy with a potential IMBH, using VLBA and VLA observations to probe compact and extended accretion-outflow emission.

quotes.quote: The above mechanisms of emission and evolution from compact to large scale (pc - kpc) structure are investigated through a set of radio observations spanning multiple resolutions (and physical scales) involving four potential IMBH hosting AGNs. This includes our VLBA L-band (1.4 GHz) high-resolution observations (milli-arcsecond scale; probing the few to tens of pc) conducted in 2021, and archival VLA A-array L and X-band (1.5 GHz and 9 GHz respectively) intermediate to larger resolution observations (sub-arcsec to arcsec scales; probing hundred pc to kpc). At the VLA scales, the source is compact and unresolved, though with a dominant emission. At the VLBA scales, the sources become marginally resolved to allow the discerning of extensions from the compact emission regions.

gmw_00011152.name: GH158
gmw_00011152.overview: GH158 is a radio-quiet dwarf galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2211.00050]. At Very Large Array scales, the galaxy's radio emission is compact and unresolved, while Very Long Baseline Array observations reveal marginally resolved structures [arXiv:2211.00050].

sources.summary: The paper treats GH158 as a radio-quiet AGN-hosting dwarf galaxy with a potential IMBH, using VLBA and VLA observations to probe compact and extended accretion-outflow emission.

quotes.quote: The above mechanisms of emission and evolution from compact to large scale (pc - kpc) structure are investigated through a set of radio observations spanning multiple resolutions (and physical scales) involving four potential IMBH hosting AGNs. This includes our VLBA L-band (1.4 GHz) high-resolution observations (milli-arcsecond scale; probing the few to tens of pc) conducted in 2021, and archival VLA A-array L and X-band (1.5 GHz and 9 GHz respectively) intermediate to larger resolution observations (sub-arcsec to arcsec scales; probing hundred pc to kpc). At the VLA scales, the source is compact and unresolved, though with a dominant emission. At the VLBA scales, the sources become marginally resolved to allow the discerning of extensions from the compact emission regions.

gmw_00011153.name: GH163
gmw_00011153.overview: GH163 is a radio-quiet dwarf galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2211.00050].
