The obtained stellar mass is high ( M_* 10^ 10.8 M_ ) and the current star formation rate is more than 1 dex below that of main-sequence galaxies at z=4.5 . Its star formation history suggests that this galaxy experienced starburst at z 5 followed by a rapid quenching phase.

An unique aspect of the galaxy is that it is in an extremely dense region; there are four massive star-forming galaxies at 4.4<z_ phot <4.7 located within 150 physical kpc from the galaxy. Interestingly, three of them have strongly overlapping virial radii with that of the central quiescent galaxy ( 70 kpc ), suggesting that the over-density region is likely the highest redshift candidate of a dense group with a spectroscopically confirmed quiescent galaxy at the center.

gmw_00011217.name: 3FGL J0109.1+1816
gmw_00011217.overview: 3FGL J0109.1+1816 is a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of z=0.443 [arXiv:2202.07353]. Although the source was found not to show intranight optical variability during monitoring, it increased in brightness by 0.1 magnitude between November 27 and December 31, 2018 [arXiv:2202.07353].

sources.summary: The paper notes this HSP BL Lac was monitored on three nights, showed no statistically significant INOV, but brightened by 0.1 magnitude between late 2018 epochs.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0109.1+1816 : This HSP BL Lac at a redshift of z=0.443 was observed on a night in December 2016 and again on two nights in November and December 2018. This source has been studied for INOV for the first time. In December 2016, there is some indication of the source to have shown flux variations, however, statistical tests show the source to be non-variable. On the other two nights too, the source was found not to show INOV, however, between 27 November 2018 and 31 December 2018, the source was found to increase in brightness by 0.1 mag.

gmw_00011218.name: 3FGL J0217.2+0837
gmw_00011218.overview: 3FGL J0217.2+0837 is classified as a low-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2202.07353]. The source exhibits intra-night optical variability, which was detected on two out of three monitored nights with variability amplitudes of approximately 5 and 10 percent [arXiv:2202.07353].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this LSP BL Lac as monitored on three nights and variable on two, with intra-night optical variability amplitudes near 10 and 5 percent.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0217.2+0837: This source is a LSP BL Lac. It was observed for three nights between 20 December 2016 and 24 December 2017. It was found to show INOV on two nights with amplitude of variability of about 10 and 5 respectively.

gmw_00011219.name: 3FGL J0846.7 0651
gmw_00011219.overview: 3FGL J0846.7 0651 is a blazar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2202.07353]. During three observing epochs in 2020 spanning approximately one month, the source exhibited no intranight optical variability [arXiv:2202.07353].

sources.summary: The paper gives first INOV measurements for this source and finds no variability in three 2020 observing epochs over about a month.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0846.7 0651: This source was observed for three epochs in the year 2020 within a span of about a month. It was found to be non-variable on all the three nights of observations. The observations reported here are the first time measurements for INOV on this source.

gmw_00011220.name: 3FGL J0912.9 2104
gmw_00011220.overview: 3FGL J0912.9 2104 is a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2202.07353]. Monitored across three epochs between March 2017 and February 2019, the blazar did not exhibit any intranight optical variability [arXiv:2202.07353].

sources.summary: The paper describes this HSP BL Lac as lacking prior INOV reports, monitored across three epochs, and not variable in any epoch.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0912.9 2104: This source is a HSP BL Lac. Reports on the INOV nature of this source are not available in literature. It was observed for three epochs between March 2017 and February 2019. The source was not found to show INOV on all the three epochs.

gmw_00011221.name: SDSS J030903+003846
gmw_00011221.overview: SDSS J030903+003846 is a dwarf active galactic nucleus candidate with cospatial continuum and emission peaks [arXiv:2405.10156]. Its gas velocity fields display a disturbed rotation pattern, and the inner region exhibits Seyfert-like excitation [arXiv:2405.10156].

sources.summary: The paper finds cospatial continuum and emission peaks, extended recombination-line emission, disturbed rotation, and inner Seyfert-like [S II] excitation in this dwarf AGN candidate.

quotes.quote: SDSS J030903+003846 The peak of the continuum and emission line fluxes for this galaxy are cospatial. Its spectra show strong \ and H recombination lines (Fig. fig:J030903_maps ). In addition, the \ and \ emission lines also present within the nuclear region ( < 1 arcsec).

As observed in Fig. fig:J030903_maps , the \ and H flux distributions are extended over the whole GMOS field, with the highest flux levels revealing a slightly extended structure to the west of the nucleus. The gas velocity fields show a disturbed rotation pattern with a projected velocity amplitude of 30 \ and orientation of the lines of nodes approximately along the north-south direction, which is similar to the orientation of the extended emission structure seen in the large scale SDSS image (top-left panel), beyond the GMOS FoV.

The spatially resolved [ S II ] - BPT diagrams reveal that most of the points are located in the Seyfert region for SDSS J030903+003846 (Fig. fig:bpt_j030903 ), but the [ S II ] emission is observed only in the inner 1 arcec radius.

gmw_00011222.name: SDSS J033549-003913
gmw_00011222.overview: SDSS J033549-003913 is a dwarf galaxy candidate that exhibits a clear rotation pattern with a projected velocity amplitude of 100 km/s [arXiv:2405.10156]. The galaxy displays extended emission-line flux distributions elongated along the east-west direction, with most regions classified as Seyfert or LINER [arXiv:2405.10156].

sources.summary: The paper reports offset emission-line peaks, extended high- and low-ionization emission, clear rotation, and mostly Seyfert/LINER [S II] excitation for this dwarf candidate.

quotes.quote: SDSS J033549-003913 For this galaxy, the peak of the emission-line flux distributions is observed 1.5 arcsec west of the nucleus. SDSS J033549-003913 presents extended emission of strong high ionization lines such as and Hydrogen recombination lines (Figure fig:J033549_maps ). Low ionization emission lines were detect and they also present extended emission for the and doublets.

As can be seen in the bottom right panel of Figure fig:J033549_maps , the emission-line flux distributions are extended and more elongated along the east-west direction, following the large-scale shape of the galaxy (top right panel of the figure). The velocity field presents a clear rotation pattern with blueshifts observed to the west and redshifts to the east of the galaxy's nucleus and a projected velocity amplitude of 100 km s ^ 1 .

For SDSS J033549-00391, the [ S II ] emission is observed over most of the GMOS FoV and the [ S II ] - BPT diagram for this galaxy (Fig. fig:bpt_j033549 ) presents most of the points in the Seyfert/LINER region.

gmw_00011223.name: SDSS J033553-003946
gmw_00011223.overview: SDSS J033553-003946 is a dwarf galaxy that exhibits extended ionized emission lines and a rotating gas pattern with low velocity dispersion [arXiv:2405.10156]. Its emission line diagnostics indicate a mixture of star formation and active galactic nucleus ionization [arXiv:2405.10156].

sources.summary: The paper finds extended ionized emission, rotating gas with low dispersion, marginal coronal-line evidence, and mixed star-forming/AGN [S II] diagnostics for this dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: This line is marginally detected for SDSS J033553-003946 and is not present or very weak for SDSS J225236-003317. SDSS J033553-003946 This galaxy presents extended high ionization emission lines such as and H recombination lines and unresolved emission of . We also detect extended low ionization emission lines as and \ (Figures fig:J033553_maps and fig:1g_j033553 ).

The flux distributions for all emission lines are extended along the east-west direction, except for the , which emission is unresolved and restricted to the nucleus. From the gas velocity fields, it can be observed that the nuclear region has velocity values near zero and a rotation pattern is revealed with positive velocity values to the south-west direction and blueshift velocities at northeast. As for the other objects, this galaxy also presents small velocity dispersion values, with a mean value of 50 .
The [ S II ] - BPT diagram for SDSS J033553-003946 present most points in the star-forming region, with some spaxels consistent with AGN ionization in regions farther than 1 arcsec from the nucleus.

gmw_00011224.name: SDSS J225236-003317
gmw_00011224.overview: SDSS J225236-003317 is a dwarf galaxy featuring extended gas emission that is displaced from its nucleus [arXiv:2405.10156]. The galaxy exhibits a disturbed rotation pattern [arXiv:2405.10156], with nuclear emission consistent with active galactic nucleus activity and outer regions ionized by young stars [arXiv:2405.10156].

sources.summary: The paper describes extended emission, an off-nuclear line-emission peak, disturbed gas rotation, weak coronal-line evidence, and nuclear AGN-like diagnostics for this dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: This line is marginally detected for SDSS J033553-003946 and is not present or very weak for SDSS J225236-003317. SDSS J225236-003317 This galaxy presents extended emission over the whole GMOS FoV for most emission lines (Figs. fig:J225236_maps and fig:1g_j225236 ). The gas emission is more extended along the east-west direction, following the morphology seen in the SDSS image, but with the peak of the line emission, displaced from the nucleus of the galaxy by 1.2 arcsec to the west.

The gas velocity field show mostly blueshifts with the highest values seen in regions close to the borders of the GMOS FoV view to the west and to the north. Values nearly zero are seen in the inner 1 arcsec radius and some redshifts are seen to the east of the nucleus, suggesting a disturbed rotation pattern. Typical values of the velocity dispersion are 50 , observed across the entire FoV.

A similar behaviour is observed for SDSS J225236-00331 (Fig. fig:bpt_j225236 ), but with the nuclear emission being consistent with AGN activity, and regions close to the gas emission peak consistent with gas ionization by young stars.

gmw_00011225.name: A0951+68
gmw_00011225.overview: A0951+68 is a dwarf galaxy in the M81 group that is classified as a dwarf elliptical but exhibits properties similar to dwarf irregulars [arXiv:astro-ph/9702217]. It contains two bright knots of emission, including a blue, resolved knot that hosts a high-excitation H II region [arXiv:astro-ph/9702217].

sources.summary: The paper presents imaging and spectroscopy of A0951+68, showing a mispositioned M81 dwarf elliptical with irregular-like properties, bright knots, an H ii region, recent star formation, and high metallicity for its luminosity.

quotes.quote: We present the results of BVRI imaging and optical spectroscopy of the dwarf galaxy A0951+68. The images reveal that, although this galaxy is classified as a dwarf elliptical, it has some properties that are similar to dwarf irregular galaxies. It contains two bright knots of emission, one of which is red and unresolved and the other blue and resolved. The blue knot also shows a high excitation emission line spectrum.

Optical images and spectroscopy of the M81 dwarf A0951+68 have provided a few surprises, not least that the published position of the galaxy was incorrect. We have calculated a new position. Another suprise was the presence in this supposed dwarf elliptical galaxy of an H ii region, with very high excitation. H ii region models suggest that the ionizing source is a single very luminous O7 star, or a few later-type O stars.

gmw_00011226.name: IGR J12288+0052
gmw_00011226.overview: IGR J12288+0052 is classified as a type 2 QSO that displays narrow emission lines in its optical spectrum [arXiv:1207.4882]. Located at a redshift of 0.5756, the active galactic nucleus is only moderately absorbed and is marginally compatible with being an extremely red object [arXiv:1207.4882].

sources.summary: The paper classifies IGR J12288+0052 as a type 2 QSO with narrow lines, moderate absorption, the highest redshift within the group, and marginal ERO compatibility.

quotes.quote: We are left with 5 objects which display evident narrow emission lines in their optical spectra and therefore qualify to be type 2 QSO: IGR J00465-4005, SWIFT J0216.3+5128, IGR J09523-6231, IGR J12288+0052 and IGR J23524+5842. Only IGR J09523-6231 is a type 1.9 AGN implying that a weak broad H emission line is present in its optical spectrum. The 2-10 keV luminosity of these objects goes from 3.6 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 to 5.6 10 ^ 45 erg s ^ 1 while in the 20-100 keV band ranges from 7.6 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 to 7.8 10 ^ 45 erg s ^ 1 .

Despite early indications that IGR J12288+0052 could be a Compton thick AGN (Vignali et al. 2010) we find that it is only moderately absorbed (Fiocchi et al. 2010, but see considerations in section 5.2); it is also the source at the highest redshift (0.5756) within this group.

We have collected this information for 4 out 5 sources and found that R-K is in the range 2.3-5.6; only IGR J12288+0052 is marginally compatible with being a ERO but its R-K value is only an upper limit.

gmw_00011227.name: IRAS 15206+3342
gmw_00011227.overview: IRAS 15206+3342 is a more evolved, single-nucleus ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0509681]. Its extended extranuclear regions exhibit a dominant H II-like ionization [arXiv:astro-ph/0509681].

sources.summary: IRAS 15206+3342 is described as a more evolved single-nucleus ULIRG; the paper finds H ii-like extranuclear ionization and limited velocity-dispersion range in the shock-correlation test.

quotes.quote: The sample of galaxies consists of six low- z ULIRGs (see general properties in table misulirgs ) covering a relative wide range of dynamical states of the merging process. Three of the galaxies (IRAS 08572+3915, IRAS 12112+0305, and IRAS 14348 1447) are interacting pairs separated by projected distances of up to 6 kpc, while the rest of the galaxies (IRAS 15206+3342, IRAS 15250+3609, and IRAS 17208 0014) are more evolved, single nucleus ULIRGs, some with a light profile and overall kinematics close to that of intermediate mass ellipticals (e.g. IRAS 17208 0014, Genzel et al. 2001). The two-dimensional kinematic properties (velocity field and velocity dispersion) have been studied in detail before using integral field optical spectroscopy (see Colina et al. 2005 and references therein).

According to this ratio, LINER-like emission is found in the extended extranuclear regions in three systems: IRAS 14348 1447 , IRAS 15250+3609 , and IRAS 17208 0014 . Contrary, for IRAS 08572+3915 , IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 12112+0305 this line ratio suggests a dominant H ii like ionization.

Individually, two systems, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 14348 1447, show a clear correlation in the three line ratios. For three of the remaining systems, IRAS 08572+3915,IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 15250+3609, the range in velocity dispersion is relatively small to show the correlation.

gmw_00011228.name: NGC 5098
gmw_00011228.overview: NGC 5098 is a galaxy group located at a redshift of z=0.036 [arXiv:2312.02283]. The group exhibits signs of a minor merger that is causing sloshing of the intragroup medium, and it features two north-south X-ray cavities filled with radio emission [arXiv:2312.02283].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5098 as a galaxy group with radio-filled north-south X-ray cavities, minor-merger sloshing, and a VLBA core-dominated source with a small jet nearly aligned with the cavities.

quotes.quote: NGC 5098 : galaxy group at z=0.036. 2009ApJ...700.1404R reported the presence of two X-ray cavities filled by radio emission and mainly oriented in the north -- south direction. The group also shows signs of minor merger with a galaxy, that is causing sloshing of the IGrM. On VLBA scales we find a core dominated source with a small jet southeast of the core, nearly aligned with the X-ray cavities.

gmw_00011229.name: SPT 0532 50
gmw_00011229.overview: SPT 0532 50 is a gravitationally lensed, dusty star-forming galaxy that acts as a galaxy-scale lens system [arXiv:2403.04850]. The system exhibits a boxy isodensity structure and a large misalignment between its light and mass profiles [arXiv:2403.04850].

sources.summary: The paper models SPT 0532 50 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, finding spectral-line affected data, decisive PL+MP evidence, boxy isodensity structure, and a large light-mass misalignment.

quotes.quote: ALMA observations We obtained data from the ALMA archive for three gravitationally lensed dusty star-forming galaxies: SPT 0532 50 (SPT S J053250 5047.1), SPT 0538 50 (SPT S J053816 5030.8), and SDP 81 (H-ATLAS J090311.6 + 003906). These are galaxy-scale lenses with known source and lens redshifts ( Negrello:2010,Spilker:2016 ; Table table:obs ). Details of the lens systems and ALMA observations are given in Table table:obs .

The observations of SPT 0532 50 and SPT 0538 50 consisted of multiple epochs in compact and extended antenna configurations: we considered only the long-baseline configurations for this experiment to maintain the higher angular resolution. The data for SDP 81 were taken during the 2014 ALMA long baseline science verification campaign, using an atypical array configuration Vlahakis2015 . For the two SPT lenses, we used the ALMA pipeline in Common Astronomy Software Applications (CASA; casa2022 ) for the initial calibration of the data. There is an apparent spectral line emission in two spectral windows for SPT 0532 50; therefore, these spectral windows are not considered further in our analysis.

However, those of SPT 0532 50 appear quite boxy (Fig. fig:converence_isophotes ). SDP 81 is largely consistent in terms of its isophote and isodensity shapes, while SPT 0532 50 and SPT 0548 50 show disagreement. For SPT 0532 50, the isophote ellipticity position angle is offset by about 40 deg relative to the isodensity profile at the Einstein radius, and for SPT 0538 50 this offset is around 25 deg.

gmw_00011230.name: COS4-11337
gmw_00011230.overview: COS4-11337 is a compact star-forming galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.02891]. It drives a fast outflow that propagates toward its companion galaxy, COS4-11363, which is located at a projected separation of 5.4 kpc [arXiv:2004.02891].

sources.summary: The paper describes COS4-11337 as a compact star-forming AGN galaxy in a close pair, driving a fast outflow that may escape the halo, affect its companion, and shorten gas depletion.

quotes.quote: K20-ID5 and COS4-11337 are compact star forming galaxies with powerful 1500 \ AGN-driven outflows that dominate their nuclear \ emission. The outflows do not appear to have any impact on the instantaneous star formation activity of the host galaxies, but they carry a significant amount of kinetic energy which could heat the halo gas and potentially lead to a reduction in the rate of cold gas accretion onto the galaxies. The outflow from COS4-11337 is propagating directly towards its companion galaxy COS4-11363, at a projected separation of 5.4 kpc. COS4-11363 shows signs of shock excitation and recent truncation of star formation activity, which could plausibly have been induced by the outflow from COS4-11337.

The K band spectrum shows very broad wings, revealing the presence of a fast outflow. Based on the FWZP of the + \ complex, we measure an outflow velocity of 1459 66 . COS4-11337 has a circular velocity of 150 60 \ Wisnioski18 , corresponding to an escape velocity of 450 . The outflow velocity is more than a factor of three larger than the escape velocity, indicating again that a significant fraction of the outflowing material could potentially be expelled from the host halo.

sources.summary: The paper identifies COS4-11363 as COS4-11337’s close companion, with weak emission, a high line ratio indicating shocks or AGN activity, and possible recent star-formation truncation caused by outflow or interaction.

quotes.quote: The outflow from COS4-11337 is propagating directly towards its companion galaxy COS4-11363, at a projected separation of 5.4 kpc. COS4-11363 shows signs of shock excitation and recent truncation of star formation activity, which could plausibly have been induced by the outflow from COS4-11337.

We measure an / \ ratio of 2.6 0.4 - a factor of 6.5 higher than expected for a pure star forming galaxy. The high \ ratio indicates that the line-emitting gas must be collisionally excited and/or ionized by sources other than young stars. We measure an \ equivalent width of 5.8 2.5 , which exceeds the maximum value of 3 to be consistent with ionization by evolved stellar populations e.g. CidFernandes11, Belfiore16 . We therefore suggest that the line emission is most likely to be powered by either shock excitation or AGN activity.

However, we cannot rule out a scenario where the shock excitation in COS4-11363 is purely induced by the interaction. It is interesting to speculate on the possible impact that the outflow from COS4-11337 may have had on the star formation activity of COS4-11363.

gmw_00011231.name: PGC 135857
gmw_00011231.overview: PGC 135857 is a small Sm-type satellite galaxy located 63.6 kiloparsecs east of NGC 5713 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611426]. It contains neutral hydrogen emission and is surrounded by two low-column-density tails that likely formed during a galaxy interaction [arXiv:astro-ph/0611426].

sources.summary: The paper reports PGC 135857 as a small Sm-type satellite with HI emission and nearby low-column-density tails, likely connected to and formed by the main interaction.

quotes.quote: We also detected \ emission from PGC 135857, a small B_t= 17.40 Sm-type satellite with v _ sys =1802 , 63.6 kpc E of the centre of NGC 5713 (see Figure fig:pgc ). Although our observations are affected by primary-beam attenuation (FWHP =30 ), we detected a fair amount of \ in it ( M_ /L_B 0.18 M _ /L_ ,B ). In its close surroundings we detected two low column density \ tails at v 1800 -- 1950 : the Northern and Western tails , see Fig. fig:3d . The Northern tail is likely physically connected to PGC 135857, as shown by the channel maps Fig. 4.

The presence of the small satellite galaxy PGC 135857 seems to be related to the interaction of the main galaxy pair, and it might have formed as a result of a large local \ reservoir being subjected to the physical conditions that triggered star formation.

gmw_00011232.name: NGC 3957
gmw_00011232.overview: NGC 3957 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1001.3067, arXiv:astro-ph/0404164] featuring a nearly box-shaped bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164, arXiv:astro-ph/0606056], an inner dust lane [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164], and a centered X-shaped structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0606056]. Ultra-deep imaging reveals a stellar halo with colors consistent with an old, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:1001.3067].

sources.summary: The paper directly detects NGC 3957's stellar halo in ultra-deep VLT images and finds halo colors consistent with an old, metal-poor population.

quotes.quote: We present a direct detection of the stellar halo of the edge-on S0 galaxy NGC 3957, using ultra-deep VLT/VIMOS V and R images. This is achieved with a sky subtraction strategy based on infrared techniques. These observations allow us to reach unprecedented high signal-to-noise ratios up to 15 kpc away from the galaxy center, rendering photon-noise negligible. The 1 detection limits are R = 30.6 mag/arcsec ^2 and V = 31.4 mag/arcsec ^2 .

We conclude that the V-R colour of the NGC 3957 halo, calculated between 5 and 8 kpc above the disc plane where the systematic errors are modest, is consistent with an old and preferentially metal-poor normal stellar population, like that revealed in nearby galaxy halos from studies of their resolved stellar content. We do not find support for the extremely red colours found in earlier studies of diffuse halo emission, which we suggest might have been due to residual systematic errors.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3957 as an S0 with an unusual inner dust lane, visible high-latitude bulge light, and radial profiles containing bulge, bar/ring, break, and outer decline components.

quotes.quote: NGC 3957: ( n3957 ) Galaxy cluster in background. Nearly box-shaped bulge . It is an 0 galaxy with unusual, inner ( R 30 ) dust lane. The bulge component is still visible on the minor axis high above the plane. The radial profiles show inner bulge, followed by a ring- or bar-like component at R 30 (visible in cuts above the dust lane), with break at R 60 and followed by a straight decline into the noise.

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 3957 likely does not belong in the round-bulge control sample, citing a close-to-boxy bulge, weak-bar kinematics, centered X structure, secondary maxima, and three profile regions.

quotes.quote: For example, ldp00a classified the bulge of NGC 3957 as close to boxy-shaped, bf99 and cb04 both found kinematic indications of weak bars in NGC 3957 and NGC 4703, and cb04 also found indication of non-axisymmetric motions in NGC 7123. The current K band imaging also shows centered X features in NGC 3957 and NGC 7123 and secondary maxima in NGC 3957 and NGC 4703. All three galaxies show at least 3 regions in their surface brightness profile.

gmw_00011233.name: EIGER-01-10308
gmw_00011233.overview: EIGER-01-10308 is a tidally disturbed merging galaxy system at redshift z ~ 4.22 [arXiv:2307.01273]. It resides in a local overdensity of seven additional galaxies that may form a proto-group [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper describes EIGER-01-10308 as a tidally disturbed z 4.22 merging system with high stellar mass and SFR, in an overdensity with complex associated absorption.

quotes.quote: EIGER-01-10308: This galaxy at z 4.22, is a tidally disturbed merging system. The tidal streams and different components of the merger can be clearly seen in high-resolution JWST imaging (Appendix fig:appendix1 , panel a). The merging system has an integrated stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 10.17, and a SFR 70 .

The galaxy EIGER-01-10308 resides in a local over-density and there are seven additional galaxies within 200 kpc from the quasar sightline. The galaxies are within 220 kpc of each other and have a velocity dispersion of 228 . This large over-density of galaxies at close physical and kinematic separation may be part of a proto-group at z 4.2234. The additional seven galaxies are at higher impact parameter than EIGER-01-10308 and at R > R_ 200 . The absorption associated with EIGER-01-10308 show complex kinematics with six distinct individual absorption components identified with a velocity spread of 500 (Figure fig:absorption_profile ).

gmw_00011234.name: EIGER-01-09950
gmw_00011234.overview: EIGER-01-09950 is a galaxy at redshift z ~ 4.5 with a stellar mass of 10^9.88 solar masses and a star formation rate of approximately 29 solar masses per year [arXiv:2307.01273]. It has three companion galaxies within 300 kpc, including two at very close separations that may form a proto-group [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper identifies EIGER-01-09950 as a z 4.5 CGM host near three companions, with measured stellar mass, SFR, and a complex 350 km/s absorption profile.

quotes.quote: EIGER-01-09950: At z 4.5, there are three additional galaxies within 300 kpc of this galaxy (Appendix fig:appendix2 ). The galaxy EIGER-01-09950 has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 9.88, and a SFR 29 , respectively. There are two other galaxies at very close kinematic separations: EIGER-01-09078 at a separation of 16 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and a velocity offset of 368 , and galaxy EIGER-01-08811, at a separation of 22 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and a velocity offset of 122 .

These galaxies all lie close to each other (within 232 kpc and 166 ) and could form a proto-group at z 4.5192 . The absorption profile is again complex for this system (Figure fig:absorption_profile ), with five identified distinct absorption components. The absorption spans a velocity range of 350 .

gmw_00011235.name: EIGER-01-10424
gmw_00011235.overview: EIGER-01-10424 is a faint galaxy at redshift z ~ 4.64 located near the line of sight of the J0100+2802 quasar [arXiv:2307.01273]. The system contains two faint emission components, suggesting it is a merging system [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper treats EIGER-01-10424 as a faint z 4.64 galaxy near a foreground source, possibly merging, and identifies it as a weak, unbound absorption host.

quotes.quote: EIGER-01-10424: At z 4.64, a faint galaxy is detected at an impact parameter of 72 kpc from the J0100+2802 quasar line of sight. This galaxy has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 9.18, and a SFR 13 , respectively. The galaxy is next to a bright z 1 foreground galaxy detected in Pa- emission (see Figure fig:galaxy_stamps ). Ground based MUSE spectrum of the bright foreground galaxy show [ OII ] emission doublet, confirming it as a low- z galaxy. There are two faint emission components detected for the target galaxy, suggesting that it is a merging system at z 4.64, however, owing to the position angle of the NIRCam grism spectra, the two components cannot be spatially resolved.

The presence of metal absorption and the emission lines in the NIRCam grism spectra has led us to conclude that EIGER-01-10424 is the host galaxy for absorption detected at z 4.6. This system only shows weak absorption doublet, kinematically offset from the host galaxy's systemic redshift by 350 (Figure fig:absorption_profile ), and beyond the inferred virial radius of the host galaxy (Figure fig:galaxy_environment ). We therefore conclude that the CGM absorption is not consistent with being bound to the host galaxy.

gmw_00011236.name: EIGER-01-06898
gmw_00011236.overview: EIGER-01-06898 is an isolated high-redshift galaxy with a stellar mass of $10^{9.1}\ M_{\odot}$ and a star formation rate of $8.4\ M_{\odot}\ \mathrm{yr}^{-1}$ [arXiv:2307.01273]. It is associated with unbound circumgalactic absorption at a redshift of approximately 6.015 [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper associates EIGER-01-06898 with z 6.015 absorption, measuring its impact parameter, stellar mass, SFR, isolation, absorption offset, and unbound location beyond R_200.

quotes.quote: Galaxies EIGER-01-06898, EIGER-01-20300: These galaxies are associated with absorption at z 6.015 and 5.33, respectively. EIGER-01-06898 is at an impact parameter of 201 kpc and has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 9.1, and a SFR 8.4 , respectively. EIGER-01-20300 is at an impact parameter of 19 kpc and has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 7.1, and a SFR 1.1 , respectively.

Both these galaxies are ``isolated", in the sense that no companion galaxy within 600 and 300 kpc of them is detected. Both galaxies are at an impact parameter beyond the R_ 200 radii of their host galaxies (Figure fig:galaxy_environment ). For EIGER-01-06898, we detect and absorption offset from the systemic redshift of the galaxy by 136 . Around EIGER-01-20300, we detect , , and absorption, kinematically offset from the systemic redshift of the host galaxy by 120 .

For both these systems, the absorption is kinematically offset from the systemic galaxy redshift and is detected beyond the inferred virial radii of their corresponding host galaxies. We therefore conclude that the absorption detected around these galaxies is not bound to the host galaxy's dark matter halo.

gmw_00011237.name: EIGER-01-20300
gmw_00011237.overview: EIGER-01-20300 is an isolated high-redshift galaxy with a low stellar mass of $10^{7.1}$ solar masses and a star formation rate of 1.1 solar masses per year [arXiv:2307.01273]. It is associated with absorption at redshift 5.33 that is kinematically offset and unbound to the host galaxy's dark matter halo [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper associates EIGER-01-20300 with z 5.33 absorption, measuring its small impact parameter, low stellar mass, SFR, isolation, absorption offset, and unbound halo location.

quotes.quote: Galaxies EIGER-01-06898, EIGER-01-20300: These galaxies are associated with absorption at z 6.015 and 5.33, respectively. EIGER-01-06898 is at an impact parameter of 201 kpc and has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 9.1, and a SFR 8.4 , respectively. EIGER-01-20300 is at an impact parameter of 19 kpc and has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 7.1, and a SFR 1.1 , respectively.

Both these galaxies are ``isolated", in the sense that no companion galaxy within 600 and 300 kpc of them is detected. Both galaxies are at an impact parameter beyond the R_ 200 radii of their host galaxies (Figure fig:galaxy_environment ). For EIGER-01-06898, we detect and absorption offset from the systemic redshift of the galaxy by 136 . Around EIGER-01-20300, we detect , , and absorption, kinematically offset from the systemic redshift of the host galaxy by 120 .

For both these systems, the absorption is kinematically offset from the systemic galaxy redshift and is detected beyond the inferred virial radii of their corresponding host galaxies. We therefore conclude that the absorption detected around these galaxies is not bound to the host galaxy's dark matter halo.

gmw_00011238.name: EIGER-01-06569
gmw_00011238.overview: EIGER-01-06569 is a high-redshift galaxy associated with weak absorption at a redshift of approximately 2.67 [arXiv:2307.01273]. Located at an impact parameter of 270 kpc, the galaxy has a stellar mass of 10.17 solar masses and is situated beyond its inferred virial radius [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper links EIGER-01-06569 to z 2.67 absorption, giving its 270 kpc impact parameter, stellar mass, SFR, weak absorption, and unbound location beyond its virial radius.

quotes.quote: Galaxies EIGER-01-06569, EIGER-01-09351: These two galaxies are associated with absorption at z 2.67 and 2.31, respectively. The galaxy 6569 is at an impact parameter of 270 kpc and has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 10.17, and a SFR 75 , respectively. EIGER-01-09351 is at an impact parameter of 172 kpc and has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 10.64, and a SFR 75 , respectively. In both these systems, absorption doublet is detected as any line with bluer rest frame wavelength will be in the Ly- forest and not observable along this high- z quasar sightline. Both these systems are weak absorption systems (Figure fig:absorption_profile ) and are beyond the inferred virial radii of their host galaxies. In both cases, the absorption is consistent with not being bound to the dark matter halos of the host galaxies.

gmw_00011239.name: EIGER-01-09351
gmw_00011239.overview: EIGER-01-09351 is a high-redshift galaxy associated with absorption at z ~ 2.31 [arXiv:2307.01273]. It has a stellar mass of 10^10.64 solar masses and is located at an impact parameter of 172 kpc, beyond its inferred virial radius [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper links EIGER-01-09351 to z 2.31 absorption, giving its 172 kpc impact parameter, stellar mass, SFR, weak absorption, and unbound location beyond its virial radius.

quotes.quote: Galaxies EIGER-01-06569, EIGER-01-09351: These two galaxies are associated with absorption at z 2.67 and 2.31, respectively. The galaxy 6569 is at an impact parameter of 270 kpc and has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 10.17, and a SFR 75 , respectively. EIGER-01-09351 is at an impact parameter of 172 kpc and has a stellar mass of M_ * /M_ sun = 10.64, and a SFR 75 , respectively. In both these systems, absorption doublet is detected as any line with bluer rest frame wavelength will be in the Ly- forest and not observable along this high- z quasar sightline. Both these systems are weak absorption systems (Figure fig:absorption_profile ) and are beyond the inferred virial radii of their host galaxies. In both cases, the absorption is consistent with not being bound to the dark matter halos of the host galaxies.

gmw_00011240.name: EIGER-01-09078
gmw_00011240.overview: EIGER-01-09078 is a high-redshift companion galaxy located at a kinematic separation of 16 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 [arXiv:2307.01273]. It has a low stellar mass and kinematically aligns with the strongest absorption component near the quasar sightline J0100+2802 [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper describes EIGER-01-09078 as a close kinematic companion 16 kpc from EIGER-01-09950, near the sightline and aligned with the strongest absorption component.

quotes.quote: There are two other galaxies at very close kinematic separations: EIGER-01-09078 at a separation of 16 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and a velocity offset of 368 , and galaxy EIGER-01-08811, at a separation of 22 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and a velocity offset of 122 . Both galaxies EIGER-01-09078 and EIGER-01-08811 are 40 and 50 kpc away from the quasar sightline J0100+2802. A third more massive galaxy (EIGER-01-06193) is detected at 235 kpc away from EIGER-01-09950 and at a velocity separation of 232 . Galaxy EIGER-01-06193 is at an impact parameter of 260 kpc from the quasar sightline J0100+2802. These galaxies all lie close to each other (within 232 kpc and 166 ) and could form a proto-group at z 4.5192 .

The strongest absorption component is offset from the systemic redshift galaxy EIGER-01-09950 by 95 , and kinematically lines up with the nearby galaxy EIGER-01-09078. But EIGER-01-09078 has a much lower stellar masses (see Table tab:galaxy_info ), and the impact parameter to it is higher than the inferred virial radius associated with it (Figure fig:galaxy_environment ).

gmw_00011241.name: EIGER-01-06193
gmw_00011241.overview: EIGER-01-06193 is a massive companion galaxy located 235 kpc away from EIGER-01-09950 [arXiv:2307.01273]. It lies 260 kpc from the quasar sightline J0100+2802 and may belong to a proto-group at redshift z ~ 4.5192 [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper presents EIGER-01-06193 as a more massive companion 235 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and 260 kpc from J0100+2802, within the possible z 4.5192 proto-group.

quotes.quote: There are two other galaxies at very close kinematic separations: EIGER-01-09078 at a separation of 16 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and a velocity offset of 368 , and galaxy EIGER-01-08811, at a separation of 22 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and a velocity offset of 122 . Both galaxies EIGER-01-09078 and EIGER-01-08811 are 40 and 50 kpc away from the quasar sightline J0100+2802. A third more massive galaxy (EIGER-01-06193) is detected at 235 kpc away from EIGER-01-09950 and at a velocity separation of 232 . Galaxy EIGER-01-06193 is at an impact parameter of 260 kpc from the quasar sightline J0100+2802. These galaxies all lie close to each other (within 232 kpc and 166 ) and could form a proto-group at z 4.5192 .

gmw_00011242.name: EIGER-01-08811
gmw_00011242.overview: EIGER-01-08811 is a high-redshift companion galaxy located 50 kpc from the quasar sightline J0100+2802 [arXiv:2307.01273]. It lies 22 kpc from EIGER-01-09950, and these systems may belong to a proto-group at z ≈ 4.5192 [arXiv:2307.01273].

sources.summary: The paper identifies EIGER-01-08811 as a close kinematic companion 22 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and 50 kpc from J0100+2802, within the possible z 4.5192 proto-group.

quotes.quote: There are two other galaxies at very close kinematic separations: EIGER-01-09078 at a separation of 16 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and a velocity offset of 368 , and galaxy EIGER-01-08811, at a separation of 22 kpc from EIGER-01-09950 and a velocity offset of 122 . Both galaxies EIGER-01-09078 and EIGER-01-08811 are 40 and 50 kpc away from the quasar sightline J0100+2802. A third more massive galaxy (EIGER-01-06193) is detected at 235 kpc away from EIGER-01-09950 and at a velocity separation of 232 . Galaxy EIGER-01-06193 is at an impact parameter of 260 kpc from the quasar sightline J0100+2802. These galaxies all lie close to each other (within 232 kpc and 166 ) and could form a proto-group at z 4.5192 .

gmw_00011243.name: SDSS J132144+033055
gmw_00011243.overview: SDSS J132144+033055 is a blue quasar located at a redshift of z=0.269 [arXiv:2209.01923]. It exhibits 3.8-year optical quasi-periodic oscillations, which strongly indicate the presence of a central binary black hole system [arXiv:2209.01923].

sources.summary: The paper reports 3.8-year optical QPOs in blue quasar SDSS J132144+033055, interpreting them as evidence for a compact binary black hole system while disfavoring disk and jet precession explanations.

quotes.quote: In the manuscript, a 3.8yr optical quasi-periodic oscillations (QPOs) is reported in blue quasar SDSS J132144+033055 (= ) at z=0.269 , based on 16.3yr-long light curve from both CSS and ZTF directly described by a sinusoidal function. The 3.8yr QPOs can be confirmed through the Generalized Lomb-Scargle periodogram with confidence level higher than 5 , through properties of the phase-folded light curve and the WWZ technique. Moreover, the collected Pan-STARRS light curves well follow the sinusoidal function described best fitting results to the CSS and ZTF light curves. The optical QPOs strongly indicate a central binary black hole (BBH) system in , with expected space separation smaller than 0.018pc, through the estimated upper limit of total BH mass 3.3 10^9 M_ through the correlation between BH mass and continuum luminosity.

However, under the disk precession assumption, the determined optical emission regions from central BH have sizes about 37 R_G similar as the sizes 35 R_G of the expected NUV emission regions through the correlation between disk size and BH mass, indicating the disk precession is not preferred. And due to undetected radio emissions, jet precession can be ruled out.

gmw_00011244.name: J0225+0033
gmw_00011244.overview: J0225+0033 is a physically small Green Pea galaxy that is comparatively massive and metal-rich [arXiv:2302.02868]. Although classified as a star-forming galaxy, the majority of its stars formed approximately 1 Gyr ago, and its line ratios align well with shock models [arXiv:2302.02868].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0225+0033 as a compact Green Pea galaxy that is comparatively massive and metal-rich, mostly formed stars about 1 Gyr ago, and has line ratios consistent with shock models.

quotes.quote: J0225+0033 is one of the two GPs found during our sample creation, as readily seen from its SDSS image (Fig. fig_SDSSoutl , top left panel). It is physically small ( r_ petro =7.8 kpc ) but with respect to the IMPs comparatively massive ( M_ / M _ =9.37 ) and metal-rich ( O / H +12=8.25 ). Most of the derived properties that would be presented in Table tab_galprops2outl cannot be constrained to physically meaningful values due to the large errors associated with the measurements of the faint emission lines present here. While readily classified as a SFG in the BPT diagnostic diagram (Fig. fig_BPToutl ), roughly 10 of its He II 4686 emission may be attributed to an AGN, as indicated by its position close to the demarcation line in Fig. fig_SBoutl . Its SFH (Fig. fig_sfhJ0225 ) reveals the majority of stars in J0225+0033 has been formed 1 Gyr ago.

Fig. fig_shockdiagsoutl reveals the line ratios of J0225+0033 to align well with the LMC shock models from Allen08 at v_ s =200-300 km s ^ 1 , and two out of three diagnostics suggest a strong magnetic field of B=10 G .

gmw_00011245.name: J1152+4005
gmw_00011245.overview: J1152+4005 is a massive compact galaxy with a physical radius of approximately 6.52 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2302.02868]. Its dominant ionization mechanism is an active galactic nucleus, which features a Seyfert 2-like spectrum and possible shock contributions to its interstellar medium [arXiv:2302.02868].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1152+4005 as the sample's most massive compact galaxy, with diagnostics favoring AGN ionisation, a Seyfert 2-like nucleus, uncertain oxygen abundance, and possible shock contribution.

quotes.quote: J1152+4005, the second GP in our initial sample, is the most massive galaxy ( M_ / M _ =9.94 ) studied in this paper, while being a compact object at r_ petro 6.52 kpc . Both diagnostic diagrams (Figs. fig_SBoutl and fig_BPToutl ) strongly suggest that an AGN is the dominant ionisation mechanism in this object. With no notable line broadening evident in this galaxy's spectrum, it is most reminiscent of a Seyfert 2 type nucleus. Its faint emission lines allow no conclusive determination of the oxygen abundance, though within their large error ranges of 1.3-1.7 dex, the direct, emprical t_ e and N2 methods (see Sect. sec_abund ) agree with a value between O / H =7.8-8.4 , whereas the R23 method would predict an unlikely value of O / H +12=10.3 . The comparison of the line ratios found in J1152+4005 to the LMC shock models from Allen08 suggests that shocks may play a significant role in the ionisation of the galaxy's ISM, but the large errors in the line ratios leave this finding somewhat unconclusive.

gmw_00011246.name: J1514+2840
gmw_00011246.overview: J1514+2840 is an object of ambiguous nature, classified either as a true dwarf galaxy or as an H II region within a larger, projected background galaxy [arXiv:2302.02868]. It experienced a young, metal-poor starburst approximately 10 million years ago and may be a candidate for ionizing radiation leakage [arXiv:2302.02868].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1514+2840 as ambiguous between an H II region and a true dwarf galaxy; despite contaminated spectra, its O32 and star-formation history make it relevant to LyC leakage.

quotes.quote: The SDSS image of J1514+2840 (Fig. fig_SDSSoutl , bottom left panel) reveals this object is of an ambiguous nature. Its (projected) proximity to a larger galaxy suggests it may be an H II region within the galaxy in question, which by itself has no spectroscopic data published in the SDSS. As it appears to be similar to the IMPs in terms of colour and morphology, another possible classification is a `true' DG which by chance is aligned with a larger background galaxy. In either case, the spectroscopic properties derived for this object are highly uncertain, as the SED is bound to include parts of both the SF object and the apparently quiescent, larger galaxy.

Even in the case of this object being a H II region, it may still be a worthwhile contender regarding the study of LyC photon loss, as the O _ 32 value of 2.7 is rather large, considering the `contamination' of the spectrum. Similar situations of localised ionised emission from star-forming regions or stellar clusters have previously been observed in other galaxies - to name one example, in case of Haro 11, its LyC leakage appears to be confined to one of its star-forming knots Keenan17 . The notion that J1514+2840 may be relevant in terms of LyC leakage is further supported by the SFH similar to that found in the IMPs, where a young and luminous metal-poor starburst occured at 10 Myr. The younger starburst episode, however, is somewhat older than the typical young population of the IMPs at 5 Myr , and its elevated metallicity of 0.4 Z_ may imply this stellar population is associated with the background source (Fig. fig_sfhJ1514 ).

gmw_00011247.name: J1657+4135
gmw_00011247.overview: J1657+4135 is a resolved spiral galaxy featuring at least three spiral arms with blue knots of enhanced star-forming activity [arXiv:2302.02868]. The galaxy has a nearby companion, and gravitational interaction between them likely triggered the current star formation episode [arXiv:2302.02868].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1657+4135 as a resolved spiral with blue star-forming knots and a nearby companion likely triggering activity; spectral features are attributed to nucleus-driven effects on the ISM.

quotes.quote: J1657+4135 is a true outlier in the sense that it is the only object considered in this paper where parts of the internal structure are resolved. Its SDSS image (Fig. fig_SDSSoutl , bottom right panel) reveals it to be a spiral galaxy with at least three spiral arms, which contain blue knots of enhanced star-forming activity. Its companion in the south (SDSS J165732.76+413434.9, visible as an elongated yellow shape at the bottom of the SDSS image in Fig. fig_SDSSoutl ) is located at a similar redshift ( z=0.02869666 ) and their centres are separated by 27 kpc in projection, so a likely scenario for triggering the current star formation episode is their gravitational interaction. While classified as a SFG in the BPT diagram (Fig. fig_BPToutl ), we cannot draw further conclusions concerning the presence of an AGN from He II 4686.

This indicates that along a few lines of sight, the density of neutral gas is low, either from kinematic effects or due to a high degree of ionisation. As discussed before, star-forming regions have the means to be a driver of both these effects. The SDSS fibre, however, only covers 3 around the galaxy's centre, and the low value of SFR H 0.191 M _ yr ^ 1 suggests no region of significantly enhanced star-formation is contained in the SDSS spectrum. We hence deem it likely that these features are related to the galaxy's nucleus, whose activity has a strong impact on the surrounding ISM.

gmw_00011248.overview: The object 253 is a nearby starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0610302]. It features a nuclear outflow that is detectable in X-rays and exhibits a variety of emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0610302].

sources.summary: The paper studies 253 as a nearby starburst galaxy, resolving its nuclear X-ray outflow, emission-line plasma, temperatures, densities, and asymmetric minor-axis morphology.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: As reference coordinate, we chose the coordinate of the centre of 253 \\ the position of the brightest IR source in the galaxy, _ 2000 =0^ h 47^ m 33 3, _ 2000 =-25 17 18

quotes.quote: Using \ data, we have aimed to study the nuclear outflow of the nearby starburst galaxy 253 \ in X-rays with respect to its morphology and to spectral variations along the outflow. We analysed \ RGS spectra, RGS brightness profiles in cross-dispersion direction, narrow band RGS and EPIC images and EPIC PN brightness profiles of the nuclear region and of the outflow of 253. We detect a diversity of emission lines along the outflow of 253. This includes the He-like ions of Si, Mg, Ne and O and their corresponding ions in the next higher ionisation state. Additionally transitions from Fe XVII and Fe XVIII are prominent.

gmw_00011249.name: J1625+2712
gmw_00011249.overview: J1625+2712 is a radio galaxy with a steep-spectrum core straddled by weak large-scale lobes [arXiv:1212.2072]. High-resolution observations resolve this core into a compact double misaligned with the outer lobes, indicating that the galaxy is a concealed X-shaped radio source [arXiv:1212.2072].

sources.summary: EVN observations resolve J1625+2712's apparent core into a compact double misaligned with its large-scale lobes, leading the paper to identify it as a concealed X-shaped radio source.

quotes.quote: In the case of J1625+2712, one of those six targets, the EVN result was very surprising. J1625+2712 is a CDT source where, according to FIRST, the core of 261 mJy is straddled by weak lobes of 5 and 1 mJy, respectively. The spectral index of the core between 1.4 GHz and 5 GHz is =-0.68 so it is clearly a steep spectrum source. The map in the left panel of Fig. 1 is a cut-out from FIRST showing the whole J1625+2712.

However, in the 6-cm EVN map, the alleged ``core'' has been resolved into a double where the separation of the two peaks is only 18 milliarcseconds -- see the right panel of Fig. 1. The P.A. of the line connecting the maxima is 7 6 , whereas the P.A. of the large-scale structure -- its angular size reaches 53 5 - is 42 . It follows that the misalignment between the two pairs of lobes is nearly 50 . The most straightforward interpretation of J1625+2712 is that this is an X-shaped source, but since one arm of the cross is 3,000 times shorter than the other, the actual structure of J1625+2712 cannot be recognised in the FIRST map showing the whole radio source.

gmw_00011250.name: NGC 5947
gmw_00011250.overview: NGC 5947 is a galaxy that hosts regions analogous to the solar neighborhood [arXiv:2204.13725]. These likely solar-neighborhood analog spaxels group together to form a ring-like structure within the galaxy [arXiv:2204.13725].

sources.summary: NGC 5947 is one of three CALIFA examples hosting solar-neighborhood analog regions; its SNA spectrum matches the reference within uncertainties and likely SNA spaxels form a ring-like structure.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:sna-showcase summarizes our main results for three galaxies hosting SNA regions, as an illustrative example: NGC 5947 , NGC 5000 and NGC 309 . For each galaxy we show the contour of the likelihood map ( L _ ij ^2 _ ij /2 ) on top of the RGB composite image, highlighting the location of the most likely SNA. In addition we show the corresponding spectra of those regions together with the reference SNA spectrum. We observe that the SNA spectrum of the three example galaxies is well within the uncertainty band, _ (shaded region), for most of the wavelength range. A higher L _ SNA indicates that the likelihood of finding a SNA within the target galaxy is more concentrated around a specific region.

In top panels of Fig. fig:sna-showcase , NGC 5000 shows the most concentrated likelihood of hosting SNA spaxels, with an apparent azimuthal symmetry. At first sight, this pattern is also followed by NGC 5947 and NGC 309 , whereby likely SNA spaxels group in a ring-like structure.

gmw_00011251.name: maxBCG 2596
gmw_00011251.overview: The galaxy maxBCG 2596 is an optically faint radio source with a redshift of 0.56 [arXiv:1110.0339]. Its central near-infrared counterpart coincides with its radio core, while nearby galaxy-like detections aligned with its radio lobes are judged to be chance alignments [arXiv:1110.0339].

sources.summary: The paper describes maxBCG 2596 as an optically faint radio source whose central NIR counterpart coincides with its radio core, while nearby lobe-aligned galaxy-like detections are judged chance alignments.

quotes.quote: maxBCG 2596 In this object we have identified the NIR counterpart (component N; Table 3) to the radio core (Nucleus; Table 1) at the center of the radio structure, which is the brightest NIR component in our sample, but still falls below the 22 mag r_ AB band limit of the SDSS. We have also detected NIR sources (components W and E; Table 3) coincident with the termination of the radio lobes (components East and West Lobe, Table 1), the latter of which display extended NIR morphology. The NIR E and W components are detected in the SDSS image, where they are identified with extended, galaxy-type objects -- J010202.12-103258.3 and J010159.33-103311.2 (Table 4) -- with somewhat similar r_ AB band magnitudes of 19.4 and 20.9 (Table 4), but distinct photometric redshifts ( z =0.09 and 0.19; Table 4). Because radio lobes do not produce significant amounts of optical emission and due to the redshift discrepancy between the radio source ( z =0.56; Table 7; see also Section 5.3) and the corresponding optical/NIR components ( z =0.09 and 0.19; Table 4), we conclude that this is a chance alignment.

gmw_00011252.name: maxBCG 3131
gmw_00011252.overview: The galaxy maxBCG 3131 is a core-jet radio source [arXiv:1110.0339]. It exhibits a complex near-infrared morphology and contains a faint, blue central source with colors consistent with a quasar [arXiv:1110.0339].

sources.summary: The paper identifies maxBCG 3131 as the sample's core-jet radio source, with complex NIR morphology and a faint blue central NIR source consistent with quasar-like colors at the estimated radio-source redshift.

quotes.quote: For the only core-jet radio source in our sample (maxBCG 3131; Fig. 1), we have designated the radio emission regions North Comp and South Comp 1 and 2 (Table 1).

maxBCG 3131 The HAWKI-I image shows a complex NIR morphology. All of the NIR components can be identified on the Stripe 82 images with r_ AB band magnitudes ranging from 19 to 24 mag (Table 4). The central NIR source (component N; Table 3), coincident with the center of the radio structure (between North Comp and South Comp 1 components; Table 1) is the faintest, with a very blue g- =0.35 colour index, consistent with the optical colour of a quasar (Richards 2001) at the estimated redshift of our radio source ( z =1.03; Table 7; see also Section 5.3). This result is confirmed by the photometric redshift estimate for the Stripe 82 detection ( z =1.57; Table 7; see also Section 5.3).

gmw_00011253.name: maxBCG 6167
gmw_00011253.overview: The galaxy maxBCG 6167 is a very faint radio, near-infrared, and optical source [arXiv:1110.0339]. It exhibits central near-infrared emission coincident with its radio structure and has a photometric redshift estimated at approximately 1.00 [arXiv:1110.0339].

sources.summary: The paper describes maxBCG 6167 as a very faint radio/NIR/optical source with central NIR emission, a Stripe 82 galaxy-type counterpart, and a photometric redshift consistent with the radio-source estimate.

quotes.quote: maxBCG 6167 Like its radio emission, the NIR emission associated with this source is faint. We detect central NIR emission (component N; Table 3) coincident with the center of the radio structure. An optical counterpart has been detected in Stripe 82 -- J002452.65-005201.7 (Table 4) -- as a galaxy-type source with an r_ AB band magnitude of about 25 mag (Table 4). This is the faintest radio, NIR and optical (Stripe 82) source in our sample.

Its extreme g- blue colour of 0.44 is affected by large magnitude errors ( 0.4 mag in each of the 2 bands), rendering it too blue, even for the expected values of a quasar (Richards 2001) at the estimated redshift of our radio source ( z =1.13; Table 7; see also Section 5.3). This result is confirmed by the photometric redshift estimate for the Stripe 82 detection ( z =1.00; Table 7; see also Section 5.3).

gmw_00011254.name: maxBCG 8495
gmw_00011254.overview: The radio source maxBCG 8495 is a galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 0.88 [arXiv:1110.0339]. It exhibits a disky near-infrared morphology with a double-nuclear emission region and red colors suggestive of a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1110.0339].

sources.summary: The paper describes maxBCG 8495 as having double-nuclear NIR emission, extended galaxy-type Stripe 82 morphology, red colors suggestive of a late-type spiral, and a photometric redshift near the radio-source estimate.

quotes.quote: maxBCG 8495 The NIR image shows a "double nuclear" emission region (components Na and Nb; Table 3) at the center of the radio structure. A similarly extended galaxy-type morphology is seen in the Stripe 82 image, although Stripe 82 identifies a sole, quite faint, component -- J004359.47+001230.4 (Table 4) -- with an r_ AB band magnitude of about 24 mag and g- =1.31 (Table 4). Given its red colour and the estimated redshift of the radio source ( z =0.96; Table 7; see also Section 5.3), we can conclude that the Stripe 82 optical counterpart has a g- colour index suggestive of a late-type spiral galaxy (Fukugita 1995), which is coherent with the disky appearance in the NIR. The red colour generally excludes a quasar host (Richards 2001) at the estimated redshift of our radio source ( z =0.96; Table 7; see also Section 5.3). These results are confirmed by the photometric redshift estimate for the Stripe 82 detection ( z =0.88; Table 7; see also Section 5.3).

gmw_00011255.name: [ ] 1030A
gmw_00011255.overview: [ ] 1030A is a compact, line-emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.7354 [arXiv:2305.13550]. It has a spatially unresolved size of less than 2.4 kiloparsecs and is situated near a high-ionization absorber [arXiv:2305.13550].

sources.summary: The paper reports [ ] 1030A as a compact z=5.7354 line-emitting galaxy near a high-ionization absorber, with 86 pkpc impact parameter, narrow line width, and no UV counterpart.

quotes.quote: The first source, referred to as [ ] 1030A, at z_ [ C ii ] =5.7354 , has an impact parameter to the quasar sightline The transverse separation from the QSO line of sight at the redshift of the intervening system. of 86 pkpc. The second source, [ ] 1030B, at z_ [ C ii ] =5.7101 is at 109  pkpc (Fig  fig:main a). These sources have the maximum peak signal-to-noise ratios (S/N) of >6.0 when collapsing the emission line channels over 66  km s^ 1 (Methods). The [ ] moment-0 intensity maps of these [ ] sources are shown in Fig.  fig:main b and . Interestingly, these two sources are not spatially resolved under the synthesized FWHM beam size of 0.35 , putting a strong constraint on the FWHM size of the [ ] sources to be less than 2.4 pkpc.

By fitting a simple Gaussian profile, we estimate the FWHM line widths to be 64 11  km s^ 1 ( [ ] 1030A) and 62 11  km s^ 1 (B), and the velocity-integrated [ ] flux densities to be 0.174 0.027  Jy km s^ 1 (A) and 0.276 0.044  Jy km s^ 1 (B) after correcting for the primary beam response (Methods). The two dotted lines represent the four spectral channels, equivalent to a velocity width of 66  km s^ 1 , used to create the moment-0 maps. The total [ ] luminosity is L_ [ C ii ] = (1.61 0.24) 10^8  L_ and (2.21 0.36) 10^8  L_ , respectively, for [ ] 1030A and B. Figure  fig:main_spec d summarizes the kinematic structure of the [ ] sources relative to the absorption systems. The [ ] 1030A ( z_ [ C ii ] =5.7354 ) has a velocity difference of V = +485  km s^ 1 relative to the stronger absorption system at z_ abs = 5.7246 . The [ ] 1030B ( z_ [ C ii ] =5.7101 ) is at V =-645  km s^ 1 . Note that [ ] 1030A has a velocity difference of V=-250  km s^ 1 relative to its closest (and weakest) absorption system at z_ abs =5.7411 .

gmw_00011256.name: [ ] 1030B
gmw_00011256.overview: [ ] 1030B is a compact, high-redshift emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.7101 [arXiv:2305.13550]. It has a spatially unresolved size of less than 2.4 physical kiloparsecs and is situated at an impact parameter of 109 physical kiloparsecs from a nearby quasar sightline [arXiv:2305.13550].

sources.summary: The paper reports [ ] 1030B as a compact z=5.7101 line-emitting galaxy near the absorber complex, with 109 pkpc impact parameter, narrow line width, and no UV counterpart.

quotes.quote: The first source, referred to as [ ] 1030A, at z_ [ C ii ] =5.7354 , has an impact parameter to the quasar sightline The transverse separation from the QSO line of sight at the redshift of the intervening system. of 86 pkpc. The second source, [ ] 1030B, at z_ [ C ii ] =5.7101 is at 109  pkpc (Fig  fig:main a). These sources have the maximum peak signal-to-noise ratios (S/N) of >6.0 when collapsing the emission line channels over 66  km s^ 1 (Methods). The [ ] moment-0 intensity maps of these [ ] sources are shown in Fig.  fig:main b and . Interestingly, these two sources are not spatially resolved under the synthesized FWHM beam size of 0.35 , putting a strong constraint on the FWHM size of the [ ] sources to be less than 2.4 pkpc.

By fitting a simple Gaussian profile, we estimate the FWHM line widths to be 64 11  km s^ 1 ( [ ] 1030A) and 62 11  km s^ 1 (B), and the velocity-integrated [ ] flux densities to be 0.174 0.027  Jy km s^ 1 (A) and 0.276 0.044  Jy km s^ 1 (B) after correcting for the primary beam response (Methods). The two dotted lines represent the four spectral channels, equivalent to a velocity width of 66  km s^ 1 , used to create the moment-0 maps. The total [ ] luminosity is L_ [ C ii ] = (1.61 0.24) 10^8  L_ and (2.21 0.36) 10^8  L_ , respectively, for [ ] 1030A and B. Figure  fig:main_spec d summarizes the kinematic structure of the [ ] sources relative to the absorption systems. The [ ] 1030A ( z_ [ C ii ] =5.7354 ) has a velocity difference of V = +485  km s^ 1 relative to the stronger absorption system at z_ abs = 5.7246 . The [ ] 1030B ( z_ [ C ii ] =5.7101 ) is at V =-645  km s^ 1 . Note that [ ] 1030A has a velocity difference of V=-250  km s^ 1 relative to its closest (and weakest) absorption system at z_ abs =5.7411 .

gmw_00011257.name: J0341 + 1720
gmw_00011257.overview: J0341 + 1720 is an ultra-luminous quasar located at a redshift of z=3.69 [arXiv:2010.14433]. Its host galaxy is classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy with a star formation rate of approximately 1500 solar masses per year [arXiv:2010.14433].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0341 + 1720 as an ultra-luminous quasar with a massive, possibly super-Eddington SMBH and a highly star-forming ULIRG host detected in CO and continuum.

quotes.quote: J0341 + 1720 was discovered as part of the Extremely Luminous Quasar Survey (ELQS) survey Schindler2017 and published in Schindler2019a with a redshift of z=3.69 determined from the optical discovery spectrum. J2125 1719 was later discovered during an extension of the ELQS to the Panoramic Survey Telescope and Rapid Response System Pan-STARRS 1, Kaiser2002, Kaiser2010 3 survey PS1, Chambers2016 footprint PS-ELQS, Schindler2019b with a discovery redshift of z=3.90 . Table table:sample provides an overview over the general properties of both quasars, their redshifts, absolute magnitudes at a rest-frame wavelength of 1450 , coordinates and discovery photometry. J0341 + 1720 and J2125 1719 are ultra-luminous quasars with M_ 1450 =-29.46 and 29.35 , respectively, as derived from their i-band magnitudes (see Figure fig:zM1450 ).

We further observed the host galaxy emission of J0341 + 1720 at mm wavelengths with a clear detection of the CO(4-3) transition and the underlying continuum. We estimate dispersion dominated and rotationally supported dynamical masses of M_ dyn,disp 3 10^ 10 M _ and M_ dyn,rot 2 10^ 11 M_ , respectively. Similarly to quasars at z=5-7 , J0341 + 1720 lies above the local SMBH galaxy scaling relations (see Figure fig:coevolution ). Based on its total infrared luminosity ( L_ TIR 1.0 10^ 13 L_ ) the host galaxy of J0341 + 1720 can be classified as a ULIRG with an approximate star formation rate of SFR 1500 M_ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00011258.name: J2125 1719
gmw_00011258.overview: J2125 1719 is an ultra-luminous quasar discovered at a redshift of z=3.90 [arXiv:2010.14433]. The system harbors a supermassive black hole with a mass of approximately 3 to 10 billion solar masses that accretes at a rate moderately above the Eddington limit [arXiv:2010.14433].

sources.summary: The paper describes J2125 1719 as an ultra-luminous quasar whose spectra imply a massive SMBH and an accretion rate moderately above the Eddington limit.

quotes.quote: J0341 + 1720 was discovered as part of the Extremely Luminous Quasar Survey (ELQS) survey Schindler2017 and published in Schindler2019a with a redshift of z=3.69 determined from the optical discovery spectrum. J2125 1719 was later discovered during an extension of the ELQS to the Panoramic Survey Telescope and Rapid Response System Pan-STARRS 1, Kaiser2002, Kaiser2010 3 survey PS1, Chambers2016 footprint PS-ELQS, Schindler2019b with a discovery redshift of z=3.90 . Table table:sample provides an overview over the general properties of both quasars, their redshifts, absolute magnitudes at a rest-frame wavelength of 1450 , coordinates and discovery photometry. J0341 + 1720 and J2125 1719 are ultra-luminous quasars with M_ 1450 =-29.46 and 29.35 , respectively, as derived from their i-band magnitudes (see Figure fig:zM1450 ).

J0341 + 1720 and J2125 1719 are much more luminous compared to the population of low- and mid-redshift SDSS quasars. They harbor SMBHs with masses of (M_ BH /M_ )=9.5-10 at the massive end of the low-redshift SDSS quasar distribution. While both quasars are similarly luminous to their three ultra-luminous siblings, they have lower BH masses, resulting in accretion rates moderately above the Eddington limit with _ Edd =2-3 .

gmw_00011259.name: 8 o'clock arc
gmw_00011259.overview: The 8 o'clock arc is a gravitationally lensed Lyman Break Galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.73 [arXiv:1010.0655]. It has a star-formation rate of 270 solar masses per year derived from optical and near-infrared spectroscopy [arXiv:1010.0655].

sources.summary: The paper describes the 8 o'clock arc as a z=2.73 lensed Lyman Break Galaxy with high optical/NIR star formation but no detected uncontaminated radio emission.

quotes.quote: The 8 o'clock arc is a gravitationally lensed Lyman Break Galaxy (LBG) at redshift z=2.73 that has a star-formation rate (SFR) of 270 M _ yr ^ 1 (derived from optical and near-infrared spectroscopy). Taking the magnification of the system ( 12 ) and the SFR into account, the expected flux density of any associated radio emission at 1.4 GHz is predicted to be just 0.1 mJy. However, the lens system is found to be coincident with a radio source detected in the NRAO Very Large Array (VLA) Sky Survey with a flux density of 5 mJy.

We find that the radio emission is dominated by a radio-loud AGN associated with the lensing galaxy. The radio-jet from the AGN partially covers the lensed arc of the LBG, and we do not detect any radio emission from the unobscured region of the arc down to a 3 flux-density limit of 108 Jy beam ^ 1 . Using the radio data, we place a limit of 750 M _ yr ^ 1 for the SFR of the LBG, which is consistent with the results from the optical and near-infrared spectroscopy.

gmw_00011260.name: South BCG
aliases_external: Musket Ball South BCG candidate, Dawson catalog recno 398
gmw_00011260.overview: The South BCG is an early-type brightest cluster galaxy that is largely offset from its cluster's galaxy density peak [arXiv:1611.07040]. Morphological and spectroscopic data indicate that this object is a galaxy-galaxy merger of two elliptical galaxies with resolved nuclei [arXiv:1611.07040].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the South BCG as a displaced early-type galaxy-galaxy merger with resolved nuclei, arguing a nearby second BCG likely occupied the central BCG role before the merger.

quotes.quote: The DEIMOS confirmed BCGs for the North and South have spectra with absorption features and a strong continuum, typical of bright, red sequence galaxies, which ultimately form into the BCGs of regular clusters seen at lower redshifts 2013hsa7.conf..433A . For the North and South clusters, the BCG is also the MMCG, seen as the brightest and most massive object in each cluster CMD and CSMD, respectively, in Fig. fig:completeness and indicated with luminosity-scaled yellow dots in Fig. fig:map . In this section, we quantify the qualities that distinguish the North and South BCGs from those typical of relaxed clusters.

The South BCG is largely offset from the galaxy density peak (369 h ^ 1 _ 70 kpc) . However, morphology and spectroscopy indicate this object is a galaxy-galaxy merger of two ellipticals (seen individually as two small, filled red diamonds in the left-center panel of Fig. fig:morph ), so may not have been the BCG prior to the merger. Both nuclei are resolved in the HST bands, so we perform aperture photometry to estimate the V and R magnitudes of the individual galaxies, along with a projected companion spectroscopically confirmed to be a background object.

gmw_00011261.name: SDSS J0015+0104
gmw_00011261.overview: SDSS J0015+0104 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy located at a distance of 28.4 Mpc [arXiv:1304.7983]. It resides deep within the Eridanus void and is recognized as the lowest-metallicity low surface brightness dwarf galaxy known [arXiv:1304.7983].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J0015+0104 as the lowest-metallicity known LSBD galaxy, with a low oxygen abundance, 28.4 Mpc distance, and location deep in the Eridanus void.

quotes.quote: Received on 2012 July 10 firstpage - lastpage 2013 SDSS J0015+0104 is the lowest metallicity low surface brightness dwarf (LSBD) galaxy known. The oxygen abundance in its \ region SDSS J001520.70+010436.9 (at 1.5 kpc from the galaxy centre) is 12+ (O/H)=7.07 (Guseva et al.). This galaxy, at the distance of 28.4 Mpc, appears to reside deeply in the volume devoid of luminous massive galaxies, known as the Eridanus void.

gmw_00011262.name: SDSS J235437.29 000501.6
gmw_00011262.overview: SDSS J235437.29 000501.6 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy located in the Eridanus void [arXiv:1304.7983]. It is characterized by an unevolved status, featuring extremely low metallicity, blue colors, and a high ratio of neutral hydrogen gas to light [arXiv:1304.7983].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J235437.29 000501.6 as an Eridanus void LSBD galaxy with very low metallicity, high H I-to-light ratio, blue colours, and unevolved status.

quotes.quote: SDSS J235437.29 000501.6 is another Eridanus void LSBD galaxy, with parameter 12+ (O/H)=7.36 (also Guseva et al.). We present the results of their \ observations with the Nan ay Radio Telescope revealing their high ratios of M ( )/L _ B 2.3. Based on the Sloan Digital Sky Survey images, we derived for both galaxies their radial surface brightness profiles and the main photometric parameters. Their colours and total magnitudes are used to estimate the galaxy stellar mass and ages. The related gas mass-fractions, f_ g 0.98 and 0.97, and the extremely low metallicities (much lower than for their more typical counterparts with the same luminosity) indicate their unevolved status.

gmw_00011263.name: ESO 287-G13
gmw_00011263.overview: ESO 287-G13 is a disk galaxy with a dark matter halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0701550]. Detailed modeling of its rotation curve has been used to analyze the density profile and mass parameters of this outer halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0701550].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 287-G13 as a detailed outer-halo discrepancy test case, fitting its rotation curve and comparing derived halo density and mass parameters against NFW expectations.

quotes.quote: From our sample 1 we select DDO 47 and ESO 287-G13, as the best examples to show individually the outer NFW CDM-data discrepancy. Then, we use the combined samples 1-2 to compare, at the outermost radii, the NFW CDM predictions, obtained by a newly discovered structural CDM relation, with the values of the mass, radius and density of the DM halos around galaxies. We analyse in detail the density profiles of the DM halos for the two best cases in our sample: DDO 47 (Salucci, Walter and Borriello 2003, Gentile et al. 2005) and ESO 287-G13 (Gentile et al. 2004).

Eq. masseq allows to derive the values for and M_ vir : 18.4 and 6 10^ 10 M_ for DDO 47, and 13.3 and 7 10^ 11 M_ for ESO 287-G13. Let us anticipate that this assumption is very conservative; in fact, if the mass equality expressed by eq. masseq occurs at radii smaller than R_ f the result we claim here will be even more prominent; on the other hand, if the mass equality occurs at radii larger than R_ f , a discrepancy from R=0 out to R=R_ f will be set by definition. This assumption has also the desirable by-product, in the NFW framework, of implying reasonable values for the stellar mass-to-light ( M/L ) ratios. Given the importance of a careful analysis, we have also considered a different implementation of the NFW halo + disk + gas mass modelling: in ESO 287-G13 we have fitted the rotation curve with this mass model by leaving M/L and M_ vir as free parameters; this latter procedure, shown in Fig. densprof , yields very similar results with respect to those obtained by means of eq. masseq .

gmw_00011264.name: 3C 274.1
gmw_00011264.overview: 3C 274.1 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of 0.422 [arXiv:2009.03440]. The major axis of this source spans an observed size of 460 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2009.03440].

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 274.1 as an illustrative radio source at redshift 0.422, requiring model cocoons to grow to its observed 460 kpc major-axis size.

quotes.quote: We consider two sources for illustrative purpose: 3C 274.1 and B2 1358+30C ( , their Table 1), observed at z=0.422 and 0.206 respectively. The size of their major axis are 460 kpc and 1400 kpc respectively. We require that cocoon starting at z_ st >z with some Q_j will grow to size ( 2L_j ) 460 10 kpc and 1400 10 kpc by z=0.422 and 0.206 respectively.

gmw_00011265.name: B2 1358+30C
gmw_00011265.overview: B2 1358+30C is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of 0.206 [arXiv:2009.03440]. Observations of the source reveal a major axis size of 1400 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2009.03440].

sources.summary: The paper uses B2 1358+30C as an illustrative radio source at redshift 0.206, requiring model cocoons to reproduce its observed 1400 kpc major-axis size.

quotes.quote: We consider two sources for illustrative purpose: 3C 274.1 and B2 1358+30C ( , their Table 1), observed at z=0.422 and 0.206 respectively. The size of their major axis are 460 kpc and 1400 kpc respectively. We require that cocoon starting at z_ st >z with some Q_j will grow to size ( 2L_j ) 460 10 kpc and 1400 10 kpc by z=0.422 and 0.206 respectively.

gmw_00011266.name: HerBS-33C
gmw_00011266.overview: HerBS-33C is an off-centre infrared galaxy identified in ACA data [arXiv:2301.02584]. Its emission is visible as a separate source at 250 Ιm, but it blends with two central sources at 500 Ιm [arXiv:2301.02584].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HerBS-33C as an off-centre 101 GHz source, discusses possible 151 GHz non-detection causes, and notes the lack of a corresponding VLASS source.

quotes.quote: The positions of Band 3 (101 GHz) sources with no Band 4 (151 GHz) counterparts should be accurate to within 0.16 arcsec except for HerBS-33C, which was identified in ACA data and should have a position accurate to within 0.65 arcsec.

In the case of HerBS-33, it is apparent that the off-centre source (labelled C) is visible as a separate source in the Herschel 250 m image but that its emission is blended together with the central two sources (A and B) in the Herschel 500 m image.

Since both 101 GHz sources lie at the edge of the imaged 151 GHz field (where the interferometer drops to 20 of the sensitivity at the centre of the field), it is possible that HerBS-33C and HerBS-63C were not detected at 151 GHz because of sensitivity issues. Nevertheless, we cannot immediately rule out the possibility that the 101 GHz emission is simply brighter. For example, it is possible that HerBS-33C and HerBS-63C are z<2 galaxies with AGN that produce synchrotron emission with a spectral index of 0.3 that would be detectable in the 101 GHz band but not in the 151 GHz band. The Very Large Array Sky Survey VLASS; gordon2021 reported a 3 GHz source with a flux density of 1.3 mJy at the position of HerBS-63C, and the spectral slope from 3 to 101 GHz is consistent with a spectral index of 0.3. However, no source from that survey corresponded to the position of HerBS-33C, although the 3 GHz emission would be expected to be 1.7 mJy and should have been detectable by the VLASS.

gmw_00011267.name: HerBS-63C
gmw_00011267.overview: HerBS-63C is an off-centre 101 GHz source that lacks a clear 250 Ιm counterpart [arXiv:2301.02584]. It is possibly an active galactic nucleus source at a redshift of less than two, and it has been detected in the Very Large Array Sky Survey [arXiv:2301.02584].
