sources.summary: The paper describes HerBS-63C as an off-centre 101 GHz source without a clear 250 micron counterpart, possibly an AGN source, and notes a VLASS detection.

quotes.quote: However, the off-centre source in the HerBS-63 field (labelled C) does not have a clear 250 m counterpart. Since both 101 GHz sources lie at the edge of the imaged 151 GHz field (where the interferometer drops to 20 of the sensitivity at the centre of the field), it is possible that HerBS-33C and HerBS-63C were not detected at 151 GHz because of sensitivity issues. Nevertheless, we cannot immediately rule out the possibility that the 101 GHz emission is simply brighter. For example, it is possible that HerBS-33C and HerBS-63C are z<2 galaxies with AGN that produce synchrotron emission with a spectral index of 0.3 that would be detectable in the 101 GHz band but not in the 151 GHz band. The Very Large Array Sky Survey VLASS; gordon2021 reported a 3 GHz source with a flux density of 1.3 mJy at the position of HerBS-63C, and the spectral slope from 3 to 101 GHz is consistent with a spectral index of 0.3.

gmw_00011268.name: SDSS J233635.75-010733.7
gmw_00011268.overview: SDSS J233635.75-010733.7 is a high-redshift binary quasar system [arXiv:1607.00898]. The system consists of a standard quasar with a blue continuum and broad emission lines, paired with a broad absorption line quasar at a separation of 1.67 arcseconds [arXiv:1607.00898].

sources.summary: The paper tests a centroid-shift binary AGN selection method on SDSS J233635.75-010733.7, detecting excess positional dispersion and noting its unresolved high-redshift binary quasar nature.

quotes.quote: In this paper, a known binary AGN (SDSS J233635.75-010733.7) is employed to verify the ability of this method. Using 162 exposures in the R band of Palomar Transient Factory (PTF), an excess of dispersion in the positional distribution of the binary AGN is detected, though the two nuclei cannot be resolved in the images of PTF. We also propose a new method to compare the position of the binary AGN in PTF g and R band and find the difference is highly significant even only with 20 exposures.

This source is discovered by an infrared imaging survey at the Keck Observatory and confirmed to be a binary quasar (a standard quasar with a blue continuum and broad emission lines and a BAL quasar) by resolved optical spectra (Gregg et al. 2002). The separation of the two quasars is 1.67'' , while the mean seeing of PTF observations is 2.1'' and 2.2'' in R and g band, respectively. The pixel scale of PTF CCD is 1.01'' (Law et al. 2009). Thus, the two quasars cannot be resolved in the image of PTF (Figure img right ), though the source slightly deviates from the point spread function (PSF).

gmw_00011269.name: SPT0113-46
gmw_00011269.overview: SPT0113-46 is a high-redshift dusty star-forming galaxy that is gravitationally lensed by a group of galaxies [arXiv:2102.05671]. It is a main-sequence galaxy with a low gas fraction that is likely consuming its residual gas to quench star formation [arXiv:2102.05671].

sources.summary: The paper models SPT0113-46 as a group-lensed DSFG with a main-sequence status, low gas fraction, and likely evolution toward a quiescent early-type galaxy.

quotes.quote: We note that four out of the five sources are lensed by a galaxy, while SPT0113-46 is lensed by a group of galaxies spilker . When modelling SPT0113-46, we therefore also included the contribution to the lensing potential of the three closest galaxies in the form of three elliptical power-law components. We found that this was sufficient to fit the data to the noise level (see column three in Fig. fig:03 ).

We find that four out of five galaxies in our sample are consistent with the starburst sequence, while SPT0113-46 is a main-sequence galaxy. These findings are not surprising, given that the selection criteria used for the identification of the SPT DSFGs are based on the observed flux rather than the intrinsic one vieira10, vieira . SPT0113-46 has, indeed, an observed infrared luminosity of L_ IR,obs which is similar to the rest of the sample (see column two in Table tab:sfr ). However, the large magnification factor of 36 leads to an intrinsic luminosity L_ IR and SFR which are on average a factor of five below the other galaxies in the sample.

Interestingly, SPT0113-46 has the lowest gas fraction within our sample and, is the only galaxy on the main sequence. All these properties seem to be indicating that SPT0113-46 is in the process of consuming its residual gas, quenching its star formation and transforming into a typical ETG.

gmw_00011270.name: SPT0441-46
gmw_00011270.overview: SPT0441-46 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy characterized by a steeply rising rotation curve [arXiv:2102.05671]. It exhibits anomalous kinematic components, including faint emission in forbidden regions that suggests non-circular motions, rotational lag, or extraplanar gas [arXiv:2102.05671].

sources.summary: The paper finds SPT0441-46 has a steeply rising rotation curve and anomalous kinematic components, including forbidden-region emission attributed to lag, non-circular motions, or extraplanar gas.

quotes.quote: In particular, we can distinguish three main classes: rotation curves that steeply rise and then decline before flattening out to the outermost measured value (SPT0113-46, SPT0441-46). These curves are typical of nearby massive spiral galaxies with stellar bulges cimatti ; rotation curves with an inner slow rise and then a flattening (SPT2132-58), typical of present-day disc galaxies; rotation curves with an intermediate behaviour (SPT0345-47, SPT2146-55).

We note also that on top of the bulk rotation motions two galaxies of the sample, SPT0441-46 and SPT2146-55, have anomalous kinematic components, features very common in nearby spiral galaxies fraternali01, fraternali02 . In particular, SPT0441-46 and SPT2146-55 show faint emission in the so-called forbidden regions (i.e., forbidden for rotation) of the p-v diagrams (see the black contours in the lower left quadrants in Fig. fig:ap8 and fig:ap11 ). These features are due to lag in rotation and non-circular motions and they are usually ascribed to extraplanar gas, bars or lopsided structures fraternali06, sancisi . The inspection of the p-v diagrams and channel maps indicates that the derived kinematic parameters are robust also for these two galaxies.

gmw_00011271.name: SPT2132-58
gmw_00011271.overview: SPT2132-58 is a high-redshift dusty star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2102.05671]. It exhibits a rotation curve with an inner slow rise and subsequent flattening that is typical of present-day disc galaxies [arXiv:2102.05671].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes SPT2132-58 as a high-redshift DSFG with a slow-rising rotation curve, high inferred stellar-to-halo mass ratio, and low feedback efficiency requirement.

quotes.quote: These curves are typical of nearby massive spiral galaxies with stellar bulges cimatti ; rotation curves with an inner slow rise and then a flattening (SPT2132-58), typical of present-day disc galaxies; rotation curves with an intermediate behaviour (SPT0345-47, SPT2146-55). The variety of the stellar distributions in these DSFGs is, therefore, imprinted in their dynamics.
SPT0345-47 and SPT2132-58 have, instead, a M_ star /M_ DM of 0.12 ^ +0.2 _ 0.06 and 0.6 ^ +0.2 _ 0.3 , respectively.

For two galaxies (SPT2146-55, SPT2132-58), the _ SFR values at _ SN = 0.1 overestimate _ m by a factor of 3, indicating that low efficiency values 0.01 _ SN 0.001 are needed, similarly to what found both for high- and low- z star-forming galaxies fraternali20, bacchini .

gmw_00011272.name: SPT0345-47
gmw_00011272.overview: SPT0345-47 is a high-redshift dusty star-forming galaxy that exhibits an intermediate rotation-curve behavior [arXiv:2102.05671]. The region probed by its carbon emission is strongly baryon-dominated, with a stellar and gas component ratio to total matter of 0.90 [arXiv:2102.05671].

sources.summary: The paper places SPT0345-47 among DSFGs with intermediate rotation-curve behavior, strong baryon dominance in the probed region, and feedback-consistent velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: These curves are typical of nearby massive spiral galaxies with stellar bulges cimatti ; rotation curves with an inner slow rise and then a flattening (SPT2132-58), typical of present-day disc galaxies; rotation curves with an intermediate behaviour (SPT0345-47, SPT2146-55). The variety of the stellar distributions in these DSFGs is, therefore, imprinted in their dynamics.

We note that the regions probed by the [ CII ] emission line are baryon dominated: the ratio between the stellar and gas component to the total matter at the outermost radius ranges from 0.66 0.02 (SPT0441-46) to 0.90 0.03 (SPT0345-47). For three out of the five galaxies of our sample, global measurements of the stellar-to-halo mass ratios ( M_ star /M_ DM ) are in the range 10^ 2 10^ 3 , in agreement with theoretical expectations e.g., behroozi . SPT0345-47 and SPT2132-58 have, instead, a M_ star /M_ DM of 0.12 ^ +0.2 _ 0.06 and 0.6 ^ +0.2 _ 0.3 , respectively.
We note that three galaxies in the sample (SPT0113-46, SPT0345-47, SPT0441-46) have values of velocity dispersions fully consistent with _ SFR at _ SN = 0.1 within 1.5- .

gmw_00011273.name: SPT2146-55
gmw_00011273.overview: SPT2146-55 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy that exhibits a rotation curve with intermediate behavior [arXiv:2102.05671]. It displays anomalous kinematic components, including faint emission in forbidden regions that suggests non-circular motions or extraplanar gas [arXiv:2102.05671].

sources.summary: The paper reports SPT2146-55 has intermediate rotation-curve behavior, anomalous forbidden-region emission, robust kinematic parameters, and a low-efficiency feedback interpretation for its dispersion.

quotes.quote: These curves are typical of nearby massive spiral galaxies with stellar bulges cimatti ; rotation curves with an inner slow rise and then a flattening (SPT2132-58), typical of present-day disc galaxies; rotation curves with an intermediate behaviour (SPT0345-47, SPT2146-55).

We note also that on top of the bulk rotation motions two galaxies of the sample, SPT0441-46 and SPT2146-55, have anomalous kinematic components, features very common in nearby spiral galaxies fraternali01, fraternali02 . In particular, SPT0441-46 and SPT2146-55 show faint emission in the so-called forbidden regions (i.e., forbidden for rotation) of the p-v diagrams (see the black contours in the lower left quadrants in Fig. fig:ap8 and fig:ap11 ). These features are due to lag in rotation and non-circular motions and they are usually ascribed to extraplanar gas, bars or lopsided structures fraternali06, sancisi . The inspection of the p-v diagrams and channel maps indicates that the derived kinematic parameters are robust also for these two galaxies.

For two galaxies (SPT2146-55, SPT2132-58), the _ SFR values at _ SN = 0.1 overestimate _ m by a factor of 3, indicating that low efficiency values 0.01 _ SN 0.001 are needed, similarly to what found both for high- and low- z star-forming galaxies fraternali20, bacchini .

gmw_00011274.name: J103408.58+600152.1
gmw_00011274.overview: J103408.58+600152.1 is classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1511.03263]. It is identified as a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus with a high column density [arXiv:1511.03263].

sources.summary: The paper treats J103408.58+600152.1 as a Compton-thick Seyfert source: a Brightman-Nandra fit reconciles its high FeK equivalent width with a high column density.

quotes.quote: Source 9 - J103408.58+600152.1 Because of the large EW of the FeK line, but the relatively low N_H , we fit the spectrum with the model of Brightman Nandra (2011). The result of the fit is a high column density, N_H=220^ + _ 70 , characteristic of the CT sources. Other useful values: p1/p2=0.008, cstat/dof=51.9/34, _ soft =3^ +0.2 _ 0.4 , _ hard =1.8 (fixed).

gmw_00011275.name: J121839.40+470627.6
gmw_00011275.overview: J121839.40+470627.6 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1511.03263]. It is identified as a Compton-thick source based on its high column density and a large equivalent width of the iron K line [arXiv:1511.03263].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J121839.40+470627.6 as Compton-thick after fitting its high-FeK, lower-NH spectrum with the Brightman-Nandra model and deriving a high column density.

quotes.quote: Source 16 - J121839.40+470627.6 Because of the large EW of the FeK line, but the relatively low N_H , we fit the spectrum with the model of Brightman Nandra (2011). The result of the fit is a high column density, N_H=2009^ + _ 135 , characteristic of the CT sources. Other useful values: p1/p2=0.003, cstat/dof=145/155, _ soft =3.4^ +0.8 _ 0.6 , _ hard =1.8 (fixed).

gmw_00011276.name: J135436.29+051524.5
gmw_00011276.overview: The active galactic nucleus J135436.29+051524.5 is classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1511.03263]. It exhibits an extremely flat photon index and a strong FeK line, though low-quality X-ray spectra prevent its definitive classification as a Compton-thick source [arXiv:1511.03263].

sources.summary: The paper flags J135436.29+051524.5 as ambiguous: it has a flat photon index and strong FeK line, but partial covering and low data quality prevent firm Compton-thick classification.

quotes.quote: Source 25 - J135436.29+051524.5 We chose not to include this source in the unabsorbed list because its photon index is extremely flat ( 0.8) if left as a free parameter, and in addition there seems to be a strong FeK line. It may be a reflection -dominated Compton-thick source, but we cannot confirm this because of the relatively low quality X-ray spectrum. Also, even though the EW seems high, it cannot be considered as a Compton-thick candidate because the scattered percentage is too large ( >30 ) implying partial covering instead of scattered emission.

gmw_00011277.name: J150754.38+010816.8
gmw_00011277.overview: J150754.38+010816.8 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1511.03263]. Spectral modeling of its high iron K-alpha line equivalent width reveals a high column density, identifying it as a Compton-thick source [arXiv:1511.03263].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J150754.38+010816.8 as Compton-thick because Brightman-Nandra modeling of its high-FeK, lower-NH spectrum yields a high characteristic column density.

quotes.quote: Source 29 - J150754.38+010816.8 Because of the large EW of the FeK line, but the relatively low N_H , we fit the spectrum with the model of Brightman Nandra (2011). The result of the fit is a high column density, N_H=211^ + _ 61 , characteristic of the CT sources. Other useful values: p1/p2=0.003, cstat/dof=320/355, _ soft =3.2^ +0.4 _ 0.4 , _ hard =1.8 (fixed).

gmw_00011278.name: J215649.51--074532.4
gmw_00011278.overview: J215649.51--074532.4 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1511.03263]. It is identified as a Compton-thick source due to its high column density and a large equivalent width of the FeK line [arXiv:1511.03263].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J215649.51--074532.4 as Compton-thick, using Brightman-Nandra modeling to infer a high column density despite lower initial NH and high FeK equivalent width.

quotes.quote: Source 30 - J215649.51--074532.4 Because of the large EW of the FeK line, but the relatively low N_H , we fit the spectrum with the model of Brightman Nandra (2011). The result of the fit is a high column density, N_H=1500^ + _ 1200 , which is characteristic of the CT sources. Other useful values: p1/p2=0.003, cstat/dof=265/3, _ soft =3.5^ +0.5 _ 0.5 , _ hard =1.8 (fixed).

gmw_00011279.name: host of SN 2008es
gmw_00011279.overview: The host galaxy of SN 2008es is characterized as a blue, low-metallicity galaxy [arXiv:1807.07859]. It has negligible host-galaxy extinction and is modeled with a young stellar population estimated at 200 million years of age [arXiv:1807.07859].

sources.summary: The paper models the host of SN 2008es as a blue, low-metallicity galaxy with negligible extinction and a best-fit young stellar population used for host subtraction.

quotes.quote: Host-galaxy extinction was assumed to be negligible because the host of SN 2008es is blue and has low metallicity Angus2016,Schulze2016 , and then host subtraction was performed via adopting a host-galaxy model from http://www.stsci.edu/science/starburst99/docs/default.htm Starburst99 Leitherer1999.starburst99,Leitherer2010.starburst99,Vazquez2005.starburst99,Leitherer2014.starburst99 . These templates, which we believe to be a good representative for the host of SN 2008es, are simulated for an instantaneous burst of star formation given the initial mass function with power-law index 2.35 over the range 1--100 , and nebular emission included. The templates include metallicity 0.001--0.04 and age 1--900 Myr.

The best galaxy model was selected by fitting the measured BgR/F160W emission of the host of SN 2008es from and , as shown in table-hostemission . We note that the host images in the bands BR and F160W , which is equivalent to the H band, were taken at phase 1700 days, much later than the last H data presented in fig-photo . We assume that there is no SN contamination at 1700 days. The best-fit galaxy, determined by the lowest summed squared residuals, has metallicity 0.001 and an age of 200 million years, which is consistent with the results of .

gmw_00011280.name: Leo M
gmw_00011280.overview: Leo M is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy located within the Local Group but outside the virial radius of the Milky Way [arXiv:2307.08738]. The galaxy has a low stellar mass, was quenched approximately 10.9 billion years ago, and may have historically interacted with the nearby dwarf galaxy Leo T [arXiv:2307.08738].

sources.summary: The paper reports Leo M as an ultra-faint Local Group dwarf outside the Milky Way virial radius, with low stellar mass, faint luminosity, early quenching, and possible past interaction with Leo T.

quotes.quote: Discovery and Characterization of Two Ultra Faint-Dwarfs Outside the Halo of the Milky Way: Leo M and Leo K 0000-0001-5538-2614 Kristen. B. W. McQuinn Department of Physics and Astronomy, Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey, 136 Frelinghuysen Rd, Piscataway, NJ 08854, USA kristen.mcquinn@rutgers.edu 0000-0002-1200-0820 Yao-Yuan Mao Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of Utah, 115 South 1400 East, Salt Lake City, UT 84112, USA 0000-0002-2970-7435 Roger E. Cohen Department of Physics and Astronomy, Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey, 136 Frelinghuysen Rd, Piscataway, NJ 08854, USA 0000-0003-3408-3871 David Shih Department of Physics and Astronomy, Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey, 136 Frelinghuysen Rd, Piscataway, NJ 08854, USA 0000-0003-1109-3460 Matthew R. Buckley Department of Physics and Astronomy, Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey, 136 Frelinghuysen Rd, Piscataway, NJ 08854, USA 0000-0001-8416-4093 Andrew E. Dolphin RTX, 1151 E. Hermans Road, Tucson, AZ 85756, USA University of Arizona, Steward Observatory, 933 North Cherry Avenue, Tucson, AZ 85721, USA We report the discovery of two ultra-faint dwarf galaxies, \ and , that lie outside the halo of the Milky Way. Using Hubble Space Telescope imaging of the resolved stars, we create color-magnitude diagrams reaching the old main sequence turn-off of each system and (i) fit for structural parameters of the galaxies; (ii) measure their distances using the luminosity of the Horizontal Branch stars; (iii) estimate integrated magnitudes and stellar masses; and (iv) reconstruct the star formation histories. Based on their location in the Local Group, neither galaxy is currently a satellite of the Milky Way, although \ is located 22 kpc from the low-mass galaxy Leo T and these two systems may have had a past interaction. \ and \ have stellar masses of 1.5 0.2 10^4 \ and 1.0 0.2 10^4 , and were quenched 10.9^ +1.8 _ 0.6 Gyr and 12.6^ +0.2 _ 5.8 Gyr ago, respectively.

Summary of Properties \ and \ have very low stellar masses ( \ =1.5 0.2 10^4 ; 1.0 0.2 10^4 ), and are very faint ( M_V=-5.3^ +0.3 _ 0.4 ; 4.7^ +0.7 _ 0.4 mag). Both galaxies are located outside the virial radius of the MW at 457 ^ +87 _ 18 and 428 ^ +28 _ 109 kpc, respectively, based on the luminosity of their HB stars (Figures fig:cmds fig:SG ). Their integrated V- band magnitudes and half-light radii are consistent with the properties of other local low-mass galaxies, although both are somewhat more compact than the majority of known systems (Figure fig:Mv_rh ). Based on our analysis, \ and \ are ultra faint dwarfs located within the Local Group but are not currently satellites of a more massive host.

gmw_00011281.name: Leo K
gmw_00011281.overview: Leo K is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy located within the Local Group but outside the virial radius of the Milky Way [arXiv:2307.08738]. It has a low stellar mass of approximately 10,000 solar masses and was quenched about 12.6 billion years ago [arXiv:2307.08738].

sources.summary: The paper reports Leo K as an ultra-faint Local Group dwarf outside the Milky Way virial radius, with low stellar mass, faint luminosity, and an early quenching time inferred from HST data.

gmw_00011282.name: PKS 1622-253
gmw_00011282.overview: PKS 1622-253 is a classical triple radio galaxy classified as an under-luminous quasar or blazar [arXiv:astro-ph/0509832]. The system features Fanaroff-Riley class II radio lobes and launches a superluminal jet with a high kinetic luminosity [arXiv:astro-ph/0509832].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1622-253 as an under-luminous quasar/blazar with a powerful superluminal jet, FRII radio lobes, and jet kinetic power comparable to its accretion luminosity.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we discuss new deep radio observations of PKS 1622-253 and their implications for the energetics of the central engine that powers this strong high energy gamma-ray source. Combining archival infrared and optical measurements with new millimeter observations, we show that even though the accretion flow in PKS 1622-253 is under-luminous by quasar standards, a powerful super-luminal jet is launched with a higher kinetic luminosity than most EGRET blazars. Only a few percent of the total jet kinetic luminosity is required to power even the most powerful gamma ray flares that are observed.

We performed deep radio observations of PKS 1622-253 with the VLA and also extricated the intrinsic IR/optical spectrum from the attenuation due to the Ophiuchus cloud. Our conclusion is that this source is a classical triple radio galaxy with a powerful superluminal radio core and powerful FRII radio lobes. The optical/near UV emission is dominated by the high frequency tail of a very broad synchrotron spectrum associated with the powerful radio core.

gmw_00011283.name: H79b
gmw_00011283.overview: H79b is a galaxy located in the Seyfert's Sextet group that is classified as a minor merger [arXiv:1204.0367]. It displays evidence of nuclear activity at radio, mid-infrared, and optical wavelengths, and it is associated with a very low luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1204.0367].

sources.summary: The paper describes H79b as a minor-merger galaxy in Seyfert's Sextet with possible weak nuclear X-ray activity and an eastern count concentration dominating detected group emission.

quotes.quote: H79a and H79b show evidence for nuclear activity at radio, MIR and optical wavelengths with H79a a bone fide AGN and H79b a minor merger (D08); the X-ray sources associated with these two galaxies, even if coincident with the nuclear position, are at best very low luminosity AGN.
This suggests that the diffuse emission in HCG 79 involves two separate components: 1) the region of galaxy H79a and 2) the region of galaxy H79b and its surroundings, of about 100 counts each.

Most of the detected counts appear to be associated with individual galaxies (primarily H79a to the W and H79b and its surroundings to the E, cf. Table tab:1 and Fig. fig:2 left) and therefore it is unlikely that we have detected ``bona-fide'' diffuse group emission.

gmw_00011284.name: H79d
gmw_00011284.overview: H79d is a galaxy associated with the Hickson Compact Group 79 [arXiv:1204.0367]. It contains an associated X-ray point source and is coincident with a substantial neutral hydrogen mass of at least 2 billion solar masses [arXiv:1204.0367].

sources.summary: The paper includes H79d in the Sextet spectral extraction, associates an X-ray point source with it, and notes substantial H I mass coincident with the galaxy.

quotes.quote: We analyzed the spectral data of the Sextet in a slightly smaller region than described above, with inner and outer radii of 40 '' and 70 '' to obtain a better S/N (see the region showed in Fig. fig:5 ); moreover, in order to include H79d as well, we added a box that contains this galaxy rather than expanding the wedge to a larger radius which would add a large portion of the annulus with no emission.

We also considered the counts of the X-ray point sources associated to the optical galaxies H79a, H79b and H79d (which contribute 137 net counts), but we have excluded the point sources unrelated to HCG 79.
The total HI mass (4.3 10 ^9 M _ , borth ) associated with the group is typical of a single spiral galaxy, and at least 2 10 ^9 M _ is coincident with H79d (D08).

gmw_00011285.name: H79e
gmw_00011285.overview: H79e is a background galaxy that appears projected within the boundaries of a galaxy group [arXiv:1204.0367]. A non-nuclear X-ray source is located near the galaxy, which may also host a moderate starburst [arXiv:1204.0367].

sources.summary: The paper identifies H79e as a projected background galaxy, separates a nearby non-nuclear X-ray source from it, and treats its possible luminosity as contamination.

quotes.quote: A background galaxy H79e appears projected within the boundaries of the group ( v_r 19809 \ km \ s^ 1 ).
Background source 10 in Table tab:100 is located near H79e but it does not coincide with the galaxy nucleus.

The presence of the background galaxy H79e represents an additional source of noise to our analysis, since in spite of it being further away, it could still contribute: for instance a reasonable luminosity of 10^ 40 \ would contribute 10 net counts, and a moderate starburst could give a higher contribution.

gmw_00011286.name: NGC 684
gmw_00011286.overview: NGC 684 is classified as an untruncated disk galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0609384]. Photometric observations show no detected disk truncation out to 3.5 radial scale-lengths down to a limit of J < 23 mag/arcsec^2 [arXiv:astro-ph/0609384].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 684 as untruncated within the observations, reporting no detected disk truncation down to J < 23 mag/arcsec ^2 and out to 3.5 radial scale-lengths.

quotes.quote: b) Untruncated galaxies: NGC 684, UGC 4906, NGC 2654 and NGC 6835. Of course we cannot assure that these are not truncated, but only state that no truncation was observed for face-on values of J < 23 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 684, for J < 24 mag/arcsec ^2 in UGC 4906, for J < 25 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 2654 or for J < 22 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 6835. We refer to the J filter limits since in these galaxies the J band data covers a more extended region in the object, as can be seen in Fig. 4. In consequence we did not detect truncations out to 3.5 radial scale-lengths for NGC 684, 5.0 for UGC 4906, 5.9 for NGC 2654 or 3.3 for NGC 6835.

gmw_00011287.name: UGC 4906
gmw_00011287.overview: UGC 4906 is classified as an untruncated galaxy based on near-infrared observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0609384]. No disk truncation has been detected in the galaxy down to a J-band surface brightness of 24 magnitudes per square arcsecond, extending out to 5.0 radial scale-lengths [arXiv:astro-ph/0609384].

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 4906 as untruncated within the observations, reporting no detected disk truncation down to J < 24 mag/arcsec ^2 and out to 5.0 radial scale-lengths.

quotes.quote: b) Untruncated galaxies: NGC 684, UGC 4906, NGC 2654 and NGC 6835. Of course we cannot assure that these are not truncated, but only state that no truncation was observed for face-on values of J < 23 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 684, for J < 24 mag/arcsec ^2 in UGC 4906, for J < 25 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 2654 or for J < 22 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 6835. We refer to the J filter limits since in these galaxies the J band data covers a more extended region in the object, as can be seen in Fig. 4. In consequence we did not detect truncations out to 3.5 radial scale-lengths for NGC 684, 5.0 for UGC 4906, 5.9 for NGC 2654 or 3.3 for NGC 6835.

gmw_00011288.name: NGC 6835
gmw_00011288.overview: NGC 6835 is classified as an untruncated galaxy based on photometric observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0609384]. No disk truncation has been detected in the galaxy down to J < 22 mag/arcsec^2 and out to 3.3 radial scale-lengths [arXiv:astro-ph/0609384].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 6835 as untruncated within the observations, reporting no detected disk truncation down to J < 22 mag/arcsec ^2 and out to 3.3 radial scale-lengths.

quotes.quote: b) Untruncated galaxies: NGC 684, UGC 4906, NGC 2654 and NGC 6835. Of course we cannot assure that these are not truncated, but only state that no truncation was observed for face-on values of J < 23 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 684, for J < 24 mag/arcsec ^2 in UGC 4906, for J < 25 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 2654 or for J < 22 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 6835. We refer to the J filter limits since in these galaxies the J band data covers a more extended region in the object, as can be seen in Fig. 4. In consequence we did not detect truncations out to 3.5 radial scale-lengths for NGC 684, 5.0 for UGC 4906, 5.9 for NGC 2654 or 3.3 for NGC 6835.

gmw_00011289.name: NGC 5981
gmw_00011289.overview: NGC 5981 is a slightly disturbed and asymmetric disk galaxy with a tilted inner disk and bulge component [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768]. It has possible dwarf elliptical and superimposed companions, as well as a nearby large elliptical galaxy, NGC 5982 [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5981 as disturbed and asymmetric, with companions, tilted disk and bulge components, and a shallower broken-exponential outer slope on the more extended side.

quotes.quote: NGC 5981: There is a possible dwarf elliptical companion visible at (R,z) (-10 ,+30 ), and another companion superimposed on the disk at (R,z) (+77 ,-5 ). For both we have no velocity information, but there is another large elliptical nearby (NGC 5982, 6.2 \ away with v 1300 ). NGC 5981 is slightly disturbed and asymmetric. The inner disk is 1.5 tilted against the outer disk and the bulge component is furthermore slightly tilted against the inner disk. This asymmetry is reflected in the radial profiles.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5981 as a repeated near-infrared truncation case whose K_s profile shows gradual decline and a continuous first derivative, with new images matching earlier data.

quotes.quote: In the previous paper by F01, the deprojected profiles of NGC 4013 (both J and K_s , both sides), NGC 4217 ( K_s , left side) and NGC 5981 ( K_s , right side) are clear examples of a gradual decline and a continuous first derivative. Notice that NGC 5981 is present in both papers (in the first one only in K _s ). The K _s images are very similar as expected, but in the first campaign better observing conditions allowed us to reach slightly more distant radii.

gmw_00011290.name: HCG 2a
gmw_00011290.overview: HCG 2a is a late-type barred spiral galaxy that exhibits a highly disturbed velocity field [arXiv:0908.2798]. The galaxy features a strong bar that causes kinematic misalignment, asymmetric rotation, and H II regions located within its arms and bar [arXiv:0908.2798].

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 2a as a late-type barred galaxy with a disturbed velocity field, strong bar-driven kinematic misalignment, asymmetric rotation, and HII regions in its arms and bar.

quotes.quote: HCG 2a shows a very disturbed velocity field as can be seen in Fig. maps_hcg2ab . No symmetry is observed with respect to the major axis and moreover one cannot see a constant velocity along the minor axis. The PA of the velocity field changes rapidly, by about 90 ^ from the North to the South of the galaxy. The velocity field shows the clear signature of a strong bar, almost vertical, which is responsible for the PA variation and for the misalignment between the morphological and kinematic orientations. There is a misalignment of 30 ^ between the bar and the kinematic minor axis.

gmw_00011291.name: HCG 2b
gmw_00011291.overview: HCG 2b is a galaxy that exhibits a disk-like rotation curve with a regular velocity field [arXiv:0908.2798]. It displays central hydrogen emission, a central velocity dispersion of 47 km/s, and two distinct bumps on the approaching side of its rotation curve [arXiv:0908.2798].

sources.summary: The paper reports central H emission in HCG 2b, a mostly regular velocity field, central velocity dispersion enhancement, disk-like rotation, and inner rotation-curve bumps.

quotes.quote: In the case of HCG 2b, we detected H emission at its center (within 2.8 kpc, also reported in Vilchez et al. 1998). The velocity field (Fig. maps_hcg2ab ) seems to be regular. The kinematic position angle differs from the morphological one (Mendes de Oliveira et al. 1992) by 20 ^ at 2 h ^ 1 kpc, however, at R _ 25 , the optical position angle is in good agreement with the kinematic one. We noticed that the continuum and monochromatic images peak at the same position. The galaxy center displays a velocity dispersion of 47 km s ^ 1 while, in the outskirts the value is 34 km s ^ 1 .

The rotation curve of HCG 2b (Fig. hcg22c b) shows a disk-like rotator shape. For the adopted systemic velocity, both sides match at radii larger than 2 , however, in the inner part of the rotation curve, (inside 2 ) the approaching side shows a plateau and the two sides do not match. We detected two bumps in the approaching side of the rotation curve, placed between 0 and 4 and 4 and 7 , respectively.

gmw_00011292.name: HCG 2c
gmw_00011292.overview: HCG 2c is a spiral galaxy featuring a regular velocity field and a suspected central bar [arXiv:0908.2798]. It displays a relatively symmetric rotation curve, a tidal arm, and several bright H II regions located in its center, bar, and northern arm [arXiv:0908.2798].

sources.summary: The paper finds HCG 2c has a regular velocity field, suspected central bar, bright HII regions, a tidal arm, and a relatively symmetric rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The velocity field of HCG 2c is regular (see Fig. maps_hcg2c ). A signature of the bar is suspected in the central regions. The PA is almost constant along the major axis, staying around a value of 168 ^ . The monochromatic map shows several bright HII regions in the bar, in the center of the galaxy and across the northern arm. A tidal arm is identified in the monochromatic map, in a similar way to UGC 7861, as was reported by Epinat el al. (2008a).

There are no bright HII regions in the south side of this object. The velocity dispersion map does not reveal any peculiarities. HCG 2c displays a more symmetric rotation curve than the other two members of this group, except in the central regions ( 10 ).

gmw_00011293.name: HCG 7a
gmw_00011293.overview: HCG 7a is classified as an H II-type galaxy that exhibits hydrogen emission in several knots along its arms [arXiv:0908.2798]. Its central region displays a peak in emission that may indicate a small bar, while its velocity field shows strong asymmetries and central non-circular motions [arXiv:0908.2798].

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 7a as an HII-type galaxy with arm knots, peculiar asymmetric velocity fields, non-circular central motions, and a likely small bar associated with central emission.

quotes.quote: Shimada et al. (2000) classified the nuclear activity in HCG 7a as an HII type. HCG 7a shows H emission in several knots through the arms (see Fig. maps_hcg7ad ) as was also noted in Vilchez et al. (1998). The central region displays a peak in the monochromatic and continuum image. The velocity field of this galaxy is very peculiar, strong asymmetries around the major axis are observed on both sides of the galaxy. Moreover, in the center (inside 3 kpc), strong non-circular motions are observed with the velocities reaching high values of 290 km s ^ 1 in the rotation curve (see Fig. hcg22c d).

The peak in the inner part corresponds to a region of high monochromatic emission and might be the result of a small bar, associated with the HII regions in the central parts. However, there is no previous mention of a stellar bar present for this galaxy. Both stellar and gaseous major axes appear to be aligned, probably due to the high inclination of the galaxy.

gmw_00011294.name: HCG 7d
gmw_00011294.overview: HCG 7d is a barred spiral galaxy classified as SBc [arXiv:0908.2798]. It features a bar-dominated velocity field, strong H-alpha emission at the end of its bar, and uneven emission across its spiral arms [arXiv:0908.2798].

sources.summary: The paper reports that HCG 7d has strong H emission at its bar, uneven arm emission, a bar-dominated velocity field, and a regular rotation curve reaching a plateau.

quotes.quote: The SBc galaxy HCG 7d has been observed twice, the two observations are presented in Figs maps_hcg7ad and maps_hcg7bd . This galaxy shows a strong H emission at the end of its bar, visible in the HST archival image and in several knots in their southwest arm. In the northeast side of this object, the intensity of H emission is lower than that in the south-west side. The monochromatic map of HCG 7d shown in Fig. maps_hcg7bd displays emission in the east and west side of the galaxy, which is not detected in the monochromatic image of Fig. maps_hcg7ad neither in the -band image.

The velocity field of HCG 7d clearly shows the presence of the bar (see Figure maps_hcg7bd ). The kinematic PA differs in 27 ^ and 50 ^ compared to the optical PA given in Mendes de Oliveira et al. (2003) and Hyperleda, respectively. The velocity dispersion map does not show any particular features. The rotation curve is quite regular, reaching a plateau of 100 km s ^ 1 from 7 (2 kpc) to 30 (8 kpc).

gmw_00011295.name: HCG 22b
gmw_00011295.overview: HCG 22b is classified as an early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0908.2798]. It features extended shells visible in the B and R bands, a high central velocity dispersion, and weak central hydrogen emission [arXiv:0908.2798].

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 22b as an early-type spiral with weak central H emission, poorly determined uniform velocity field, high central velocity dispersion, and extended shells.

quotes.quote: The early-type spiral HCG 22b only shows a weak H emission in its central region. Therefore, the velocity field, which appears to be uniform, is poorly determined and it is not possible to obtain a rotation curve with the data in hands. HCG22b shows a high velocity dispersion equal to 48 km s ^ 1 in its center, which corresponds to the bright emission visible in the monochromatic map. HCG 22b exhibits extended shells clearly seen in the B and R-band images, as shown in Fig shellhcg22b .

gmw_00011296.name: NGC 92
aliases_local: the object in the tidal tail of NGC 92
gmw_00011296.overview: NGC 92 is an Sa spiral galaxy [arXiv:0908.2798] and a LINER [arXiv:0908.2798] located within the Phoenix compact group [arXiv:0908.2798]. The galaxy exhibits a regular disk-like rotation [arXiv:0908.2798], a strong central starburst [arXiv:0708.0076], and a 30-kiloparsec tidal tail containing a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate [arXiv:0908.2798, arXiv:astro-ph/0411405].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 92 as an Sa LINER in the Phoenix compact group with a prominent tidal tail, regular disk-like rotation, central double components, and tail emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 92 This galaxy is part of the Phoenix compact group formed by NGC 92, NGC 88 and NGC 89 and NGC 87 (Rose 1979). This group is also known as Rose 34 (Rose 1977) and SCG 0018-4854 (Iovino et al. 2002). NGC 92 and NGC 88 are LINERs (Coziol et al. 2000) while NGC 89 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy (e.g. Pompei et al. 2007). NGC 92 is classified as an Sa galaxy (Prugniel et al. 1998). It shows a prominent tidal tail in the South East direction clearly visible also in the H image of Temporin et al. (2005).

The velocity field presented in Fig. maps_s01a shows a disk-like rotation with a large radial velocity range (from 3641 km s ^ 1 to 3183 km s ^ 1 ). The position angle of the velocity field changes from 8 ^ to 28 ^ over a 40 arcsec radius. Even with this strong change in the position angle, the velocity field is fairly regular.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 92 as the brightest disturbed member of SCG0018-4854, with an extended HI tidal tail, circumnuclear star formation, and a strong starburst.

quotes.quote: All four close members show a disturbed morphology and a late type appearance: the brightest galaxy, a (=NGC 92), has an extended tidal tail, with many HII regions very prominent in H , a bright circumnuclear ring of star formation, with a pronounced knot and a strong central starburst, with a SFR, as determined from the ATCA radio continuum observations, of 14.7 \ and a SN rate of about 0.6 yr ^ 1 . The galaxy has a strong central radio continuum source, which causes the absorption hole visible in Fig. fig:scg9hi . 5 \ gas is clearly detected in its disk and along the whole length of its southern tidal tail.

Fig. fig:scgvel_0018 shows that the rotational amplitude of galaxy a encompasses the total velocity range observed in both companions detected in \ line emission, and d , and the gas bridge connecting them. The NW half of galaxy a is the receding side. The velocities of the \ gas in the tidal tail are an extension of those observed on that side of the galaxy disk, clearly suggesting a physical link between disk gas and tail.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 92 as the disturbed SCG 0018-4854 member with a 30 kpc tidal tail and an unusually flattened high-luminosity HII-region luminosity function.

quotes.quote: All members exhibit strong signs of disturbances, the most outstanding being the 30 kpc-long tidal tail of NGC 92. Here we present a preliminary analysis of the H images of the 5 galaxies. The observations were carried out in 2002 September and 2003 January at the ESO-VLT-UT4 telescope equipped with FORS2.

The LF of NGC 92 exhibit a considerable flattening at the high luminosity end. This effect is not shared by the companion galaxies, so it might be a real feature, rather than an artificial effect due to source blending. When the highest luminosity H ii regions are excluded from the fit of the LF, we obtain a slope = 1.73 0.11 consistent with the morphological type and in agreement, within errors, with the observed size distribution ( = 3.71 0.54).

sources.original_name: the object in the tidal tail of NGC 92
sources.summary: The paper identifies the object at the end of NGC 92's tidal tail as a 45 Myr-old TDG candidate detected in H and associated with the group.

quotes.quote: Our detected region at the end of the tidal tail is only 45 Myr old. Bournaud Duc (2006) performed simulations in order to relate TDGs and satellite galaxies. They found that the most massive objects located at the end of tidal tails are likely to be the TDG progenitors. Therefore, considering its location and age, we classify the object in the tidal tail of NGC 92 as a TDG candidate. We also note that this object is detected in H (Fabry Perot image) confirming its group membership.

gmw_00011297.name: Host galaxy of GRB 090424
gmw_00011297.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 090424 is a bright galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.544 [arXiv:1306.4585]. Spectra of the galaxy reveal both absorption and emission lines, including calcium, hydrogen, and oxygen features [arXiv:1306.4585].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 090424 host as a bright z=0.544 galaxy whose luminosity and host-frame extinction obscure any supernova contribution, with VLT/GTC spectra showing absorption and emission lines.

quotes.quote: The VLT observed and i band magnitudes of the host are = 22.07 0.12 and i = 21.82 0.12 , corresponding to a luminosity four times brighter than SN 1998bw at maximum light. An underlying SN akin to SN 1998bw would only have produced little additional brightening, at a level below the uncertainty in the galaxy luminosity. Additionally to the bright host galaxy, the afterglow suffers from significant host-frame extinction according to Kann10,Schady12 and Covino13 , which will further dim any SN component.

The GRB 090424 host-galaxy spectrum was obtained with VLT-FORS2 and grism 300I on 2009 May 22, and with the Gran Telescopio Canarias (GTC) on 2013 April 8, about a month and 4 years after the high energy event respectively. These spectra were reduced following standard methods. The signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) of our VLT-FORS2 spectrum is 10-20 in the blue part and goes down to 5-10 in the red part, the strong residual around 9400 is due to telluric water vapor. Two absorption lines at 6077 and 6133 and emission lines at 6704, 7508, 7733, and 10135 are clearly detected, which are identified as Ca 2 K and H, H , H , [ O 3 ] and H at z = 0.544 (see Figure GRB090424host ). The S/N of our GTC spectrum is 5-10 , it extends the VLT-FORS2 spectrum to the blue-ward, and an extra strong emission line at 5758 is detected, it is identified as [ O 2 ] at z = 0.544 (see Figure GRB090424host ).

gmw_00011298.name: NGC 4070
gmw_00011298.overview: NGC 4070 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0711.4548] that exhibits deviations from a perfectly ellipsoidal shape [arXiv:0711.4548]. This distortion, along with a faint bridge of luminous matter connecting it to NGC 4066, indicates a fairly recent galaxy interaction [arXiv:0711.4548].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4070 as SN 2005bl's elliptical host, with host extinction, likely internal dust, shape deviations, and a faint bridge to NGC 4066.

quotes.quote: The SN is located in the elliptical galaxy NGC 4070 (de Vaucouleurs morphological type 4.9 ; LEDA Lyon-Meudon Extragalactic Database http:/ /leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ ), projected on a region of steep yet smooth background variation (Fig. fig:chart ). Details on the SN and host galaxy properties are summarised in Table properties .

In NGC 4070 no such dust lane is visible even in deep images, but SN 2005bl showed signs of extinction within its host galaxy, the most prominent being a narrow interstellar Na i D line in the spectra at the redshift of the host, with an equivalent width (EW) of 2.6 0.3 . Since elliptical galaxies lack H ii regions with strong emission lines, their surface colours are good tracers of the internal dust distribution Patil07 . Hence, to investigate the dust content in NGC 4070, we constructed a B-R image of the galaxy from the templates obtained with CAFOS on UT 2006 March 28, following largely the prescription of Patil07 : after the usual pre-reduction, the B and R band images were spatially aligned, and the sky background was subtracted.

Deep images of NGC 4070 reveal some deviation from a perfectly spherical or ellipsoidal shape (Fig. fig:long_exp ). This is an indication of fairly recent interaction, either with the galaxy 2MASX J12040831+2023280 about 1.3 arcmin to its south-west (labelled `A' in Fig. fig:long_exp ), or with a small knot about 0.8 arcmin to its south (labelled `B', and most easily seen as irregularly-shaped bright patch in the lower part of Fig. fig:BminR ). Furthermore, in Fig. fig:long_exp a faint, broad bridge of luminous matter between NGC 4070 and its equally massive elliptical neighbour galaxy NGC 4066 can be detected.

gmw_00011299.name: SDSS J003413.34 + 024832.9
gmw_00011299.overview: SDSS J003413.34 + 024832.9 is an emission-line host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0603 [arXiv:1709.08386]. It features a bright H II region and has a subsolar oxygen abundance of approximately 40 percent of the solar value [arXiv:1709.08386].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J003413.34 + 024832.9 as PTF11mnb's host, measuring its redshift, distance, low extinction, integrated magnitudes, and subsolar oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of PTF11mnb is named SDSS J003413.34 + 024832.9. We determined its redshift to be z = 0.0603 0.0001 from the Gaussian fits of the H and [ O iii ] 5007 host-galaxy emission lines superimposed on the SN spectra (see Sect. sec:spec ), as well as from the host-galaxy spectrum obtained in 2016 (see Fig. fc ). This redshift corresponds to a luminosity distance D_L = 268.5 Mpc and distance modulus = 37.14 mag when WMAP 5-years cosmological parameters komatsu09 are assumed. Given the absence of any narrow Na i D absorption line at the host-galaxy redshift, we assume that no host extinction affected PTF11mnb.

SDSS J003413.34 + 024832.9 has integrated magnitudes of M_g = -17.8 mag, M_r = -18.0 mag, M_i = -18.3 mag. Based on these absolute magnitudes, a global metallicity of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.29 ( Z/Z_ = 0.4 , where Z_ =8.69 , asplund09 ) is inferred from the luminosity-color-metallicity relation by sanders13 , or Z/Z_ =0.25 if we make use of the luminosity-metallicity relation by arcavi10 . Since PTF11mnb sits on a bright H ii region, we could measure the host emission-line fluxes at the exact SN position from its spectra. Using the high signal-to-noise Keck spectrum, we derived the line ratios needed for the O3N2 method by pp04 , which resulted in a metallicity of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.29 0.20 ( Z/Z_ = 0.40 ).

gmw_00011300.name: B0847+548
gmw_00011300.overview: B0847+548 is a quasar that has transitioned from a radio-loud to a radio-quiet state [arXiv:1010.0651]. Its large-scale radio lobes are no longer fueled by the nucleus, while high-resolution observations reveal two-sided mini-jets characteristic of radio-quiet quasars [arXiv:1010.0651].

sources.summary: The paper describes B0847+548 as a quasar recently transitioning from radio-loud to radio-quiet behavior, with relic large-scale lobes and EVN morphology showing two-sided mini-jets.

quotes.quote: The quasars we investigated have large-scale radio lobes that are clearly asymmetric -- one lobe is of Fanaroff-Riley II type, while the other one is a diffuse relic devoid of a hotspot. We suggest that the prime cause of the asymmetry of these radio sources is that the nuclei of their host galaxies currently produce no jets. To prove that, we observed them with milliarcsecond resolution to check if they are similar to those in radio-quiet quasars. The observations carried out with the EVN revealed that the nuclei of the quasars under investigation are not of a core-jet type that is characteristic for radio-loud, lobe-dominated quasars. It follows that the lobes are no longer fuelled and that the apparent asymmetry results from the orientation, which causes a time lag of the order of 10 ^6 years between their images: the lobe perceived as a relic is nearer than the lobe with a hotspot and so it is observed in a later stage of the decay.

Our findings are fully consistent with those by Ulvestad2005 both in terms of brightness temperatures and milliarcsecond-scale morphologies: B1351+267 is point-like, J115139.6+335541 is nearly point-like with two protrusions on the eastern and western side, and B0847+548 has two-sided mini-jets. All three have low radio flux densities and yet high brightness temperatures. Altogether, the galactic nuclei we imaged with the EVN are characteristic of RQQs.

gmw_00011301.name: J115139.6+335541
gmw_00011301.overview: J115139.6+335541 is a quasar that has recently transitioned from a radio-loud to a radio-quiet state [arXiv:1010.0651]. Its nucleus is no longer producing jets, leaving asymmetric, unfueled large-scale radio lobes [arXiv:1010.0651].

sources.summary: The paper describes J115139.6+335541 as a quasar recently transitioning from radio-loud to radio-quiet behavior, with relic large-scale lobes and EVN morphology showing a nearly point-like core with two protrusions.

quotes.quote: The quasars we investigated have large-scale radio lobes that are clearly asymmetric -- one lobe is of Fanaroff-Riley II type, while the other one is a diffuse relic devoid of a hotspot. We suggest that the prime cause of the asymmetry of these radio sources is that the nuclei of their host galaxies currently produce no jets. To prove that, we observed them with milliarcsecond resolution to check if they are similar to those in radio-quiet quasars. The observations carried out with the EVN revealed that the nuclei of the quasars under investigation are not of a core-jet type that is characteristic for radio-loud, lobe-dominated quasars. It follows that the lobes are no longer fuelled and that the apparent asymmetry results from the orientation, which causes a time lag of the order of 10 ^6 years between their images: the lobe perceived as a relic is nearer than the lobe with a hotspot and so it is observed in a later stage of the decay.

Our findings are fully consistent with those by Ulvestad2005 both in terms of brightness temperatures and milliarcsecond-scale morphologies: B1351+267 is point-like, J115139.6+335541 is nearly point-like with two protrusions on the eastern and western side, and B0847+548 has two-sided mini-jets. All three have low radio flux densities and yet high brightness temperatures. Altogether, the galactic nuclei we imaged with the EVN are characteristic of RQQs.

gmw_00011302.name: B1351+267
gmw_00011302.overview: B1351+267 is a quasar that has recently transitioned from a radio-loud to a radio-quiet state [arXiv:1010.0651]. Its host galaxy nucleus currently produces no jets, leaving asymmetric, large-scale radio lobes that are no longer fueled [arXiv:1010.0651].

sources.summary: The paper describes B1351+267 as a quasar recently transitioning from radio-loud to radio-quiet behavior, with relic large-scale lobes and EVN morphology showing a point-like core.

quotes.quote: The quasars we investigated have large-scale radio lobes that are clearly asymmetric -- one lobe is of Fanaroff-Riley II type, while the other one is a diffuse relic devoid of a hotspot. We suggest that the prime cause of the asymmetry of these radio sources is that the nuclei of their host galaxies currently produce no jets. To prove that, we observed them with milliarcsecond resolution to check if they are similar to those in radio-quiet quasars. The observations carried out with the EVN revealed that the nuclei of the quasars under investigation are not of a core-jet type that is characteristic for radio-loud, lobe-dominated quasars. It follows that the lobes are no longer fuelled and that the apparent asymmetry results from the orientation, which causes a time lag of the order of 10 ^6 years between their images: the lobe perceived as a relic is nearer than the lobe with a hotspot and so it is observed in a later stage of the decay.

Our findings are fully consistent with those by Ulvestad2005 both in terms of brightness temperatures and milliarcsecond-scale morphologies: B1351+267 is point-like, J115139.6+335541 is nearly point-like with two protrusions on the eastern and western side, and B0847+548 has two-sided mini-jets. All three have low radio flux densities and yet high brightness temperatures. Altogether, the galactic nuclei we imaged with the EVN are characteristic of RQQs.

gmw_00011303.name: MAGIC J2001+439
gmw_00011303.overview: MAGIC J2001+439 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar detected at very high energies [arXiv:2012.05176, arXiv:1606.06431]. Its host galaxy has a spectroscopic redshift of z = 0.174 [arXiv:2012.05176] and exhibits a correlated decreasing trend in its radio and optical emission [arXiv:1606.06431].

sources.summary: The paper measures a z=0.174 spectroscopic redshift during an optical low state, using absorption features and NII emission from the host.

quotes.quote: MAGIC J2001 + 435 is a BL Lac Bas09 detected at TeV energies Ale14b . The host galaxy has been detected in deep imaging secured during low blazar activity. The host magnitude was measured to be I=17.15 0.06 which was translated into a photometric redshift z = 0.18 0.04 Ale14b . A high signal to noise (S/N 150-200) optical Keck/LRIS spectrum was taken by Shaw13 on August 18 2009 but no spectral features could be detected.

In the final spectrum we estimate a redshift z =0.174 from the detection of the CaHK, Mgb, CaFe and possibly, NaID absorption features (Fig. fig_spec2 , fourth row, right). At a 4.6 level we also detect the [ NII ] 6583 emission line at the same redshift. The result is consistent with the imaging estimate in Ale14b at less than 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes MAGIC J2001+439 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: MAGIC J2001+439 : The common decreasing trend and significant correlation between the radio and optical bands found in Alek14b are confirmed by our results using slightly more data (see Fig. Fig:lc29 ). The flat DCF curve, with many subsequent data points above 3 limit, suggests that the correlation is due to common decreasing trend. Also the polynomial subtraction suggests that at least 50 of the optical flux would originate from common radio-optical emission region.

gmw_00011304.name: MIPS464
gmw_00011304.overview: MIPS464 is a deeply obscured ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0907.5377]. The galaxy exhibits convincing hydrogenated amorphous carbon absorption, signs of 4.67 micron carbon monoxide absorption, and a high silicate optical depth [arXiv:0907.5377].

sources.summary: The paper treats MIPS464 as a deeply obscured ULIRG with convincing HAC absorption, signs of 4.67 micron CO absorption, and a silicate optical depth consistent with earlier estimates.

quotes.quote: For MIPS464, MIPS22303, and MIPS22633, the observed features are of a redshift consistent with the silicate feature and sufficiently strong in themselves to make the feature identification convincing.
Of these, three sources show signs of absorption in this regime: MIPS464, MIPS16059, and MIPS22432 (see Figure fig_specs ).
For example, for MIPS464 we derived model _ 9.7 = 4.9 1.6 which translates to observed _ 9.7,obs = 3.4 1.1.

gmw_00011305.name: MIPS16059
gmw_00011305.overview: MIPS16059 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0907.5377]. It has a secure redshift confirmed by Keck rest-frame optical spectra, which is consistent with its observed silicate and 3.4 micron features [arXiv:0907.5377].

sources.summary: MIPS16059 has a secure Keck redshift, a possible 3 micron absorption feature treated as an upper limit, and silicate and 3.4 micron features consistent with that redshift.

quotes.quote: As discussed previously, the reliability of weakly detected features is complicated by the frequently uncertain redshifts (except for MIPS16059, MIPS16080, and MIPS22530 which sources have Keck rest-frame optical spectra).
The 3.0 \ absorption feature in MIPS16059 is at somewhat lower wavelengths than expected for water ice and therefore we treat this as an upper limit only and not as a detection.
For MIPS16059, the redshift is based on Keck spectra, but is also consistent with the silicate and 3.4 \ features.

gmw_00011306.name: MIPS16080
gmw_00011306.overview: MIPS16080 is a composite ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0907.5377]. It exhibits starburst-like optical line ratios alongside an active galactic nucleus-like infrared spectrum, with both components contributing comparable power [arXiv:0907.5377].

sources.summary: MIPS16080 is described as a composite ULIRG with tentative 3.3 and 6.2 micron PAH features, starburst-like optical ratios, AGN-like infrared spectrum, and comparable AGN and starburst power.

quotes.quote: Overall, the most significant deviation is observed for MIPS16080, MIPS22303 and MIPS22633 all of which show an excess at 3.3 \ in addition to the likely water ice and/or 3.4 \ HAC absorption.
MIPS16080, whose 3.3 \ PAH feature is too weak to be considered a detection, does show a tentative detection of the 6.2 \ feature.
In addition, MIPS16080 has starburst-like optical line ratios, but AGN-line IR spectrum suggesting it is a composite source sajina08 .

gmw_00011307.name: MIPS22303
gmw_00011307.overview: MIPS22303 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0907.5377] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0907.5377]. Its spectrum displays reliable absorption features [arXiv:0907.5377] and a 3.3 micron excess well described by a combination of water ice and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons [arXiv:0907.5377].

sources.summary: The paper finds MIPS22303 has reliable absorption features and a 3.3 micron excess well described by water ice plus PAH without requiring HAC absorption.

quotes.quote: For MIPS464, MIPS22303, and MIPS22633, the observed features are of a redshift consistent with the silicate feature and sufficiently strong in themselves to make the feature identification convincing.
For MIPS22303 we find an excellent local analog in the ISO SWS spectrum of the Galactic Center source GC Ring NE (see Figure fig_mips22303 ).
This comparison suggests, that MIPS22303 is well described by a combination water ice+3.3 \ PAH without any clear need for HAC absorption (as shown in Figure hac_det ).

gmw_00011308.name: MIPS22530
gmw_00011308.overview: MIPS22530 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy characterized as a starburst-dominated source [arXiv:0907.5377]. The galaxy exhibits a flat spectrum and displays unambiguous 3.3 micron polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission [arXiv:0907.5377].

sources.summary: MIPS22530 is the paper's strongest PAH, starburst-dominated ULIRG, with unambiguous 3.3 micron PAH emission and starburst-dominated criteria satisfied.

quotes.quote: MIPS22530 is our strongest-PAH, starburst-dominated source and it has the flattest spectrum with = 1.9.
MIPS22530, on the other hand, shows an unambiguous 3.3 \ PAH feature (see Figure fig_specs ).
On both counts, MIPS22530 satisfies the starburst-dominated ULIRG criteria, consistent with our prior knowledge of this source.

gmw_00011309.name: MIPS22432
gmw_00011309.overview: MIPS22432 is a bright infrared galaxy with a flux density of 0.5 mJy at 8 microns [arXiv:0907.5377]. It is classified as a strong-PAH source that exhibits potential carbon monoxide absorption features [arXiv:0907.5377].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MIPS22432 as a bright strong-PAH source with a PAH-based redshift and possible CO absorption complexity relative to simple starburst corrections.

quotes.quote: One of our targets, MIPS22432 is very bright ( F_8 = 0.5 mJy), and was already observed in SL1 as part of our GO2 program.
For MIPS22432, the only strong-PAH source of the three, the redshift is based on the 6.2 \ PAH feature.

The fact that one of our two strong-PAH sources (MIPS22432) is also one of three sources showing some hints of CO absorption, suggests that this is much more complicated that a simple correction scaled to the starburst strength (as revealed by the PAH).

gmw_00011310.name: NGC 4237
gmw_00011310.overview: NGC 4237 is an unbarred spiral galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2308.03913]. The galaxy hosts a nuclear star cluster with an effective radius of 0.08 arcseconds [arXiv:2308.03913].

sources.summary: For NGC 4237, the paper compares its nuclear star cluster to earlier HST work, finding a similar effective radius but a fainter F814W magnitude.

quotes.quote: NGC 4237 - This unbarred Virgo Cluster spiral galaxy has been previously studied in georgiev2014 .
From our work, the NSC in this galaxy has an effective radius of 0 08 , with an F814W magnitude of 18.37.
So while the effective radii are similar, our fit to the NSC is considerably fainter than georgiev2014 .

gmw_00011311.name: NGC 4612
gmw_00011311.overview: NGC 4612 is a barred lenticular galaxy located within the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2308.03913]. Its galaxy nucleus hosts a two-component nuclear star cluster, which is structured as a compact Seersic component surrounded by a larger exponential component [arXiv:2308.03913].

sources.summary: For NGC 4612, the paper finds a two-component nuclear star cluster model and an F475W magnitude agreeing well with earlier ACS Virgo Cluster Survey work.

quotes.quote: NGC 4612 - This barred S0 Virgo Cluster galaxy was previously studied in cote2006 and spengler2017 as part of the ACS Virgo Cluster Survey.
In our model, we find that the NSC is best fit by a two component model, a compact S ersic surrounded by a larger exponential with a combined effective radius of 0.14 .
We can directly compare the F475W magnitudes of the sources; they find 17.73 (after conversion to Vega magnitudes), while our combined NSC has a magnitude of 17.66, thus these agree quite well.

gmw_00011312.name: NGC 7513
gmw_00011312.overview: NGC 7513 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2308.03913]. It hosts a large, bright nuclear star cluster at its center with an effective radius of approximately 43.54 parsecs [arXiv:2308.03913].

sources.summary: For NGC 7513, the paper finds a much larger, brighter nuclear star cluster than earlier modeling and attributes the discrepancy to galaxy-background treatment.

quotes.quote: NGC 7513 - This barred galaxy was previously studied in carollo2002 .

We find a much bigger and brighter NSC component in this galaxy, with an effective radius of 0.45 (43.54 pc) and an F160W magnitude of 15.66; an unresolved point source or compact component provides a much worse fit to the nuclear regions.
The carollo2002 fits did not model the galaxy background at all, and we suspect that this methodological difference may be responsible for this discrepancy.

gmw_00011313.name: SDSS J004150.75-091824.3
gmw_00011313.overview: SDSS J004150.75-091824.3 is a background quasar located at a redshift of z=1.560 [arXiv:2012.10774]. In optical and X-ray observations, it appears as a point-like source and is not associated with the Abell 85 cluster [arXiv:2012.10774].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies SDSS J004150.75-091824.3 as a background quasar at z=1.560 rather than a third black hole associated with Abell 85.

quotes.quote: Likewise, SDSS J004150.75-091824.3 was postulated to be ``a third" SMBH associated with the BCG. In the optical and X-rays, SDSS J004150.75-091824.3 is a point-like source located 14 away from the nucleus of the Abell 85 BCG. A new spectrum of SDSS J004150.75-091824.3, obtained with the 10.4-m Gran Telescopio Canarias, reveals that this source is a background quasar at a redshift of z=1.560 0.003 and not associated in any way with the Abell 85 cluster.

gmw_00011314.name: S3
gmw_00011314.overview: S3 is an ultra-massive, optically dark galaxy at a redshift of approximately 5.5 [arXiv:2309.02492]. It exhibits an elongated disk morphology and undergoes highly dust-obscured star formation, with no evidence of a strong type I active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2309.02492].

sources.summary: The paper identifies S3 as an ultra-massive, optically dark z~5.5 galaxy with heavy dust-obscured star formation and an elongated disk morphology rather than strong type-I AGN contamination.

quotes.quote: Based on the deep JWST observations, we find that the three galaxies have extremely red properties (F182M F444W > 3.5 mag; Fig. fig1 , Extended Data Fig. Extended_color_mag ), due to their significant dust attenuation ( A_ V >3 mag). Self-consistently, we find that they also have highly dust-obscured star formation rates (SFR _ IR ), which are obtained from far-infrared SED fits with CIGALE , specifically 625 31 M_ yr ^ 1 for S1, 1030 _ 150 ^ +190 M _ yr ^ 1 for S2, and 1137 31 M _ yr ^ 1 for S3 (see Methods). This indicates that they are in the process of very efficient stellar mass build-up.

Specifically, we note that S3 shows a relatively broader apparent line width compared to S1 and S2. However, the intrinsic v_ FWHM, H of S3 is only found to be about 75 km s ^ 1 . Its broad appearance in the 1D spectra is mainly due to the coincidence of alignment between the dispersion direction of the F444W grism and the elongated disk morphology of S3. Furthermore, if S3 has a strong type I AGN, we should see the H line to be significantly wider than the [ NII ] line, but is not actually the case.

gmw_00011315.name: J0405-3648
gmw_00011315.overview: J0405-3648 is an optical galaxy with an extended central star-forming region [arXiv:1904.01615]. It has a typical stellar mass of approximately 10^5.3 solar masses, a less dominant young stellar population, and deep stellar P-Cygni absorption [arXiv:1904.01615].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes J0405-3648 as an extended central star-forming target with typical stellar mass, weaker young-star dominance, no detected target nebular UV lines, and deep P-Cygni absorption.

quotes.quote: J0405-3648: The target region in this case is at the center of the optical galaxy, but is somewhat more extended than the other targets. The measured stellar mass of this object at 10^ 5.3 0.2 M_ is fairly typical for the sample, but the H equivalent width of 18 measured by Guseva2009 suggests a significantly less dominant young stellar population in this system than the other targets (spanning 26--113 , Table tab:basicopt ). The UV spectra show no detections of the target nebular lines (Figure fig:majorlines_comp ), with 3 limiting equivalent widths of <2.6 for ] and <0.3 for ] 1666. However, a P-Cygni profile is clearly visible with an absorption W_0=-1.9 , the deepest measured in the sample.

After subtracting nebular emission and interstellar absorption, the detected stellar absorption is found to vary in equivalent width from 1.9 to 0.2 , with the two deepest features occurring in J0405-3648 (-1.9 ) and HS1442+4250 (-1.6 ).

gmw_00011316.name: J1119+5130
gmw_00011316.overview: J1119+5130 is a galaxy that appears relatively compact in SDSS imaging [arXiv:1904.01615]. It has a stellar mass of 10^6.4 solar masses in its spectroscopic aperture and exhibits weak P-Cygni absorption alongside nebular ultraviolet emission [arXiv:1904.01615].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1119+5130 as compact in SDSS imaging, with measured stellar mass, moderate H equivalent width, detected nebular UV emission, weak P-Cygni absorption, and nebular-line kinematics.

quotes.quote: J1119+5130: This system is relatively compact in SDSS imaging, with stellar mass 10^ 6.4 0.2 M_ in the spectroscopic aperture and an H equivalent width of 36 2 just below the median for our sample. The UV spectra reveal nebular emission in ] 1666 at W_0 = 1.5 , but non-detections of the other lines (with ] W_0<0.8 ). P-Cygni absorption is barely detected in J1119+5130, at only 0.2 equivalent width.

The linewidths measured for HS1442+4250, UM133, and J1119+5130 in the high-resolution data strongly suggests that emission in these systems arises in gas similar to that which dominates the emission in nebular H and [ ] , rather than a stellar wind or shocked outflow.

gmw_00011317.name: UM133
gmw_00011317.overview: UM133 is a star-forming complex situated at the end of an extended tadpole structure [arXiv:1904.01615]. The system is dominated by young massive stars and exhibits ultraviolet nebular emission lines alongside P-Cygni absorption [arXiv:1904.01615].

sources.summary: The paper describes UM133 as a tadpole-structure star-forming complex dominated by young massive stars, with prominent UV nebular lines, large C equivalent width, and P-Cygni absorption.

quotes.quote: UM133: The targeted region in UM133 is a star-forming complex at the end of an extended tadpole structure (Figure fig:cutouts ). We infer a stellar mass of 10^ 5.9 0.2 M_ and measure an H equivalent width of 85 7 (the second largest in the sample), suggestive of a system particularly dominated by young massive stars. The UV spectra of UM133 show prominent nebular emission in , ] , and ] , with the ] doublet measured at a very large equivalent width of 11 1 . P-Cygni absorption is also visible, at W_0=-1.3 .
Our sample of XMPs with new UV spectra includes two with very prominent ] emission, HS1442+4250 and UM133, both at an equivalent width of 11 .

gmw_00011318.name: 3C 346
gmw_00011318.overview: The narrow-line radio galaxy 3C 346 is a luminous source that exhibits a jetted-double radio morphology with a strong, one-sided jet [arXiv:0809.2829, arXiv:astro-ph/9907034]. It features an optical jet and both compact and extended X-ray emission, which is dominated by an advection-dominated accretion flow [arXiv:0809.2829, arXiv:astro-ph/9907034].

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 346 as a luminous FR source whose radio and optical emission are fit by a jet, while its Chandra X-ray emission is dominated by an ADAF.

quotes.quote: ADAF dominated the nuclear X-ray emission for higher Eddington ratio sources The radio morphology and power of 3C 346 would rank as either a low-power FR II source or a high-power FR I . Chandra observations have detected an unresolved core with L_ X (2-10 \ keV)=1.9 10^ 43 \ erg \ s^ 1 and a photon index of =1.69 0.09 (Donato et al 2004).

The parameters of the jet are m _ jet =3.5 10^ 5 , _ e =0.14 , _ B =0.02 , and p=2.4 . We find that the jet model can describe well the radio and optical data. But the X-ray emission of the jet model is several times lower than the Chandra observations, and the X-ray data can be well fitted by the ADAF.
This source is relatively luminous, with L_ X /L_ Edd =1.8 10^ 4 , and the X-ray emission is dominated by the ADAF, not by the jet.

sources.summary: The narrow-line radio galaxy 3C 346 is detected with compact and extended X-ray emission and has a jetted-double radio morphology with optical jet evidence.

quotes.quote: 3C 346 This narrow-line radio galaxy is clearly detected with the PSPC, showing both extended and compact X-ray emission; fitting a PSPC PRF alone to the radial profile gives unacceptable results. In the radio, 3C 346 is another `jetted double' source with a strong one-sided jet and a weak hot spot (compare 3C 288, section jetted ). It has an unusually bright core (Spencer \ 1991) and an optical jet (e.g. \ de Koff \ 1996) and exhibits a strong Laing-Garrington effect Laing (1988); Garrington \ (1988). (Akujor Garrington 1995) which suggests a cluster environment for the source.

gmw_00011319.name: HCC 007
gmw_00011319.overview: HCC 007 is a rotating lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1809.01163, arXiv:1205.5289] that exhibits a low velocity dispersion [arXiv:1809.01163]. It is embedded in a thick, 110-kiloparsec tidal stream [arXiv:1205.5289] and shows signs of interaction with the nearby galaxy NGC 3311 [arXiv:1809.01163, arXiv:1205.5289].

sources.summary: The paper describes HCC 007 as a lenticular galaxy south of NGC 3311 with a velocity gradient, clear rotation, low dispersion, and possible interaction traced by a tidal feature.

quotes.quote: Regions of high V_ LOS values are found around NGC 3309 ( 4095 km s ^ 1 ) and HCC 007 ( 4760 km s ^ 1 ), which is expected due to their higher systemic velocities (see open orange symbols in Fig. fig:velsig_profiles ). It is interesting to note that around HCC 007 there seems to exist a velocity gradient with lower velocities towards NGC 3311 and larger ones away from it (see black line bottom panel of Fig. fig:velocity_folded ). The galaxy HCC 007 itself shows a clear rotation signal with receding velocities on its east side and approaching velocities on the West (see Figures fig:hcc007_maps and fig:hcc007_kinematic ).

The high velocity dispersion east of HCC 007 is probably caused by the superposition of the galaxy's stellar population at high radial velocity (see Fig. fig:hcc007_maps ) with that of NGC 3311's stellar halo at lower radial velocities. Low velocity dispersion regions are those around the giant elliptical NGC 3309 ( 200 km s ^ 1 ) and HCC 007 ( 80 km s ^ 1 ), which dominate the light in those slits.
The h_3 and h_4 values around HCC 007 are interesting. They show the typical pattern of negative to positive h_3 values on both sides of a rotating galaxy (see Fig. fig:hcc007_maps ).

sources.summary: The paper describes HCC 007 as an S0 galaxy embedded in a thick 110 kpc tidal stream whose luminosity resembles the stripped-down galaxy and may join NGC 3311's halo.

quotes.quote: We have also discovered two tidal streams in the cluster center, emerging from the dwarf galaxy HCC 026 and from the S0 galaxy HCC 007. The HCC 026 stream is redshifted by 1200 \ with respect to NGC 3311 ( V_ N3311 3800 ), similarly as HCC 026 itself, a fraction of PNs in the off-centered envelope, and several other dwarf galaxies nearby. The stars in one of the HCC 026 tails are known to be consistent with the low-metallicity population of HCC 026, and our photometry shows that this galaxy is already mostly dissolved in the tidal field. The tidal stream around HCC 007 extends over at least 110 kpc. It is fairly thick and is brighter on the side of the asymmetric outer halo of NGC 3311, which it may join. Its luminosity is several 10^9 L_ , similar to the luminosity of the stripped-down galaxy HCC 007. The redshift of the stream is determined from a few PN velocities and is similar to that for HCC 007 and HCC 026.

gmw_00011320.name: GOODS-N i' drop 6
gmw_00011320.overview: GOODS-N i' drop 6 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/0308124]. It exhibits a spatially extended emission line that is tentatively identified as Lyman-alpha at a redshift of z=6.24 [arXiv:astro-ph/0308124].

sources.summary: The paper describes GOODS-N i' drop 6 as a high-redshift candidate with a spatially extended emission line, tentatively identified as Lyman-alpha at z=6.24 despite ambiguity.

quotes.quote: GOODS-N i' Drop 6 The 2D spectrum of GOODS-N i' drop 6 showed one significant emission line detected at 20 at a central wavelength of _ obs 8804 1 . Both 2D and 1D extracted spectra for this line are shown in figure fig:c6 , centered on the spatial location of the i' drop object and the central wavelength of the emission line. The emission line is spatially extended, stretching from the location of the i' drop in the ACS imaging to peak in intensity approximately 2 away .
The second scenario is that we are seeing spatially-extended Lyman- emission at z=6.24 , as has been observed in a number of star forming galaxies.

If we identify the emission line as Lyman- then the emission wavelength is _ rest 1216 \ giving a redshift for candidate 6 of z=6.24 . We tentatively adopt this identification and candidate 6 remains part of our high redshift galaxy sample.

gmw_00011321.name: CO emitting source
gmw_00011321.overview: The CO emitting source is located approximately 9 kpc southeast of the nucleus of the giant elliptical galaxy H1821+643 [arXiv:1105.4912]. It is likely an optically faint, gas-rich companion galaxy undergoing a merger, hosting a massive molecular gas reservoir of about 8.0 billion solar masses [arXiv:1105.4912].

sources.summary: The paper argues the offset CO emitting source near H1821+643 is likely an optically faint, gas-rich companion or merger remnant with a massive molecular gas reservoir.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The source is centered at =18^h21^m57.43^s and =64^ 20'34''.80
