quotes.quote: The bulk of the CO emission is located at 9 kpc south-east from the nuclei position, and close to a faint optical structure, suggesting that the CO emission could either represent a gas-rich companion galaxy merging with the elliptical host or a tail-like structure reminiscent of a previous interaction. We argue that the first scenario is more likely given the large masses implied for the CO source, which would imply a highly asymmetric elliptical host.

Thus, this suggest that the CO emission is possibly associated with either an optically faint but gas-rich companion galaxy that is merging with the giant elliptical host, or with an asymmetric tail-like structure within the host galaxy reminiscent of a previous merger event.

We thus estimate a molecular gas mass of (8.0 1.7) 10^ 9 M _ for the CO emitting source. This molecular gas mass is at the high end of the mass range found for local ULIRGs, and is typical of luminous submillimeter galaxies and massive disk galaxies at high-redshift Greve2005,Solomon2005,Daddi2010 .

gmw_00011322.name: NGC 4872
gmw_00011322.overview: NGC 4872 is a lenticular S0 galaxy that features a bar structure [arXiv:1604.01768]. It is the closest bright companion to the galaxy NGC 4874, with which it may share a dynamical interaction [arXiv:1604.01768].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4872 as the closest bright neighbor to NGC 4874, an S0 with a prominent bar, similar velocity, little obvious GC system, and possible dynamical influence.

quotes.quote: However, the offset does align closely with the direction towards NGC 4872, an S0 galaxy with a prominent bar. At a separation of only 0 82, or 24 kpc, NGC 4872 is the closest of the bright neighboring galaxies, and its velocity (from NED) differs by only 17 4 \ from NGC 4874. Despite its luminosity, NGC 4872 does not have an obvious GC system of its own (unlike NGC 4871 and NGC 4873). It would be interesting to explore through dynamical modeling if the observed offset of the NGC 4874 GC distribution could be related to dynamical interaction with NGC 4872.

gmw_00011323.name: SDSS J125935
gmw_00011323.overview: SDSS J125935 is a faint dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1604.01768]. It features a relatively rich globular cluster system with an asymmetric point-source density, suggesting it is undergoing stripping during its first infall into the cluster core [arXiv:1604.01768].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J125935 as a faint dwarf elliptical with asymmetric point-source density, a relatively rich GC system, and likely stripping during first infall into Coma.

quotes.quote: Searching its coordinates in NED, we found that the galaxy was catalogued in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and is designated SDSS J125935.18 + 275605.0; for convenience, we refer to it hereafter as SDSS J125935. This is the faintest of the ten galaxies in our ACS images for which we performed isophotal modeling, and it lies near the top right corner of our ACS field (outside our WFC3/IR imaging area); see the galaxy model panel in Figure fig:imgf814w . Figure fig:prettypic shows an 84 50 cutout of the region around this galaxy in our \ image; the much brighter galaxy at lower left in this figure is NGC 4872, discussed above. Several fainter, more diffuse, objects in this field appear similar to the extremely diffuse galaxies first systematically catalogued in the Coma cluster by van Dokkum \ (2015), and shown from deep Subaru imaging to be ubiquitous throughout the Coma cluster (Koda \ 2015). The image also shows that the density of point sources around the dE SDSS J125935 is not symmetric about the galaxy's center.

Numerical simulations indicate that dEs at such small clustercentric radii and high relative velocities are likely to be on their first infall into the cluster core (Smith \ 2013, 2015). The same simulations show that dwarfs that pass through the cluster centers can lose a large fraction, even the majority, of their GC systems (see also Aguilar White 1986 for illustrations of how similar encounters can result in asymmetric distributions of GC-like test masses). We suggest that SDSS J125935 is a dwarf elliptical with a relatively rich GC system, similar to some dEs in Virgo, that has recently fallen at high velocity into the core of the Coma cluster and is undergoing stripping of its GC system.

gmw_00011324.overview: 8608 is a high-redshift galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 2.9 [arXiv:1101.3478]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with an intrinsic X-ray luminosity in the quasar regime and is classified as a possible borderline Compton-thick source [arXiv:1101.3478].

sources.summary: The paper describes 8608 as a z~2.9 galaxy with a flat X-ray spectrum, a prominent emission line, quasar-regime intrinsic X-ray luminosity, and possible borderline Compton-thick AGN.

quotes.quote: 8608 has a photometric redshift z _ phot =2.94 (from D07; consistent with z _ phot =2.88 from Santini et al. 2009).
The spectra for both galaxies show a flat continuum and prominent emission lines peaking at 1.78 keV for 4892 and 1.64 keV for 8608 (Fig. 1).

The intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity of 8608 is likely 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 . The implied column density is likely in the range N_ H 1 - 5 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , hence this object might be a borderline CT AGN. We notice that both sources have estimated intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosities in the QSO regime.

gmw_00011325.name: MACS2129 00899
gmw_00011325.overview: MACS2129 00899 is a high-confidence emission-line galaxy candidate located at a high redshift of z=8.10 [arXiv:1511.04205]. The galaxy exhibits emission lines detected in both G102 spectra, with a marginal line detection also observed in G141 spectral data [arXiv:1511.04205].

sources.summary: The paper highlights MACS2129 00899 as a high-confidence z=8.10 emission-line candidate, with G102 detections in both spectra and a marginal G141 detection.

quotes.quote: MACS2129 00899 Another object worth high-lighting is MACS2129 00899. It is a high confidence \ emitter candidate at z=8.10 which shows emission lines in both of the G102 spectra at 11065 . This wavelength is also covered by the G141 grisms, albeit at low sensitivity (see Figure fig:fluxlimits ). Despite the low sensitivity and relatively high contamination there appears to be a marginal detection of the line in one of the G141 spectra as well.

gmw_00011326.name: RXJ2248 00404
gmw_00011326.overview: RXJ2248 00404 is a photometric dropout galaxy with an inconclusive redshift [arXiv:1511.04205]. It is identified as a candidate high-redshift object at z ~ 10, but it may alternatively be a lower-redshift emission line contaminant [arXiv:1511.04205].

sources.summary: The paper presents RXJ2248 00404 as one of two possible z~10 Silver EL objects, selected as a dropout but potentially a lower-redshift [O II] contaminant.

quotes.quote: As presented in Table tab:dropouts_EL , RXJ2248 00404 and MACS1423 01018 of the Silver EL sample appear to have emission lines at 1.32 m and 1.37 m, respectively. If these lines are confirmed to be \ this would place these objects at z 10 . Figure fig:z10obj shows the G141 spectra, marking the detected emission lines with white circles. Both objects are selected as photometric dropouts, i.e. selected based on a few detections red-wards of the Lyman break and non-detections in bands blue-wards of the break. In both cases the EA z Y photometric redshift distributions have highly probable solutions at z 2 -3, and the photometry is therefore inconclusive as to whether the objects are at high redshift or low redshift.

gmw_00011327.name: MACS1423 01018
gmw_00011327.overview: MACS1423 01018 is a photometric dropout galaxy that has been identified as a candidate high-redshift object [arXiv:1511.04205]. However, its photometric redshift distribution remains inconclusive, suggesting it could alternatively be a lower-redshift emission line contaminant [arXiv:1511.04205].

sources.summary: The paper presents MACS1423 01018 as one of two possible z~10 Silver EL objects, selected as a dropout but potentially a lower-redshift [O II] contaminant.

quotes.quote: As presented in Table tab:dropouts_EL , RXJ2248 00404 and MACS1423 01018 of the Silver EL sample appear to have emission lines at 1.32 m and 1.37 m, respectively. If these lines are confirmed to be \ this would place these objects at z 10 . Figure fig:z10obj shows the G141 spectra, marking the detected emission lines with white circles. Both objects are selected as photometric dropouts, i.e. selected based on a few detections red-wards of the Lyman break and non-detections in bands blue-wards of the break. In both cases the EA z Y photometric redshift distributions have highly probable solutions at z 2 -3, and the photometry is therefore inconclusive as to whether the objects are at high redshift or low redshift.

gmw_00011328.overview: The active galaxy 0810+077 is a low-excitation radio galaxy classified as a quasar [arXiv:1311.6633]. Its radio morphology consists of three components, including two jets or lobes and a weak central component that tentatively identifies as a radio core [arXiv:1311.6633].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0810+077 as a LEG quasar in the Chandra LLC sample, with a three-component radio morphology, tentative core identification, and X-ray emission between radio components.

quotes.quote: 0810+077 is a quasar classified as LEG. Its radio morphology consist of three components: the weak central one (C) and two jets/lobes (E and W). The optical counterpart is coincident with the component C and we suggested this could be a radio core Kunert10a .

However, this is based on observations at only one radio frequency so it should be treated as tentative. The brightest part of the X-ray emission lies between the components C and W. The potential offset between the centroid of the X-ray emission and component C or W can be consistent with the astrometric uncertainty of Chandra .

In the pilot sample of LLC sources observed with Chandra we have found that two sources, 0810+077 and 0942+355, have the same radio and X-ray luminosities (Table lumins ). However, 0942+355 (HEG) has higher [ OIII ] emission than 0810+077 (LEG).

gmw_00011329.name: S04590
gmw_00011329.overview: S04590 is a star-forming galaxy located at a high redshift of z=8.5 [arXiv:2312.08432]. It is characterized as a metal-poor system that exhibits a high emission line ratio [arXiv:2312.08432].

sources.summary: The paper discusses S04590 as a z=8.5 metal-poor star-forming galaxy whose high line ratio would require implausibly high X-ray luminosity in other models.

quotes.quote: They find that reproducing the surprisingly high / ratio of 0.048 (0.055 when dust-corrected) in the /NIRSpec spectrum of the metal-poor star-forming galaxy S04590 at redshift z=8.5 requires L_ X,2-10 keV ^ tot / SFR >10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 , which translates into / SFR >3 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 when adopting the energy band 0.5--8 keV used by senchyna2020 and in Fig. fig:brorby_met of the present paper.

Yet, the X-ray luminosity per unit star formation rate required by katz2023 to significantly impact the / ratio of S04590 appears improbably high, as it is, respectively, 20 and 11 larger than the mean observed values for metal-poor galaxies in the samples of lehmer2019 and douna2015 .

Intriguingly, the value for J1631+4426 is the same as that found in the high-redshift galaxy S04590 studied by katz2023 , for which no simple explanation, other than an improbably high contribution by XRBs (see above) or a high cosmic-ray background (not applicable to nearby galaxies), could be found.

gmw_00011330.name: SDSS J1226 0006
gmw_00011330.overview: SDSS J1226 0006 is a gravitational lensing system containing a doubly imaged quasar at a redshift of 1.120 [arXiv:astro-ph/0511026]. The lensing galaxy itself is located at a redshift of 0.516 and exhibits the spectrum of an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0511026].

sources.summary: The lensing galaxy in SDSS J1226 0006 lies between the quasar images, is measured at redshift 0.516, and has an elliptical-galaxy spectrum.

quotes.quote: For the first time we measure the redshift of the lensing galaxy in three multiply-imaged quasars: SDSS J1138 + 0314 ( =0.445 ), SDSS J1226 0006 ( =0.517 ), SDSS J1335 + 0118 ( =0.440 ), and we give a tentative estimate of the redshift of the lensing galaxy in Q 1355 2257 ( =0.701 ).

SDSS J1226 0006 : A doubly imaged quasar at z=1.120 found in the course of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) by Inada et al. ( ). This system is doubtlessly lensed, as the lensing galaxy is seen on archival HST/ACS images, between both quasar images, at only 0.4 away from image A. We measure =0.516 0.001 (Fig. SDSS1226_lens ). The spectrum of the lensing galaxy is well matched by the spectrum of an elliptical galaxy.

gmw_00011331.name: SDSS J1335 + 0118
aliases_external: LBQS1333+0113 / SDSS J1335+0118 lens galaxy G
gmw_00011331.overview: SDSS J1335 + 0118 is an early-type galaxy located at a redshift of 0.440 [arXiv:astro-ph/0511026]. It acts as a gravitational lens that doubly images a background quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0511026].

sources.summary: The lens galaxy in SDSS J1335 + 0118 is found to be an early-type galaxy, with the paper reporting its previously unmeasured redshift as 0.440.

quotes.quote: For the first time we measure the redshift of the lensing galaxy in three multiply-imaged quasars: SDSS J1138 + 0314 ( =0.445 ), SDSS J1226 0006 ( =0.517 ), SDSS J1335 + 0118 ( =0.440 ), and we give a tentative estimate of the redshift of the lensing galaxy in Q 1355 2257 ( =0.701 ).

SDSS J1335 + 0118 : A doubly imaged quasar with a 1.56 \ separation, discovered by Oguri et al. ( ). The quasar is at z=1.57 and the spectra of the quasar images show evidence of strong absorption systems at lower redshifts. Based on the color of the galaxy, Oguri et al. ( ) conclude that the lens galaxy is consistent with an early-type galaxy at z 0.5 . Our spectrum (Fig. J1335_lens ) is indeed that of an early-type galaxy. Its redshift =0.440 0.001 has not been measured before.

gmw_00011332.name: ESO 102-G2
gmw_00011332.overview: ESO 102-G2 is a galaxy with a neutral hydrogen disk spanning approximately 110 arcseconds [arXiv:1409.0566]. It lies southwest of PKS B1718-649, and asymmetries in the neutral hydrogen suggest a past interaction between the two galaxies [arXiv:1409.0566].

sources.summary: The paper detects ESO 102-G2 in H I near PKS B1718--649, gives its coordinates, velocity, H I mass, and disk size, and suggests a past interaction between them.

quotes.quote: Inside the primary beam of the observations, we detected the H I emission of also another galaxy: ESO 102-G2 Ra =17^ h 21^ m 38^ s , Dec =-65^ 10^ ' 27^ '' , z =0.0147 . This galaxy lies 16.2 arcmin ( 285 kpc) SW of PKS B1718--649 at a systemic velocity of = 4415 15 km s ^ 1 . The total inferred H I mass is: M_ H I =1.1 10^ 10 ( veron ) and the disk has a size of approximately 110 arcsec. The small difference in redshift, as well as the external asymmetries of the H I disk of PKS B1718--649 , slightly oriented towards ESO 102-G2, suggest a past interaction between the two galaxies. This event may have strongly influenced the kinematics of the neutral hydrogen in both galaxies.

gmw_00011333.name: J120337.92+153006.7
gmw_00011333.overview: J120337.92+153006.7 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1507.07247]. This active galactic nucleus exhibits broad absorption variability across nearly its entire wavelength coverage from 1940 to 3000 angstroms [arXiv:1507.07247].

sources.summary: The paper finds J120337 shows broad absorption variability across much of the spectrum and argues the signal is not caused by calibration, slit losses, or normal continuum fluctuations.

quotes.quote: J120337.92+153006.7 Fig. 3 shows the ratio between the DR7 and MDM epochs in J120337. Inspection of Fig. 3 reveals indication of variability over nearly the entire wavelength coverage from 1940--3000 . The global change is not a flux calibration issue because this source was observed on the same night as other objects that do not show any discrepancies in flux of this magnitude when compared to DR7. Slit losses from atmospheric dispersion are also likely unimportant given that this source was observed at very low airmass (i.e. 1.1). Fluctuations due to quasar continuum emission are also unexpected as they typically occur over a much larger wavelength interval.

gmw_00011334.name: J031856.62-060037.7
gmw_00011334.overview: J031856.62-060037.7 is classified as a broad-absorption line quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1507.07247]. Spectroscopic monitoring of the source over timescales of 3.4 to 3.8 years detected no absorption line variability in its Si IV, C IV, Al II, or Al III transitions [arXiv:1507.07247].

sources.summary: The paper examines J031856 across Si IV, C IV, Al II, and Al III coverage and finds no significant absorption variability over 3.4 to 3.8 years.

quotes.quote: J031856.62-060037.7 We have three observations for J031856 spanning time-scales from 3.4--3.8 yr. The wavelength coverage for studying variability was from 1300--2030 , a range that included the resonance transitions Si iv 1393, 1402, C iv 1548, 1550, Al ii 1670, and Al iii 1854, 1862. The Fe ii and Mg ii transitions are found in redder regions of the spectrum outside of our observational coverage. We do not detect any significant absorption line variability using the 8 criterion for these ions across the observed time-scales, consistent with the results of viv12 who also study this source over 3 yr between 2001 and 2009 and had additional wavelength coverage extending out to 2850 .

gmw_00011335.name: SDSS J092422.92+400527.1
gmw_00011335.overview: SDSS J092422.92+400527.1 is a low-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:1512.00853]. It is identified as a possible host galaxy for the J0925+4004 absorber, with gas that may be moving in a high-velocity outflow [arXiv:1512.00853].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J092422.92+400527.1 as a possible low-surface-brightness host galaxy for the J0925+4004 absorber, with uncertain redshift and impact parameter.

quotes.quote: Another galaxy, SDSS J092422.92+400527.1, occurs at 141 kpc and 1.2 if the velocity window is widened to 850 km/s. However, this low-surface brightness galaxy's SDSS spectrum is quite noisy, and if its redshift is equal to that of the absorber, the impact parameter decreases to 56 kpc. If the SDSS redshift is accurate, this last absorber presents a similar issue to that presented by Burchett:2013qy , where a candidate host galaxy may have a smaller impact parameter if the gas is moving at a very high velocity relative to the galaxy, e.g., a high-velocity outflow. Such outflow speeds are certainly plausible and have in fact been observed unambiguously in `down-the-barrel' studies where the ejecting galaxy itself was used as a background source to study the outflowing gas in absorption e.g., Rubin:2014fk, Tremonti:2007fk .

gmw_00011336.name: NGC 7174
gmw_00011336.overview: NGC 7174 is an irregular galaxy classified as a LINER [arXiv:0708.0076]. It is strongly interacting with the elliptical galaxy NGC 7176, displaying two tidal plumes and radio continuum star formation [arXiv:0708.0076].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7174 as an irregular LINER in HCG90, strongly interacting with NGC 7176, with tidal plumes and radio continuum star formation.

quotes.quote: This is a group with mixed morphology, with one spiral galaxy, a (=NGC 7172), which is a Sa, two ellipticals, b and and one irregular, d . Galaxies a through d have X-ray emission (Mulchaey et al., 1998); galaxy a is a Sy2 (Coziol et al., 1998), while galaxies b and have no optical line emission at all and galaxy d is a LINER.

Two of the four original members of the group, b (=NGC 7176) and d (=NGC 7174), are strongly interacting with each other; galaxy d shows two tidal plumes, while galaxy b shows a luminous bridge towards d and distorted isophotes (Longo et al., 1994).
Galaxy d however is the strongest continuum emitter, with an estimated star formation rate of 0.92 see Table scg_rc .

gmw_00011337.name: NGC 89
gmw_00011337.overview: NGC 89 is a disturbed Seyfert 2 galaxy located in the middle of an interacting group [arXiv:0708.0076]. The galaxy features strongly twisted isophotes, a northeast-oriented jet, and molecular gas, but it is completely devoid of neutral hydrogen [arXiv:0708.0076].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 89 as a disturbed Seyfert 2 galaxy in SCG0018-4854, with twisted isophotes, a jet, radio continuum emission, molecular gas, and HI deficiency.

quotes.quote: Galaxy b (=NGC 89) has strongly twisted isophotes, a Sy2 nucleus and a NE oriented jet, well visible on H images (G. Temporin, private communication). It is the only galaxy in the group not being detected in with an upper limit of 3 10^7 \ . However we detected radio continuum emission (Table scg_rc ), albeit fainter than galaxy a .

All galaxies have been observed in CO by Boselli et al. (1996): galaxies a and b contain significant amount of M(H _ 2 ), 10 ^ 9.3 M _ and 10 ^ 8.6 M _ , respectively; surprisingly enough galaxy a has not been detected at all at 60 m from IRAS. The log(M_ H_ 2 /L_ B ) is equal to -0.068 and -0.84 respectively for galaxies a and b : according to these measures, both galaxies are perturbed.

However, while SCG2159-3210 a is located outside the main site of interaction and it has retained some of its \ gas, SCG0018-4854 b is sitting in the middle of an interacting group and it is completely devoid of .

gmw_00011338.name: ESO 296-G 026
gmw_00011338.overview: ESO 296-G 026 is a spiral galaxy belonging to the SCG0122-3819 group [arXiv:0708.0076]. It exhibits strong star formation and shows signs of tidal disturbances, with disturbed gas distribution and kinematics indicating that it is interacting with the group [arXiv:0708.0076].

sources.summary: The paper adds ESO 296-G 026 as an accordant-redshift spiral in SCG0122-3819, finding strong star formation, disturbed HI, and evidence that it interacts with the group.

quotes.quote: Our \ data prove that there is at least one more galaxy with accordant redshift, the spiral galaxy ESO 296-G 026, named galaxy e . This galaxy is also strongly forming stars, with a SFR of almost 2

The disturbed distribution of \ emission in galaxy e , together with the equally disturbed distribution of galaxy , led us to believe that galaxy e is interacting with the group and we decided to include it in the number of member galaxies.

The velocity fields of both (=NGC 546), and e (=ESO 296-G 026; Fig. fig:scgvel_0122 ) indicate that the receding side in both galaxies is the north-eastern half. 8 Both galaxies exhibit signs of tidal disturbances, not only in the gas distribution, but also its kinematics.

gmw_00011339.name: IC1722
gmw_00011339.overview: IC1722 is a member of the SCG0141-3429 group with a disturbed lenticular or spiral morphology [arXiv:0708.0076]. The galaxy features a displaced nucleus, bright star-forming knots, strong infrared emission, and strong optical emission lines [arXiv:0708.0076].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC1722 as a disturbed lenticular or spiral member of SCG0141-3429, with displaced structure, bright star-forming knots, IR emission, and strong optical lines.

quotes.quote: The whole group is composed of lenticulars and spirals, two of which, b (=IC1722) and , have strongly disturbed morphologies, with a displaced nucleus and bright knots of star formation. Residual maps in Figure 1 clearly show the irregular distribution of bright knots in galaxy , which are likely star forming regions. Both galaxies are detected by IRAS as strong infrared emitters. Galaxies a and d are normal spirals, while e is a late type galaxy with many SF regions. Galaxies b and show strong emission lines, H , [ NII ] , [ SII ] [ OIII ] and [ OII ] , while e has very faint emission and off-centre with respect to the slit position, which was centered on the galaxy nucleus.

Surface photometry for galaxies b and was not straightforward, due to the irregular morphology of the targets and due to the bright SF knot visible in the northern part of galaxy . However good results were obtained after some trial and error and the best fitting parameters are reported in Table scg_pho . The \ emission properties of the four detected galaxies vary widely, see Figure fig:scg0141 and Table scg_hi 9 With the angular resolution of the current data it is not entirely clear whether the \ gas distribution in galaxy b is disturbed or not.

gmw_00011340.name: SDSS J120409.47--015552.4
gmw_00011340.overview: SDSS J120409.47--015552.4 is a faint host galaxy with an apparent magnitude of g = 21.92 [arXiv:1808.04382]. It is a dwarf galaxy that exhibits low metallicity and contains an H II region near its center [arXiv:1808.04382].

sources.summary: The paper describes the faint host galaxy of SN 2017ens, measuring emission near its center, a redshift slightly offset from the supernova, and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: In addition, we see emission from the H 2 region close to the host-galaxy center (see Fig. fig:spec_vlt , marked B1), as part of a faint galaxy (SDSS J120409.47--015552.4) with g = 21.92 0.24 mag ( M_ g 16.5 mag). These lines have a slightly different redshift ( z=0.1084 ) than SN 2017ens. In particular, the (noisy) detection of the weak auroral [ O 3 ] 4363 line indicates a low host-galaxy metallicity of 0.04 - 0.4 Z_ using the direct T_ e based method.

gmw_00011341.name: IRAS 06206-6315
gmw_00011341.overview: IRAS 06206-6315 is classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2402.17851]. High fluxes measured in its mid-J carbon monoxide lines indicate an elevated average molecular hydrogen volume density, and the galaxy exhibits marked line broadening in its higher-J transitions [arXiv:2402.17851].

sources.summary: The paper finds IRAS 06206-6315 has high mid-J CO fluxes, an unusually high inferred density, possible need for a second excitation component, and marked high-J line broadening.

quotes.quote: For instance, the high fluxes measured in the CO( J_ up =4,6 ) lines for IRAS 06206-6315 boost the average H _2 volume density of the galaxy to n_ H_2 cm^ 3 5.6^ +1.1 _ 1.4 , and a similar effect is observed in IRAS F20551-4250, though in this latter case, the parameter boosted to higher value is the gas kinetic temperature to T_ kin K 2.8^ +0.2 _ 0.2 .
A similar effect will be at play for those solutions with very high density (e.g., IRAS 06206-6315).

The sources showing the most remarkable line broadening in higher- J CO transitions are: IRAS 06035-7102 (Figure fig:spec06035 ), IRAS 06206-6315 (Figure fig:spec06206 ), IRAS 08311-2459 (Figure fig:spec08311 ) and IRAS 16090-0139 (Figure fig:spec16090 ).

gmw_00011342.name: IRAS 10378+1109
gmw_00011342.overview: IRAS 10378+1109 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy that exhibits some of the highest excitation CO spectral line energy distributions [arXiv:2402.17851]. Its CO(7-6) emission is redshifted by approximately 300 km/s relative to its systemic velocity, though the galaxy shows no evidence of being particularly perturbed [arXiv:2402.17851].

sources.summary: The paper highlights IRAS 10378+1109 as a high-excitation source with redshifted CO(7--6), puzzling systemic-component behavior, and almost thermalized emission up to CO(7--6).

quotes.quote: Sources IRAS 10378+1109 and IRAS 16090-0139 present some of the highest excitation SLEDs.

In particular, the CO(7--6) emission from IRAS 10378+1109 appears to be red-shifted from the systemic velocity inferred from the low- J CO lines by v 300 , whereas no other source in the sample exhibits a similar behavior We checked the literature, and ALMA archival data for this galaxy, and it does not show evidence to be a particularly perturbed source. .

The apparent decrease of CO(7--6) flux in the systemic velocity component of IRAS 10378+1109 is indeed puzzling and deserves further inspection, ideally with spatially resolved data that allow for a kinematical analysis of the gas distribution in this galaxy.

gmw_00011343.name: ESO 380-019
gmw_00011343.overview: ESO 380-019 is an inclined disk galaxy with a moderately asymmetric structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768]. It features an uncertain bulge component, a patchy dust lane, and a broken exponential radial profile along its major axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 380-019 as an inclined, moderately asymmetric disk with uncertain bulge and dust structure, a major-axis broken exponential profile, and a shorter southeastern side.

quotes.quote: ESO 380-019: Owing to a not well defined thick patchy dust lane the inclination is uncertain (clearly not 90 , but closer to 85 ). The extent and size of the bulge component is also uncertain. The major axis radial profile shows a broken exponential structure with a break at R (95 100) \ ( _ V 23.8 ). The galaxy is moderately asymmetric. In the contour map the SE(left)-side is obviously shorter.

gmw_00011344.name: ESO 404-018
gmw_00011344.overview: ESO 404-018 is an almost edge-on disk galaxy that exhibits a knotty disk structure and has virtually no bulge component [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768]. The galaxy's disk appears bent like a wave, and its radial surface brightness profile is well described by a broken exponential [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 404-018 as an almost edge-on galaxy with little bulge, knotty bent disk structure, and a broken exponential radial profile with an extended break region.

quotes.quote: ESO 404-018: This galaxy shows hardly any bulge component. The disk structure is quite knotty and appears bended like a wave weinberg2001 in addition to being slightly u-shaped at the edges. The radial surface brightness profile shows quite clearly the deviation from a simple not-truncated exponential model on both sides with a sharp break on the NE(left)-side, and a smoother one on the SW(right)-side starting at R 70 \ and is well fitted by a broken exponential. The single disk fit by yields a vertical scaleheight of 2.8 , a radial scalelength of h 31.3 , and an inclination of 90.0 . The face-on-equivalent profile of ESO 404-018 is well described as a with an inner shallow exponential (of 34.0 3.6 ) and an outer steeper exponential ( 9.6 ) with a break at a radial distance of 76 2.2 and a surface brightness of 24.6 V- .

gmw_00011345.name: FGC 2339
gmw_00011345.overview: FGC 2339 is an asymmetric disk galaxy with an inclination of 88.5 degrees [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768]. It features slightly off-centered ring- or bar-like knots on both sides, displaying a broken exponential profile on its northern side and a more sharply truncated profile on its southern side [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768].

sources.summary: The paper describes FGC 2339 as an asymmetric disk with off-centered ring- or bar-like knots, a broken exponential on one side, and a more sharply truncated profile on the other.

quotes.quote: FGC 2339: This galaxy shows on both sides slightly off-centered ring-, or bar-like knots at (R,z) (+24 ,+1 ) and (R,z) (-26 ,+1 ). The underlying disk is quite asymmetric on both sides. The major axis profile on the N(left)-side is satisfactorily fitted with a broken exponential showing a break at R (55-60) \ and _ R 24.0 . On the S(right)-side the profile looks more like a sharply-truncated profile. The single disk fit by yields a vertical scaleheight of 1.8 , a radial scalelength of h 19.9 , and an inclination of 88.5 .

gmw_00011346.name: IC 4871
gmw_00011346.overview: IC 4871 is an edge-on disk galaxy with an inclination of 87.5 degrees [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768]. The galaxy exhibits a patchy structure containing numerous H II regions, including a warped star-forming patch along its major axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4871 as a patchy disk with many H II regions, a warped star-forming patch, and a steep broken exponential major-axis decline.

quotes.quote: IC 4871: (alternative name: IC 4872) This galaxy is overall very patchy with many distinct \ regions. On the S(right)-side, along the major axis at R +100 \ there is an unusually warped patch of star formation visible. The major axis radial profile can be well described with a broken exponential with a very steep outer decline (dropping by 2 mag within 10 ) on both sides (with slightly different outer slopes), starting at R 115 ( _ V 23.5 ). The single disk fit by yields a vertical scaleheight of 3.8 , a radial scalelength of h 42.3 , and an inclination of 87.5 .

gmw_00011347.name: NGC 3390
gmw_00011347.overview: NGC 3390 is an edge-on galaxy with an inner structure resembling a normal Sb-type galaxy with a well-defined dust lane [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768]. However, its outer region is embedded in a huge spheroidal envelope, and it has a nearby low surface brightness companion [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3390 as an unusual edge-on Sb-like galaxy embedded in a huge spheroidal envelope, with nearby companions and profiles unsuited to a sharp broken-exponential model.

quotes.quote: NGC 3390: There is a nearby, low surface brightness companion, of unknown distance, visible at (R,z) (+140 ,-125 ) and another unusually small and round object at R +85 \ on the major axis. This galaxy exhibits a quite unusual appearance. The inner high surface brightness part looks like a normal edge-on Sb galaxy with a well defined dust-lane. However, the outer low surface brightness part shows that the galaxy is embedded in a huge spheroidal envelope much more like an 0. The galaxy was already classified as an 0-a by and 0? by , whereas and quote Sb or Sbc.

gmw_00011348.name: NGC 4179
gmw_00011348.overview: NGC 4179 is a disk galaxy featuring a bright inner bulge and a rotated inner bar or bulge component [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164]. Its structure includes a disk- or bar-like radial feature [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164] and a face-on-equivalent broken profile with an increasing scalelength away from the major axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4179 with a face-on-equivalent broken profile, increasing scalelength away from the major axis, and agreement with previous thin/thick disk model fits.

quotes.quote: NGC 4179: See for additional notes. We find that the face-on-equivalent profile of NGC 4179 is well described as a with an inner shallow exponential (of 30.9 1.4 ) and an outer slightly steeper exponential ( 22.6 ) with a break at a radial distance of 98 3.2 and at a surface brightness of 23.3 V- . In the inner disk we find an increase in scalelength of 36 between the major axis and 4.5 times the thin disk scaleheight ( 6.1 ) reported by . Overall the scalelength increases by a factor of 1.8 to the highest measured cut at z 45 . This is very similar to the factor of 1.9 reported by for the ratio of thick to thick disk scalelength, obtained with a completely different method (3D model fitting).

sources.summary: NGC 4179 is described as having a rotated inner bar/bulge, a bright inner bulge, disk or bar-like radial feature, and residual evidence for a spheroidal bulge with an inner disk or bar.

quotes.quote: NGC 4179: ( n4179 ) The inner bar/bulge component is rotated 0.5 against the outer disk. The major axis shows a bright inner bulge, a disk- or bar-like feature between R 40 ( z 5 ), a more shoulder-like decline out to R 80 , here taken to be the main underlying disk, followed by a straight decline into the noise. The residual resembles a spheroidal bulge with an indication of an inner disk or bar extending slightly to both sides.

gmw_00011349.name: UGC 10459
gmw_00011349.overview: UGC 10459 is a disk galaxy characterized by a very small bulge component [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768]. Its major-axis profile displays a broken exponential structure with a shallow inner slope and a steeper outer slope [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 10459 as a small-bulge disk with a possible background galaxy and an obvious broken exponential major-axis profile separating shallow inner and steeper outer slopes.

quotes.quote: UGC 10459: This galaxy has only a very small bulge component. There is a possible background galaxy, with unknown distance, visible at (R,z) (+33 ,-9 ). The major axis profile shows an obvious broken exponential structure with a break radius at R 45 \ ( _ R 23.0 ) separating a shallow inner and a steeper outer slope. The single disk fit by yields a vertical scaleheight of 2.0 , a radial scalelength of h 13.4 , and an inclination of 87.0 . The face-on-equivalent profile of UGC 10459 is well described as a with an inner shallow exponential (of 15.6 2.7 ) and an outer rather steep exponential ( 5.7 ) with a break at a radial distance of 44 2.8 and at a surface brightness of 25.3 R- .

gmw_00011350.name: The BCG of RDCS 1252
gmw_00011350.overview: The BCG of RDCS 1252 is a brightest cluster galaxy that is not at rest with respect to its host cluster [arXiv:1305.0882]. It is centered in a merging subcluster and is expected to merge with an interacting companion galaxy [arXiv:1305.0882].

sources.summary: The paper describes the BCG of RDCS 1252 as offset in velocity within a merging subcluster system and likely to merge with a nearby interacting companion.

quotes.quote: The BCG of RDCS 1252, however, is not at rest with respect to its cluster. As noted in the previous section, RDCS 1252 appears to consist of two merging sub-clusters Demarco2007 , with the BCG centered in one sub-cluster and its companion centered in the other. It seems likely that cluster tides will play an important role in setting the timescale over which these two galaxies will merge. There seems little doubt that they will merge, as there is evidence of an interaction between the BCG and the 2nd brightest galaxy in this cluster Blakeslee2003,Rettura2006 Looking at the entire sample, we see that a few other BCGs are not at rest within their respective clusters.

gmw_00011351.name: Lac I
gmw_00011351.overview: Lac I is a gas-poor dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of Andromeda [arXiv:1701.08168]. Its stellar population is dominated by old stars that are predominantly metal-poor [arXiv:1701.08168].

sources.summary: The paper presents deep WIYN pODI imaging of Lac I, deriving its distance, structure, gas-poor dwarf-spheroidal nature, old stellar population, and metal-poor RGB metallicity distribution.

quotes.quote: Box 17000, Station Forces, Kingston, Ontario, K7K 7B4, Canada Kristine.Spekkens@rmc.ca We present results from WIYN pODI imaging of Lacerta I (And XXXI), a satellite dwarf galaxy discovered in the outskirts of the Andromeda galaxy (M31) in Pan-STARRS1 survey data. Our deep, wide-field g,i photometry reaches 3 magnitudes fainter than the photometry in the Pan-STARRS1 discovery paper and allows us to trace the stellar population of Lac I beyond two half-light radii from the galaxy center. We measure a Tip of the Red Giant Branch (TRGB) distance for Lac I of (m-M)_0=24.44 0.11 mag (773 40 kpc, or 264 6 kpc from M31), which is consistent with the Pan-STARRS1 distance. We use a maximum-likelihood technique to derive structural properties for the galaxy, and find a half-light radius ( r_h ) of 3.24 0.21 arcmin (728 47 pc), ellipticity ( ) of 0.44 0.03, total magnitude M_V = 11.4 0.3, and central surface brightness _ V,0 = 24.8 0.3 mag arcsec ^ 2 . We find no HI emission in archival data and set a limit on Lac I's neutral gas mass-to-light ratio of M_ HI /L_V < 0.06 M_ / L_ , confirming Lac I as a gas-poor dwarf spheroidal galaxy.

In this paper we presented results from deep, wide-field WIYN pODI g,i imaging of the M31 dwarf satellite galaxy Lac I (And XXXI), acquired in order to investigate the galaxy's structure, stellar populations, and metallicity. The CMD of this galaxy is dominated by an old ( 12 Gyr) stellar population and no intermediate-age stars (AGB stars) are apparent in our data.

Our investigation of the MDF of Lac I -- which we derived from photometry of the RGB stars with i_0 < 23 mag --- indicates that this galaxy is metal-poor, with a median [ Fe/H ] of 1.68 0.03 for stars within 2  r_h , but a broad range of stellar metallicities, from [ Fe/H ] 1 to 2.5 .

gmw_00011352.name: UM 462
gmw_00011352.overview: UM 462 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy characterized by relatively low metallicity [arXiv:astro-ph/0307062]. Optical observations of the galaxy reveal several young super star clusters, a stellar continuum, and two large bubble-like structures [arXiv:astro-ph/0307062].

sources.summary: The paper presents optical observations of UM 462, finding young super star clusters, low extinction, concentrated star formation, evolved underlying stars, and bubble-like structures likely from previous star formation.

quotes.quote: The galaxy UM 462 (UGC 06850) is a Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) of relatively low metallicity. Low resolution spectroscopy of this object was obtained by the objective prism survey of MacAlpine et al. (1983), higher quality data were collected later on by Terlevich et al. (1991). From these spectra Masegosa et al. (1994) derived a metallicity 12+Log(O/H)=7.98.

High quality optical data of the BCD galaxy UM 462 have been presented. The broad band images reveal several compact sources and two large bubbles. A narrow band image in H evidences the presence of further compact sources. Their luminosities qualify all compact sources observed as young Super Star Clusters or very large HII associations. The colors of the stellar continuum and the EW of H and Br all give fully consistent ages between few and about 25 Myr .

gmw_00011353.name: GTC-1
gmw_00011353.overview: GTC-1 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy with a diffuse, non-point source morphology [arXiv:2307.09521]. It is located near the warped disk of UGC 11859 and is considered a potential satellite companion [arXiv:2307.09521].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GTC-1 as a previously undetected low-surface-brightness diffuse galaxy near UGC 11859's warped disk, estimating its color, effective radius, stellar mass, and possible satellite status.

quotes.quote: Identification of a potential satellite galaxy Located close to the edge of the disk in the northeast region of \ we found a diffuse object previously not detected in SDSS and Gaia data ( =329.5591^ , =+1.0367^ ). The new object, which we identified as 21h58m07.33s/+01°00'31.95".Grantecan 1 and we will refer tentatively as GTC-1 due to its apparent -- although not yet confirmed -- association, shows a diffuse, non-point source morphology, eliminating the possibility of it being a faint star. The central surface brightness is particularly low _ g = 26.06^ +0.04 _ 0.08  mag arcsec ^ 2 ( g band) and _ = 25.50^ +0.15 _ 0.13  mag arcsec ^ 2 ( band), being similar to low surface brightness galaxies _0 > 24 \ in the SDSS g band, see Roman2021A&A656A44R . The very low surface brightness of GTC-1 explains its absence in the catalogs from previous surveys (SDSS), but the object is detectable in the Stripe 82 mosaics as well IACStripe82 .

GTC-1 is located at an angular distance of 45 arcsec from the detectable edge of , in the same direction of the distortion that we identified as a galactic warp. The average color ( g- ) of this object is 0.63^ +0.05 _ 0.07 mag (Fig. fig:ugc11859_df1 bottom left panel), a value significantly redder than the extremities of the main galaxy , but compatible with that of 's inner disk (see Fig. fig:UGC11895_radialprofiles ). Under the assumption that GTC-1 and \ have similar redshifts ( D=51.4  Mpc for , from 2010Ap&SS.325..163P ), GTC-1 would have a projected distance of 35  kpc from the center of . Following the recipes from Bakos2008 to estimate the surface stellar mass density from SDSS g and photometry, we compute the total stellar mass of GTC-1 (see right panel of Fig. fig:UGC11895_satelite ) to be _ 10 ( M_ ) =6.39^ +0.03 _ 0.03 (Fig. fig:ugc11859_df1 bottom right panel).

gmw_00011354.name: 1RXS J011501.3 340008
gmw_00011354.overview: 1RXS J011501.3 340008 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar located at a redshift of z = 0.482 [arXiv:2012.05176]. Its spectrum exhibits CaHK and CaIG absorption features, and it is hosted by a bright galaxy with an absolute magnitude of -23.3 [arXiv:2012.05176].

sources.summary: The paper confirms a firm z=0.482 redshift, finds CaHK and CaIG features, and fits a bright host galaxy with a possible anomalous star-formation history.

quotes.quote: The resulting spectrum has a median S/N = 20 and it has a general shape remarkably similar to the first one with a spectral break at the same wavelength. Given the similarity of the two spectra, we averaged them to measure the properties of the total emission ( Fig. fig_spec1 , first row, left). We detect the CaHK feature at 4 and the CaIG feature at 3.5 both at redshift z = 0.482. Given the stability of the source spectrum and despite the relatively low S/N we consider that this is a firm redshift. The spectral fit gives a bright host galaxy magnitude M _ R =-23.3 0.2.

gmw_00011355.name: 1RXS J015658.6 530208
gmw_00011355.overview: 1RXS J015658.6 530208 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar located at a redshift of z=0.304 [arXiv:2012.05176]. Its host galaxy is bright, with an absolute R-band magnitude of -22.7 [arXiv:2012.05176].

sources.summary: The paper detects host-galaxy absorption features at z=0.304 and derives a bright host magnitude after matching the spectrum to Swift/UVOT photometry.

quotes.quote: The source was clearly detected in both observations at a median continuum signal to noise ratio of 100. Inspection of the average spectrum shows the presence of clear CaHK and CaIG features at z =0.304 ( Fig. fig_spec1 , first row, right). This result is confirmed by the presence of a weaker Mgb feature at the same redshift while the CaFe feature falls into residual telluric absorption and is therefore undetectable.
The fit with a power law plus galaxy template model results in a bright host galaxy with M_ R =-22.7 0.5.

gmw_00011356.name: 1RXS J020922.2 522920
gmw_00011356.overview: 1RXS J020922.2 522920 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar [arXiv:2012.05176]. Its host galaxy is very bright, with a spectroscopic redshift of z = 0.2105 determined from absorption features [arXiv:2012.05176].

sources.summary: The paper measures a firm z=0.2105 spectroscopic redshift from absorption features and obtains a very bright host-galaxy magnitude.

quotes.quote: The source was clearly detected at a median continuum signal to noise ratio of 160. Careful inspection of the spectrum revealed the presence of a clear CaHK feature at z = 0.2105 (Fig. fig_spec1 , second row, left). This result is confirmed by the presence of weaker CaIG, Mgb and NaID features at the same redshift while the CaFe feature falls into residual telluric absorption and is therefore undetectable. This spectroscopic redshift contradicts the previously published statistical lower limit.
The host galaxy magnitude obtained from our fit is very bright at M_ R =-23.2 0.4.

gmw_00011357.name: PMN J1457 4642
gmw_00011357.overview: PMN J1457 4642 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2104.13333] located at a redshift of approximately z = 0.10 [arXiv:2104.13333] to z = 0.1116 [arXiv:2012.05176]. Its optical spectrum is dominated by the host galaxy [arXiv:2012.05176], showing absorption lines [arXiv:2104.13333] alongside weak hydrogen emission [arXiv:2012.05176, arXiv:2104.13333].

sources.summary: The paper detects absorption and possible H emission features in 3FHL J1457.8 4642, measures z=0.10, classifies it as a BL Lac, and notes no Fermi SED class.

quotes.quote: In the optical spectrum, absorption lines were detected at 5744 and 5851 . These lines are attributed to Mg i and Ca + Fe lines, respectively. An emission line detected at 5372 (EW = 0.6 ) could possibly be due to H . These features give us a redshift measurement of z= 0.10. We classify this source as a BL Lac.
This source has no reported SED class in the Fermi LAT catalogs.

sources.summary: The paper presents the first reported spectroscopy, finding a host-dominated z=0.1116 spectrum with weak emission and an imperfect old stellar-population template fit.

quotes.quote: PMN J1457 4642 is one of the weakest and least studied of the gamma-ray sources in our sample and no spectroscopic observation has been reported. Its EFOSC2 spectrum (see Fig. fig_spec1 , third row, left) is clearly dominated by the emission of the host galaxy at a redshift z = 0.1116, with a measurable component from a non-thermal power law and a weak (EW 5 ) H [ NII ] emission complex. The rest-frame power law/galaxy ratio at 5500 is 0.3 0.2. We note that the galaxy spectral shape cannot be well fitted with the local template of Man01 . A better, though not completely satisfying fit can be achieved with a Bru03 template built with a 11 Gyr simple stellar population.

gmw_00011358.name: NVSS J151148 051345
gmw_00011358.overview: NVSS J151148 051345 is a high-energy blazar [arXiv:2012.05176] that exhibits a non-thermal, featureless optical continuum [arXiv:1711.05476]. Spectroscopic observations of a magnesium II absorption doublet establish a firm lower limit on its redshift of z > 0.449 [arXiv:2012.05176, arXiv:1711.05476].

sources.summary: The paper observes NVSS J151148 051345 to test a prior MgII interpretation and concludes that its redshift is firmly greater than 0.449.

quotes.quote: The HBL Tak13 NVSS J151148 051345 displays a featureless optical spectrum Alv16 . Recently, in an S/N 200, resolution / 250, GTC spectrum, a single feature around 4053 with EW=2.1 was detected Pai17 . Interpreting this feature as an unresolved MgII doublet implies that the redshift of the source is greater than 0.45. To investigate this result we observed the source with EFOSC2 using Gr 14 which allows for / 600 in the 3500-5000 wavelength range.

We obtained a S/N=23 spectrum in which we detect a double-peaked spectral feature at the position discussed above with total equivalent width 2.6 0.3 (see Fig. fig_spec1 , third row, right). We fit the feature with a MgII doublet using vpfit Cars14 obtaining a ^2_ dof 1.07 for a redshift z = 0.449 0.0003. We consider as a firm result, therefore, that the redshift of NVSS J151148 051345 is greater than 0.449.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3FGL1511.8-0513 as a BCU-III source associated with NVSSJ151148-051345, finding a high-S/N featureless continuum and a single absorption line implying z > 0.45.

quotes.quote: 3FGL1511.8-0513 The source is classified as a BCU-III and associated to the radio source NVSSJ151148-051345 in the 3LAC catalog. The optical spectrum of alvarez2016A was found featureless. We obtain a high S/N ratio ( 200) spectrum that exhibits a marked non thermal featureless continuum form 4100 to 8500 . We detect a single absorption at 4053 with EW = 2.1 that, if interpreted as Mg II (2800 ), sets a spectroscopic lower limit of the redshift of z > 0.45.

gmw_00011359.name: NVSS J152048 034850
gmw_00011359.overview: NVSS J152048 034850 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2012.05176], classified as either a low-frequency peaked or intermediate-frequency peaked blazar [arXiv:2012.05176]. Spectroscopic observations of a magnesium II absorption doublet confirm that the galaxy has a redshift greater than 0.868 [arXiv:2012.05176].

sources.summary: The paper treats NVSS J152048 034850 as a possible high-redshift BL Lac and confirms a firm MgII lower limit of z>0.868.

quotes.quote: NVSS J152048 034850 is a BL Lac possibly an LBL or an IBL Tak13 . Shaw13 report a low S/N detection of an MgII system at z = 0.867 in a Palomar spectrum. Additionally Kau17 report a redshift estimation of z = 1.46 0.1 from a photometric detection of the Ly break using Swift /UVOT and GROND photometry. In order to assess this lower limit, we observed this source with EFOSC2 employing Gr 8 with wavelength range 4500-6200 and resolution 7.4 achieving signal-to-noise ratio S/N=43.

We detect a clearly asymmetric spectral feature at the position reported by Shaw13 with total equivalent width 3.65 0.7 . Single component fits yielded unacceptable chi square values and line widths up to three times the spectral resolution. We then fit the feature with a MgII doublet using vpfit Cars14 obtaining a ^2_ dof 1.0 for a redshift z =0.868 0.002 (see Fig. fig_spec1 , fourth row, right). Therefore we consider a firm result that the redshift of NVSS J152048 034850 is greater than 0.868.

gmw_00011360.name: WISE J154824.39 + 145702.8
gmw_00011360.overview: WISE J154824.39 + 145702.8 is a gas-rich BL Lacertae object host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.231 [arXiv:2012.05176]. Its optical spectrum is dominated by the host galaxy emission, which is classified as a LINER [arXiv:2012.05176].

sources.summary: The paper confirms host-dominated z=0.231 emission, detects multiple lines, and classifies the gas-rich host galaxy as a LINER.

quotes.quote: The optical counterpart of WISE J154824.39 + 145702.8 is classified as a galaxy in SDSS Ahu19 with photometric redshift z = 0.217 0.02 . Alv16b performed a 40 minute observation with DOLORES at the Telescopio Nazionale Galileo. The resulting 3700 - 8100 spectrum is dominated by the host galaxy emission at a redshift of z = 0.231. We took a one hour spectrum of WISE J154824.39 + 145702.8 with ESI at Keck. We confirm the result of Alv16b that the optical emission of the source is dominated by the host galaxy at z = 0.231 (see Fig. fig_spec2 , first row, right).

Some lines have equivalent width greater than 5 , the standard BL Lac limit, showing that this host galaxy is peculiarly active and gas-rich for a BL Lac. In order to investigate this issue, we measured their equivalent widths by fitting Gaussian functions, the results are listed in Table WISEtab . Computing the ratios [ OIII ] / [ OII ] and [ OI ] /H and using Fig. 5 in Kew06 , the host galaxy can be classified as a LINER.

gmw_00011361.name: NVSS J163750 344915
gmw_00011361.overview: NVSS J163750 344915 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2012.05176]. Its spectrum is featureless, leaving the redshift of the galaxy currently undetermined [arXiv:2012.05176].

sources.summary: The paper confirms NVSS J163750 344915 as a BL Lac with a new but featureless EFOSC2 spectrum, leaving the redshift undetermined.

quotes.quote: Pen17 reported on a spectrum of NVSS J163750 344915 obtained with the Goodman Spectrograph at SOAR (4000-7000 ). The spectrum has S/N = 60 and is featureless confirming its BL Lac classification. We took an EFOSC2 spectrum of the object aiming to obtain a better S/N. However we could only integrate for 45 min due to bad weather.
The resulting spectrum has a S/N = 80 and is featureless (see Fig. fig_spec2 , second row, left). The redshift of NVSS J163750 344915 remains undetermined.

gmw_00011362.name: GB6 J1838 + 4802
gmw_00011362.overview: GB6 J1838 + 4802 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar with a photometrically estimated redshift of approximately 0.3 [arXiv:2012.05176]. Although its host galaxy has been detected in imaging observations, spectroscopic observations yielded a featureless spectrum due to the bright nuclear emission outshining the host [arXiv:2012.05176].

sources.summary: The paper reports a featureless high-S/N spectrum, arguing the bright nuclear state likely hid the host-galaxy features needed for a redshift.

quotes.quote: No spectroscopic redshift measure is available for GB6 J1838 + 4802 but its redshift has been photometrically estimated at z 0.3 from the detection of the host galaxy at magnitude R _ =19.12 0.05 in imaging observations Nil03 . This detection supports the possibility of measuring the redshift. However our observation of GB6 J1838 + 4802 resulted in a featureless spectrum (Fig. fig_spec2 , second row, right), despite its very high S/N ( 250). We note that this non-detection may be due to the brightness of the source, at R _ 15.8, the nuclear emission is about 3.3 magnitudes (about 20 times) stronger than the host emission. In a case like this one, the best option may be to trigger another spectroscopic observation during a minimum in the optical light curve.

gmw_00011363.name: MITG J184126 + 2910
gmw_00011363.overview: MITG J184126 + 2910 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2012.05176]. Its optical spectrum is mostly featureless, but weak absorption features from the host galaxy indicate a redshift of z = 0.288 [arXiv:2012.05176].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a BL Lac counterpart, detects weak host-galaxy absorption features at z=0.288, and finds a bright but expected host magnitude.

quotes.quote: The optical counterpart of MITG J184126 + 2910 was identified by Marchesini16 . The moderate S/N, featureless, optical spectrum they took with the DOLORES spectrograph at the Telescopio Nazionale Galileo confirmed its classification as a BL Lac. A later spectrum Marchesi18 taken with the KOSMOS spectrograph at the Mayall Telescope is also featureless. We note that the infrared counterpart, 2MASX J18412170 + 2909404, is extended, suggesting that the emission of the host galaxy may be detectable.

Our Keck/ESI spectrum (see Fig. fig_spec2 , third row, left) reaches an average S/N ratio of 100. In the mostly featureless spectrum we could identify the CaHK doublet and CaIG features at 5 and 4 respectively at a redshift z = 0.288. We also possibly identify at the same redshift the Mgb and CaFe features at about 3-4 . The absolute magnitude of the host galaxy is M _ R =-22.9 0.3, bright but within the expected range.

gmw_00011364.name: SDSSJ1020+1122
gmw_00011364.overview: SDSSJ1020+1122 is a lens galaxy observed by the Hubble Space Telescope [arXiv:2403.16253]. Photometric modeling of the galaxy's emission reveals that it possesses twisted isophotal contours [arXiv:2403.16253].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ1020+1122 as an example where the MGE model fits lens-galaxy emission with 180 Gaussians and captures twisted isophotal contours.

quotes.quote: Overview An illustrative example of applying our MGE lens light model to a real HST observed image, SDSSJ1020+1122, is shown in Fig. fig: overview . The lens galaxy's emission is fitted using a total of 180 Gaussians, which are grouped into 6 sets of 30 Gaussians, where in each set the Gaussians share the same axis ratios, position angles and centre. The source galaxy is fitted simultaneously with the MGE, using a Voronoi mesh which reconstructs its irregular morphological appearance. The MGE fits all of the lens galaxy's emission and effectively captures its twisted isophotal contours, as illustrated in the bottom middle panel of Fig. fig: overview .

gmw_00011365.name: SDSS J0807+4946
gmw_00011365.overview: SDSS J0807+4946 is a radio-loud type 2 quasar [arXiv:1301.1677]. It is identified as a Fanaroff-Riley II double-lobed radio galaxy that exhibits an extended emission feature aligned with its radio axis [arXiv:1301.1677].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J0807+4946 as a radio-loud type 2 quasar with an FR II double-lobed radio morphology and one extended emission feature aligned with its radio axis.

quotes.quote: The FIRST image centered on the position of SDSS J0807+4946 shows an FR II fana74 double-lobed radio galaxy oriented at 60 ^ East of North. The maximal lobe distance from the host galaxy is 50 \ (330 kpc). While there is no apparent radio core component, the two lobes are symmetric around the SDSS position, and there is no other compelling candidate host galaxy for the radio source. Therefore, we identify SDSS J0807 + 4946 with the radio source 87GB 0804 + 4955, which has a spectral index of =-1.0 ( F_ ^ ) between 80 cm and 6 cm beck91 .
Of the four extended emission features seen in our three radio-loud sources, only one feature in SDSS J0807+4946 lies along its radio axis.

gmw_00011366.name: SDSS J1101+4004
gmw_00011366.overview: SDSS J1101+4004 is a radio-intermediate type 2 quasar [arXiv:1301.1677]. It features a pair of symmetric radio lobes located 230 kiloparsecs away from its point-source nucleus [arXiv:1301.1677].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J1101+4004 as a radio-intermediate type 2 quasar with symmetric radio lobes around the nucleus and measured northwest and southeast lobe fluxes.

quotes.quote: The radio flux of SDSS J1101+4004 places it in the radio-intermediate regime xu99,zaka04 , but the FIRST image clearly shows a pair of radio lobes oriented at 112 ^ East of North and symmetrically located at 40 \ (230 kpc) away from the nucleus, which is a point source with a peak flux of 17.48 mJy/beam beck95 . Using the FIRST data, we find the integrated flux to be 45 mJy and 21 mJy for the northwest and southeast radio lobes, respectively.

For each object in each band, we took two dithered exposures of 1800 sec each with an 0 5 offset along the direction of the longer (7 ) side of the rectangular field of view, with the exception of SDSS J1101+4004 where only one 1800 sec exposure was obtained.

gmw_00011367.name: SDSS J010649.39+010322.4
gmw_00011367.overview: SDSS J010649.39+010322.4 is a LINER galaxy located at the center of the RXC J0106.8+0103 galaxy cluster [arXiv:1204.3162]. It is luminous at ultraviolet wavelengths and hosts extended star formation that is resolved out by MERLIN interferometry [arXiv:1204.3162].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J010649.39+010322.4 as a LINER cluster-center galaxy whose X-rays are cluster dominated, with faint possible jet emission and extended star formation resolved out by MERLIN.

quotes.quote: SDSS J010649.39+010322.4 (5): This LINER galaxy lies at the heart of the galaxy cluster RXC J0106.8+0103 or Zw 348 e.g., 2000MNRAS.318..333E,2000ApJS..129..435B . In this cluster, the AGN is expected to be a major contaminant of the overall X-ray emission 2000ApJS..129..435B . However, from the small R_X and R^* values in Fig. fig:RXRO , it can be seen that the X-rays still appear to be dominated by the cluster emission. The FIRST image reveals a slight extended emission component to the southeast in addition to the compact core (the extend parameter _ FIRST 1.3 ), which, being barely above the noise level, could be an indication for a jet component. Our MERLIN image does not reveal any extended emission, since it might resolve out faint extended emission.
This galaxy is also luminous at UV wavelengths (based on GALEX data). It lies on the boundary of between large UV luminous galaxies (UVLGs) and compact UVLGs as defined by .
Our MERLIN results show a much smaller SFR, which indicates that the star formation is significantly extended throughout the host and is resolved out by the interferometer.

gmw_00011368.name: SDSS J074738.38+245637.3
gmw_00011368.overview: SDSS J074738.38+245637.3 is an early-type Seyfert 1 galaxy that has a companion galaxy [arXiv:1204.3162]. Low surface-brightness features between the two galaxies suggest a gravitational interaction, which may drive the system's star formation [arXiv:1204.3162].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J074738.38+245637.3 as an early-type Seyfert 1 with a companion, interaction signatures, large-aperture star formation, and mostly resolved-out FIRST radio emission.

quotes.quote: SDSS J074738.38+245637.3 (6): This early-type Seyfert 1 has a companion SDSS J074738.25+245626.1 at z=0.131 . Low surface-brightness features in the SDSS images are located between the two galaxies. Gravitational interaction could therefore be responsible for the SFR of 50M_ yr ^ 1 estimated from the large aperture data. MERLIN appears to resolve out a fraction of about 80 of the FIRST emission. The resulting SFR estimate is comparable to the [ OII ] one, which is about 10M_ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00011369.name: SDSS J081026.07+234156.1
gmw_00011369.overview: SDSS J081026.07+234156.1 is classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1204.3162]. It exhibits a compact optical appearance and is characterized by very faint radio emission [arXiv:1204.3162].

sources.summary: The paper classifies SDSS J081026.07+234156.1 as an NLS1 with compact optical appearance and very faint radio emission, becoming the second most radio-quiet AGN under the MERLIN measurement.

quotes.quote: SDSS J081026.07+234156.1 (8): classified this AGN as an NLS1 galaxy. Its optical appearance is compact. It is the radio faintest object in our sample. With the MERLIN measurement, it becomes the second most radio quiet AGN, R^*_ MERLIN 0.3 .

gmw_00011370.name: SDSS J162332.27+284128.7
gmw_00011370.overview: SDSS J162332.27+284128.7 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1204.3162]. It exhibits modest radio loudness despite a jet that is likely viewed almost face-on [arXiv:1204.3162].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J162332.27+284128.7 as a BL Lac object with no usable spectral features for a black-hole mass estimate and unexpectedly modest radio loudness despite likely face-on jet viewing.

quotes.quote: SDSS J162332.27+284128.7 (9): This optically unresolved object has been classified as a BL Lac object 2008AJ....135.2453P . Due to the absence of spectral features, no black hole mass could be estimated. This might also be a problem for its redshift, which is taken from the SDSS database. This object is not particularly radio loud (Fig. fig:RXRO ), though we are supposed to observe the jet almost face on.

gmw_00011371.name: SDSS J080644.41+484149.2
gmw_00011371.overview: SDSS J080644.41+484149.2 is a radio-loud quasar classified as a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy [arXiv:1204.3162]. The object appears optically unresolved and features compact nuclear radio emission that may not be strongly dominated by its jet component [arXiv:1204.3162].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J080644.41+484149.2 as the sample's only FRII radio-loud QSO, optically unresolved, with compact nuclear emission that may not be strongly jet dominated.

quotes.quote: SDSS J080644.41+484149.2 (11): This source is the only FRII RL QSO 2006AJ....131..666D in our sample, having a nearby natural guide star ( SDSS J080645.63+484156.6 ). Its optical appearance is unresolved. Although being RL in the classical sense, the R_X measurement would still result in a RQ AGN. We have only used the compact nuclear radio emission. This could mean that the 40pc scale emission not that strongly influenced by the jet component.

gmw_00011372.name: SDSS J092710.60+532731.6
gmw_00011372.overview: SDSS J092710.60+532731.6 is a radio galaxy that exhibits a passive, elliptical optical spectrum [arXiv:1204.3162]. It is likely the brightest cluster galaxy in the Zw 2379 galaxy cluster and hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1204.3162].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J092710.60+532731.6 as a radio galaxy with a passive elliptical spectrum, likely the brightest galaxy in Zw 2379, and AGN-like high EVN brightness temperature.

quotes.quote: SDSS J092710.60+532731.6 (12): This radio galaxy shows a passive, elliptical optical spectrum. It has a nearby natural guide star ( SDSS J092707.09+532742.3 ) and is likely the brightest cluster galaxy in the Zw 2379 galaxy cluster z=0.205 ; 2008MNRAS.384.1502S . Its high EVN brightness temperature underlines the AGN nature of the radio emission.

gmw_00011373.name: J2156+1103
gmw_00011373.overview: The galaxy J2156+1103 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1208.1303]. Its broad emission lines confirm the presence of an active galactic nucleus, though its Balmer emission is contaminated by substantial star formation within the host [arXiv:1208.1303].

sources.summary: The paper says J2156+1103 falls in the star-forming region of the diagnostic diagram; broad lines confirm an AGN, but host star formation likely contaminates its Balmer emission.

quotes.quote: Two of our galaxies, J1143+5500 and J2156+1103, fall into the star-forming region as denoted by the Kauffman et al. dashed line, indicating contamination from H 2 regions within the host galaxies. While the presence of broad emission lines in our objects confirms the presence of an AGN in these two galaxies, they must have substantial contributions from star formation within the host galaxy.
However, only two sources, J1143+5500 and J2156+1103, lie far enough from the AGN locus to be consistent with this level of contamination (Fig. contour ).

gmw_00011374.overview: The radio source 0044+00 is a quasar that exhibits a resolved, misaligned structure at radio frequencies [arXiv:1304.3021]. Its morphology is interpreted as a core-jet system, where component A likely represents the core region and jet emission [arXiv:1304.3021].

sources.summary: The paper resolves 0044+00 into misaligned VLBA components, interprets component A as a likely core region with jet emission, and classifies the morphology as probably core-jet.

quotes.quote: 0044+00 Both maps at 5 GHz and 8.4 GHz show a resolved structure. In particular at 5 GHz, three misaligned components are present: two of them are clearly visible, the third is embedded in some diffuse structure. While a Gaussian fit was possible for components A and B, for component C it did not converge since it barely stands against some diffuse emission, and only an upper limit for its flux-density could be obtained. In case the map at 8.4 GHz was not available, component C would not have been considered. In this 8.4 GHz map, much deeper than that at 5 GHz, only components A and C are visible, suggesting a steep spectral index for component B whose surface brightness drops below the detection level.

Component A has a rather flat spectral index ( 0.52 0.27) and could be most probably classified as the core region, in which part of the jet is also visible, as suggested by the elongation in p.a. 176^ . Although it is not straightforward, the morphology of this source can be interpreted as a core--jet.

gmw_00011375.overview: The radio-loud quasar 1102+11 features a parsec-scale structure resolved into two components separated by 4.1 milliarcseconds [arXiv:1304.3021]. The strongest component exhibits a steep spectral index, making it unlikely to be the source core or an active hot spot [arXiv:1304.3021].

sources.summary: The paper resolves 1102+11 into two steep-spectrum components, finds component A unlikely to be the core or an active hot spot, and leaves the parsec-scale morphology unclear.

quotes.quote: 1102+11 The map at 5 GHz shows a structure resolved into two components, which are separated by 4.1 mas ( 34 pc), in p.a. 53^ . At 8.4 GHz only the strongest component (A) has been detected, and it turned out to be resolved along the same direction. Its spectral index is 0.97 0.29. Such a steep spectrum makes it unlikely that component A is the source core.

It is also too steep to be an active hot spot. Component B has probably an even steeper spectral index, since the flux density is below the 3- significance limit at 8.4 GHz. All this makes the morphological classification of the pc-scale structure rather unclear.

gmw_00011376.overview: The radio source 1237+47 is resolved into three aligned components with a projected linear size of 40 pc [arXiv:1304.3021]. Its central component is interpreted as a core, while the outer components represent jets and a hot spot from a young, variable active phase [arXiv:1304.3021].

sources.summary: The paper resolves 1237+47 into aligned components, interprets component A as a possible core with jet and hot-spot features, and suggests a young variable radio source.

quotes.quote: 1237+47 This source is resolved into three components at 5 GHz, with the strongest one in the centre (A). At 8.4 GHz, the eastern component C is resolved in two subcomponents (C1 and C2), both aligned with A and B. Component A has an inverted spectral index (0.41 0.29) and thus could be interpreted as the core, while B and C1 present elongated emission toward A, with a similar flux density at 8.4 GHz. It could be the inner part of the jets, with an additional hot spot (C2) visible in the direction of C1. The projected linear size from X band is 4.8 mas (40 pc).

Thus, this object can be a young radio source in which the core region is still the brightest in the source. Moreover, at 8.4 GHz its flux density exceeds ( 37 ) the total flux density in our earlier VLA observation. This could indicate that there is still a very active phase in which new components are likely to be formed on short timescales, creating the variability in the flux density.

gmw_00011377.overview: The radio-loud quasar 1304+13 has a steep overall radio spectrum that does not peak in the gigahertz range [arXiv:1304.3021]. High-resolution observations resolve the source into a central component and two bright edge components, spanning a total projected linear size of 44 parsecs [arXiv:1304.3021].

sources.summary: The paper resolves 1304+13 at 5 and 8.4 GHz, locating its brightest components at the source edges, detecting a central component only at 8.4 GHz, and finding a steep spectrum.

quotes.quote: 1304+13 It was possible to resolve this source both at 5 GHz and at 8.4 GHz: in the maps, the two brightest components are on the edges of the radio source (A and B), while the central component C is distinguishable only at 8.4 GHz. The fit did not converge for this last component, so we could only estimate the flux density upper limit. The total projected linear size of the source at 8.4 GHz is 5.3 mas (44 pc). The overall spectrum is steep, and does not present a peak in the GHz range, which is the only one that could be investigated at the moment.

gmw_00011378.overview: The quasar 1406+34 is unresolved in VLBA observations, indicating a compact size of less than 14 parsecs [arXiv:1304.3021]. It exhibits optically thick, self-absorbed synchrotron emission with an inverted spectrum on parsec scales, alongside possible remnants of older radio activity [arXiv:1304.3021].

sources.summary: The paper finds 1406+34 unresolved in both VLBA bands, with compact inverted-spectrum pc-scale synchrotron emission and possible older steep-spectrum radio activity resolved out on larger scales.

quotes.quote: 1406+34 This source is unresolved in both bands with the VLBA. We can set an upper limit of 1.8 mas (14 pc) to its size. The map at 8.4 GHz shows a noise pattern that is not related to any extended structure. The pc-scale emission revealed by the present observations has an inverted spectrum ( =0.36 0.26), and we are therefore seeing the optically thick, self-absorbed part of the synchrotron emission. The overall radio spectrum peaks at about 5.4 GHz.

The VLBA flux-density measurements at 5 GHz accounts only for 67 that of the VLA ( _ var =4.8), owing either to some intrinsic variability or to an additional component fully resolved on the pc-scale. Such a component, with a steep spectrum, may be responsible for the 327 MHz emission detected in the WENSS survey ( Bruyn ), which could be the remnant of an earlier episode of radio activity and which also modifies the spectral slope at low frequencies.

gmw_00011379.overview: The radio-loud quasar 0816+48 is elongated toward the southeast on milliarcsecond scales, indicating a projected linear size of less than 51 parsecs [arXiv:1304.3021]. At larger scales, it exhibits a southwest elongation that suggests intermittent activity and a change in jet orientation [arXiv:1304.3021].

sources.summary: The paper finds 0816+48 elongated on EVN scales, with a differently oriented VLA-scale extension, suggesting intermittent activity and a possible change in jet orientation.

quotes.quote: 0816+48 This source shows an elongation toward SE. From the major axis of the Gaussian fit (6.8 mas) we can infer an upper limit of projected linear size of 51 pc. A clear classification of the spectrum was not possible in our previous work, but the VLA map at 1.4 GHz already showed an elongation toward SW, not corresponding with the one present here, and with a major and minor axes of 29 and 14 arcsec, corresponding to 217 and 105 kpc, respectively. This could be another case of intermittent activity with a change in the jet orientation between the two phases. Further observations at the intermediate VLA resolution could confirm this hypothesis.

gmw_00011380.overview: The radio source 1014+05 exhibits a complex morphology with a possible core region and two northern components [arXiv:1304.3021]. This structure is aligned at a position angle of 0 degrees and has a projected linear size of 229 parsecs [arXiv:1304.3021].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1014+05 as morphologically complex, with a possible core and two northern components, a 229 pc projected size, and missing EVN flux from variability or unsampled structure.

quotes.quote: 1014+05 This source presents a complex morphology whose proper classification is rather difficult, because the brightest component (A) can be interpreted as the core region, and there are two more components (B and C) toward north. The overall structure is well aligned in p.a. 0 ^ . The distance between the centroids of A and C components is 26.6 mas, corresponding to a projected linear size of 229 pc. In our earlier work we found a power-law spectrum for this source, but more data below 1.4 GHz are needed to exclude any turnover in the MHz range. Our EVN map can account for 59 of the total flux density measured in the VLA map ( _ var =8.2), since this discrepancy may arise either from some flux-density variability or from an additional structure not sampled by the EVN spacings.

gmw_00011381.overview: The quasar 1327+03 is an unresolved radio source [arXiv:1304.3021]. It exhibits a power-law spectrum that shows no turnover down to a frequency of 1.4 GHz [arXiv:1304.3021].

sources.summary: The paper reports 1327+03 as unresolved, gives beam-limited upper limits to its linear size, and notes that its power-law spectrum shows no turnover down to 1.4 GHz.

quotes.quote: 1327+03 This is an unresolved source. The upper limit for the linear size, estimated from the Gaussian fit, is 46 pc (5.9 mas) and 11 pc (1.4 mas), respectively, on the major and minor axes, but the beam is considerably elliptical in this map so this can only be an indicative value. A power-law spectrum down to 1.4 GHz does not show any turnover for this source.

gmw_00011382.name: HSC J083913.17+011308.1
gmw_00011382.overview: HSC J083913.17+011308.1 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at z=4.72 [arXiv:2208.14632]. It resides on the outskirts of a Lyman break galaxy overdensity, suggesting that the galaxy is associated with a protocluster [arXiv:2208.14632].

sources.summary: The paper describes HSC J083913.17+011308.1 as a z=4.72 radio galaxy on the outskirts of a strong LBG overdensity, likely associated with a protocluster.

quotes.quote: We report on the galaxy density environment around a high- z radio galaxy (HzRG) at z=4.72 , HSC J083913.17+011308.1 (HSC J0839+0113), probed using an dropout Lyman break galaxy (LBG) sample from the Hyper Suprime-Cam Subaru Strategic Program data. We find that HSC J0839+0113 resides in the outskirt of an overdense region identified by the dropout galaxies at a 4.7 significance level. The projected distance between HSC J0839+0113 and the peak position of the overdense region is 0.4 physical Mpc which is shorter than the typical protocluster radius in this epoch. According to the extended Press Schechter and the light cone models, the HSC J0839+0113-hosted overdense region is expected to evolve into a halo >10^ 14 M_ at z=0 with a high probability of >80 . These findings suggest that HSC J0839+0113 is associated with a protocluster.

gmw_00011383.name: WMH 13
