gmw_00011383.overview: WMH 13 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6 [arXiv:1202.5330]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy reveal strong Lyman-alpha emission with a rest-frame equivalent width exceeding 25 angstroms [arXiv:1202.5330].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WMH 13 as an unambiguous z~6 Lyman-break galaxy with strong Ly emission, the strongest line among the noisy spectra and rest-frame equivalent width above 25 A.

quotes.quote: Strong \ emission was only found in two of the z 6 galaxies, WMH 5 and WMH 13. These are assigned QF=A.

These two spectra have the highest noise (along with WMH 13 which has the strongest \ line) which likely accounts for the lack of a clear redshift solution. The best fit redshift of z=5.645 for WMH 6 is quite unlikely given its color of i'-z'=2.1 (see Section completeness ). We note that in any color-selected sample there is a possibility for rare interlopers with unusual colors (e.g. Capak et al. 2011; Hayes et al. 2012) to masquerade as high-redshift galaxies. However, based on the evidence we have and the stringent color cuts, we expect there to be very few of these in our sample. Only WMH 13 out of the seven color-selected spectroscopic targets has a \ line with rest-frame equivalent width >25 \ giving a fraction of 14 .

gmw_00011384.name: WMH 29
gmw_00011384.overview: WMH 29 is a Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 5.757 [arXiv:1202.5330]. It exhibits weak Lyman-alpha emission, with an observed continuum break below 8500 Angstroms that disfavors a lower-redshift interpretation [arXiv:1202.5330].

sources.summary: The paper treats WMH 29 as a likely z~5.757 Lyman-break galaxy with weak but significant Ly emission; the line is too weak to prove asymmetry, but its continuum break disfavors a lower-redshift interpretation.

quotes.quote: In addition, weak, but still significant, \ was observed in WMH 29 which is assigned QF=B. Note that this \ line at z=5.757 is in a region of very low sky noise. It is doubtful that such a line would have been significantly detected at slightly higher redshift. Due to the weakness of this line it cannot be determined if it is asymmetric and therefore definitely . If it is instead \ at z=1.20 then the 4000 \ break would be expected at 8790 , whereas the continuum break is observed to be at <8500 .

gmw_00011385.name: J122531.50-360715.3
gmw_00011385.overview: J122531.50-360715.3 is a highly disturbed dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1511.06707]. It is located at a projected distance of approximately 100 kpc from Tol 65, serving as its only potential tidal perturber [arXiv:1511.06707].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J122531.50-360715.3 as a likely disturbed dwarf near Tol 65, with similar systemic velocity and possible relevance as the only potential perturber.

quotes.quote: The first one J122531.50-360715.3 is an irregular (dIrr) and likely dwarf galaxy with a difference between their respective systemic velocities of V = -4 km s ^ 1 . ESO Digitized Sky Survey (DSS) images show that this object is highly disturbed with an apparent size of 30 equivalent with 6.2 kpc, which is two times the apparent size of Tol 65. The projected distance between these galaxies is _ p 100 kpc.

In any case, J122531.50-360715.3 remains as the only potential perturber. In this sense, the slight enhancement of the SFR in region no. 1 with respect to region no. 2 and the lopsidedness of those star-forming regions could be the consequence of this tidal interaction. Although it is unlikely that another dwarf galaxy of low mass, as it seems, could have a tidal effect at this distance.

gmw_00011386.name: ESO 380-G029
gmw_00011386.overview: ESO 380-G029 is a spiral galaxy located in the same field as the galaxy Tol 65 [arXiv:1511.06707]. Due to its velocity, it is unlikely that this galaxy is currently producing the tidally-disturbed tail present in Tol 65 [arXiv:1511.06707].

sources.summary: The paper discusses ESO 380-G029 as a spiral near Tol 65 whose velocity makes current tidal influence unlikely, while leaving a speculative past interaction unresolved.

quotes.quote: The other object in the field is the spiral galaxy ESO 380-G029. For this galaxy we found a V = 1249 km s ^ 1 , thus it is unlikely that this galaxy is producing the tidally-disturbed tail present in the galaxy, because companions with V > 500 km s ^ 1 have not a significant dynamical influence Noeske2001 . It is interesting to note that the tail of the galaxy is oriented, in the plane of the sky, to this spiral galaxy. Possibly ESO 380-G029 had an stronger influence over Tol 65 in the past (several Gyr ago), thus the tail in the latter could be a remnant of that tidal interaction. However, there is no observational evidence showing that this galaxy had an interaction in the past with Tol 65 to produce a tidal tail.

gmw_00011387.name: NGC 0165
gmw_00011387.overview: NGC 0165 is a spiral galaxy that contains numerous star-forming regions [arXiv:1601.01542]. Observations of the galaxy have mapped seventy-two distinct ionized regions and revealed a clear oxygen-abundance gradient across its structure [arXiv:1601.01542].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 0165 as a CALIFA spiral example, mapping star-forming spaxels, identifying false AGN-classified regions, detecting 72 ionized regions, and illustrating its oxygen-abundance gradient.

quotes.quote: The top right panel of Fig. fig:bpt_Hamaps shows the location of the selected spaxels in a particular spiral galaxy of the sample, NGC 0165, over-plotted to the H _ map. Blue dots correspond to the spaxels classified as SF regions and red dots are those that lie higher than the kewley2001 curve and can therefore be associated with AGNs. The figure shows that the selected spaxels follow the H _ emission. The classification of red points as ionised by AGNs in this galaxy as well as in other cases is clearly false because of their distance to the galactic centres.

Figure fig:bpt_Hamaps (bottom right) shows an example of an H map for one spiral galaxy of the sample, NGC 0165, where the location of the regions is superimposed as black segmented contours. For this galaxy we detected 72 regions.

In Fig. fig:grads_examples we present an example of the abundance gradient for the same galaxy shown in the right panels of Fig. fig:bpt_Hamaps , NGC 0165, using both the spaxel-wise information (colour map) and the individual regions (violet dots). The diamonds represent the mean oxygen abundance values of the radial bins, with the error bars indicating the corresponding standard deviations.

gmw_00011388.name: LADUMA J033046.20 275518.1
gmw_00011388.overview: LADUMA J033046.20 275518.1 is an infrared-luminous radio galaxy classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2204.02523]. The system hosts a luminous hydroxyl megamaser [arXiv:2204.02523], exhibits a bent-tail radio morphology [arXiv:2204.02523], and shows indications of a hydroxyl outflow [arXiv:2204.02523].

sources.summary: The paper reports LADUMA J033046.20 275518.1 as a z>0.5 OH megamaser in an infrared-luminous radio galaxy, with ULIRG luminosity, bent-tail morphology, and possible OH outflow.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The spatial centroid of LADUMA J033046.20--275518.1 lies at right ascension 03:30:46.20 and declination 27:55:18.17 (J2000) with uncertainties of 0.7^ (calculated as beam size divided by S/N) --- consistent with the centroids determined for its counterparts at other wavelengths, within their own uncertainties of 0.5^ or greater.

quotes.quote: Using observations for the Looking At the Distant Universe with the MeerKAT Array (LADUMA) deep H i survey, we report the first untargeted detection of an OHM at z > 0.5 , LADUMA J033046.20 275518.1 (nicknamed ``Nkalakatha''). The host system, WISEA J033046.26 275518.3, is an infrared-luminous radio galaxy whose optical redshift z 0.52 confirms the MeerKAT emission line detection as OH at a redshift z_ OH = 0.5225 0.0001 rather than H i at lower redshift. The detected spectral line has 18.4 peak significance, a width of 459 59 km s^ 1 , and an integrated luminosity of (6.31 0.18 statistical 0.31 systematic ) 10^3 L_ , placing it among the most luminous OHMs known. The galaxy's far-infrared luminosity L_ FIR = (1.576 0.013) 10^ 12 L_ marks it as an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy; its ratio of OH and infrared luminosities is similar to those for lower-redshift OHMs. A comparison between optical and OH redshifts offers a slight indication of an OH outflow.

Archival data LADUMA J033046.20 275518.1 has a clear galaxy counterpart in WISE imaging (WISEA J033046.26--275518.3) and in previous radio continuum mapping by the Australia Telescope Large Area Survey 2012MNRAS.426.3334M,2015MNRAS.453.4020F . The galaxy has a bent-tail radio morphology 2014AJ....148...75D , and has been classified as a narrow-angle (or head-tail) radio galaxy (that is, the bent radio structure lies on one side of the optical host galaxy). The source has also been detected in dust emission by the Multiband Imaging Photometer for Spitzer MIPS; 2004ApJS..154...25R on the Spitzer Space Telescope 2015ApJS..217...17H and by the Spectral and Photometric Imaging Receiver SPIRE; 2010A&A...518L...3G on the Herschel Space Observatory 2012MNRAS.424.1614O,2021MNRAS.507..129S .

gmw_00011389.name: Q0050+0051
gmw_00011389.overview: Q0050+0051 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits exceptionally bright carbon monoxide emission [arXiv:2304.02041]. This gas extends toward a companion galaxy located 45 kiloparsecs away, likely representing tidal debris from an ongoing galaxy interaction [arXiv:2304.02041].

sources.summary: Q0050+0051 is the brightest CO source in the sample, with CO emission extending toward companions and interpreted as likely tidal debris from galaxy interaction.

quotes.quote: Q0050+0051 : This QSO shows the brightest CO emission among our sample of 10 z 2 QSOs. The P-V map in Fig. fig:spectrum3 reveals CO emission at v +100 \ that extends out to 20 kpc in the direction of the companion A, which is located at a projected distance of 45 kpc from the QSO. The P-V map further shows that, at the same velocity of +100 , also some CO-emitting gas seems to stretch off the companion in the direction of the QSO. It is likely that the CO extensions are tidal debris from an interaction between the galaxies. The QSO field contains another companion B almost in the same direction as companion A at a projected distance of 135 kpc from the QSO.

gmw_00011390.name: Q0107+0314
gmw_00011390.overview: Q0107+0314 is a quasar that shows evidence of a massive carbon monoxide disk [arXiv:2304.02041]. The system is kinematically resolved with a double-peaked emission profile and is accompanied by two carbon monoxide-emitting companion galaxies [arXiv:2304.02041].

sources.summary: Q0107+0314 is kinematically resolved with double-peaked CO emission, two CO companions, and evidence for a massive CO disk associated with the QSO.

quotes.quote: Q0107+0314 : This source is kinematically resolved (Fig. fig:0107 ), and both the 1D spectrum and 2D P-V map of the QSO show a double peaked emission profile (Fig. fig:spectrum5 ). In addition, the field of this source contains two companions (CO emitters), one of them shows a small velocity dispersion (FWHM 143 ) at a projected distance of 160 kpc from the QSO, and the other is at a projected distance of 230 kpc from the QSO. The continuum image taken with the ALMA 12m array reveals an unresolved point source associated with the QSO.

Here we report a target in our sample, Q0107, which shows evidence for a massive CO disk associated with the QSO (see Appendix.A, Fig. fig:optical ). Fig. fig:0107 and Fig. fig:spectrum5 show an offset 4 kpc and 293 km s ^ 1 between the double peak emission in spatial and frequency dimension, respectively. Although the CO signal is kinematically resolved, the spatial shift of the signal-peak is significantly smaller than the beam-size.

gmw_00011391.name: Q1230+3320
gmw_00011391.overview: Q1230+3320 is a quasar that exhibits extended carbon monoxide emission spanning 15 kiloparsecs in the southeast direction [arXiv:2304.02041]. A brighter, unresolved companion galaxy emitting carbon monoxide is located 80 kiloparsecs away, and continuum observations reveal point sources associated with both objects [arXiv:2304.02041].

sources.summary: Q1230+3320 has the sample's third-brightest CO emission, 15 kpc extended QSO-associated CO, a brighter nearby CO emitter, and continuum detections.

quotes.quote: Q1230+3320 : This QSO shows the third brightest CO emission in our sample and the P-V map in Fig. fig:spectrum8 reveals extended CO emission associated with the QSO across 15 kpc in SE direction. There is an even brighter unresolved CO emitter located at the SE direction at a projected distance of 80 kpc from the QSO (Fig. fig:spectrum8 ). The continuum image taken with the ALMA 12m array reveals two unresolved point sources associated with the QSO and companion.

gmw_00011392.name: Q1416+2649
gmw_00011392.overview: The quasar Q1416+2649 exhibits widespread CO emission extending across 45 kpc in the southwest direction [arXiv:2304.02041]. Its field contains at least two companion galaxies, with a plume of gas and low-level emission connecting one of these companions to the quasar [arXiv:2304.02041].

sources.summary: Q1416+2649 shows widespread CO emission, multiple CO companions, an extremely broad companion line, and a gas plume plus low-level emission connecting the companion and QSO.

quotes.quote: Q1416+2649 : This QSO reveals widespread CO emission across 45 kpc in SW direction (Fig. fig:spectrum9 ). The QSO field contains at least two companions (CO emitters A and B), which are at a projected distance of 28 and 75 kpc from the QSO, respectively. Companion A shows a quite narrow FWHM 156 km s ^ 1 , while companion B shows an extremely broad FWHM 853 km s ^ 1 and extended CO emission across 70/40 kpc in NW/NE direction. The Position-Velocity (P-V) map shows a plume of gas stretching off companion B in the direction of the QSO, and low-level emission in between companion B and the QSO. Component C is the the brightest part of the extended emission between B and the QSO with a narrow FWHM 133 km s ^ 1 , but we note that it could also be a companion galaxy.

gmw_00011393.name: Q2121+0052
gmw_00011393.overview: Q2121+0052 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits extended carbon monoxide emission around the central source [arXiv:2304.02041]. This emission extends across 35 kiloparsecs in the southern direction, with the central quasar itself showing a spatial carbon monoxide extent of approximately 33 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2304.02041].

sources.summary: Q2121+0052 has significant extended CO around the QSO, 35 kpc southern CO emission, higher tapered flux, and a measured central-QSO spatial extent.

quotes.quote: Q2121+0052 : This is another object which also shows significant extended emission around the QSO. Fig. fig:spectrum0 reveals extended CO emission across 35 kpc in Southern direction. The flux of the tapered spectrum (0.37 0.04 Jy ) is roughly 32 higher than that of the untapered spectrum (0.28 0.02 Jy ). Furthermore, Fig. fig:uv in Appendix B shows an analysis of the CO emission of the central QSO in the ( u , v )-domain through plotting the ( u , v ) distance of the baselines against the real part of the visibility amplitudes. From the Gaussian fitting (red line in Fig. fig:uv ), we derived a zero-baseline flux density = 1.96 0.24 mJy and a FWHM = ( 4.0 0.2 ) ^ for the CO emission of the central QSO in the image plane, which corresponds to a spatial extent 33 2 kpc.

gmw_00011394.name: GraL J024848.7+191331
gmw_00011394.overview: GraL J024848.7+191331 is a quadruply imaged broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2012.10051]. Its four lensed components are visible in PanSTARRS imaging, and Keck spectra reveal a foreground absorption line system that may be associated with the lensing galaxy [arXiv:2012.10051].

sources.summary: The paper confirms GraL J024848.7+191331 as a quadruply imaged BAL quasar, with PanSTARRS showing four components and Keck spectra revealing matching lensed images and absorption systems.

quotes.quote: GraL J024848.7+191331 --- This source was presented in as a candidate lensed quasar with three detected lensed images, an ERT probability of P_ ERT = 0.88 , and a maximum separation of 1 7. The PanSTARRS image shows all four components. A single Keck spectrum, aligned to observe the two brightest components, confirms the lensed nature of the source with two spatially separated, nearly identical spectra of the source broad absorption line (BAL) quasar. The spectra show strong O 6   1032.0, 1037.6 emission, as well as a foreground absorption line system at z = 1.037 , potentially associated with the lensing galaxy. There are also strong, broadened unidentified absorption features at observed wavelengths of 4525 and 6292  , corresponding to rest-frame 1321 and 1838  respectively, at the quasar redshift.

gmw_00011395.name: GraL J060710.8 215217
gmw_00011395.overview: GraL J060710.8 215217 is a compact lensed quasar with a separation of 1.7 arcseconds [arXiv:2012.10051]. It is modeled as a fold-configuration lens, where the brightest component is actually an unresolved merging pair [arXiv:2012.10051].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GraL J060710.8 215217 as a compact lensed quasar, confirmed by Keck spectra at multiple position angles and modeled as a fold-configuration lens.

quotes.quote: GraL J060710.8 215217 --- This candidate was selected from a more recent ERT run using \ DR2, and has a compact, 1 7 separated configuration with \ colors indicative of the presence of an AGN. The lensed quasar nature was confirmed in January 2020 based on Keck spectroscopy at multiple position angles (PAs) that all clearly showed traces of two very close quasars at the same redshift. Figure  fig:spectra1 presents the spectrum obtained at PA=20 , obtained with a single 3 0 wide extraction aperture. No radio or X-ray counterparts are reported by NED.

GraL J060710.8 215217 --- Our model for this compact quad consists of a fold-configuration lens. Our SIS+ model requires a moderate shear ( 0.08 ), but this shear could effectively be caused by the lens ellipticity. This model implies that the brightest component visible in the ground-based imaging is in fact composed of a merging pair, with the two, roughly equal brightness components unresolved by .

gmw_00011396.name: GraL J065904.1+162909
gmw_00011396.overview: GraL J065904.1+162909 is a wide quadruply imaged lensed quasar at a redshift of z = 3.083 [arXiv:2012.10051]. The system is gravitationally lensed by a red early-type galaxy located at z = 0.766 [arXiv:2012.10051].

sources.summary: The paper confirms GraL J065904.1+162909 as a wide quadruply imaged quasar with a red early-type lensing galaxy, multiple quasar absorption systems, and difficult lens modeling.

quotes.quote: GraL J065904.1+162909 --- This source was presented in as a candidate lensed quasar with three detected lensed images, an ERT probability of P_ ERT = 0.94 , and a wide maximum separation of 5 2; including SDSS imaging which detects a fourth image, the maximum separation increases to 6 8. The PanSTARRS image shows all four components, in a kite-like pattern of blue sources around the clearly detected lens galaxy with red optical colors. Owing to this configuration and its location on the sky, we have dubbed this source ``Orion's Crossbow''. We obtained several Keck spectra over multiple observing runs at multiple PAs, and confirmed that all four blue components correspond to a quasar at z = 3.083 ; Figure 2 presents just the two spectra of the most separated components, obtained in January 2019 at PA = 66 .

That spectrum also confirmed the lensing galaxy to be an early-type galaxy at z = 0.766 with strong absorption from Ca HK and Mg Ib, as well as a pronounced 4000  \ break. The quasar spectra show multiple absorption line systems, at redshifts of z = 2.066 , 2.424, and 2.500. Absorption lines from the lensing galaxy are not seen, likely due to its low redshift placing key features in the dense Ly forest.

gmw_00011397.name: GraL J165105.3 041725
gmw_00011397.overview: GraL J165105.3 041725 is a quadruply imaged quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.451 [arXiv:2012.10051]. It is gravitationally lensed by an early-type galaxy at z = 0.591, which produces a very large separation between the lensed images [arXiv:2012.10051].

sources.summary: The paper presents GraL J165105.3 041725 as the sample’s widest quadruply imaged quasar, with four spectra at z = 1.451 and an early-type lens galaxy at z = 0.591.

quotes.quote: GraL J165105.3 041725 --- This is the widest separation quadruply-imaged quasar in our sample, with a maximum separation of 10 1. It was identified from an ERT analysis, initially confirmed at NTT and then deeper spectra were obtained with Keck with two longslit spectra aligned to observe both pairs of lensed quasar images. All four spectra show a quasar at z = 1.451 ; Figure 3 presents the Keck/LRIS spectra for the two components observed with the PA = 142 \ slit. That spectrum also confirmed the lensing galaxy to be an early-type galaxy at z = 0.591 with strong absorption from Ca HK and Mg Ib, as well as a pronounced 4000  \ break. No absorption from the lensing galaxy is evident in the quasar spectra, though an absorption line system at z = 1.375 with strong \ and \ absorption is evident.

GraL 165105.3 041725 --- The lensed images of this very large separation system are well reproduced by our simple models provided that a large external shear is included, as one already expects from the elongated cross-like morphology. In addition, the centroid of the mass model is found to be significantly offset (0 15) from the observed position of the main lensing galaxy.

gmw_00011398.name: GraL J210329.0 085049
gmw_00011398.overview: GraL J210329.0 085049 is a compact, doubly-imaged lensed quasar that has been detected as an X-ray source [arXiv:2012.10051]. The system features a foreground absorption line system at z = 0.768, which is potentially associated with the lensing galaxy [arXiv:2012.10051].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GraL J210329.0 085049 as a compact lensed quasar with two blue spectra, foreground absorption likely tied to the lensing galaxy, and an X-ray counterpart.

quotes.quote: GraL J210329.0 085049 --- This lensed quasar was identified as a candidate from a \ ERT analysis subsequent to Delchambre:19 , with a compact maximum separation of 1 0. Two lensed quasar spectra are clearly identified in the blue arm of the LRIS data, though they merge at longer wavelengths. The spectra of this source presented in Figure 4 show the two blue arms (extracted with 0 5 apertures), but a single, scaled red spectrum (extracted with a 2 0 aperture). We identify a foreground absorption line system at z = 0.768 , potentially associated with the lensing galaxy. Similar to the doubly-imaged quasar GraL J234330.6+043557.9 discussed in KroneMartins:20 , the absorption is significantly stronger in one lensed image (see Fig. 4) than the other.

It is assumed that this foreground absorption corresponds to the lensing galaxy, with one of the lensed quasar images intercepting a more dense region of the lensing galaxy. Because of its compact configuration and location on the celestial sphere, we've named this source ``Aquarius' Tear''. This system is detected as the X-ray source 1RXS 210328.9 085039 Voges:99 , and no radio counterpart is identified by NED.

gmw_00011399.name: ASXDF1100.053.1
gmw_00011399.overview: ASXDF1100.053.1 is an unlensed, optically dark submillimetre galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 5.2383 [arXiv:2112.09026]. It is a massive, dusty star-forming galaxy characterized by a compact starburst, a low gas fraction, and a short gas-depletion timescale of 50 million years [arXiv:2112.09026].

sources.summary: The paper confirms ASXDF1100.053.1 as an unlensed optically dark z = 5.2383 SMG, with CO lines, compact intense star formation, low gas fraction, and a short depletion time.

quotes.quote: We have obtained deep 1 and 3 mm spectral-line scans towards a candidate z 5 ALMA-identified AzTEC submillimetre galaxy (SMG) in the Subaru/ XMM-Newton Deep Field (or UKIDSS UDS), ASXDF1100.053.1, using the NOrthern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA), aiming to obtain its spectroscopic redshift. ASXDF1100.053.1 is an unlensed optically dark millimetre-bright SMG with S_ 1100 m =3.5 mJy and K_ AB >25.7 ( 2 ), which was expected to lie at z=5 -7 based on its radio--submillimetre photometric redshift. Our NOEMA spectral scan detected line emission due to ^ 12 CO( J=5 -4) and ( J=6 -5), providing a robust spectroscopic redshift, z_ CO = 5.2383 0.0005 .

These physical properties indicate that ASXDF1100.053.1 is a massive dusty star-forming galaxy with an unusually compact starburst. It lies close to the star-forming main sequence at z 5 , with low M_ gas / M_ stellar =0.09 , SFR/SFR _ MS (R_ SB )=0.6 , and a gas-depletion time _ dep of 50 Myr, modulo assumptions about the stellar initial mass function in such objects. ASXDF1100.053.1 has extreme values of M_ gas /M_ stellar , R_ SB , and _ dep compared to SMGs at z 2 -4, and those of ASXDF1100.053.1 are the smallest among SMGs at z>5 . ASXDF1100.053.1 is likely a late-stage dusty starburst prior to passivisation.

gmw_00011400.overview: The active galactic nucleus 0035 + 227 is a steep-spectrum radio source with relatively low radio polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. Its optical spectrum reveals a nuclear region resembling a Seyfert 2 galaxy, which may be a Compact Symmetric Object [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0035 + 227 as a steep-spectrum, weakly polarized radio source whose nuclear spectrum resembles Seyfert 2 emission in double-lobed FR II radio galaxies.

quotes.quote: 0035 + 227 is a radio-source detected at 5 GHz (Griffith et al. 1990); it has been studied at higher frequencies by Dennett-Thorpe March a (2000) and more recently at 1.4 GHz (Dennett-Thorpe, private communication). This source has a steep spectral index between 8 and 43 GHz ( 843) and a relatively low radio polarization; this suggests that the object is not highly boosted, as we would expect from a BL Lac. Following Dennett-Thorpe March a (2000), this could be a Compact Symmetric Object (CSO). Our spectroscopic analysis points towards a nuclear region with characteristics reminiscent of those of Seyfert 2s; such an optical spectrum is more common amongst double-lobed, FR II radio-galaxies.

gmw_00011401.overview: 0149 + 710 is a core-dominated radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.023 [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. It is classified as a BL Lac candidate and a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus with a LINER spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0149 + 710 as a core-dominated radio galaxy with asymmetric jets, BL Lac-like indicators, LINER emission, and X-ray support for a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: 0149 + 710 is a core dominated radio galaxy at z = 0.023 . It has been observed at 1.4 GHz by Lara et al. (2001); its radio structure shows a strong asymmetry, with a wide jet in the W-NW direction and a fainter component in the opposite direction, probably related to the counter-jet. At 5 GHz, the VLBA image shows a bright core and a collimated jet (Bondi et al. 2001). Thanks to a relatively high optical polarization (3.3 ) and a \ break contrast smaller than 0.4, this object has been classified as a BL Lac candidate by March a et al. (1996); in addition, it shows a high radio polarization and a flat 8.4--43 GHz radio spectrum.

Our spectroscopic analysis, carried on the nuclear data after correction for the stellar contribution, reveals a low ionization spectrum typical of LINERs. Detected in the X-rays by ROSAT (Voges et al. 1999), this source is probably a low luminosity AGN observed close to the jet.

gmw_00011402.overview: The radio source 0651 + 410 is associated with an elliptical host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. Spectroscopic analysis corrected for host starlight reveals that the object contains a LINER, and it may be a weak BL Lacertae object [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0651 + 410 with an elliptical galaxy, discusses its peaked radio spectrum and possible weak BL Lac nature, and reports LINER emission after starlight correction.

quotes.quote: 0651 + 410 is a radio source associated with the elliptical galaxy Zw 204 27. It shows a convex radio spectrum, peaking at frequencies above a few GHz; this source is thus a High Frequency Peaker, or HFP (Dallacasa et al. 2000). However, it has been included by Marecki et al. (1999) in their GPS (GHz-Peaked Spectrum) candidate list. This source has a break contrast 0.47 and an optical polarization below the 2 level; due to its borderline properties, March a et al. (1996) could not classify this object. 0651 + 410 could be a weak BL Lac diluted by the starlight of the host galaxy, as well as a flat radio spectrum galaxy with a naturally low break contrast.

Spectroscopic observations by Merighi et al. (1991) reveal the presence of , 6548, 6584 and 6300 in emission. Analysis of our nuclear spectrum, corrected for the host galaxy starlight, shows the presence of a LINER.

gmw_00011403.overview: 0729 + 562 is a weak-lined galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.107 [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. Its nuclear spectrum indicates the presence of a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission-line Region, and it hosts a variable, collimated radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: 0729 + 562 is described as a weak-lined galaxy with variable, collimated radio emission and a nuclear spectrum indicating a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission-line Region.

quotes.quote: 0729 + 562 is a steep 843 \ radio source displaying evidence of variability at 1.4 GHz (Dennett-Thorpe March a 2000); although a number of BL Lacs and BL Lac candidates show variability at radio wavelengths, this source does not seem to be a BL Lac object and remains intriguing. VLBI observations at 5 GHz (Bondi et al. 2001) show that the radio source is rather collimated and presents a series of bright and relatively compact knots; however, the image does not allow us to decide whether the object is one- or two-sided, nor to identify the core; the best candidate, based on observations at 1.6 GHz, seems to be the southernmost component. 0729 + 562 has been identified with a weak-lined galaxy at z = 0.107 . Our spectroscopic data suggests the presence of a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission-line Region.

gmw_00011404.overview: 0733 + 597 is an S0 lenticular galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.040538 [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. It hosts a compact radio source with a core-jet structure, a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus, and LINER-type optical emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0733 + 597 as an S0 galaxy hosting a compact radio source, core-jet morphology, LINER emission, and X-ray evidence for a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: 0733 + 597 is a compact radio source (Patnaik 1992) identified with an S0 galaxy at z = 0.040538 . Observations with the VLBI (Taylor et al. 1994) revealed a core-jet structure with a faint counter-jet to the south; this source has been included in the extragalactic radio-jets catalogue of Liu Zhang (2002). Following Dennett-Thorpe March a (2000), 0733 + 597 would constitute a good beamed object candidate. Analysis of our optical data reveals the presence of a LINER, which is most probably associated with a low luminosity AGN. The detection of this source at X-ray wavelengths with ROSAT (Voges et al. 1999) comes in support of this interpretation.

gmw_00011405.overview: The radio source 0848 + 686 exhibits a flat radio spectrum and high radio polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. Its optical spectrum reveals a Seyfert-like nuclear emission region, suggesting it may be a hidden BL Lacertae object diluted by host-galaxy starlight [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0848 + 686 has flat-spectrum, polarized radio emission, a Seyfert-like nuclear spectrum, and could be a hidden BL Lac diluted by host-galaxy starlight.

quotes.quote: 0848 + 686 is a radio source with relatively flat 8.4--43 GHz radio spectrum and high radio polarization ( 2.5 ); its optical polarization is lower than 1 . Our spectroscopic analysis, carried out on the nuclear data after correction for the stellar contribution, reveals the presence of a Seyfert-like nuclear emission region. At the present time, we do not dispose of additional pieces of information supporting the presence of an AGN in this object. X-ray observations could contribute to enlighten the situation and provide confirmation of the AGN nature of this source. With a contrast value of 0.46 (March a et al. 1996), this could then be another example of a ``hidden'' BL Lac whose non-thermal continuum is being swamped by the host galaxy starlight.

gmw_00011406.overview: The galaxy 0902 + 468 is a compact radio source located at a redshift of z = 0.0848 [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. Spectral analysis of its nucleus indicates that the galaxy hosts a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0902 + 468 as a compact radio source in a galaxy, with low optical and radio polarization and a nucleus consistent with LINER emission.

quotes.quote: 0902 + 468 is a compact radio source identified with a galaxy at z = 0.0848 . Its optical polarization value is less than 1.2 , and the radio polarization is inferior to 0.99 (March a et al. 1996; Dennett-Thorpe March a 2000). For Caccianiga et al. (2002), this could be a narrow emission-line galaxy; our analysis of the nuclear spectrum, corrected for the stellar contribution, suggests that this object harbours a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission-line Region.

gmw_00011407.overview: The object 1241 + 735 is a core-jet radio source identified with a bright galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. It hosts a low-power active galactic nucleus with a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region and exhibits high, variable radio polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper presents 1241 + 735 as a bright-galaxy core-jet radio source with unusual one-sided radio morphology, high polarization, and LINER emission compatible with a low-power AGN.

quotes.quote: 1241 + 735 is a core-jet radio source (Augusto et al. 1998) identified with a bright galaxy at z = 0.075. VLBI imaging by Bondi et al. (2001) confirms the compact core and one-sided jet and reveals an unusual radio morphology at larger distances from the core, where the jet starts bending and ends in a compact component. There is no hint of a counter-jet or of any extended emission on the side opposite to the main radio jet. This object shows very high and variable radio polarization and a flat 843 \ index, which led Dennett-Thorpe March a (2000) to suggest this could be an active nucleus with a jet close to the line-of-sight. Analysis of our spectroscopic data shows the presence of a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission-line Region, which is compatible with the presence of a low power AGN.

gmw_00011408.overview: The radio source 1558 + 595 is identified with an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.0602 [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. It exhibits a compact triple-component radio morphology, and optical observations suggest the presence of a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1558 + 595 with an elliptical galaxy, notes compact triple-component radio morphology and extended features, and finds optical evidence for a LINER.

quotes.quote: 1558 + 595 is a radio source identified with an elliptical galaxy at z = 0.0602 . It displays a high contrast value and little radio polarization (Dennett-Thorpe March a 2000); from its steep 843 \ index, these authors conclude that this is not a highly boosted object and suggest it could be a CSO. VLBA images at 5 GHz show a compact triple-component morphology (Bondi et al. 2001); it is not clear which component is the core. NVSS maps reveal the presence of two slightly resolved radio features, connected by fainter emission, plus another two features to the East, with a two-sided jet visible (Denett-Thorpe, private communication).

Spectroscopic observations of this object point towards the presence of a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission-line Region. This is not supported by Caccianiga et al. (2002), who classify 1558 + 595 as a simple PEG.

gmw_00011409.name: 4C 30.31
gmw_00011409.overview: 4C 30.31 is a Fanaroff-Riley type I radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620] that exhibits a LINER-like spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. The galaxy features a 32 kpc one-sided radio jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620] and a co-spatial, faint optical jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: 4C 30.31 is described as an FR I radio galaxy with radio and optical one-sided jets, low polarization, weak blazar candidacy, and LINER-like spectral evidence.

quotes.quote: 1658 + 302 or 4C 30.31, is a radio galaxy with FR I type morphology; it shows a 32 kpc one-sided jet embedded in diffuse emission (Gonzalez-Serrano et al. 1993). Capetti et al. (2000) revealed the presence, in this galaxy, of a faint one-sided optical jet, co-spatial with the radio jet. Images obtained at 1.4 GHz show a small double with a compact point source at some distance (Dennett-Thorpe, private communication). This object's radio polarization is below the 2 level; the optical polarization value is even lower (Dennett-Thorpe March a 2000; March a et al. 1996).
1658 + 302 does not seem to constitute a good blazar candidate. Analysis of our spectrum suggests the presence of a LINER.

gmw_00011410.overview: Associated with an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of 0.04977, 1703 + 223 is a radio source that displays a compact double structure at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. Although previously classified as a BL Lac candidate, spectroscopic analysis corrected for starlight reveals it is a LINER galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper presents 1703 + 223 as an elliptical-galaxy radio source formerly considered a BL Lac candidate, with compact double radio structure and LINER emission after starlight correction.

quotes.quote: 1703 + 223 is a radio source associated with an elliptical galaxy at z = 0.04977 . Although its optical polarization level is inferior to 2 , March a et al. (1996) have classified this object as a BL Lac candidate based on the contrast value; this has been recalculated by Dennett-Thorpe March a (2000), whom no longer classify 1703 + 223 as a beamed object. Observed at 1.4 GHz, this source displays two small compact doubles (Dennett-Thorpe, private communication). Analysis of our spectroscopic data, after correction for the stellar contribution, shows the presence of a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission-line Region.

gmw_00011411.name: NGC 6521
gmw_00011411.overview: NGC 6521 is a galaxy located within a cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. It hosts a low-power active galactic nucleus classified as a LINER, which may contain a hidden BL Lacertae object [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: NGC 6521 is identified as the galaxy host of 1755 + 626, whose radio properties suggest a hidden BL Lac and whose optical and X-ray data support an active nucleus.

quotes.quote: 1755 + 626 is a radio source identified with NGC 6521, a galaxy in a cluster. It has a radio polarization value inferior to 2 and a flat high frequency radio spectrum; based on these characteristics, Dennett-Thorpe March a (2000) have suggested this could be a ``hidden'' BL Lac whose thermal continuum is swamped by starlight. Analysis of our optical spectrum, after correction for the stellar contribution, reveals the presence of a LINER; this could be associated with a low power AGN. The X-ray detection of this source by Zimmermann et al. (2001) supports this hypothesis. 1755 + 626 is thus likely to host a beamed active nucleus.

gmw_00011412.overview: The object 2202 + 363 is a compact radio source identified with a galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.075 [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. It hosts a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region associated with a low-power active galactic nucleus, and it exhibits radio variability alongside low polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2202 + 363 as a compact galaxy-hosted radio source with VLBA compactness, variability, low polarization, and LINER emission related to a low-power AGN.

quotes.quote: 2202 + 363 is a compact radio source identified with a galaxy at z = 0.075 . Unresolved at 1.6 GHz, this source has been observed with the VLBA at 5 GHz (Bondi et al. 2001) showing a single component, barely resolved, and no indications of extended emission. This object shows variability at 1.4 GHz (Dennett-Thorpe, private communication); its radio polarization level is inferior to 1.26 , the optical polarization being significatively lower (March a et al. 1996; Dennett-Thorpe March a 2000). Our spectrum shows the presence of a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission-line Region, which could be related to the presence of a low power AGN.

gmw_00011413.overview: The object 2320 + 203 is a compact radio source identified with a galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.039 [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. It hosts a low-power active galactic nucleus with a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region and displays high radio polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2320 + 203 as a compact galaxy-hosted radio source with polarized flat-spectrum emission, LINER evidence, X-ray support, and a likely jet-aligned active nucleus.

quotes.quote: 2320 + 203 is a compact radio source identified with a galaxy at z = 0.039 . This is another example of an object displaying high radio polarization and a flat 843 index (Dennett-Thorpe March a 2000). Our spectroscopic analysis reveals the presence of a Low onization Nuclear Emission-line Region; this is most probably associated with a low power AGN. Detection of this object at X-rays wavelengths by Brinkmann et al. (1995) comes in support of this hypothesis. 2320 + 203 is thus likely to host an active nucleus observed close to the jet.

gmw_00011414.name: GAMA228432
gmw_00011414.overview: The galaxy GAMA228432 is modeled by an exponential disc featuring a central hole [arXiv:2204.02925]. This structure, along with observed spatial and velocity asymmetries, may indicate the presence of a bar that drives internal radial gas motion [arXiv:2204.02925].

sources.summary: The paper models GAMA228432 with a central-hole exponential disc, flux and velocity asymmetries, and minor-axis twisting that may indicate bar-related radial gas motion.

quotes.quote: GAMA228432 is modelled by an exponential disc with a central hole. This profile could again be indicative of a resonance system where the inner orbits have been evacuated (the linear shape of the inner region of the PVD is also what would be expected for a ring viewed in projection). Furthermore, there is a flux enhancement in the approaching side of its PVD (which we also observe in the ionised gas distribution) and the receding side extends to higher velocities and offsets than the approaching end with respect to the kinematic centre.

GAMA228432 : we observe a clear ``bending'' in this PVD, represented as spatial and velocity asymmetries, possibly indicative of radial motion and inflow or outflow of gas along the rotation plane. As detailed in Section subsubsec: majorPVD , the central hole in the exponential profile used to model this object may suggest the presence of a bar, which would exert gravitational torques on the surrounding gas and, subsequently, internal radial motion.

gmw_00011415.name: GAMA238125
gmw_00011415.overview: GAMA238125 is modeled by an exponential molecular disc with a velocity curve that closely matches its multi-Gaussian expansion model [arXiv:2204.02925]. The galaxy exhibits an asymmetric spatial distribution of gas [arXiv:2204.02925], minor-axis position-velocity deviations [arXiv:2204.02925], and no evidence of a ring-type gas distribution [arXiv:2204.02925].

sources.summary: The paper finds GAMA238125 has an exponential molecular disc matching the MGE velocity curve, asymmetric gas distribution, minor-axis deviations, and no ring-type gas evidence.

quotes.quote: GAMA238125 is modelled by an exponential disc with a velocity curve in very close agreement with the MGE-derived curve. Like GAMA228432, the spatial distribution of the gas is asymmetric with respect to the kinematic centre (see Figure fig: modplots ) which can be observed in the approaching side of the PVD.

GAMA238125 : we do not observe asymmetry in this object, but we note there are deviations from the minor-axis PVD extracted from the model. The low S/N of this object may restrict our ability to discern kinematic features clearly.

However, objects GAMA238125, GAMA574200 and GAMA618906 show no evidence of a ring-type gas distribution, which further hinders the argument for a consistent kinematic process occurring in all of our outflow-type objects, as we find no evidence in the gas kinematics for a resonance system.

gmw_00011416.name: GAMA574200
gmw_00011416.overview: GAMA574200 is a galaxy modeled by an exponential disc structure [arXiv:2204.02925]. Its molecular gas appears relatively symmetric with respect to the kinematic centre, showing no evidence of a ring-type gas distribution but extending further at receding velocities [arXiv:2204.02925].

sources.summary: The paper describes GAMA574200 as an exponential-disc object with mostly symmetric molecular gas, some receding-side extension, low-S/N limits, and no ring-type gas distribution.

quotes.quote: GAMA574200 is modelled by an exponential disc and appears relatively symmetric with regard to the kinematic centre. The object does extend further at receding velocities, which can be observed in Figure fig: modplots , where the object is asymmetric with respect to the kinematic centre.
GAMA574200 : the lower S/N is this object is again limiting, but we do not observe notable asymmetry in this object.

However, objects GAMA238125, GAMA574200 and GAMA618906 show no evidence of a ring-type gas distribution, which further hinders the argument for a consistent kinematic process occurring in all of our outflow-type objects, as we find no evidence in the gas kinematics for a resonance system.

gmw_00011417.name: DLA-B/FJ0812+32
gmw_00011417.overview: DLA-B/FJ0812+32 is a metal-strong damped Lyman-alpha system located at a redshift of 2.626 [arXiv:astro-ph/0607430]. It exhibits a chemical enrichment pattern dominated by short-lived, massive stars and is considered a progenitor of a massive elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607430].

sources.summary: The paper uses DLA-B/FJ0812+32 as the archetypal metal-strong DLA, noting its many detected elements, chemical enrichment pattern, massive-star enrichment, and possible massive elliptical progenitor.

quotes.quote: Prochaska, Howk, and Wolfe (2003) reported the discovery of a metal-strong DLA at z_ abs =2.626 towards the quasar FJ0812+32 (hereafter DLA-B/FJ0812+32). In contrast with the majority of damped \ systems, the authors detected over 20 elements in this single DLA system and revealed the detailed chemical enrichment pattern of this galaxy. Many of the detected transitions had never before been observed outside of the Local Group and are important diagnostics to theories of nucleosynthesis and galaxy enrichment. The authors suggested that this system was enriched mainly by short-lived, massive stars and that it is the progenitor of a massive elliptical galaxy see also fenner04 .

gmw_00011418.name: BD46
gmw_00011418.overview: BD46 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.914 [arXiv:astro-ph/0612454]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy reveal a characteristically asymmetric Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:astro-ph/0612454].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BD46 as a z=5.914 Lyman-break galaxy with an asymmetric Ly-alpha line and a Keck redshift consistent with deeper FORS2 data.

quotes.quote: VLT Data BD46 The two-dimensional FORS2 spectrum of BD46 showed a robust emission line at the central wavelength of 8406 . Two- and one-dimensional extracted spectra for this emission are shown in Figure 5727 a. A clear asymmetric profile for the line is seen, which we identify as yielding a redshift of z =5.914 for this source.

One item to note is BD46's \ emission was detected with LRIS before the VLT data was available. Our methodology accurately measured this redshift (it was within z=0.0005 of the FORS value), despite a much weaker signal due to the smaller integration time in our Keck LRIS data.

gmw_00011419.name: BD03
gmw_00011419.overview: BD03 is a starburst galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 5.98 [arXiv:astro-ph/0612454]. It exhibits a resolved morphology, a blue near-infrared color, and a sharp continuum break caused by intergalactic hydrogen gas absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0612454].

sources.summary: The paper interprets BD03 as a z=5.98 starburst galaxy, based on its sharp continuum break, blue near-infrared color, resolved morphology, and lack of detectable Ly-alpha emission.

quotes.quote: BD03 While this object was undetected in the LRIS (4 hour integration) spectrum, the FORS2 spectrum shown in Figure 620 contains a noisy but clearly flat continuum redward of 8500 , with a sharp discontinuity at 8485 3 , where the flux drops to approximately zero blueward of 8400 . Using a constant step function to fit the continuum, we find the average flux density above the discontinuity is f^ red (8500-9100 ) = 5 1 counts ^ 1 . Below the break, this reduces to f^ blue (7700-8300 ) = 1 1 counts ^ 1 , consistent with no detected flux. This discontinuity is much larger than would be expected from UV/optical spectral breaks of galaxies at rest wavelengths 4000 \ [ D(4000) ] , 2900 \ [ B(2900) ] , and 2640 \ [ B(2640) ] Stern2000,Spinrad1997,Spinrad1998 . Another option is that this object is a radio-loud broad absorption line quasar. Such objects can have large continuum breaks of this amplitude. However, such objects also possess very red near-IR colors Hall1997 , unlike BD03 ( J-Ks < 0.03). Furthermore, the resolved morphology of BD03 is atypical for luminous AGNs at high redshift, which are generally unresolved. The abruptness of the break and the lack of slope in the continuum at larger wavelengths are strong signs that the discontinuity is due to absorption by intergalactic HI gas. Coupling this with the i_ 775 z_ 850 > 2.1 colors (the second reddest in our spectroscopic sample), we interpret this object as being a starburst galaxy at z = 5.98 0.1.

gmw_00011420.name: NuSTAR J033202--2746.8
gmw_00011420.overview: NuSTAR J033202--2746.8 is a heavily obscured, radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of approximately z=2.00 [arXiv:1403.2491]. Its broad-band X-ray spectrum reveals a reflection component and a high column density of N_H = 5.6 x 10^23 cm^-2 [arXiv:1403.2491].

sources.summary: The paper presents NuSTAR J033202--2746.8 as a heavily obscured radio-loud quasar at z=2.00, constraining its hard X-ray spectrum, reflection component, luminosity, and host-galaxy SED.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: coordinates: RA =53.0083 deg and Dec= 27.7807 deg

quotes.quote: We report \ observations of NuSTAR J033202--2746.8, a heavily obscured, radio-loud quasar detected in the Extended \ Deep Field-South, the deepest layer of the \ extragalactic survey ( 400 ks, at its deepest). NuSTAR J033202--2746.8 is reliably detected by \ only at E>8 keV and has a very flat spectral slope in the \ energy band ( =0.55^ +0.62 _ 0.64 ; 3-30 keV). Combining the \ data with extremely deep observations by \ and \ (4 Ms and 3 Ms, respectively), we constrain the broad-band X-ray spectrum of NuSTAR J033202--2746.8, indicating that this source is a heavily obscured quasar ( N_ H =5.6^ +0.9 _ 0.8 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) with luminosity L_ 10-40 \ keV 6.4 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 .

Although existing optical and near-infrared (near-IR) data, as well as follow-up spectroscopy with the Keck and VLT telescopes, failed to provide a secure redshift identification for NuSTAR J033202--2746.8, we reliably constrain the redshift z=2.00 0.04 from the X-ray spectral features (primarily from the iron K edge). The \ spectrum shows a significant reflection component ( R=0.55^ +0.44 _ 0.37 ), which was not constrained by previous analyses of \ and \ data alone. The measured reflection fraction is higher than the R 0 typically observed in bright radio-loud quasars such as NuSTAR J033202--2746.8, which has L_ 1.4 \ GHz 10^ 27 W Hz ^ 1 .

gmw_00011421.name: LEDA 2725953
gmw_00011421.overview: LEDA 2725953 is a quiescent galaxy nucleus that lacks emission lines [arXiv:1908.01065]. Its stellar population consists of a young component aged 1 to 5 Gyr alongside an older population, yielding a current stellar mass of 5.7 billion solar masses [arXiv:1908.01065].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LEDA 2725953 as the quiescent host nucleus of a slow TDE, modelling its stellar populations, mass, emission-line absence, and UV behavior.

quotes.quote: During the slew 9305900002, performed on August 22, 2016, jansen detected a source, (hereafter ), with an EPIC-pn, medium filter, 0.2--2 keV count rate of 1.2 0.4 count s ^ 1 . The source position, with a 1 error of 8 , lies 6 from the galaxy (hereafter ) and 18 from the 11 th magnitude A star, HD 23235465 (Fig. fig:swiftimage ). On September 12 , 2016, made a pointed observation of the source and found an X-ray source consistent with the coordinates of the nucleus of (also known as LEDA 2725953) and no emission from the star.

The fit needs a relatively young stellar population of 1-5 Gyr to model the blue flux in conjunction with an older population. The total initial mass of the galaxy is 1.1 10^ 10 and the current mass 5.7 10^ 9 after correcting for the gas restored to the ISM. The residuals clearly show that no emission lines are present in .

There is no detection of the galaxy present in the catalogue Bianchi ; however, a faint enhancement is visible by eye in the near-UV (NUV filter) image (2267 ) lying within the radial profile of the bright A star, HD 23235465. By using the catalogue magnitudes of the surrounding stars and galaxies, and an analysis of the count rates in this image we calculate that the NUV magnitude of the galaxy was 21.0 0.15 (AB magnitude) during the GALEX observations, which is 1.2 magnitudes below the OM-UVM2 magnitude from September 8, 2016.

gmw_00011422.name: BCG
gmw_00011422.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy of RXCJ1111 exhibits a velocity offset of nearly 800 km/s relative to its cluster's mean velocity [arXiv:2309.00412]. It displays outer non-axisymmetric structures, including northern shells and a southern tidal tail, which suggest a past minor merger [arXiv:2309.00412].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the RXCJ1111 BCG as velocity-offset from the cluster mean and showing residual shells, a tidal-tail-like feature, and signs of past mergers within a transitional fossil group.

quotes.quote: The BCG of RXCJ1111 (the ID 83) presents a velocity of 23428 5 km s ^ 1 (according to the SDSS spectroscopic database), which is almost 800 km s ^ 1 higher with respect to the mean velocity of the cluster. Lop18 obtained a similar offset, 704 km s ^ 1 . In addition to BCG , which shows a magnitude '=14.17 , we identify two further bright galaxies more (IDs 60 and 67, with magnitudes '=14.55 and 15.21), that we label as BCG2 and BCG3 , respectively.

Our model generated by GASP2D does not reveal signs of a double nucleus in BCG core. However, the excess of light (over the S ersic model) right to the south of the galaxy center resembles what would be the final stages of a merger with a relatively massive and concentrated companion hendel15 . The outer non-axysimmetric structure has a different apparent shape in its northern and southern part with respect to the galaxy center. The former resembles the typical shells formed during a minor merger with a relatively gas-poor companion mancillas19 . The southern part shows a tidal tail shape, which could also have been created by a minor merger, but on a less radial orbit with respect to the shells.

gmw_00011423.name: BCG2
gmw_00011423.overview: BCG2 is a bright galaxy located north of the brightest cluster galaxy in its system [arXiv:2309.00412]. Its position coincides with the northwest peak of a double-peaked X-ray emission, and it exhibits extended head-tail radio emission with lobes dragged toward the southwest [arXiv:2309.00412].

sources.summary: The paper labels BCG2 as a bright galaxy north of the BCG, coincident with the northwest X-ray peak, with extended head-tail radio emission dragged southwest.

quotes.quote: In addition to BCG , which shows a magnitude '=14.17 , we identify two further bright galaxies more (IDs 60 and 67, with magnitudes '=14.55 and 15.21), that we label as BCG2 and BCG3 , respectively. These two galaxies are located at 1 ^ .30 and 2 ^ .25 toward the north and south of BCG , respectively, configuring almost an alignment in the North-South direction. The X-ray surface brightness shows a double peak inside a SE-NW elongated inner region, and the BCG2 position coincides completely with the NW maximum of this double peaked emission (see Fig. fig:contours and Fig. fig:BCG left panel).

BCG presents a very weak central emission, while that from BCG2 and BCG3 is more extended. The emission corresponding to the BCG2 resembles a classical head and tail source. That is, we see central emission and lobes dragged to the south-west.

gmw_00011424.name: BCG3
gmw_00011424.overview: BCG3 is a bright galaxy located south of the main brightest cluster galaxy in its cluster [arXiv:2309.00412]. It features extended radio lobes on both sides that are misaligned with the galaxy center, suggesting that BCG3 is moving through its surrounding medium [arXiv:2309.00412].

sources.summary: The paper labels BCG3 as a bright galaxy south of the BCG near an X-ray elongation, with extended radio lobes misaligned with the galaxy center, implying motion through the medium.

quotes.quote: In addition to BCG , which shows a magnitude '=14.17 , we identify two further bright galaxies more (IDs 60 and 67, with magnitudes '=14.55 and 15.21), that we label as BCG2 and BCG3 , respectively. These two galaxies are located at 1 ^ .30 and 2 ^ .25 toward the north and south of BCG , respectively, configuring almost an alignment in the North-South direction. The X-ray surface brightness shows a double peak inside a SE-NW elongated inner region, and the BCG2 position coincides completely with the NW maximum of this double peaked emission (see Fig. fig:contours and Fig. fig:BCG left panel). On the other hand, the main BCG is shifted about 20 arcsec with respect to the SE X-ray peak, while BCG3 is placed to the south, where the X-ray profile shows an elongation in the external part.

BCG presents a very weak central emission, while that from BCG2 and BCG3 is more extended. The emission corresponding to the BCG2 resembles a classical head and tail source. That is, we see central emission and lobes dragged to the south-west. On the other hand, the BCG3 presents lobes that are still on both sides of the galaxy, but are not strictly aligned with the galaxy centre. This fact suggests that BCG3 is moving with respect to its surrounding medium.

gmw_00011425.name: GS-NDG-9422
gmw_00011425.overview: GS-NDG-9422 is a high-redshift emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.94 [arXiv:2405.01823]. Spectroscopic observations show no detected nitrogen lines, and its helium emission is moderately stronger than that of local galaxies [arXiv:2405.01823].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes JWST NIRSpec data for GS-NDG-9422 at z=5.94, finding no detected nitrogen line and helium-emission behavior moderately above local galaxies.

quotes.quote: GS-NDG-9422 Spectroscopic data of GS-NDG-9422 at z=5.94 was obtained with JWST NIRSpec via the JWST Advanced Deep Extragalactic Survey (JADES; PID: 1210, PI: Luetzgendorf; Bunker+2023, Eisenstein+2023 ).
Since no nitrogen line is detected in GS-NDG-9422, derived 3 upper limits of ( N/O ) < -0.85 and 1.01 using N iii ] 1750 / [ O iii ] 5007 and N iii ] 1750 / [ O iii ] 1666, respectively.

RXCJ2248-ID and GLASS150008 show significantly intense He i emission compared to those of the local galaxies, while GS-NDG-9422 shows the He i emission comparable to (but moderately stronger than) those of the local galaxies.

gmw_00011426.name: A1689a
gmw_00011426.overview: A1689a is a gravitationally lensed galaxy that hosts a luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0711.1902]. Its mid-infrared spectrum rises steeply with wavelength, showing weak aromatic emission and a high ratio of ionized neon [arXiv:0711.1902].

sources.summary: The paper identifies A1689a as a lensed source with several mid-infrared AGN diagnostics, including weak aromatic contribution, a rising spectrum, ionized neon, and a high [NeIII]/[NeII] ratio.

quotes.quote: This discriminant selects the following mid-IR spectra For A2390b, we scale from the flux in the 11 \ aromatic feature, using the average 7.7/11 \ flux ratio of 3.6 from jdsmith07 . as having substantial AGN contribution: A370a, MS0451a, A1689a, A2218c, A2261a, A2390a. Thus, of 19 galaxies with adequate spectra, 6 show strong indications of AGN contribution to their mid-IR outputs. A third indication of AGN activity is a mid-IR spectrum that rises steeply with increasing wavelength. Four spectra (A2261a, A2218c, A1689a, and A370a) show this behavior; none has detected aromatic emission.

Of the spectroscopy that has been published, or is in preparation, for our sample (see references in Table tab:sample ), three show evidence for AGN: A1689a has a Keck spectrum showing highly ionized neon (J. Richard \ in prep.); A2390a has a Keck spectrum (see Appendix) which shows Lyman with FWHM 945 km s ^ 1 , typical of an AGN narrow line region; and the spectrum of A370a is reported to contain AGN lines lutz05 . A fifth indication of AGN activity is a high ratio of [ Ne III ] 15.5 to [ Ne II ] 12.8 . Solar--metallicity starbursts are not observed to excite this line ratio above unity thornley,rigby-rieke , whereas the harder continuum of AGN do. Eight spectra cover rest-frame 12.8 ; [ Ne II ] is detected in 6 of these. Six spectra cover rest-frame 15.5 ; [ Ne III ] is detected in 4. Of the four galaxies where both lines are detected (A1689a, Ab2218b, A2390b, and A2667a), only in A1689a does the [ NeIII ] / [ NeII ] ratio exceed unity: 2.15 0.76 . This high line ratio offers additional evidence that A1689a hosts a luminous AGN.

gmw_00011427.name: A2261a
gmw_00011427.overview: A2261a is an X-ray-detected submillimeter galaxy that lacks a spectroscopic redshift [arXiv:0711.1902]. Mid-infrared diagnostics indicate that the galaxy has active galactic nuclei-like properties, showing a steeply rising spectrum with no apparent aromatic features [arXiv:0711.1902].

sources.summary: The paper describes A2261a as an X-ray-detected submillimeter galaxy lacking a spectroscopic redshift, with AGN-like mid-infrared diagnostics and no apparent aromatic features.

quotes.quote: Two of the four galaxies from the literature, and three of the galaxies in our program are detected in X-rays: two have no apparent aromatic features (sources A2261a and A2390a); and the other, source A2390b, is interesting in that it contains a luminous X-ray--emitting AGN, yet its spectrum still shows aromatic features. That source emits roughly equal power in the 7.7 \ aromatic feature (scaled from the 11 \ feature) and at 10--30 keV, reminiscent of the more luminous z=1.15 source CXO GWS J141741.9+522823 discussed by lefloch07 . We now consider a second AGN diagnostic, the relative contribution of aromatic versus continuum emission to the mid-IR flux. At low redshift, low aromatic feature equivalent widths have been demonstrated as an effective AGN diagnostic ( armus07,brandl06 , and Devost \ (in prep.)). However, it is extremely difficult to measure equivalent widths accurately for the high--redshift IRS sources, due to the limited wavelength baseline, the fact that the aromatic features have broad wings, and most importantly, imperfect sky subtraction which adds a (positive or negative) pedestal to each spectrum. For example, for \ (our highest--quality spectrum), we cannot measure an accurate equivalent width due to the difficulty in determining the true continuum level. We are able to set a lower limit of >0.4 \ (rest-frame, conservatively assuming a high continuum level), which is a typical value for low--redshift star--forming galaxies. For the other spectra in the sample, equivalent widths are even harder to measure. Therefore, we instead create an aromatic-to-mid-IR flux ratio, which we define as the ratio of the flux in the 7.7 \ PAH feature (in , as fit by PAHFIT) to the MIPS photometry at 24 \ (in Jy, corrected for bandwidth compression to z=0 .) This metric does not suffer as strongly from the difficulty in determining the continuum level. In the low--redshift, star--forming comparison sample we construct in sec:pahtotrat , this ratio ranges from 7--32 10^ 11 . We thus take 7 10^ 11 as a dividing line between spectra dominated by star formation (above the value), and spectra with substantial AGN contribution (below the value). This discriminant selects the following mid-IR spectra For A2390b, we scale from the flux in the 11 \ aromatic feature, using the average 7.7/11 \ flux ratio of 3.6 from jdsmith07 . as having substantial AGN contribution: A370a, MS0451a, A1689a, A2218c, A2261a, A2390a. Thus, of 19 galaxies with adequate spectra, 6 show strong indications of AGN contribution to their mid-IR outputs. A third indication of AGN activity is a mid-IR spectrum that rises steeply with increasing wavelength. Four spectra (A2261a, A2218c, A1689a, and A370a) show this behavior; none has detected aromatic emission.

Source A2261a This sub-mm galaxy chapman02 lacks a spectroscopic redshift; submillimeter and radio-based photometric estimates place it at z>1.6 and 2.5<z<4 aretxaga03 . It is the only source without a redshift in our sample. Our spectrum should be sensitive to the 11 \ complex for 0.9<z<1.8 , and to the 7.7 \ aromatic feature for 1.8<z<3.1 .

gmw_00011428.name: A2390a
gmw_00011428.overview: A2390a is a lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.858 [arXiv:0711.1902]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus, as indicated by its X-ray detection, substantial mid-infrared continuum contribution, and a broad Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:0711.1902].

sources.summary: The paper treats A2390a as an X-ray-detected lensed source with weak aromatic emission, substantial AGN contribution, and a Keck spectrum whose broad Ly-alpha indicates an AGN.

quotes.quote: Two of the four galaxies from the literature, and three of the galaxies in our program are detected in X-rays: two have no apparent aromatic features (sources A2261a and A2390a); and the other, source A2390b, is interesting in that it contains a luminous X-ray--emitting AGN, yet its spectrum still shows aromatic features. That source emits roughly equal power in the 7.7 \ aromatic feature (scaled from the 11 \ feature) and at 10--30 keV, reminiscent of the more luminous z=1.15 source CXO GWS J141741.9+522823 discussed by lefloch07 . We now consider a second AGN diagnostic, the relative contribution of aromatic versus continuum emission to the mid-IR flux. At low redshift, low aromatic feature equivalent widths have been demonstrated as an effective AGN diagnostic ( armus07,brandl06 , and Devost \ (in prep.)). However, it is extremely difficult to measure equivalent widths accurately for the high--redshift IRS sources, due to the limited wavelength baseline, the fact that the aromatic features have broad wings, and most importantly, imperfect sky subtraction which adds a (positive or negative) pedestal to each spectrum. For example, for \ (our highest--quality spectrum), we cannot measure an accurate equivalent width due to the difficulty in determining the true continuum level. We are able to set a lower limit of >0.4 \ (rest-frame, conservatively assuming a high continuum level), which is a typical value for low--redshift star--forming galaxies. For the other spectra in the sample, equivalent widths are even harder to measure. Therefore, we instead create an aromatic-to-mid-IR flux ratio, which we define as the ratio of the flux in the 7.7 \ PAH feature (in , as fit by PAHFIT) to the MIPS photometry at 24 \ (in Jy, corrected for bandwidth compression to z=0 .) This metric does not suffer as strongly from the difficulty in determining the continuum level. In the low--redshift, star--forming comparison sample we construct in sec:pahtotrat , this ratio ranges from 7--32 10^ 11 . We thus take 7 10^ 11 as a dividing line between spectra dominated by star formation (above the value), and spectra with substantial AGN contribution (below the value). This discriminant selects the following mid-IR spectra For A2390b, we scale from the flux in the 11 \ aromatic feature, using the average 7.7/11 \ flux ratio of 3.6 from jdsmith07 . as having substantial AGN contribution: A370a, MS0451a, A1689a, A2218c, A2261a, A2390a. Thus, of 19 galaxies with adequate spectra, 6 show strong indications of AGN contribution to their mid-IR outputs. A third indication of AGN activity is a mid-IR spectrum that rises steeply with increasing wavelength. Four spectra (A2261a, A2218c, A1689a, and A370a) show this behavior; none has detected aromatic emission. A fourth indication of AGN activity is the presence of broad or highly excited lines in an optical spectrum. Of the spectroscopy that has been published, or is in preparation, for our sample (see references in Table tab:sample ), three show evidence for AGN: A1689a has a Keck spectrum showing highly ionized neon (J. Richard \ in prep.); A2390a has a Keck spectrum (see Appendix) which shows Lyman with FWHM 945 km s ^ 1 , typical of an AGN narrow line region;

Source A2390a Johan Richard obtained a Keck spectrum for this source, shown in Figure fig:johan_spec . The strong Lyman emission is redshifted by z=2.858 , and the line velocity spread (FWHM 945 km s ^ 1 ) indicates that the source is an AGN.

gmw_00011429.name: A2390b
gmw_00011429.overview: A2390b is a gravitationally lensed galaxy detected in the mid-infrared [arXiv:0711.1902]. It hosts a luminous X-ray-emitting active galactic nucleus while still exhibiting aromatic dust features and detectable neon emission lines [arXiv:0711.1902].

sources.summary: The paper notes A2390b is an ISO-detected lensed source containing a luminous X-ray AGN while still showing aromatic features and measured [Ne II]/[Ne III] lines.

quotes.quote: Four sources were detected at 15 \ by ISO: A851a and A2218b barvainis , A2390b lemonon98 , and A2390c metcalfe . We will use this photometry to help determine the total infrared luminosities of the sources. Aromatic Feature Flux Ratios in High--Redshift Galaxies We now test whether the mid-IR spectra of star--forming galaxies, with their prominent aromatic emission features, show evidence for evolution from z=0 to high redshift. Given its high quality, we first examine the IRS spectrum for \ in detail. The image is amplified by a factor of 22 2 kneib04 , such that without lensing, its 24 \ flux density would have been 53 , at the MIPS confusion limit. The galaxy redshift, from H , is z=2.5165 0.0015 kneib04 . Its IRS spectrum, presented in Figure fig:a2218_spec , shows highly--significant detections of aromatic features at 6.22, 7.7, 8.33, and 8.61 . (The lower-quality spectrum from program 3453 confirms the detection of these aromatic features, though the two longer--wavelength features are detected at low significance.) Overplotted with arbitrary normalization are two spectral templates: a) the starburst galaxy NGC 2798 from the SINGS survey dale06 ; and b) the average spectrum of 13 nearby starburst galaxies with little apparent AGN contribution brandl06 , whose mean IR luminosity is 4.9 10^ 10 . Coincidentally, this is also the L(TIR) of NGC 2798. The z=2.516 spectrum in Figure fig:a2218_spec closely resembles the low--redshift, lower--luminosity (by a factor of 15) templates. Aromatic flux ratios are reported in Table tab:pahrats , along with those we measure for the Brandl template, and those reported for the SINGS sample. The strength of the 8.6 \ feature in \ relative to those at 6.2 \ and 7.7 , is within the 10 - 90 variation within the SINGS sample. In addition, the flux ratios of \ are a close match to those of the brandl06 average starburst template; while 7.7 \ is relatively stronger than the template, the difference is comparable to the standard deviation of line ratios for individual galaxies that were averaged to create the template. Thus, we conclude that this z=2.5 galaxy has aromatic feature flux ratios that are consistent with those observed for lower--luminosity, z=0 starbursting galaxies. While the other spectra in the sample have lower signal-to-noise ratios, they are still sufficient, in the aggregate, to test whether high--redshift galaxies have markedly different aromatic flux ratios. Figure fig:pahrats compares, for four aromatic feature flux ratios, the observed ratios in our sample with those measured for the low--redshift brandl06 template. Though the errorbars in individual measurements are sizable, Figure fig:pahrats confirms the result seen for : star--forming galaxies at 1<z<3 have aromatic feature ratios consistent with those of z=0 star--forming galaxies. Variations in aromatic feature ratios have been reported in certain HII regions---this may be attributable to variations in size, composition, and ionization state of the carriers ( draineli07 and references therein). In particular, large PAHs emit more strongly at 11.3 , and neutral PAHs emit much more strongly at 3.3 and 11.3 \ than charged PAHs draineli07,galliano06,allam89 . That our measured feature ratios at z>1 are consistent with those measured at z 0 suggests that the dust grain size and ionization distributions are not strongly evolving. Evidence for Compact Source Accretion Active galactic nuclei are detectable in multiple ways---by the dust they heat that radiates in the mid-IR; by X-rays emitted from the accretion disk coronae; and by high--excitation or broad emission lines. We now examine the evidence for AGN activity in our extended sample. The first AGN diagnostic we examine is X-ray luminosity, plotted in Figure fig:fpahX against aromatic luminosity. Most of the sample are X-ray non--detections, at limiting luminosities that rule out X-ray--loud QSOs or bright Seyferts, although an X-ray--weak or highly obscured AGN could still be present (.f. jennradio,almu-plagn ). Two of the four galaxies from the literature, and three of the galaxies in our program are detected in X-rays: two have no apparent aromatic features (sources A2261a and A2390a); and the other, source A2390b, is interesting in that it contains a luminous X-ray--emitting AGN, yet its spectrum still shows aromatic features.

Of the four galaxies where both lines are detected (A1689a, Ab2218b, A2390b, and A2667a), only in A1689a does the [ NeIII ] / [ NeII ] ratio exceed unity: 2.15 0.76 . This high line ratio offers additional evidence that A1689a hosts a luminous AGN. The other, lower line ratios are more difficult to interpret.

gmw_00011430.name: NGC 1569-HI
gmw_00011430.overview: NGC 1569-HI is a neutral hydrogen feature located east of the dwarf galaxy NGC 1569 [arXiv:astro-ph/9806267]. It lacks an optical counterpart and may represent primordial gas, a separate object in a close encounter, or a low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9806267].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1569-HI as an H I feature east of NGC 1569, lacking an optical counterpart and plausibly being primordial gas or a separate close-encounter object.

quotes.quote: We will refer to this feature as NGC 1569-HI. There is further weak emission between NGC 1569 and NGC 1569-HI in the velocity range 130 \ km \ s^ 1 to 90 \ km \ s^ 1 (also visible in Reakes's 100 \ km \ s^ 1 channel map. A second filamentary structure is visible in the channel maps with velocities -86 and -69 km \ s^ 1 near = 4^h 25^m 30^s , = 64^o 44' . It appears as a westward extension of the brightest emission. It is considerably closer to NGC 1569 than NGC 1569-HI and always connected to the brightest emission. For this reason, we treat it as a part of NGC 1569 and refer to it as the `western arm'. This feature was first noticed by Reakes (1980). A map of the total HI column density is shown in Fig. profig , together with line profiles summed over selected regions. The emission of NGC 1569 is dominated by the high-column-den sity ridge and the western arm. NGC 1569-HI is 8' east of the centre of NGC 1569, and has a FWHM velocity width of 50 \ km \ s^ 1 . The spatially-integrated emission of NGC 1569-HI and of the bridge over the velocity range 190 \ km \ s^ 1 to 60 \ km \ s^ 1 , is 12.4 Jy \ km \ s^ 1 , corrected for 16 primary-beam attenuation. The line flux of NGC 1569-HI itself is 6.3 Jy \ km \ s^ 1 , half of the total. If they are at a distance of 2.2 Mpc, the masses of NGC 1569-HI and of the bridge are 7 10^6 \ M_ each.

We have scanned the red and blue POSS II plates on the Leiden ASTROSCAN, but failed to find an optical counterpart of NGC 1569-HI. The detection limit on the blue plates for a smooth disk of 1' diameter should be about 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 ; foreground stellar confusion may raise this somewhat. With 2.3 magnitudes foreground extinction (Israel 1988), we arrive at a limit of about 23.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 . As this is close to the median surface brightness of DDO dwarf galaxies (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1981), the optical non-detection of NGC 1569-HI therefore allows it to be a low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy with a foreground extinction similar to that of NGC 1569, an intergalactic HI cloud without any stars, or a Galactic high-velocity cloud. The central velocity of NGC 1569-HI is 130 5 \ km \ s^ 1 , which is in the extreme blue wing of the line-of-sight Galactic HI line profile (Hartmann Burton 1997). The Galactic foreground is clearly present at v > -70 \ km \ s^ 1 throughout the field of view, but with a velocity width less than 10 \ km \ s^ 1 . However, we find no emission other than that of NGC 1569 and NGC 1569-HI at any velocity v_ hel < -120 \ km \ s^ 1 , even at 60'' resolution. We have summed the spectra of background point sources to look for absorption (Fig. contabs ). The 3 \ upper limit to the optical depth per channel is 0.12 in the velocity range 300 \ km \ s^ 1 to 30 \ km \ s^ 1 . The corresponding upper limit to the HI column-density per channel of an extended component with spin temperature T_s , is N_ HI < 9.6 10^ 19 (T_s/100 K) . We first consider whether a Galactic origin is consistent with both the large velocity range of the eastern emission and its small angular size. The high-velocity-cloud complex closest to NGC 1569 is complex H, at a distance of 5 degrees (Wakker 1991) and is in the relevant velocity range of 120 \ km s^ 1 to 140 \ km s^ 1 .
