In Table compare-tab we compare the properties of NGC 1569-HI to those of HVC cores, the dwarf galaxy M81 dwA, and a sample of dwarf galaxies. No distinction can be made from the column density. However, the linewidth of NGC 1569-HI is much higher than expected for small-scale structure in the Galactic foreground. The timescale for free expansion to double its present size is short, 2 10^4 d_ kpc year, where d_ kpc is the distance in kpc. Together with the lack of other isolated HI clouds in the field of view, the apparent connection (bridge) with NGC 1569, and the relatively high contrast with the surroundings, leads us to conclude that NGC 1569-HI is not a condensation in the Galactic foreground. Although NGC 1569 shows the signs of a global outflow of gas (see e.g. Heckman et al. 1995), the mass of NGC 1569-HI and the bridge ( 7 10^6 \ M_ each) as well as their location projected along the major axis all rule out that they result from this outflow. Extended, low-column-density HI haloes have been detec ted in a number of late type galaxies (Huchtmeier Richter 1982). NGC 1569-HI and the western arm are possibly high density regions in a similar halo. The only evidence for extended emission with a column density N_ HI 10^ 20 cm^ 2 from the present data is the emission in the north-west of NGC 1569. Its possible counterpart, symmetric in position and in velocity with respect to the centre of NGC 1569 should appear in the channel maps near 60 km \ s^ 1 . Negative sidelobes of Galactic foreground emission inhibit the detection of an extended, low-sur face-brightness component (see e.g. Fig. bridgeXV ). NGC 1569-HI cannot be identified with the symmetric counterpart of the extended emission in the northwest, because its velocity is 80 km \ s^ 1 lower. An extended HI halo is sometimes inferred from the difference in flux measured by an interferometer and a single dish radio telescope. However, single dish observations are heavily contaminated by the large column of Galactic HI. Furthermore, there is no evidence for an HI halo in the maps presented by Reakes (1980). The apparent retrograde rotation of NGC 1569-HI does not exclude a prograde orbit, e.g. as a part of an extended HI disk. High resolution observations of the haloes of IC 10 (Shostak Skillman 1989) and NGC 4449 (Bajaja et al. 1994) show a reversal of the velocity field. Such a reversal can be the result of a warped HI disk bending through the plane of the sky. However, the warp angle must then be larger than the inclination angle, which is 60^o for NGC 1569 (Israel 1988). There is no indication from the HI distribution that such an extreme warp exists in NGC 1569. As it is unlikely that NGC 1569-HI is an extension of the rotating disk of NGC 1569, it is either primordial gas which has not yet fallen into NGC 1569, or a separate object in a close encounter with NGC 1569.

gmw_00011431.name: NGC 7392
gmw_00011431.overview: NGC 7392 is a nearby star-forming galaxy located at a distance of approximately 42 Mpc [arXiv:2303.02710]. It hosted a dust-obscured mid-infrared tidal disruption event candidate, whereas optically and X-ray detected candidates are found predominantly in green valley galaxies [arXiv:2303.02710].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7392 as the nearby star-forming host of a dust-obscured mid-infrared TDE candidate and derives its stellar mass and color.

quotes.quote: The transient event took place in the nearby galaxy NGC 7392, at a distance of around 42 Mpc; yet, no optical or X-ray flare is detected. We interpret the transient as the nearest TDE candidate detected in the last decade, which was missed at other wavelengths due to dust obscuration, hinting at the existence of TDEs that have been historically overlooked. Unlike most previously detected TDEs, the transient was discovered in a star forming galaxy, corroborating earlier suggestions that dust obscuration suppresses significantly the detection of TDEs in these environments.

We found that ( M_ gal M_ ) = 11.08 0.01 and ^ 0,0 u- = 1.96 0.04 . Figure fig:urmgal plots the u- color versus the stellar mass for NGC 7392 and for the latest sample of TDE candidates from the ZTF survey 2022arXiv220301461H and SRG /eROSITA 2021MNRAS.508.3820S . It is interesting to note that while optically and X-ray detected TDEs are found predominantly in galaxies in the green valley parameter space, NGC 7392 lies in the star forming region of the plot, corroborating previous suggestions that optical and X-ray surveys may provide a biased view of the TDE population.

gmw_00011432.name: S4 1413+34
gmw_00011432.overview: S4 1413+34 is a radio active galactic nucleus classified as a compact steep-spectrum source [arXiv:2106.00100]. This source is associated with the transient 1FLT J1416 + 3447, which was detected over two consecutive temporal bins spanning late 2017 to early 2018 [arXiv:2106.00100].

sources.summary: The paper associates S4 1413+34 with a compact steep-spectrum 1FLT transient source, reports two overlapping temporal-bin detections, and builds an SED for the counterpart.

quotes.quote: Compact Steep Spectrum source 1FLT J1416 + 3447 was detected in two consecutive and overlapping TBINs, TBIN 111.5 and TBIN 112 (2017 November 19 to 2018 January 04), which strengthens the reliability of the detection. It is associated, through the Bayesian method, with a probability of 0.85, with a radio Compact Steep Spectrum (CSS) source, S4 1413+34. The SED of S4 1413+34 is shown in Figure figure:S41413 .

gmw_00011433.name: 3C 226
gmw_00011433.overview: 3C 226 is a Fanaroff-Riley II narrow-line radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.818 [arXiv:2106.00100]. The galaxy exhibits an asymmetric radio structure spanning 260 kpc, with its southeastern lobe positioned closer to the radio core than its northwestern lobe [arXiv:2106.00100].

sources.summary: The paper associates 3C 226 with a transient gamma-ray source and describes it as a z=0.818 FRII narrow-line radio galaxy with asymmetric radio structure.

quotes.quote: 3C 226 1FLT J0943 + 0940 is positionally consistent with 3C 226 and was detected in the TBIN 44 (2012 April 05 to 2012 May 05) of our analysis. 3C 226, located at z=0.818 hewitt1991optical , is an FRII radio galaxy laing1983bright with an optical spectrum that shows the presence of narrow emission lines (NLRG). The FRII radio morphology is clearly seen over a span of 260 kpc best1997hst . The source has an asymmetric radio structure, having the southeastern lobe closer to the radio core than the northwestern lobe. However, when the radio and optical observations are combined, the source shows a large opening angle.

gmw_00011434.name: And XXIV
gmw_00011434.overview: And XXIV is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the constellation Andromeda [arXiv:1102.2902]. It is characterized by a sparsely populated but clearly discernible metal-poor red giant branch sequence [arXiv:1102.2902].

sources.summary: The paper identifies And XXIV as a newly discovered M31 dwarf spheroidal with sparse RGB stars but a clear metal-poor RGB sequence.

quotes.quote: The five new dwarf galaxies were straightforward to detect as over-densities in the matched-filter surface density maps of metal-poor RGB stars. There are no other obvious candidate satellites visible in this map or in our equivalent average metallicity and metal-rich maps. For simplicity and following convention, we have named them Andromeda XXIII - XXVII (And XXIII - XXVII) after the constellation they are found in. As noted earlier the locations of all previously known dSphs (blue circles) and the five new dwarfs (red circles) are readily visible in Fig. fig_map . Moving from East to West (left to right in the Figure) the new galaxies are And XXIII, And XXIV, And XXVII, And XXV and And XXVI.

And XXIV and And XXVI have the most sparsely populated RGBs of the dSphs resulting in much noisier LFs. However, in both cases a metal-poor RGB sequence can still be clearly discerned, which is manifestly absent in the companion reference fields.

gmw_00011435.name: UGC 9050
gmw_00011435.overview: UGC 9050 is a low-surface-brightness spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 35.2 Mpc [arXiv:2306.06164]. It is the presumed host galaxy of the companion UGC 9050-Dw1, which lies at a projected separation of 69 kpc [arXiv:2306.06164].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 9050 as a low-surface-brightness spiral and presumed host or companion of UGC 9050-Dw1, sharing similar velocity, distance, environment, and H I properties.

quotes.quote: UGC 9050 is UGC 9050-Dw1's nearest apparent neighbor and presumed host galaxy. The two are separated by less than 50 km/s in radial velocity (see subsec:hi_props ). We adopt the distance of UGC 9050 as measured from the Virgo Infall Hubble flow mould2000 ( H_ 0 =67.8 km/sec/Mpc) of 35.2 2.5 Mpc, which we also adopt for UGC 9050-Dw1 given their extremely similar recessional velocity. At the adopted distance the two are separated by 69 kpc in projection. UGC 9050-Dw1 and UGC 9050 also both lie close in proximity ( 300 kpc) and within redshift space ( v_ helio <100 km/s) of the NGC 5480 and 5481 pair, indicating that they may also be fringe members of the NGC 5481 group ( D = 35 2.5 kpc).

This returned just two objects, UGC 9050-Dw1 and UGC 9050 (the complementary moment zero map for UGC 9050 is shown in sec:appendix , fig:host_radio_img ). The integrated \ fluxes (corrected for the primary beam response) of each are 0.95 0.03 and 3.02 0.05 Jy , which at 35.2 Mpc equate to \ masses of M_ HI /M_ = 8.44 0.04 and 8.94 0.04 , respectively, which are strikingly similar.

gmw_00011436.name: A+C
gmw_00011436.overview: A+C is a complex located at the center of the Hickson Compact Group HCG 31 [arXiv:astro-ph/0407525]. It is an ongoing galaxy merger characterized by double kinematic components and a double photometric nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0407525].

sources.summary: The paper identifies A+C as the central ongoing merger in HCG 31, with double kinematic components and overlapping disks indicating a merger rather than a single spiral.

quotes.quote: We have obtained high spectral resolution (R = 45900) Fabry-Perot velocity maps of the Hickson Compact Group HCG 31 in order to revisit the important problem of the merger nature of the central object A+C and to derive the internal kinematics of the candidate tidal dwarf galaxies in this group. Our main findings are: (1) double kinematic components are present throughout the main body of A+C, which strongly suggests that this complex is an ongoing merger (2) regions A2 and E, to the east and south of complex A+C, present rotation patterns with velocity amplitudes of 25 km s^ 1 and they counterrotate with respect to A+C, (3) region F, which was previously thought to be the best example of a tidal dwarf galaxy in HCG 31, presents no rotation and negligible internal velocity dispersion, as is also the case for region A1 . HCG 31 presents an undergoing merger in its center (A+C) and it is likely that it has suffered additional perturbations due to interactions with the nearby galaxies B, G and Q.

Our two main results are: 1) We measure multiple kinematic components throughout the body of A+C which we interpret as a strong indication that this complex is an ongoing merger. The double photometric nucleus has been identified in several previous images of HCG 31 including the spectacular HST image published by Johnson and Conti (2000) and in Fig. 1a. The double kinematic component is shown here for the first time in Fig. 1d.

gmw_00011437.name: E
gmw_00011437.overview: The object E is a candidate tidal dwarf galaxy located near the merging complex A+C [arXiv:astro-ph/0407525]. It exhibits ordered motion with a velocity gradient ranging from 3950 to 4000 km/s, and it counterrotates with respect to the main merging body [arXiv:astro-ph/0407525].

sources.summary: The paper treats E as a candidate tidal dwarf near A+C, finding the strongest velocity gradient, ordered motion, counterrotation, and probable fallback onto the merger.

quotes.quote: Our main findings are: (1) double kinematic components are present throughout the main body of A+C, which strongly suggests that this complex is an ongoing merger (2) regions A2 and E, to the east and south of complex A+C, present rotation patterns with velocity amplitudes of 25 km s^ 1 and they counterrotate with respect to A+C, (3) region F, which was previously thought to be the best example of a tidal dwarf galaxy in HCG 31, presents no rotation and negligible internal velocity dispersion, as is also the case for region A1 .

Fig. 2 shows the velocity gradients of the objects A1 , A2 , E and F, situated around the pair A+C (see Fig 1a) and which have been thought to be candidate tidal dwarf galaxies (Hunsberger et al. 1996, Iglesias-P aramo V i lchez 2001 and Richer et al. 2003). The curves are not corrected for inclination and the central position and velocity were chosen such that the curves were as symmetric as possible, with both sides matching, when possible. The object with the highest velocity gradient is E, with ordered velocities which range from 3950 km s ^ 1 to 4000 km s ^ 1 . Object A2 also shows ordered motion, with velocities going from 4125 to 4175 km s ^ 1 . Surprisingly, object F, thought to be the best tidal dwarf galaxy candidate in the group, shows a completely flat rotation curve, as does also the smaller object to the northeast of A+C, fragment A1 . In addition, A1 and F have low gaseous velocity dispersions.

As shown in Fig. 2, we detect ordered motions only for objects A2 and E and not for A1 and F. It might be suspected that the internal velocity motions measured in A2 and E could be due to streaming motions in incipient tidal tails in formation (see Fig. 1a). This is, however, not the case because these objects are counterrotating with respect to the main body of A+C. The discontinuity of the isovelocities can be clearly seen from Fig. 1c: the velocities go from high (northeast) to low (southwest) in A+C, towards object E. Then, along the body of E they go in the opposite sense. Similarly, for object A2 , it presents counterrotation with respect to its immediate neighbor to the west: galaxy A.

gmw_00011438.name: F
gmw_00011438.overview: The object F is a region of tidal debris located within a main galaxy merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0407525]. It exhibits a flat rotation curve, low gaseous velocity dispersion, and lacks an old stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0407525].

sources.summary: The paper argues that F is not a self-gravitating tidal dwarf, but tidal debris in the main merger with flat rotation and insignificant velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: Fig. 2 shows the velocity gradients of the objects A1 , A2 , E and F, situated around the pair A+C (see Fig 1a) and which have been thought to be candidate tidal dwarf galaxies (Hunsberger et al. 1996, Iglesias-P aramo V i lchez 2001 and Richer et al. 2003). The curves are not corrected for inclination and the central position and velocity were chosen such that the curves were as symmetric as possible, with both sides matching, when possible. The object with the highest velocity gradient is E, with ordered velocities which range from 3950 km s ^ 1 to 4000 km s ^ 1 . Object A2 also shows ordered motion, with velocities going from 4125 to 4175 km s ^ 1 . Surprisingly, object F, thought to be the best tidal dwarf galaxy candidate in the group, shows a completely flat rotation curve, as does also the smaller object to the northeast of A+C, fragment A1 . In addition, A1 and F have low gaseous velocity dispersions.

Region F, which was previously thought to be the best example of a tidal dwarf galaxy in HCG 31, is indeed part of the main merger, following the same kinematic pattern of the parent galaxy and presenting no rotation nor significant internal velocity dispersion. Region F was found to have low or inexistent old stellar population by Johnson and Conti (2000). If there is no old stellar population the velocity dispersion of the gas is mainly indicating the dynamics of the cloud. The range of values derived for the internal velocity dispersion of F, 15-25 km s ^ 1 , is too close to the natural turbulence of the gas and/or the expanding velocity due to starburst winds. The lack of rotation and the continuity of the kinematics between the main body of the merger and object F suggest it is simply a tidal debris, although, considering its projected distance from A+C (16 kpc) and the large amount of fuel available in the whole area of the merger (2.1 10^ 10 M_ of HI gas), it could perhaps develop into a tidal dwarf galaxy in the future, by accretion of infalling material.

gmw_00011439.name: AzTEC6
gmw_00011439.overview: AzTEC6 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1312.1173]. It lacks a reliable optical-infrared counterpart, meaning its stellar mass cannot be estimated and a nearby low-redshift optical object has been excluded as its correct identification [arXiv:1312.1173].

sources.summary: The paper says AzTEC6 lacks a reliable identification, rejects a nearby low-redshift optical object because of offset and mm/radio ratio, and gives no stellar mass.

quotes.quote: AzTEC6. Not identified with any method either using the AzTEC position or the refined SMA position. There is an optical object 1 arcsec from the SMA position for which we find z_ phot =1.12 (this is also the ID adopted by Smolcic et al. 2012, with z_ spec =0.82 ), but as with AzTEC2 this optical counterpart can be excluded as the correct identification not just because of its relatively large positional offset, but also because its mm/radio flux-density ratio of 150 is inconsistent with z < 1.5 ( z_ LW 3.9 ; see Fig. 4). The lack of any optical-infrared counterpart means that no stellar mass estimate for this object can be included in Table 5.

gmw_00011440.name: AzTEC10
gmw_00011440.overview: AzTEC10 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1312.1173]. It is undetected in optical wavebands, which suggests a redshift greater than 5 [arXiv:1312.1173].

sources.summary: The paper selects a red, 8 micron-bright counterpart for AzTEC10, argues optical non-detection supports a high redshift, and adopts z around 5 despite a lower mm/radio estimate.

quotes.quote: AzTEC10. There are three potential counterparts within 2 arcsec of the SMA position. Using the SMA coordinates alone we would choose the closest and the brightest one, but because of the 8 m flux and the very red i-K colour of the more distant object ( 1.5 arcsec from the SMA position), we chose it as the most likely identification. The photometric redshift determination yielded a very flat ^2 curve with a formal minimum at z>7 . Even though such an extreme redshift is very unlikely, stacking the optical data shows that it is undetected in the optical wavebands suggesting z>5 .

Also our mm/radio estimate gives a redshift of z_ LW = 3.12 (arguably biased low due to using a cold SED template appropriate for lower-redshift objects). Considering this, and the probability distribution for the optical-infrared z_ phot , for this object we adopt a redshift z 5 . This object would have been correctly identified using all but the radio identification technique on the basis of the original AzTEC position.

gmw_00011441.name: AzTEC12
gmw_00011441.overview: AzTEC12 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1312.1173]. It is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.5 [arXiv:1312.1173].

sources.summary: The paper describes AzTEC12 as a robust SMA-position counterpart at about z = 2.5 that would be securely identified by all four original AzTEC-position association methods.

quotes.quote: AzTEC12. A robust single identification 0.16 arcsec from the SMA position. z_ phot and z_ LW are in good agreement that the source has a redshift z 2.5 . Again, this source would have been securely identified using all four types of statistical association on the basis of the original AzTEC position.

gmw_00011442.name: AzTEC15
gmw_00011442.overview: AzTEC15 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1312.1173]. Photometric and long-wavelength estimates suggest that the source is located at a redshift of approximately 3 [arXiv:1312.1173].

sources.summary: The paper describes AzTEC15 as a robust counterpart offset from the SMA position, not recoverable from the AzTEC position, with photometric and long-wavelength estimates suggesting z around 3.

quotes.quote: AzTEC15. A robust single identification 1.05 arcsec away from the SMA position. This source could not have been identified on the basis of the AzTEC position because the SMA centroid is shifted by more than 10 arcsec. z_ phot and z_ LW suggest z 3 .

gmw_00011443.name: COSLA-6S
gmw_00011443.overview: COSLA-6S is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy with an estimated redshift of approximately 4 [arXiv:1312.1173]. It has an optical counterpart located 0.5 arcseconds from its Plateau de Bure Interferometer position, which may potentially act as a lensing galaxy [arXiv:1312.1173].

sources.summary: The paper says COSLA-6S has an optical counterpart but rejects its low photometric redshift using the mm/radio estimate, adopting zLW around 4 without a stellar mass.

quotes.quote: COSLA-6S. This object has an optical counterpart 0.5 arcsec from the PdBI position, for which Smolcic et al. (2012) derived z_ phot =0.48 . However, once again because our mm/radio redshift estimate yields z_ LW 4 , and completely excludes z < 1 , we conclude that this cannot be the correct identification (although clearly it could be a lensing galaxy; see Fig. 4). We thus adopt z_ LW 4 as the best estimate of the redshift of the sub-mm source, but cannot provide a stellar mass estimate in Table 5.

gmw_00011444.name: COSLA-17S
gmw_00011444.overview: COSLA-17S is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1312.1173]. The source lacks any optical or IRAC counterpart, and it has a weak radio flux measurement that indicates a redshift near z = 4 [arXiv:1312.1173].

sources.summary: The paper says COSLA-17S lacks an optical or IRAC counterpart, has a weak-radio redshift near z = 4, and rejects a much lower published redshift as implausible.

quotes.quote: COSLA-17S. This object was not associated with any optical or IRAC counterpart. A weak radio flux measurement leads to z_ LW 4 , but we cannot provide a stellar mass estimate in Table 5. We note that Smolcic et al. (2012) adopted a redshift z 0.7 , but such a redshift is implausible for this source (see Fig. 4).

gmw_00011445.name: COSLA-23S
gmw_00011445.overview: COSLA-23S is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1312.1173]. It lacks an optical or IRAC counterpart, and its mm/radio redshift is estimated to be 4.80 [arXiv:1312.1173].

sources.summary: The paper says COSLA-23S lacks an optical or IRAC counterpart and favors a mm/radio redshift of 4.80 over a weak low-confidence optical counterpart estimate.

quotes.quote: COSLA-23S. This object was not associated with any optical or IRAC counterpart. Smolcic et al. (2012) found an optical counterpart 0.9 arcsec from the PdBI peak with a redshift of z_ phot =2.58^ +1.52 _ 2.48 based on one 3 data point. We derive a mm/radio redshift estimate of z_ LW = 4.80 , and take it to be a more reliable redshift estimate, but cannot provide a stellar mass estimate in Table 5.

gmw_00011446.name: NGC 2082
gmw_00011446.overview: NGC 2082 is a barred spiral galaxy of SBb morphology located approximately 18.5 Mpc away [arXiv:2205.11144]. It features a bulge that is younger and more enriched than its surrounding disk, suggesting gas inflow and recent star formation in its center [arXiv:astro-ph/0610638].

sources.summary: The paper presents radio continuum observations of the nearby SBb spiral NGC 2082, giving its distance, redshift, and poorly studied status before analyzing its radio properties.

quotes.quote: 1 ^1 Western Sydney University, Locked Bag 1797, Penrith South DC, NSW 1797, Australia ^2 CSIRO Astronomy and Space Sciences, Australia Telescope National Facility, PO Box 76, Epping, NSW 1710, Australia We present radio continuum observations of NGC 2082 using ASKAP, ATCA and Parkes telescopes from 888 MHz to 9000 MHz.

NGC 2082 is G-type spiral galaxy (of SB()b morphology) in the Dorado constellation. It has an absolute B-band magnitude of M_B = 12.79 appmag , a diameter of 10.16 kpc, and is located at a distance of 18.5 Mpc NGC2082distance and redshift z = 0.00395 .

NGC 1566), NGC 2082 remains poorly studied, with its most notable feature being a type II supernova, SN1992ba SN1992ba . Here, we study the radio properties of NGC 2082 using ASKAP, Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) and Parkes radio telescope observations.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 2082 as having unusual bulge and disk colors: its bulge is bluer in B-I, redder in I-H, more enriched, and younger than its disk.

quotes.quote: The bulge-to-disk B-I color difference varies significantly from galaxy to galaxy, ranging from 0.2 mag in NGC 2082, the only galaxy in our sample with a bulge bluer in B-I than the disk, to 0.5 magnitudes.

It is in fact difficult to interpret a single (e.g., the B-I ) color in terms of stellar population properties: the point is made by e.g., NGC 2082, which, in contrast with what is observed in B-I , in I-H has a bulge that is redder than the inner disk (Figure fig:colorprofiles ).
For example, in NGC 2082, the bulge is more enriched but younger than the surrounding disk, suggesting the occurrence of gas inflow and the generation of new stars in the center of this galaxy.

gmw_00011447.name: NGC 4461
gmw_00011447.overview: NGC 4461 is classified as a non-star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1308.1380]. Although its nuclear region shows an anomalous fit suggesting a young stellar population, the galaxy lacks detected emission lines and exhibits an almost featureless near-infrared spectrum [arXiv:1308.1380].

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 4461 as an anomalous non-star-forming galaxy whose nuclear synthesis fit suggests a young population despite absent emission lines and a nearly featureless NIR spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 4461 the only non-star-forming galaxy with anomalous result, has a young population fraction of x _Y 48 in the nuclear region. This is surprising, as no emission lines were detected in that object. Moreover, its NIR spectrum is almost featureless, with no apparent CaII or CO 2.3 m absorption features, leading us to consider this result with caution. Indeed, the remaining non-star-forming galaxies of our sample, as well as those of kotilainen+12 , display these two systems of absorption lines/band. We therefore believe that our fits failed because no constraint could be set either from the continuum or from absorption features.

However NGC 4461 has an important contribution of x _Y . The result for this galaxy is somewhat puzzling because the quality of the fit, as measured by the adev value is not bad (at least for the nuclear aperture). We do not expect this galaxy to have any contribution of a young, ionising stellar population to its continuum, otherwise strong emission lines should be seen.

gmw_00011448.name: SDSS J131642.90+175332.5
gmw_00011448.overview: SDSS J131642.90+175332.5 is an active emission-line galaxy [arXiv:0908.3140] and a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:2206.05803]. It features double-peaked narrow emission lines [arXiv:0908.3140] and low-power radio jets that trigger star formation while driving turbulent gas outflows [arXiv:2206.05803].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J131642.90+175332.5 as an active emission-line galaxy with double-peaked narrow lines, an intermediate broad component, and interpretations favoring a single galaxy or merger over unrelated AGNs.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the active galaxy SDSS J131642.90+175332.5, which is remarkable because all of its narrow emission lines are double-peaked, and because it additionally shows an extra broad component (FHWM 1400 km s ^ 1 ) in most of its forbidden lines, peaking in between the two narrow systems. The peaks of the two narrow systems are separated by 400--500 \ in velocity space. The spectral characteristics of double-peaked \ emission have previously been interpreted as a signature of dual or binary active galactic nuclei (AGNs), among other models.

In the context of the binary scenario, SDSS J131642.90+175332.5 is a particularly good candidate because not just one line but all of its emission lines are double-peaked. However, we also discuss a number of other scenarios which can potentially account for double-peaked narrow emission lines, including projection effects, a two-sided outflow, jet-cloud interactions, special narrow-line region (NLR) geometries (disks, bars, or inner spirals), and a galaxy merger with only one AGN illuminating two NLRs. We argue that the similarity of the emission-line ratios in both systems, and the presence of the very unusual broad component at intermediate velocity, makes a close pair of unrelated AGNs unlikely, and rather argues for processes in a single galaxy or merger.

sources.summary: J1316+1753 is described as a radio-quiet quasar where low-power jets disturb gas, align with the stellar bulge, and produce both positive and negative feedback.

quotes.quote: In the `radio-quiet' quasar J1316+1753 the close alignment of the jet and the position angle of the stellar bulge is also suggestive of star formation triggered by the jet which contributes to the stellar bulge ( Girdhar2022 ; see Section 8.2) Suppression of star formation Since the early evidence of radio jets affecting the observed properties of the narrow-line regions in samples of Seyfert galaxies e.g. Whittle1992 , as well as in detailed studies of individual sources such as for example NGC 1068 Axon1998 and Mkn 3 Capetti1999 , examples of such interaction have also been found in other low-luminosity AGN e.g. May2018 .

A similar result has been seen in the `radio-quiet' quasar J1316+1753 which have low-power radio jets inclined to the galaxy disk plane Girdhar2022 . Combining MUSE and ALMA observations Girdhar2022 report evidence of turbulent gas driven perpendicular to the jet axis, and extending to 7.5 kpc on opposite sides. They also find evidence of increased stellar velocity dispersion along the jet axis and co-spatial with it, and evidence of both positive and negative feedback. While highly turbulent material appears to escape the galaxy inhibiting star formation, the jets also appear to compress gas in the disk forming new stars which contribute to the stellar bulge. The stellar bulge is closely aligned with the radio jet axis Girdhar2022 .

gmw_00011449.name: 5BZB J1322+3216
gmw_00011449.overview: 5BZB J1322+3216 is a moderately beamed blazar with a very steep radio spectrum [arXiv:2112.05394]. Its large radio power is dominated by extended or host-galaxy emission rather than its core [arXiv:2112.05394].

sources.summary: The paper discusses 5BZB J1322+3216 as a non-masquerading, moderately beamed blazar whose steep radio spectrum and large radio power are dominated by extended or host-galaxy emission.

quotes.quote: Note that 5BZB J1322+3216 (a.k.a. 4C+32.43, the rightmost blue triangle) would also have fulfilled (only) the P_ 1.4GHz criterion but its radio spectrum is very steep ( _ 0.8 between 130 MHz and 4.8 GHz) and not blazar-like. Its large radio power is therefore due to extended emission and not to the core. This fits with the fact that the host galaxy component is quite visible in its SED and suggests this is a moderately beamed blazar with its non-thermal emission swamped by the galaxy in the radio and near-IR bands see giommibsv1 .

gmw_00011450.name: NGC 5375
gmw_00011450.overview: NGC 5375 is a barred galaxy that exhibits a clear ellipticity maximum at a radius of 27 arcseconds [arXiv:1509.05328]. The galaxy's spiral arms begin to dominate its structure at a radius of approximately 40 arcseconds [arXiv:1509.05328].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5375 as an ideal bar-length measurement example, where the ellipticity maximum, visual estimate, and spiral-arm dominance set the bar scale.

quotes.quote: NGC 5375 represents an ideal case where a clear ellipticity maximum appears. The radius of this maximum gives the same barlength as estimated visually ( =27''). The position angle is maintained nearly constant to =32'', and the first minimum after the ellipticity maximum appears at 40''. Looking at the image it is clear that at =40'' the spiral arms are already dominating. The position angle is maintained constant even after the ellipticity maximum, which in this galaxy could give an upper limit of the barlength.

gmw_00011451.name: UGC 685
gmw_00011451.overview: UGC 685 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy that is classified as an isolated system [arXiv:1112.2548]. The galaxy hosts active star formation within its central region, and its distance has been determined using the tip of the red giant branch method [arXiv:1112.2548].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 685 as an isolated blue compact dwarf with central active star formation and a tip-of-the-red-giant-branch distance measurement.

quotes.quote: UGC 685 = KIG 45. A BCD galaxy with active star formation in its center. It is listed in the catalog of isolated galaxies [ 15 ] . The distance to it was determined from the tip of the red giant branch.

gmw_00011452.name: KDG 58
gmw_00011452.overview: KDG 58 is an irregular galaxy characterized by a very low surface brightness [arXiv:1112.2548]. Located at a nominal distance of 10 Mpc, the galaxy lacks signs of neutral hydrogen emission in the HIPASS survey but hosts a faint H II region near its optical boundary [arXiv:1112.2548].

sources.summary: The paper presents KDG 58 as a very-low-surface-brightness irregular galaxy, lacking HIPASS H I emission, with a nominal 10 Mpc distance and a faint HII region near its optical boundary.

quotes.quote: KDG 58 = KKH 53. An irregular galaxy with a very low surface brightness and without signs of HI emission in the HIPASS survey. The distance to it is nominally 10 Mpc. A faint HII region, indicated in the figure by a circle, can be seen at the optical boundaries of this galaxy.

gmw_00011453.name: KKH 60
gmw_00011453.overview: KKH 60 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located outside of the Local Volume [arXiv:1112.2548]. It lies at a distance of approximately 16.5 Mpc, determined by its H I line radial velocity [arXiv:1112.2548].

sources.summary: The paper revises KKH 60 from a possible Local Volume dwarf to a more distant galaxy, using its H I velocity and noting the resulting underestimate of H flux.

quotes.quote: KKH 60. This dIrr galaxy has been assigned to the Local volume based on a radial velocity V_ LG = +108 km/ s measured in a noisy optical spectrum [ 18 ] . However, its radial velocity V_ LG = +1188 km s ^ 1 in the HI line [ 19 ] indicates that the galaxy lies outside the Local volume at a distance of 16.5 Mpc. A shift of the H line by 30 \ leads to an underestimate in our measured H flux.

gmw_00011454.name: UGC 11411
gmw_00011454.overview: UGC 11411 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy that may be a distant satellite of NGC 6503 [arXiv:1112.2548]. The galaxy contains three bright compact HII regions embedded in a diffuse emission medium and exhibits an exceptionally high specific star formation rate [arXiv:1112.2548].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 11411 as a high-declination blue compact dwarf, probably a distant NGC 6503 satellite, with three bright compact HII regions and unusually high specific star formation.

quotes.quote: UGC 11411. A dwarf BCD galaxy at a high declination. Its low optical radial velocity V_h = +69 38 km/ s measured by Falco et al. [ 20 ] , was confirmed by our observations with the 6-m telescope, which gave V_h = +45 35 km s ^ 1 . UGC 11411 may be a distant satellite of NGC 6503 at a projected distance of 500 kpc. Three bright compact HII regions stand out in the body of UGC 11411, embedded in a common diffuse emission medium. UGC 11411 has one of the highest specific star formation rates per unit luminosity in the Local volume.

gmw_00011455.name: MACS0454-1251
gmw_00011455.overview: MACS0454-1251 is a highly magnified, gravitationally lensed galaxy at a systemic redshift of 6.315 [arXiv:2309.11548]. It is classified as a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy and has been detected in ionized carbon line emission using ALMA [arXiv:2309.11548].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA detection of line emission from the lensed z>6 galaxy MACS0454-1251, measuring its systemic redshift, line luminosity, velocity offset, and spatial offset from UV emission.

quotes.quote: We observed three highly magnified ( 4-20 ) Lyman alpha emitting (LAEs) galaxies with ALMA at z_ Ly =6.323 0.003 (MACS0454-1251), 6.846 0.001 (MACS2129-1412), and 7.161 0.001 (RXJ1347-018). We report a single detection (4 , Fig MACS0454_contour ) from MACS0454-1251. We extracted the spectrum over an extended elliptical aperture (semi-axes: 1. 5 0. 5 , pitch angle: 35^ ) to account of the slightly oblong shape in the contours of the moment0 map (see Fig MACS0454_contour ).

We fit a Gaussian (see Fig fig:Glazer_spectrum ) to estimate the peak line flux S_ line =3.7 0.9 mJy . We calculate the integrated line flux ( S_ line,g v ) as 0.64 0.15 Jykm s ^ 1 with a FWHM of 163 46 km s ^ 1 . We determine the systemic redshift z_ [ CII ] =6.315 0.001 which is in close agreement with the redshift found via ( z_ spec = 6.323 ).

gmw_00011456.name: MACS2129-1412
gmw_00011456.overview: MACS2129-1412 is a highly magnified Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 6.846 [arXiv:2309.11548]. Observations with ALMA yielded a non-detection for [C II] emission, allowing researchers to derive upper limits for the line [arXiv:2309.11548].

sources.summary: The paper observes MACS2129-1412 as a highly magnified z=6.846 Lyman-alpha emitter, finds no line detection, and derives upper limits using matched channel widths and HST-centered aperture measurements.

quotes.quote: We observed three highly magnified ( 4-20 ) Lyman alpha emitting (LAEs) galaxies with ALMA at z_ Ly =6.323 0.003 (MACS0454-1251), 6.846 0.001 (MACS2129-1412), and 7.161 0.001 (RXJ1347-018).

The remaining two galaxies, MACS2129-1412 and RXJ1347-018, yielded non-detections for emission (see Fig fig:contour_figure ). Because their intrinsic line widths are unknown, we estimate their L_ ( 3 ) upper limits by integrating over the same width of channels ( 9 channels, 225 km s ^ 1 ) as was done in MACS0454-1251. In the absence of known systemic redshifts, we use redshifts to predict expected emitting frequency of each object This assumes there is no velocity offset between and emission for these two galaxies . The RMS uncertainty was calculated using the 1 intensity value extracted from a 0. 5 aperture centered to the HST imaging centroid of the target.

gmw_00011457.name: J1150+1016
gmw_00011457.overview: J1150+1016 is a galaxy-galaxy gravitational lens candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421]. Subtracted imaging of the system reveals a promising incomplete, horseshoe-like residual ring structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1150+1016 as a galaxy-galaxy lens candidate with a horseshoe-like residual ring and spectroscopic multiple images of an [OII] background source.

quotes.quote: J1150+1016: The subtracted image shows a promising incomplete--ring structure like a horseshoe. At the same time there are relatively large +/- residuals near the galaxy centre, and we found that the flux distribution around the candidate ring was somewhat sensitive to the exact masking of the source employed. This is as expected given the rather poor seeing (Table 1) and the relative faintness of the source (Table 2).

J1150+1016: The emission line is clearly resolved into multiple spatial components. The evidence that the line is [ OII ] 3726,3729 is the observation that the lines are double peaked at a separation corresponding to the [ OII ] doublet. The absence of other emission lines in the spectrum corroborates this identification but the definitive evidence comes from the detection of very strong [ OIII ] 4960,5008 emission in the original SDSS spectrum.

gmw_00011458.name: J1246+0440
gmw_00011458.overview: J1246+0440 is a strong gravitational lens system [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421]. Subtraction of the lens galaxy reveals a pair of faint residual images on either side of the galaxy center, and its emission-line components are spatially resolved [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421].

sources.summary: The paper confirms J1246+0440 as a lens system with two faint residual images, resolved emission-line components, corroborating H and [OIII] detections, and SIS-like flux and separation behavior.

quotes.quote: J1246+0440: The subtraction reveals a pair of faint images either side of the galaxy centre, consistent with the lensing hypothesis. The flux ratio between the two residual images was rather sensitive to the exact mask used. The positive residuals are substantially larger than the negative residuals.

J1246+0440: The emission line is clearly resolved into multiple spatial components. The emission line is not resolved (recall that this spectrum has lower resolution than the others). The evidence that the line is [ OII ] 3726,3729 is the detection of the H emission line (just blueward of a sky emission line near 6360), visible as the blip at the red end of the spectrum.

gmw_00011459.name: J1446-0248
gmw_00011459.overview: J1446-0248 is a gravitational lens candidate that displays two relatively bright residual images on either side of the target galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421]. Its resolved emission-line components show double-peaked [OII], H-beta, and [OIII] features that support this lensing interpretation [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1446-0248 as a lens candidate with two bright residual images and resolved emission-line components whose [OII], H, and [OIII] features support the lensing interpretation.

quotes.quote: J1446-0248: We see two relatively bright residual images, either side of the target galaxy, consistent with the lensing hypothesis. We aligned the slit to include both images.

J1446--0248: The emission line is resolved into multiple spatial components, but the significance of the detection of the weaker image, S/N =5 , is the weakest of all the candidates, and the image is only visible in Fig. 3 as a faint extension to the left. The evidence that the line is [ OII ] 3726,3729 is the observation that the lines are double peaked at a separation corresponding to the [ OII ] doublet, corroborated by the detection of H at the correct wavelength. The original SDSS spectrum also clearly shows the [ OIII ] 4960,5008 doublet.

gmw_00011460.name: J2156+1204
gmw_00011460.overview: J2156+1204 is a target galaxy with a morphologically complicated structure that is suggestive of gravitational lensing [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421]. It features a faint residual structure reminiscent of a horseshoe, with source flux detected on both sides of the target along the north-south axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421].

sources.summary: The paper finds J2156+1204 morphologically complicated but lens-like, with source flux on both sides of the target and spectroscopically resolved [OII] emission from a background source.

quotes.quote: J2156+1204: This image is rather complicated. There is an elongated image, resembling an inclined spiral at 3 to the S, and a smaller image 1 further to the S. In our spectrum we detect continuum from the latter, and an emission line near 7233 from the former.

Closer in, a faint residual structure reminiscent of the horshoe seen for J1150+1016 is weakly visible. Similar remarks about the morphology of the structure apply here i.e. it is suggestive of lensing, but a higher-resolution image may look somewhat different. We aligned the slit NS, as the image indicates flux from the source on either side of the target galaxy along this axis.
J2156+1204: The emission line is clearly resolved into multiple spatial components.

gmw_00011461.name: J2231-0849
gmw_00011461.overview: J2231-0849 is a bright Einstein-ring gravitational lens system [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421]. Spectroscopic observations of the ring reveal multiple spatial components identified through [OII], H-beta, and [OIII] emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0603421].

sources.summary: The paper presents J2231-0849 as a bright Einstein-ring lens, with a robust residual ring and spectroscopic multiple components identified through [OII], H, and [OIII] emission.

quotes.quote: J2231-0849: The residual image for this source shows a remarkably bright ring. Varying the region masked made little difference to this conclusion, and we considered it highly likely that this is a bright example of an Einstein ring. We aligned the slit to intersect the brightest part of the ring.

J2231--0849: The emission line is clearly resolved into multiple spatial components. The evdence that the line is [ OII ] 3726,3729 is the detection of the emission lines H and [ OIII ] 4960,5008 at the correct wavelengths (Fig. 4) in agreement with the features seen in the original SDSS spectrum.

gmw_00011462.name: CEERS2 588
gmw_00011462.overview: CEERS2 588 is a high-redshift galaxy with a spectroscopically confirmed systemic redshift of 11.04 [arXiv:2306.00487]. Its spectrum exhibits a softened Lyman-alpha break, which is explained by strong intergalactic medium absorption, a small ionized region, and damping wing absorption from the surrounding circumgalactic medium [arXiv:2306.00487].

sources.summary: The paper uses CEERS2 588 as a z=11.04 example whose softened Ly break is explained by strong IGM absorption, a small ionized region, and CGM damping absorption.

quotes.quote: In , the authors derive the spectroscopic redshift for z>11 object via (and ) for Maisie's galaxy (CEERS2 588).

The 2D and 1D spectra of CEERS2 588 (00021842) with systemic redshift at 11.04 (7.980) is shown in the left (right) panel. The red dotted lines represent the systemic redshift determined by emission line. The spectra of CEERS2 588 shows redshifted, softened Ly break relative to systemic redshift, while we see blueshifted flux with 4.2 significance beyond the rest-frame 1216 for 00021842.

For CEERS2 588, in addition to strong IGM absorption (i.e., x_ 1 ) and small ionized region (i.e., R_b/ cMpc <0 ), Ly damping wing absorption by CGM with column density of N_ / cm^ 2 >22 surrounding the galaxy explains strongly softened break feature around the rest-frame 1216 .

gmw_00011463.name: Fornax
gmw_00011463.overview: Fornax is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612705]. Its measured Galactic rest-frame proper motion is 24.4 mas per century in right ascension and -14.3 mas per century in declination [arXiv:astro-ph/0612705].

sources.summary: The paper measures Fornax proper motion from HST fields, derives its Galactic rest-frame motion, and integrates an orbit with large perigalacticon and apogalacticon distances.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Discovered by Shapley sh38a,sh38b , Fornax is at the celestial location ( , )=(02^ h 39^ m 53 1, -34^ 30^ 16.0^ ) vdb99 in the J2000.00 equatorial coordinate system.

quotes.quote: Box 1149, Flagstaff, AZ 86002-1149 E-mail address: hch@nofs.navy.mil Mario Mateo Dept. of Astronomy, University of Michigan, 830 Denninson Building, Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1090 E-mail address: mmateo@umich.edu Dante Minniti Universidad Catolica de Chile, Department of Astronomy and Astrophysics, Casilla 306, Santiago 22, Chile E-mail address: dante@astro.puc.cl Christopher G. \ Tinney Anglo-Australian Observatory, PO Box 296, Epping, 1710, Australia E-mail address: cgt@aao.gov.au The measured proper motion of Fornax, expressed in the equatorial coordinate system, is ( _ , _ )=(47.6 4.6,-36.0 4.1) mas century ^ 1 . This proper motion is a weighted mean of four independent measurements for three distinct fields. Each measurement uses a quasi-stellar object as a reference point. Removing the contribution of the motion of the Sun and of the Local Standard of Rest to the measured proper motion produces a Galactic rest-frame proper motion of ( _ ^ Grf , _ ^ Grf ) = (24.4 4.6,-14.3 4.1) mas century ^ 1 . The implied space velocity with respect to the Galactic center has a radial component of V_ =-31.8 1.7 km s ^ 1 and a tangential component of V_ t =196 29 km s ^ 1 . Integrating the motion of Fornax in a realistic potential for the Milky Way produces orbital elements.

gmw_00011464.name: RX1532
gmw_00011464.overview: RX1532 is a gas-rich brightest cluster galaxy with a high star formation rate [arXiv:2004.01786]. It hosts a large reservoir of molecular gas and exhibits a complex, clumpy morphology with tentative star-forming filaments [arXiv:2004.01786].

sources.summary: The paper treats RX1532 as its primary CLASH BCG, detecting CO lines, measuring a large molecular reservoir, and linking its clumpy star-forming morphology to gas-rich BCG behavior.

quotes.quote: Our sample includes RX1532, which is our primary target as it is among the BCGs with the highest star formation rate (SFR 100 M_ /yr) in the CLASH sample. We unambiguously detected both CO(1 0) and CO(3 2) in RX1532, yielding a large reservoir of molecular gas, M_ H_2 =(8.7 1.1) 10^ 10 M_ , and a high level of excitation, r_ 31 =0.75 0.12 . A morphological analysis of the Hubble Space Telescope I-band image of RX1532 reveals the presence of clumpy substructures both within and outside the half-light radius r_e=(11.6 0.3) kpc, similarly to those found independently both in ultraviolet and in H _ in previous works.

In Fig. fig:RX1532_images , we show the HST image and both the stellar mass and SFR density maps of RX1532, taken from Fogarty2015 . The figure shows a complex morphology both in stellar mass and in star-formation for RX1532, with a tentative evidence for star-forming filaments extending from the BCG in several directions, similarly to the MACS 1931.8-2635 BCG. The complex morphology observed in RX1532 and its similarities with M1932, motivated us to further investigate it using Galfit Peng2002,Peng2010 .

Concerning the remaining six BCGs with detections in CO, RX1532 and M2129 are gas rich, with molecular gas to stellar mass ratios that are 5-6 times those of MS field galaxies. RX1532 and M2129 BCGs have indeed M_ H_2 /M_ 0.4 , 0.7, respectively, corresponding to large gas reservoirs of M_ H_2 (1-2) 10^ 11 M_ .

gmw_00011465.name: M2129
gmw_00011465.overview: M2129 is a distant brightest cluster galaxy with a large molecular gas reservoir estimated at approximately 2 * 10^11 solar masses [arXiv:2004.01786]. Its molecular gas-to-stellar mass ratio is exceptionally high, though nearby companion galaxies within the observation beam may contribute to the detected emission [arXiv:2004.01786].

sources.summary: The paper tentatively detects M2129 in two CO lines, estimates a large molecular reservoir, and warns that nearby companions may contribute to the observed CO emission.

quotes.quote: Concerning M2129, in both Tables tab:BCG_properties_mol_gas and tab:COresults , we increased by a factor of two both L^ _ CO(2 1) and the associated H _2 mass. We note however that for M2129, in this work we use the H _2 mass derived via L^ _ CO(1 0) . The L^ _ CO(2 1) correction has been done a posteriori, noting that the Gaussian fit reported in Fig. fig:hint_detection_spectra seems to miss a substantial part of the CO(2 1) emission of M2129, which artificially results in a low excitation ratio r_ 21 for the galaxy.

Concerning the remaining six BCGs with detections in CO, RX1532 and M2129 are gas rich, with molecular gas to stellar mass ratios that are 5-6 times those of MS field galaxies. RX1532 and M2129 BCGs have indeed M_ H_2 /M_ 0.4 , 0.7, respectively, corresponding to large gas reservoirs of M_ H_2 (1-2) 10^ 11 M_ . As displayed in Fig. fig:mol_gas1 , they are thus among the distant cluster galaxies with the largest molecular gas reservoirs, also considering the fact that BCGs are the most massive galaxies in the clusters. However, we stress here that some nearby galaxies are visible in the HST image of M2129 (Fig. fig:HSTimages ), that fall within the IRAM-30m beam.
M2129 is an exception, with a large molecular gas reservoir M_ H_2 2 10^ 11 M_ and also a large central entropy level K_0 200 KeV cm ^2 .

gmw_00011466.name: M0329
gmw_00011466.overview: M0329 is a brightest cluster galaxy hosted within a low-entropy cluster core [arXiv:2004.01786]. It exhibits a high star formation rate exceeding 40 solar masses per year and has been tentatively detected in CO, indicating a rich molecular gas content [arXiv:2004.01786].

sources.summary: The paper identifies M0329 as a tentatively CO-detected, highly star-forming BCG in a low-entropy core with molecular gas content among the richer detected CLASH BCGs.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, the three most star-forming BCGs, M1932, RX1532, and M0329, with SFR > SFR _ MS and both H _ and UV based SFR 40 M_ / yr, have all been detected in CO at S/N >3 .

In fact, limiting ourselves to the subsample of six BCGs with tentative or secure detections in CO, the three sources RX1532, M1932, and M0329, which are hosted in K_0<30 KeV cm ^2 cluster cores, seem to have a higher molecular gas content M_ H_2 (3-10) 10^ 10 M_ than M1206 and A1423, with M_ H_2 (2-3) 10^ 10 M_ , that have high central entropy levels K_0>30 KeV cm ^2 .

They include M0329, M1206, and A1423, with M_ H_2 (2-3) 10^ 10 M_ and M_ H_2 /M_ 0.1 . We also tentatively detected M2129, both in CO(1 0) and CO(2 1), which tends to be more gas-rich, with M_ H_2 2 10^ 11 M_ and M_ H_2 /M_ 0.7 . The four BCGs also cover a broad range of star formation activity, ranging from (0.1-3) M_ /yr (M1206, M2129, and A1423) to high levels of star formation 40 M_ /yr (M0329).

gmw_00011467.name: A1423
gmw_00011467.overview: A1423 is a brightest cluster galaxy with a tentative detection of molecular gas [arXiv:2004.01786]. It exhibits a low star-formation activity of approximately 1 to 3 solar masses per year and a molecular gas mass of a few times 10^10 solar masses [arXiv:2004.01786].

sources.summary: The paper includes A1423 among tentative CO detections with low star-formation activity and molecular gas content of a few times 10^10 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Specifically, these were M0329, A1423, M1206, and M2129.

Concerning the remaining sources, three out of four BCGs with tentative detections in CO (i.e., A1423, M1206, M2129) and the 13 BCGs with only upper limits in CO exhibit star formation activity ranging from MS values SFR SFR _ MS down to a few 10^ 3 SFR _ MS . These results suggest that tentative detections in CO as well as non-detections are found quite independently of the star formation activity of the BCG. However, A1423, M1206, and M2129 with tentative detections in CO have low star-formation activity SFR (1-3) M_ /yr, corresponding to low normalized values of (0.009-0.09) SFR _ MS , which are rarely seen associated with cluster galaxies detected in CO (see Fig. fig:mol_gas1 , right).

They include M0329, M1206, and A1423, with M_ H_2 (2-3) 10^ 10 M_ and M_ H_2 /M_ 0.1 . We also tentatively detected M2129, both in CO(1 0) and CO(2 1), which tends to be more gas-rich, with M_ H_2 2 10^ 11 M_ and M_ H_2 /M_ 0.7 . The four BCGs also cover a broad range of star formation activity, ranging from (0.1-3) M_ /yr (M1206, M2129, and A1423) to high levels of star formation 40 M_ /yr (M0329).

gmw_00011468.name: M0647
gmw_00011468.overview: M0647 is a brightest cluster galaxy with a nearby companion [arXiv:2004.01786]. This massive companion galaxy is located approximately 5 arcseconds to the west of M0647 [arXiv:2004.01786].

sources.summary: The paper observes M0647 in CO, notes a massive western companion inside the IRAM beam, averages both pointings, and finds no CO detection.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, the HST image of M0647, that we observed in CO(2 1) at 2 mm shows a nearby massive companion about 5 arcsec toward the west from the BCG. The two sources are within the IRAM-30m beam. We therefore decided to point at the location of both galaxies, independently, and then average their resulting spectra. The CO results reported for M0647 in this work correspond to this average.

gmw_00011469.name: LABOCA source C
aliases_external: DRC component C / LABOCA source C
gmw_00011469.overview: LABOCA source C is a confirmed member galaxy of the DRC protocluster at a redshift of approximately 4.002 [arXiv:2006.10753]. The galaxy is undergoing a starburst event with an estimated gas-depletion timescale of roughly 350 million years [arXiv:2006.10753].

sources.summary: The paper confirms LABOCA source C as a DRC protocluster member, noting weak continuum, CO-line confirmation, gas-mass systematics, and an estimated 350 Myr depletion time.

quotes.quote: Note that the field of LABOCA source C was found to contain two faint >4 continuum sources but the line emitter was found coincident with an even fainter continuum source, detected barely above the 3- confidence level. For the line emitters in the fields of LABOCA sources C, D and E, the line frequencies are close to that expected for CO(4--3), marked with a vertical dotted line in Fig. fig:lines . We regard these as confirmed members of the DRC protocluster at z = 4.002 , along with the ten individual line-emitting components of LABOCA sources A and B identified in .

This bias for sources C and E indicates that our standard assumptions are in some way inappropriate and instead may require a higher r_ 41 (0.8), a lower _ CO (1.8) or a higher _ GDR (270), or a combination of these effects.

SFRs Determining SFRs from far-IR luminosities is problematic in cases where the 97-GHz flux density is dominated by brighter sources in the field of the line emitters, such as for LABOCA sources C and E, but we can instead estimate the SFRs of the line emitter using their CO(4--3) luminosities, given the strong connection between the luminosity of the dense gas and SFR greve14, kennicutt98 , log SFR = -9.94 +1.08 log(L^ _ CO(4-3) )+1.2 , where SFR is sensitive to the assumed IMF, as always. Comparing these SFR estimates to the gas masses inferred independently from the submm luminosity, we estimate rough, simplistic gas-depletion timescales for the current starburst events in these systems, 60 Myr for component D and 350 Myr for C and E.

gmw_00011470.name: LABOCA source D
aliases_external: DRC component D / LABOCA source D
gmw_00011470.overview: LABOCA source D is a confirmed member galaxy of the DRC protocluster at a redshift of z = 4.002 [arXiv:2006.10753]. Unlike other bright continuum emitters in its field, it lacks an obvious IRAC counterpart, but it is located near a tentative line-emitting source designated D^ [arXiv:2006.10753].

sources.summary: The paper confirms LABOCA source D as a DRC protocluster member, discusses its lack of an obvious IRAC counterpart, nearby D ^ line source, and compact emission.

quotes.quote: For the line emitters in the fields of LABOCA sources C, D and E, the line frequencies are close to that expected for CO(4--3), marked with a vertical dotted line in Fig. fig:lines . We regard these as confirmed members of the DRC protocluster at z = 4.002 , along with the ten individual line-emitting components of LABOCA sources A and B identified in .
Except for LABOCA source D, the brightest 97-GHz continuum emitters have obvious IRAC counterparts, as is usually the case for SMGs e.g. egami04 .

Component D As well as the five line detections in sources C--E, we tentatively identify an additional line source, henceforth referred to as D ^ , approximately 3 arcsec (21 kpc) North of D, as can be seen in Figs fig:linecont , fig:irac and fig:lines . The peak flux density of the line is 0.5 mJy beam ^ 1 ( 8 in the zeroth moment map); no associated 97-GHz continuum emission is detected. Is their small angular separation merely the result of a chance alignment, or could D ^ and D be physically associated, e.g. \ with D ^ as a fast outflow from D, or with both undergoing starbursts triggered by a high-velocity fly-by? The close spatial proximity suggests a physical association; on the other hand, the velocity offset between D and D ^ , of = 1762 53 km s ^ 1 , where D ^ has a velocity close to confirmed cluster members C and E, would appear to rule this out.

gmw_00011471.name: LABOCA source E
aliases_external: DRC component E / LABOCA source E line emitter
gmw_00011471.overview: LABOCA source E is a starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z = 4.002 [arXiv:2006.10753]. It is a confirmed member of the DRC protocluster and has an estimated gas-depletion timescale of roughly 350 Myr [arXiv:2006.10753].

sources.summary: The paper confirms LABOCA source E as a DRC protocluster member, discusses gas-mass assumption tensions and estimates a roughly 350 Myr depletion time.

quotes.quote: For the line emitters in the fields of LABOCA sources C, D and E, the line frequencies are close to that expected for CO(4--3), marked with a vertical dotted line in Fig. fig:lines . We regard these as confirmed members of the DRC protocluster at z = 4.002 , along with the ten individual line-emitting components of LABOCA sources A and B identified in .

This bias for sources C and E indicates that our standard assumptions are in some way inappropriate and instead may require a higher r_ 41 (0.8), a lower _ CO (1.8) or a higher _ GDR (270), or a combination of these effects.

SFRs Determining SFRs from far-IR luminosities is problematic in cases where the 97-GHz flux density is dominated by brighter sources in the field of the line emitters, such as for LABOCA sources C and E, but we can instead estimate the SFRs of the line emitter using their CO(4--3) luminosities, given the strong connection between the luminosity of the dense gas and SFR greve14, kennicutt98 , log SFR = -9.94 +1.08 log(L^ _ CO(4-3) )+1.2 , where SFR is sensitive to the assumed IMF, as always. Comparing these SFR estimates to the gas masses inferred independently from the submm luminosity, we estimate rough, simplistic gas-depletion timescales for the current starburst events in these systems, 60 Myr for component D and 350 Myr for C and E.

gmw_00011472.name: D ^
gmw_00011472.overview: D ^ is a compact high-redshift galaxy and a line source located approximately 3 arcsec north of the galaxy D [arXiv:2006.10753]. It has no detected 97-GHz continuum emission and is likely a separate cluster member from D [arXiv:2006.10753].

sources.summary: The paper identifies D ^ as a compact line source north of D, with no 97-GHz continuum, large velocity offset, and likely separate cluster membership.

quotes.quote: Component D As well as the five line detections in sources C--E, we tentatively identify an additional line source, henceforth referred to as D ^ , approximately 3 arcsec (21 kpc) North of D, as can be seen in Figs fig:linecont , fig:irac and fig:lines . The peak flux density of the line is 0.5 mJy beam ^ 1 ( 8 in the zeroth moment map); no associated 97-GHz continuum emission is detected.

Is their small angular separation merely the result of a chance alignment, or could D ^ and D be physically associated, e.g. \ with D ^ as a fast outflow from D, or with both undergoing starbursts triggered by a high-velocity fly-by? The close spatial proximity suggests a physical association; on the other hand, the velocity offset between D and D ^ , of = 1762 53 km s ^ 1 , where D ^ has a velocity close to confirmed cluster members C and E, would appear to rule this out. The emission is relatively compact and does not extend back to D, which argues against the outflow hypothesis. Until further evidence becomes available, we assume that D and D ^ are separate cluster members, aligned by chance.

gmw_00011473.name: J101815.1+462623.9
gmw_00011473.overview: J101815.1+462623.9 is an emission-line galaxy with optical spectral features consistent with a normal star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2203.16566]. However, its flat X-ray spectral shape and elevated residuals near the Fe K line complex suggest it is a candidate for harboring a heavily obscured or Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2203.16566].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J101815.1+462623.9 as the poorest individual spectral fit, with flatter X-ray residuals near Fe K suggesting a possible obscured or Compton-thick AGN.

quotes.quote: The latter galaxy that is poorly fit by our global model, J101815.1+462623.9, is also the most poorly fit by our scaled model, suggesting some deviation of the spectral shape of this source with respect to the global-model shape. In Fig  fig:out , we show the spectrum of J101815.1+462623.9 and the global model prediction of its spectrum. Visual inspection of the spectrum and its residuals to the global model suggest that this source has a somewhat flatter spectral shape (i.e., lower values of ) with elevated residuals between 6--7 keV, where the Fe K line complex is found. These spectral features (flat spectral slope and potential Fe K feature), along with the fact that our objects were selected to have optical spectral features consistent with normal star-forming galaxies, suggests that this object is a good candidate for harboring a heavily-obscured or Compton-thick AGN. However, given that this source has been detected with only 20 net counts, we do not attempt to derive any detailed parameters using more complex models.

gmw_00011474.name: 3C 91
gmw_00011474.overview: The source 3C 91 is a radio-loud active galaxy [arXiv:1609.07484]. It is associated with matched detections across X-ray, infrared, and radio frequencies [arXiv:1609.07484].

sources.summary: The paper associates 3C 91 with matched X-ray, infrared, and NVSS radio detections, accepts them as the same source, and notes a radio-loud active-galaxy catalog classification.

quotes.quote: 3C 91 The X-ray source that we have detected (XRT J033743.0 + 504546) with S/N =11.3 and a mean count rate on the order of 4 10 ^ 2 ct/s is 7.4 arcsec from the coordinates of NVSS J033743 + 504552 and 3.3 arcsec from the center of the 3CR positional uncertainty region, as shown in the left panel of Fig. fig:400 . A source, J033743.02 + 504547.6, has been found within the XRT error circle, at close angular separation (1.4 arcsec) from its center, and is clearly detected in all filters. Considering the good positional agreement between the infrared and the X-ray objects and their low angular separation from the NVSS object (see the right panel of Fig. fig:400 ), we have accepted these as counterparts of the same source at different frequencies.
Finally, we report that J033743.02 + 504547.6 has been included by in their all-sky catalogue of infrared selected, radio-loud active galaxies due to its peculiar infrared colours.

gmw_00011475.name: 3C 131
gmw_00011475.overview: 3C 131 is a radio source associated with the counterpart J045323.34+312928.4 [arXiv:1609.07484]. An X-ray source, XRT J045323.2+312933, has been detected near the coordinates of this object [arXiv:1609.07484].

sources.summary: The paper detects an X-ray source near NVSS J045323+312924, rejects earlier optical alternatives, and identifies J045323.34+312928.4 as the most plausible counterpart to 3C 131.

quotes.quote: 3C 131 The X-ray source XRT J045323.2 + 312933 has been detected at 9.4 arcsec from the coordinates of NVSS J045323 + 312924. There are two objects at close angular separation ( 4 arcsec) from the NVSS source: in the image shown in Fig. fig:402 (right panel) they are not resolved, and reliable magnitude values of both targets are only available for the w1 and w2 filters in the AllWISE Source Catalogue. Only one, J045323.34 + 312928.4, is within the error circle of the XRT source.

However, the angular separation of candidate 4 from NVSS J045323 + 312924 is 2.9 arcsec, lower than the positional uncertainty for the HST coordinates (3 arcsec) quoted by . Therefore, despite a difference of a few arcseconds (exact values are reported in Table tab:X and Table tab:lower ) between the positions of objects detected at different frequencies, we suggest that the most plausible counterpart to the radio source 3C 131 corresponds to J045323.34 + 312928.4.

gmw_00011476.name: 3C 390
gmw_00011476.overview: The radio source 3C 390 matches the X-ray source XRT J184537.6+095349 [arXiv:1609.07484]. Its likely infrared counterpart, J184537.60+095345.0, is classified as a blazar candidate due to its peculiar infrared colors [arXiv:1609.07484].

sources.summary: The paper matches 3C 390 to XRT, NVSS, and AllWISE detections, selects J184537.60+095345.0 as the likely counterpart, and notes its blazar-candidate infrared colors.

quotes.quote: 3C 390 The X-ray source XRT J184537.6 + 095349 ( S/N =8.6) matches the radio source NVSS J184537 + 095344 in the field of view of 3C 390 at an angular separation of 4.4 arcsec equal to the XRT error radius. As shown in the left panel of Fig. fig:411 the NVSS source is not well centered with respect to the 3CR positional uncertainty region. The cross-match with the AllWISE Catalogue has provided as infrared counterpart the source J184537.60 + 095345.0, at 0.9 arcsec from the NVSS coordinates.

Moreover, we emphasise that J184537.60 + 095345.0 has been recently included in the all-sky catalogue of blazar candidates by due to its peculiar infrared colours. Also in this case, no candidate to the radio source 3C 390 has been previously presented in the literature: a spectroscopic analysis of J184537.60 + 095345.0 will finally clarify the nature of this multi-frequency source.

gmw_00011477.name: 3C 409
gmw_00011477.overview: The radio source 3C 409 is associated with the soft X-ray source XRT J201427.5 + 233455 [arXiv:1609.07484]. This source has been identified with an infrared counterpart from the AllWISE catalog and is classified as a blazar-like radio-loud object [arXiv:1609.07484].

sources.summary: The paper identifies X-ray and infrared counterparts for 3C 409, relates them to earlier Einstein X-ray emission, and presents the first literature candidate counterpart.

quotes.quote: 3C 409 The soft X-ray source XRT J201427.5 + 233455 has been detected with S/N =11.6 in the field of view of 3C 409 and matches the coordinates of NVSS J201427 + 233452 with an angular separation of 1.9 arcsec. X-ray emission in this region of the sky was indeed detected by the Imaging Proportional Counter (IPC) on board the Einstein satellite and reported by . These authors also reported of a 7.3 ks exposure with the High Resolution Imager (HRI) in which the X-ray source was located at R.A.(B1950) 20 ^h 12 ^m 18 ^s .42, Dec.(B1950) + 23 ^ 25 ^ ' 45 ^ '' with an uncertainty of 5 arcsec.

The match with the AllWISE Source Catalogue has provided the infrared conterpart J201427.59 + 233452.6 to the NVSS source, at only 0.3 arcsec from its coordinates; this infrared object is well detected in all of the four filters. As in other three cases found in our analysis, this infrared object has been included in the all-sky catalogue of blazar-like radio-loud sources recently produced by . Apart from this, it is the first candidate ever indicated in the literature as a possible counterpart to 3C 409.

gmw_00011478.name: 3C 428
gmw_00011478.overview: The active galaxy 3C 428 is associated with a radio source that exhibits contours stretched in one direction [arXiv:1609.07484]. It is identified with an infrared counterpart that is detected across multiple filters and is classified as a blazar candidate [arXiv:1609.07484].

sources.summary: The paper matches 3C 428 with X-ray, NVSS, and infrared detections, notes a blazar-candidate catalog entry, and selects J210822.08+493641.6 for spectroscopic follow-up.

quotes.quote: 3C 428 The X-ray source XRT JJ210822.1 + 493642 matches the NVSS source J210822 + 493637 in the field of view of 3C 428, at an angular separation of 5.6 arcsec; the radio contours appear to be stretched in one direction (see Fig fig:416 ). As for 3C 86 and 3C 390 (see Fig. fig:399 and Fig. fig:411 ) the coordinates of the NVSS source are not well centered with respect to the 3CR positional uncertainty region: nonetheless, a substantial overlap with its radio contours is evident. From an infrared point of view a reliable infrared counterpart, J210822.08 + 493641.6, has been found within the XRT error circle: the angular separation from its center is only 0.5 arcsec, much lower than the XRT error radius. The image in the w1 filter showing this infrared source is given in the right panel of Fig. fig:416 ; the source is well detected in all the remaining filters. As for 3C 91, 3C 390, and 3C 409, this infrared source has been recently included in the all-sky catalogue of blazar candidates 2014ApJS..215...14D .

gmw_00011479.name: B2 0756+27
gmw_00011479.overview: B2 0756+27 is a giant elliptical galaxy and the brightest cluster galaxy of Abell 610 [arXiv:0806.2969]. It is a double, wide-angle tail radio galaxy that exhibits hydrogen and nitrogen emission lines [arXiv:0806.2969].

sources.summary: The paper identifies B2 0756+27 as A610 brightest giant elliptical and double/WAT radio galaxy, with redshift, emission lines, and peculiar velocity within the merger structure.

quotes.quote: We identified the well known bright radio source B2 0756+27 (ID 91 in our catalog, see below) as a double radio galaxy associated with the optically brightest, giant elliptical of the cluster (Valentijn , see also Owen et al. ). For this galaxy Owen et al. ( ) measured a redshift of z=0.0991 0.0002 and detected H +NII emission lines. This WAT radio galaxy has been recently studied by Jetha et al. ( ), too.

Notice that the first and the second brightest galaxies of the cluster (galaxy IDs 91 and 99; hereafter we refer to them as BCMI and BCMII) are assigned to KMM3 and KMM1 groups. In particular, BCMI has a velocity very close to the mean velocity of its host KMM3 system, while it shows evidence of peculiarity according to the Indicator test by Gebhardt Beers ( , at the >95 .l.) with respect to the whole system.
As a further support to the merging scenario, we notice that the BCMI of A610 is also a bright radio source with a WAT structure.

gmw_00011480.name: BCMII
gmw_00011480.overview: BCMII is the second-brightest cluster galaxy in the galaxy cluster Abell 610 [arXiv:0806.2969]. It is hosted within a small, low-velocity group that is likely merging with another group associated with the cluster's brightest galaxy [arXiv:0806.2969].

sources.summary: The paper treats BCMII as A610 second-brightest cluster galaxy, assigning it to KMM1 and linking its group to the core bimodality and possible relic-producing merger.

quotes.quote: Notice that the first and the second brightest galaxies of the cluster (galaxy IDs 91 and 99; hereafter we refer to them as BCMI and BCMII) are assigned to KMM3 and KMM1 groups.

A third small, low velocity group hosts the BCMII. The analysis of the 2D galaxy distribution shows a bimodal distribution in the core -- elongated in the SE-NW direction and likely associated to BCMI and BCMII groups -- as well as the presence of an eastern group just outside the virialized region. In this chaotic scenario, it is not easy to unveil the event responsible for the relic. One possibility is that the relic be associated to the likely merger between the two structures corresponding to BCMI and BCMII.

gmw_00011481.name: BK 3N
gmw_00011481.overview: BK 3N is a young stellar system located at the western end of an H I tidal arm [arXiv:1002.0456]. It contains stars with a range of ages, and its youngest stars lack associated H-alpha emission [arXiv:1002.0456].

sources.summary: The paper treats BK 3N as an H I-cloud-embedded young stellar system whose CMD ages match earlier HST work and whose youngest stars lack associated H emission.

quotes.quote: As discussed in davidge08 , who had also used the MegaPrime/MegaCam imager to investigate the bright stellar content of the M81 group, the ages of the young stellar components estimated for Holmberg IX, BK 3N, and Garland from MegaCam CMDs are consistent with those obtained from CMDs obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope makarova02 , suggesting that blending is not severe, if any.

The CMDs show that the three newly reported stellar systems, similar to BK 3N and Garland, contain stars with properties consistent with a range of ages, i.e., they are not simple stellar populations. davidge08 derived a similar conclusion for three other young stellar systems of comparable extents and stellar densities located respectively close to the area identified as M81-West by sun05 and Holmberg IX. The ages of the youngest stars in BK 3N (Garland) are consistent with the absence (the presence) of associated H emission karachentsev07 .
BK 3N, located at the western end of the H i tidal arm, appears to be dominated by stars of similar ages to the other three clumps along the tidal arm.

gmw_00011482.name: RGB J1208+613
gmw_00011482.overview: RGB J1208+613 is an active galactic nucleus candidate located at a redshift of z = 0.275 [arXiv:2007.14670]. High-resolution radio observations reveal that the galaxy exhibits extended structure, including a southern hot spot that likely originates from the source itself [arXiv:2007.14670].

sources.summary: RGB J1208+613 is singled out among the MAGN candidates as a z=0.275 galaxy whose FIRST and NVSS comparison shows extended radio structure with a likely source-associated southern hot spot.

quotes.quote: The only case for which this test is possible, among our seven candidates, comes from the comparison of the low-resolution NVSS image and the high-resolution FIRST observation of RGB J1208+613 (4FGL J1208.4+6121), a galaxy located at z = 0.275 . This comparison, illustrated in Figure <first> , reveals that the extended structure is produced by the presence of two bright hot spots, symmetrically distributed around the source. While the northern feature is probably associated with the emission of a nearby quasar, SDSS J120838.85+612157.8 located at z = 0.525 , the southern one appears as the termination of an extended emission, without other counterparts, that likely originated from RGB J1208+613 itself.

gmw_00011483.name: HCG 16-LSB1
gmw_00011483.overview: HCG 16-LSB1 is a low-mass tidal dwarf galaxy with a very low surface brightness [arXiv:2105.01662]. It is characterized by a young stellar population and a high metallicity that suggests it formed from the pre-enriched gas of the HCG 16 group [arXiv:2105.01662].

sources.summary: HCG 16-LSB1 is modeled as a young, metal-rich, low-mass tidal dwarf from pre-enriched HCG 16 gas; if it survives, fading will leave a high-H I object resembling a dark galaxy.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:SED shows the best fitting model to the SED of HCG 16-LSB1 with 57.9 ^ +21.7 _ 9.4 Myr and [ Fe/H ] = 0.16 ^ +0.43 _ 0.41 (solid blue line). All photometric points fit well with the model except for the one corresponding to the z band. We think that this could be due to background sky fluctuations that are stronger in this band, producing a higher uncertainty due to the very low surface brightness of HCG 16-LSB1. The stellar-mass-to-light ratio is M/L _ V-band = 0.073 ^ +0.097 _ 0.059 _ , giving a stellar mass of M _ = 4.2 ^ +5.6 _ 3.4 10 ^6 M _ for HCG 16-LSB1. The metallicity of HCG 16-LSB1 of [ Fe/H ] = 0.16 ^ +0.43 _ 0.41 is much higher than what would be expected for a dwarf galaxy of its stellar mass, which is typically within the range [ Fe/H ] = [ 2.0, -1.5 ] 2003AJ....125.1926G, 2013ApJ...779..102K, 2017A&A...606A.115H .
